insights.title: Added memory fencing tests to ensure prefetch safety
insights.problem: Prefetched memory could be misinterpreted by the model as user input if not fenced correctly.
insights.root_cause: Lack of unit tests for fenced injection and sanitize behavior allowed regressions.
insights.solution: Added tests for build_memory_context_block, sanitize_context, and combined prompt behavior to guarantee the fence isolates recalled content.
insights.lesson_learned: Edge cases involving model prompt injection require unit tests that assert both sanitization and positioning relative to user text.

insights.title: Add tests for sanitization of skill-derived command keys
insights.problem: Previously missing tests allowed invalid characters to slip through in generated command keys.
insights.root_cause: No unit tests asserted that special characters are removed or that names that sanitize to empty are dropped.
insights.solution: Add tests that simulate SKILL.md files with names containing +, /, all-special characters and assert scan_skill_commands returns safe keys or skips them.
insights.lesson_learned: Coverage for platform-specific formatting rules prevents regressions; when adding sanitization logic, add positive and negative test cases.

insights.title: Simulate failed JSON dump to verify atomic write preserves previous cache

insights.root_cause: Write operations can fail after truncation if not atomic.
insights.solution: Monkeypatch json.dump to raise after writing partial data and assert load_directory() returns previous content.
insights.lesson_learned: Tests for file cache writing should simulate IO failures to validate atomicity.

insights.problem: Previously missing tests for BadRequest thread-not-found fallback and correct handling of other BadRequest and network errors.

insights.title: Comprehensive tests for OAuth adapter behavior and concurrency
insights.problem: OAuth flows are stateful and networked; need to test storage, corruption handling, callback isolation, port allocation, and non-interactive environments.
insights.root_cause: High complexity of OAuth flows and platform differences.
insights.solution: Added tests covering HermesTokenStorage, pre-registered client storage, scope passing, handler isolation, corrupt token/client handling, port allocation uniqueness, and browser open heuristics.
insights.lesson_learned: OAuth adapters must be validated with unit tests that simulate filesystem edge cases and HTTP callback behaviors; ensure non-interactive environments fail gracefully.

insights.title: Top-level send failure redaction test
insights.problem: Ensure that exceptions bubbled up to the tool entrypoint also have secrets redacted.
insights.root_cause: Model runner or underlying transport may raise errors including URL tokens that need sanitization.
insights.solution: Added test that stubs model_tools._run_async to raise a RuntimeError containing a URL with access_token and asserts send_message_tool output does not leak token and contains masked token representation.
insights.lesson_learned: Tool entrypoints must sanitize exceptions before returning user-visible JSON; tests should cover this surface.

acp_adapter/session.py.short_description: ACP session manager mapping ACP sessions to Hermes AIAgents
acp_adapter/session.py.description: Manages ACP (agent-client-protocol) sessions by creating, persisting, restoring, forking and deleting SessionState objects that encapsulate an AIAgent instance and conversation history. It persists sessions to a SessionDB for durability, restores sessions after restarts, and wires task-specific cwd overrides for tool execution.

key_constructs.name: SessionState
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass holding per-session agent, cwd, model, history and cancel event

callers.context: def _handle_slash_command(self, text: str, state: SessionState) -> str | None:
def _cmd_help(self, args: str, state: SessionState) -> str:
def _cmd_model(self, args: str, state: SessionState) -> str:
def _cmd_tools(self, args: str, state: SessionState) -> str:
def _cmd_context(self, args: str, state: SessionState) -> str:
def _cmd_reset(self, args: str, state: SessionState) -> str:
def _cmd_compact(self, args: str, state: SessionState) -> str:
def _cmd_version(self, args: str, state: SessionState) -> str:

key_constructs.name: SessionManager
key_constructs.purpose: Thread-safe manager providing create/get/remove/fork/list and persistence for ACP sessions

key_constructs.name: _persist
key_constructs.purpose: Persist an in-memory session and its messages to the SessionDB

key_constructs.name: _restore
key_constructs.purpose: Restore a session from the SessionDB and recreate its AIAgent

key_constructs.name: _make_agent
key_constructs.purpose: Factory that instantiates an AIAgent with resolved runtime/provider config

key_behaviors: creates and persists ACP-managed agent sessions
restores sessions from DB after restart
forks sessions and updates session working directories

insights.title: Add tests for per-group Feishu policies and admin bypass
insights.problem: New per-group policies introduced required verification across modes (allowlist/blacklist/admin_only/disabled) and admin bypass behavior.
insights.root_cause: Complex policy matrix needed regression tests to ensure correctness.
insights.solution: Add multiple focused tests verifying gating by chat_id and policies, admin bypass, and default policy fallback.
insights.lesson_learned: Policy features require exhaustive tests across combinations of allow/deny and admin overrides.

insights.title: Deduplicate EventDispatcherHandler mock builder
insights.problem: Multiple tests constructed identical mock builders inline, leading to duplication.
insights.root_cause: Repeated setup of mock handler builder in many tests.
insights.solution: Added helper _mock_event_dispatcher_builder(mock_handler_class) used by tests to centralize builder creation.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize repeated test fixture setup to reduce boilerplate and make future changes easier.

insights.title: Add regression tests for reconnect override reapplication and disconnect cleanup
insights.problem: No tests to validate that runtime websocket overrides persist after SDK configure or that thread loop cleanup happens correctly.
insights.root_cause: Complex threading/SDK interactions were previously untested.
insights.solution: Added tests including fake WS client to assert configure was called and runtime overrides re-applied, and tests ensuring event handler is rebuilt and _on_message_event path is used for webhook dispatch.
insights.lesson_learned: Add unit tests that simulate SDK behavior (e.g., configure calls) to validate runtime override application and thread/loop lifecycle.

insights.title: Tests for Anthropic adapter model output limits and clamping
insights.problem: Adapter default behavior for max_tokens changed to use model-native limits and clamp to context_length; tests needed to assert values.
insights.root_cause: Refactoring of default max_tokens required test coverage.
insights.solution: Add tests for multiple model IDs, date-stamped variants, explicit override, and context_length clamping.
insights.lesson_learned: When encoding provider-specific constants into adapters, add exhaustive tests for common variants and edge cases (dated IDs, suffixes).

insights.title: Read Python package version from pyproject.toml in Nix package
insights.problem: Version was hardcoded in nix packaging and drifted from pyproject.
insights.root_cause: Hardcoded string instead of reading canonical source of truth.
insights.solution: Use builtins.fromTOML(readFile ../pyproject.toml).project.version.
insights.lesson_learned: Avoid duplication of version strings between packaging layers; derive from pyproject to keep packaging in sync.

insights.title: Update blogwatcher skill to forked blogwatcher-cli with Docker, SSRF/SQL protections and OPML
insights.problem: Upstream blogwatcher lacked Docker support and security hardening.
insights.root_cause: Original upstream didn't provide features desired by project contributors (Docker-friendly DB persist, SSRF protection, scraping fallback).
insights.solution: Replaced references to Hyaxia/blogwatcher with JulienTant/blogwatcher-cli and updated docs and commands to blogwatcher-cli; added Docker usage, database env variable docs, migration notes.
insights.lesson: When third-party tools evolve or forks provide critical features (security, deployability), update embedded skill docs and prerequisite command names to match new tooling to reduce user friction.

insights.title: Add llm-wiki skill that uses skill config interface

insights.solution: Added a comprehensive SKILL.md for llm-wiki that declares metadata.hermes.config with key wiki.path and documentation of behavior.
insights.lesson_learned: Providing a concrete skill example helps validate migration, prompting, and runtime injection logic.

agent/redact.py.short_description: Regex-based secret redaction for logs and outputs
agent/redact.py.description: Provides deterministic, regex-driven masking of API keys, tokens, private keys, connection strings, phone numbers and other sensitive values before they reach logs or external outputs. It centralizes patterns and replacement rules and exposes a logging Formatter that applies redaction to all formatted log messages.

key_constructs.name: _REDACT_ENABLED
key_constructs.purpose: Process-level flag snapshot controlling whether redaction is active

key_constructs.name: redact_sensitive_text
key_constructs.purpose: Apply all redaction regexes to a provided text block and return masked output

key_constructs.name: _mask_token
key_constructs.purpose: Mask token strings with short/long token heuristics for debuggability

key_constructs.name: RedactingFormatter
key_constructs.purpose: logging.Formatter subclass that redacts secrets from formatted log records

key_constructs.name: _PREFIX_RE
key_constructs.purpose: Compiled regex matching known API key/token prefixes

key_behaviors: masks API keys and tokens in arbitrary text
redacts private key blocks and DB connection passwords
provides a log formatter that sanitizes messages before writing

insights.title: Extend secret-prefix redaction patterns
insights.problem: New providers and services use API key prefixes that were not included in the redaction patterns, risking accidental secret leakage in logs or outputs.
insights.root_cause: Initial prefix list was incomplete as new providers or services were added (Groq, Matrix, RetainDB, Hindsight, Mem0, ByteRover).
insights.solution: Added regex prefix patterns for gsk_, syt_, retaindb_, hsk-, mem0_, brv_.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep redaction patterns in sync with newly added providers and validate redaction by tests or fuzzing; prefer generic pattern rules where possible.

agent/skill_utils.py.short_description: Lightweight skill metadata utilities and frontmatter parsing
agent/skill_utils.py.description: Utility helpers for discovering, parsing and filtering skill metadata (SKILL.md frontmatter) without importing heavy subsystems. It supports YAML frontmatter parsing, platform compatibility checks, disabled-skill resolution, external skills directories, and extraction/resolution of skill-declared config variables.

key_constructs.name: parse_frontmatter
key_constructs.purpose: Extract YAML frontmatter and body from a markdown SKILL.md file

key_constructs.name: skill_matches_platform
key_constructs.purpose: Determine whether a skill's declared platforms match the current OS

key_constructs.name: get_external_skills_dirs
key_constructs.purpose: Read and validate external skills directories from config.yaml

key_constructs.name: discover_all_skill_config_vars
key_constructs.purpose: Scan enabled skills and collect declared configuration variables for UI or resolution

key_constructs.name: resolve_skill_config_values
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve stored or default values for skill-declared config vars from config.yaml

key_behaviors: parses skill frontmatter and extracts metadata
filters skills by platform and disabled lists
collects and resolves skill-declared configuration values

insights.title: Skill configuration interface: declare config vars in SKILL.md frontmatter
insights.problem: Skills had no formal mechanism to declare non-secret configuration values (paths/preferences) that should be prompted and injected at runtime.
insights.root_cause: No metadata-parsing path to extract config declarations from skill frontmatter.
insights.solution: Added extract_skill_config_vars(frontmatter), discover_all_skill_config_vars(), resolve_skill_config_values(), SKILL_CONFIG_PREFIX and helper _resolve_dotpath to read skill-declared config values from ~/.hermes/config.yaml under skills.config.* namespace and expand paths.
insights.lesson_learned: Skill authors should be allowed to declare non-secret settings; the platform must discover, prompt and inject them at load time; store them under a well-known prefix to avoid name collisions.

insights.title: Introduce lightweight skill utilities to decouple skills metadata logic
insights.problem: Skills parsing logic was duplicated and heavy imports in tools triggered large import chains when building prompts.
insights.root_cause: tools.skills_tool contained frontmatter parsing and platform checks; prompt_builder imported it and pulled tool registry.
insights.solution: Add agent/skill_utils.py exposing parse_frontmatter, skill_matches_platform, get_disabled_skill_names, extract_skill_conditions, extract_skill_description, iter_skill_index_files, and yaml loader; refactor caller modules to use this shared lightweight module.
insights.lesson_learned: Encapsulate small, dependency-light utilities in a module safe to import at module-level to avoid expensive side effects.

gateway/stream_consumer.py.short_description: Async consumer bridging sync agent token deltas to platform message edits
gateway/stream_consumer.py.description: Receives synchronous streaming deltas from the agent (thread-safe) and forwards them into an asyncio loop which buffers, rate-limits, and progressively edits a single platform message (send/edit) to present streamed content. It handles segment boundaries, platform limits, media tag cleanup, and disables editing fallback when edits fail.

key_constructs.name: StreamConsumerConfig
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass holding runtime tuning parameters for edit interval, buffer thresholds and cursor

key_constructs.name: GatewayStreamConsumer
key_constructs.purpose: Main consumer that queues deltas from agent threads and edits/sends messages via adapter methods

key_constructs.name: on_delta
key_constructs.purpose: Thread-safe callback to enqueue streamed text or segment sentinel

key_constructs.name: run
key_constructs.purpose: Async loop that drains queue, decides edits, splits on overflow and finalizes the stream

key_constructs.name: _clean_for_display
key_constructs.purpose: Strip MEDIA: directives and internal markers before sending to platforms

key_behaviors: buffers and rate-limits streamed tokens
progressively edits a single message on messaging platforms
finalizes streams and handles segment boundaries for tool output

insights.title: Create new message after tool boundary when streaming
insights.problem: When streaming, the consumer edited a single message for all deltas, while tool-progress messages were sent as separate messages. Edits left the final answer above tool progress lines (hard to find).
insights.root_cause: Stream consumer ignored stream_delta_callback(None) signals that mark tool boundaries; treated deltas as continuous edits.
insights.solution: Introduce _NEW_SEGMENT sentinel and handle on_delta(None) to enqueue a segment break. On segment break, finalize current message (remove cursor), reset _message_id and accumulated text so the next chunk creates a fresh message below any tool messages. Update logic to consider segment breaks when deciding whether to edit or finalize.
insights.lesson_learned: Streaming consumers must respect segment boundaries (tool calls) so final outputs appear in a logical order relative to tool progress messages. Use explicit sentinel events and clear state in a thread-safe manner.

hermes_cli/memory_setup.py.short_description: Interactive memory provider setup and status CLI
hermes_cli/memory_setup.py.description: Provides an interactive curses (fallback to text) wizard to discover, configure and activate memory provider plugins, install pip/native dependencies, write secrets to .env and activation to config.yaml, and show provider status. It also includes non-interactive helpers for installing dependencies and a status command to inspect current memory configuration.
key_behaviors: discovers available memory provider plugins
guides user through provider configuration and saves settings
installs declared Python and external dependencies

tools/file_operations.py.short_description: Shell-backed file read/write/patch/search operations for terminal backends
tools/file_operations.py.description: Abstracts file operations (read, write, patch, search) over arbitrary terminal backends by translating them into shell commands; supports binary detection, pagination, lint checks, unified diffs and safety deny-lists. It exposes an interface FileOperations and a ShellFileOperations concrete implementation along with rich result dataclasses for consistent tool outputs.

key_constructs.name: FileOperations
key_constructs.purpose: Abstract base interface defining read/write/patch/search operations for terminals

key_constructs.name: ShellFileOperations
key_constructs.purpose: Shell-command-based implementation that runs commands via a terminal_env.execute() backend

key_constructs.name: ReadResult
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing read_file outcomes with metadata and content

key_constructs.name: WRITE_DENIED_PATHS
key_constructs.purpose: Set of sensitive absolute paths that writing is forbidden to touch

key_constructs.name: MAX_FILE_SIZE
key_constructs.purpose: Configured threshold used to reject or truncate large file reads

key_behaviors: reads files with pagination and binary detection
writes and patches files while enforcing deny-lists
generates unified diffs and runs linters via shell tools

insights.title: Remove unused _read_image helper
insights.problem: A 64-line private helper to read images in ShellFileOperations was unused after refactors.
insights.root_cause: File read behavior consolidated elsewhere; helper lost its callers.
insights.solution: Removed the _read_image method to reduce code surface.
insights.lesson: Consolidate I/O helpers and remove private helpers that no longer have callers; keep one canonical implementation for binary/image handling.

insights.title: Portable base64 encoding for image reading on macOS
insights.problem: Using base64 -w 0 is GNU-specific and fails on macOS where base64 wraps output by default.
insights.root_cause: Command used GNU base64 option -w that isn't available on BSD/macOS base64.
insights.solution: Pipe base64 output through tr -d '\n' to strip newlines portably across platforms.
insights.lesson_learned: When shelling out to system utilities, prefer portable command combinations or use Python stdlib to avoid cross-platform differences.

tools/browser_tool.py.short_description: Browser automation abstraction supporting local and cloud backends
tools/browser_tool.py.description: Provides a browser tool layer that chooses between local headless Chromium, Browserbase, Browser Use and other cloud providers and exposes navigation, snapshot and interaction functionality to the agent. It manages session lifecycle, inactivity cleanup, provider resolution and CDP endpoint normalization while integrating safety checks and optional recording/cleanup threads.

key_constructs.name: _get_cloud_provider
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve and cache the configured cloud browser provider (Browserbase/Browser Use/Firecrawl)

key_constructs.name: _is_local_mode
key_constructs.purpose: Decide whether to use a local browser backend or cloud provider

key_constructs.name: _resolve_cdp_override
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize user-supplied CDP URLs into concrete websocket endpoints

key_constructs.name: _start_browser_cleanup_thread
key_constructs.purpose: Start background thread that cleans up inactive browser sessions

key_constructs.name: Cloud provider registry (_PROVIDER_REGISTRY)
key_constructs.purpose: Map provider keys to provider classes used by the tool

key_behaviors: selects appropriate browser backend (local vs cloud)
creates and cleans up per-task browser sessions
resolves CDP endpoints and applies SSRF/config protections

insights.title: Remove browser_close tool; rely on auto-cleanup
insights.problem: browser_close was rarely used and redundant with existing automatic cleanup paths; maintaining it added schema noise and an extra tool call in most browser-enabled API calls.
insights.root_cause: cleanup_browser() is already invoked at task end and by background reaper; explicit calls were redundant.
insights.solution: Removed browser_close tool and its registry entry; extended cleanup_browser() to also clean up Camofox sessions; updated browser_navigate to auto-return a compact snapshot; updated browser_scroll to repeat scroll 5x for meaningful movement.
insights.lesson: If automatic lifecycle management is comprehensive, remove rarely used manual cleanup tools to reduce API surface and tool invocation overhead. Ensure auto-cleanup covers all backends (e.g., Camofox) before removing manual close.

insights.title: Remove get_active_browser_sessions (unused)
insights.problem: Helper to enumerate active browser sessions had no callers.
insights.root_cause: Session tracking consolidated and other entry points used.
insights.solution: Removed get_active_browser_sessions.
insights.lesson: Drop helpers that hide internal state unless there's a well-defined public API use; tests and external integrations should rely on documented APIs.

insights.title: Register Firecrawl provider in browser provider registry
insights.problem: New provider must be discoverable by browser tools and setup flows.
insights.root_cause: Provider registration was missing.
insights.solution: Imported and registered FirecrawlProvider in _PROVIDER_REGISTRY and updated checks/documentation to treat multiple cloud providers equivalently.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding providers, ensure registry, CLI setup options, and runtime checks treat them as first-class cloud providers to avoid misreporting availability.

agent/context_references.py.short_description: Parse and expand @-style context references into attachable content
agent/context_references.py.description: Parses inline @ references (file:/folder:/git:/url:/diff/staged) in user messages, validates workspace bounds and sensitive paths, fetches/expands referenced content and attaches summaries to the prompt while enforcing token limits and safety. It supports file range extraction, folder listings, git diffs, URL fetching via web_extract, and blocks sensitive credential files.

key_constructs.name: ContextReference
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing a parsed @ reference with kind, target and span

key_constructs.name: ContextReferenceResult
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass containing the processed message, warnings, expanded blocks and token count

key_constructs.name: parse_context_references
key_constructs.purpose: Find @ reference tokens in a message and return structured ContextReference objects

key_constructs.name: preprocess_context_references_async
key_constructs.purpose: Asynchronously expand references, fetch URL content, enforce token limits and produce a final message

key_constructs.name: _ensure_reference_path_allowed
key_constructs.purpose: Security check to prevent attaching sensitive home or Hermes files

key_behaviors: parses @file/@folder/@git/@url references in user input
attaches referenced content or warnings to the agent prompt
blocks sensitive paths and enforces token budget limits

insights.title: Add timeouts to subprocess calls to avoid indefinite hang
insights.problem: Subprocess.run calls for git and ripgrep could hang indefinitely on large repos or slow outputs, blocking the agent.
insights.root_cause: No timeout passed to subprocess.run, so long-running or stalled external commands could freeze agent execution.
insights.solution: Add timeout=30 to git subprocess in _expand_git_reference() and timeout=10 to ripgrep calls in _rg_files(); handle subprocess.TimeoutExpired by returning error message or None fallback.
insights.lesson_learned: Always set timeouts for external process calls and provide graceful fallbacks; avoid blocking the main agent indefinitely on external tool latencies.

agent/display.py.short_description: CLI display utilities: spinners, tool previews and inline diffs
agent/display.py.description: Contains pure presentation helpers used by the CLI and tool execution: building one-line tool previews, capturing before-write snapshots, generating and rendering unified diffs inline with ANSI styling, and spinner/skin lookup helpers. These functions are UI-only and intentionally avoid agent dependencies.

key_constructs.name: build_tool_preview
key_constructs.purpose: Construct a short, human-friendly preview string for a tool call's primary argument

key_constructs.name: capture_local_edit_snapshot
key_constructs.purpose: Capture before-state of filesystem paths to later compute inline diffs after tool runs

key_constructs.name: extract_edit_diff
key_constructs.purpose: Extract a unified diff from patch/write results and snapshots for inline display

callers.context: assert extract_edit_diff("web_search", '{"diff": "--- a\\n+++ b\\n"}') is None

key_constructs.name: LocalEditSnapshot
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass storing pre-edit file contents for diff generation

key_constructs.name: _render_inline_unified_diff
key_constructs.purpose: Render unified diff lines with ANSI color segments for CLI output

key_behaviors: renders concise tool previews for CLI transcripts
produces and displays inline diffs for file-writing tools
integrates skin/theme values for spinner/faces and emojis

insights.title: Cap context pressure percentage at 100%
insights.problem: Context pressure pct_int could exceed 100 and show values like '109% to compaction', which is confusing.
insights.root_cause: percentage integer derived from compaction_progress wasn't capped, even though bar fill used min().
insights.solution: Use min(int(compaction_progress*100), 100) for pct_int in both CLI and gateway display functions.

agent/skill_commands.py.short_description: Slash-command integration and skill invocation helpers
agent/skill_commands.py.description: Common helpers used by CLI and gateway to scan installed skills, normalize slash-command names, load skill payloads (SKILL.md), inject skill-declared config values, and build formatted user/system messages for invoking skills. Also includes utilities for preloading skills into session prompts and building default plan file paths for /plan.

key_constructs.name: scan_skill_commands
key_constructs.purpose: Scan local and external skills directories to build a mapping of /command → skill metadata

key_constructs.name: build_skill_invocation_message
key_constructs.purpose: Load a skill and format the activation note + skill content into a message suitable for the model

key_constructs.name: build_preloaded_skills_prompt
key_constructs.purpose: Load multiple skills for session preloading and return combined prompt text and lists of loaded/missing skills

key_constructs.name: _load_skill_payload
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to load skill content via tools.skills_tool.skill_view and return parsed payload

key_constructs.name: build_plan_path
key_constructs.purpose: Generate a timestamped default path for storing /plan outputs under the workspace

key_behaviors: discovers and registers slash-style skill commands
loads and formats skill content into agent prompts
injects resolved skill config values into prompts

insights.title: Inject resolved skill config values into loaded skill messages
insights.problem: Agents loading skills had no way to inform the model of the user's configured skill settings.
insights.root_cause: Skill runtime didn't include config values when composing skill payloads.
insights.solution: Added _inject_skill_config which parses the skill frontmatter, extracts config declarations, resolves their values, and appends a '[Skill config (from ~/.hermes/config.yaml): ...]' block to the skill message parts so the model sees configured settings.
insights.lesson_learned: When configuration influences runtime behavior, inject resolved settings into the runtime payload (in a non-secret, readable block) so the model can act accordingly without needing to read config files itself.

insights.title: Skill-derived Telegram command names contained invalid characters
insights.problem: Skill names containing characters like +, /, @, . produced command keys with invalid characters that caused Telegram setMyCommands to fail (Bot_command_invalid).
insights.root_cause: scan_skill_commands produced raw command keys by lowercasing and replacing spaces/underscores with hyphens but did not strip other punctuation; downstream code assumed names would already be Telegram-safe.
insights.solution: Sanitize the generated command key in scan_skill_commands by removing characters outside [a-z0-9-], collapse multiple hyphens, trim edges, and skip commands that sanitize to an empty string.
insights.lesson_learned: When generating identifiers for external systems, centralize and validate format constraints early (at source), and skip entries that sanitize to empty. Don't assume human-friendly names are valid for platform APIs.

gateway/channel_directory.py.short_description: Cached mapping of reachable messaging channels/contacts per platform
gateway/channel_directory.py.description: Builds and maintains a cached directory of reachable channels and contacts across connected messaging platforms, periodically persists it to ~/.hermes/channel_directory.json, and provides lookup/resolve helpers used by send_message and the model. It can enumerate Discord/Slack channels via adapters and falls back to session histories for platforms that cannot be enumerated.

key_constructs.name: build_channel_directory
key_constructs.purpose: Aggregate channel/contact listings from platform adapters and session history and write the directory to disk

key_constructs.name: resolve_channel_name
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve a human-friendly channel label to a numeric platform ID using exact and prefix matching

key_constructs.name: load_directory
key_constructs.purpose: Load the cached channel directory JSON from disk

key_constructs.name: format_directory_for_display
key_constructs.purpose: Render the channel directory into a human-readable string for the model

key_constructs.name: DIRECTORY_PATH
key_constructs.purpose: Filesystem path to the persisted channel directory JSON

key_behaviors: builds a platform-aware directory of reachable channels
resolves user-friendly channel names to platform IDs
persists and loads directory data for model and tools

insights.title: Use atomic writes for channel directory cache to avoid truncation/corruption
insights.problem: Non-atomic JSON writes could leave the cache file truncated or corrupted if the write failed mid-stream (e.g., disk full), losing previous valid cache contents.
insights.root_cause: Direct open/write of the cache file without atomic swap.
insights.solution: Use utils.atomic_json_write(DIRECTORY_PATH, directory) to write safely, and add test that simulates json.dump raising to ensure previous cache is preserved.
insights.lesson_learned: Filesystem caches must be written atomically; always prefer write-then-rename strategies or helpers that guarantee atomicity to avoid corrupting persisted caches.

hermes_cli/claw.py.short_description: OpenClaw → Hermes migration and cleanup CLI commands
hermes_cli/claw.py.description: Provides commands to migrate user data from legacy OpenClaw installations into Hermes (interactive or dry-run), and to archive leftover OpenClaw directories to avoid state fragmentation. It dynamically loads the migration script from an optional-skill location, presents summaries, and offers to rename the source directory post-migration.
key_behaviors: runs OpenClaw → Hermes migration (dry-run or execute)
archives/renames legacy OpenClaw directories
prints migration reports and handles conflicts/overwrite options

hermes_cli/model_normalize.py.description: Translates user-supplied model identifiers into the formats required by various provider APIs (aggregator vendor/model slugs, dot→hyphen conversions for Anthropic/OpenCode, DeepSeek canonical mapping, etc.). It centralizes vendor detection and normalization rules so callers can request a model name appropriate for a given provider.

key_constructs.name: normalize_model_for_provider
key_constructs.purpose: Main entry point: translate a model name into the target provider's expected identifier

key_constructs.name: detect_vendor
key_constructs.purpose: Infer a vendor slug from a bare model name using prefix heuristics

key_constructs.name: _prepend_vendor
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure vendor/ prefix is present for aggregator providers

key_constructs.name: _normalize_for_deepseek
key_constructs.purpose: Map arbitrary DeepSeek model aliases to the two canonical identifiers

key_constructs.name: model_display_name
key_constructs.purpose: Return a human-friendly display name by stripping vendor prefixes

key_behaviors: converts model names to provider-specific formats
detects vendor from model tokens and applies aggregator prefixes
maps special-case providers (DeepSeek) to canonical IDs

insights.title: Do not mangle opencode-go model names (preserve dots)
insights.problem: OpenCode Go models (minimax-m2.7, glm-4.5, kimi-k2.5) were normalized by converting dots to hyphens, producing invalid model identifiers and auth/401 errors.
insights.root_cause: opencode-go was mistakenly included in the provider set that converts dots to hyphens (_DOT_TO_HYPHEN_PROVIDERS).
insights.solution: Remove opencode-go from _DOT_TO_HYPHEN_PROVIDERS; document that OpenCode Go preserves dots and ensure normalization logic preserves them for that provider.
insights.lesson_learned: Provider-specific normalization rules are subtle; keep a curated provider-to-behavior mapping and add unit tests covering edge cases (dot vs hyphen conventions).

hermes_cli/providers.py.short_description: Provider identity and resolution layer (models.dev + Hermes overlays)
hermes_cli/providers.py.description: Single source of truth for provider metadata: it merges models.dev provider info with Hermes-specific overlays and user-config entries to produce ProviderDef objects. It exposes normalization, provider lookup, label resolution, API-mode determination and helper functions used across runtime provider resolution.

key_constructs.name: HermesOverlay
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing Hermes-specific provider metadata layered on models.dev

key_constructs.name: ProviderDef
key_constructs.purpose: Merged provider definition used by the runtime (transport, env vars, base_url, auth)

key_constructs.name: get_provider
key_constructs.purpose: Lookup provider data by id merging models.dev and Hermes overlays

key_constructs.name: resolve_provider_full
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve provider using built-in catalog first, then user-config, then models.dev fallback

key_constructs.name: determine_api_mode
key_constructs.purpose: Determine wire/API mode (chat_completions, anthropic_messages, codex_responses) for a provider or URL

key_behaviors: resolves canonical provider IDs and aliases
merges models.dev metadata with Hermes overlays and user config
determines transport/API mode for provider endpoints

tools/env_passthrough.py.short_description: Session-scoped allowlist for environment variables to passthrough to sandboxes
tools/env_passthrough.py.description: Manages a context-local allowlist of environment variable names that are permitted to pass into sandboxed execution environments (code/terminal) even when sandboxes strip secrets by default. Sources include skill-declared required_environment_variables and user config; functions support registering, checking, clearing and caching config-based passthrough entries.

key_constructs.name: register_env_passthrough
key_constructs.purpose: Register environment variable names to be allowed in the current session/context

key_constructs.name: is_env_passthrough
key_constructs.purpose: Check whether a variable name is allowed via session registrations or config

callers.context: from tools.env_passthrough import clear_env_passthrough, is_env_passthrough, reset_config_cache

key_constructs.name: get_all_passthrough
key_constructs.purpose: Return the union of session-registered and config-declared passthrough variable names

key_constructs.name: clear_env_passthrough
key_constructs.purpose: Clear session-scoped allowlist (used on session reset)

callers.context: from tools.env_passthrough import clear_env_passthrough, is_env_passthrough, reset_config_cache

key_constructs.name: _allowed_env_vars_var
key_constructs.purpose: ContextVar backing the session-local set of passthrough environment variable names

key_behaviors: registers skill-requested env vars for sandbox passthrough
consults user config to allow additional variables
prevents cross-session leakage via ContextVar isolation

insights.title: Isolate env passthrough registry per context using ContextVar
insights.problem: Global set of allowed env var names could leak between sessions and allow unintended env passthrough.
insights.root_cause: Module-level mutable set _allowed_env_vars used across concurrent sessions in the gateway pipeline.
insights.solution: Back the allowed set with a ContextVar (_allowed_env_vars_var) and use _get_allowed() accessor; register/get/clear functions use context-local set and clear_env_passthrough resets it.
insights.lesson_learned: Session-scoped security controls (env passthrough) must be context-isolated; ContextVars provide a simple isolation mechanism for async/session flows.

tools/mcp_oauth.py.short_description: OAuth2.1 PKCE browser-based flow glue for MCP servers
tools/mcp_oauth.py.description: Implements the client-side glue to perform a browser-based OAuth 2.1 authorization code flow with PKCE for MCP servers. Provides persistent token/client storage on disk, a temporary local callback HTTP server to capture redirects, and returns an httpx.Auth-compatible OAuthClientProvider for MCP SDK integration.

key_constructs.name: HermesTokenStorage
key_constructs.purpose: Persist OAuth tokens and client registration info to per-server JSON files.

key_constructs.name: build_oauth_auth
key_constructs.purpose: Main entrypoint that configures storage, redirect/port, client metadata and returns an OAuthClientProvider.

key_constructs.name: _make_callback_handler
key_constructs.purpose: Factory that builds an HTTP callback handler class and mutable result dict to capture authorization code/state.

key_constructs.name: _redirect_handler
key_constructs.purpose: Async helper that prints the authorization URL and tries to open it in the user's browser.

key_constructs.name: _wait_for_callback
key_constructs.purpose: Async polling helper that waits for the local callback server to receive the OAuth authorization code.

key_behaviors: persist OAuth tokens and client metadata across restarts
open browser or display authorization URL for user sign-in
capture authorization code via local HTTP callback and hand off to MCP SDK

insights.title: Full MCP OAuth 2.1 PKCE client support with persistent token storage
insights.problem: MCP tool needed a robust OAuth adapter (PKCE, DCR, token persistence) with concurrency-safe callback handlers.
insights.root_cause: Previously, mcp_tool had an auth: oauth hook expected but no dedicated adapter implemented; concurrent flows and token storage patterns were not standardized.
insights.solution: Implemented HermesTokenStorage (persist tokens + client registration with 0o600 perms), callback handler factory to isolate per-flow state (safe for concurrent OAuth flows), integration with MCP SDK's OAuthClientProvider, non-interactive detection and warnings, path-traversal protection for server names, and remove_oauth_tokens cleanup API. build_oauth_auth writes pre-registered client_info if provided.
insights.lesson_learned: OAuth flows in CLI/server tools must persist credentials securely (file perms), isolate per-flow HTTP callback state to avoid cross-talk, and defend against path-traversal and non-interactive environments.

tools/send_message_tool.py.short_description: Cross-platform send_message tool for gateway and CLI
tools/send_message_tool.py.description: Exposes a send_message tool to list targets or send content across connected messaging platforms (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, etc.). Handles target parsing and resolution, message chunking, media handling (Telegram-first), config checks, duplicate-cron dedupe, and mirrors sent content back into gateway sessions.

key_constructs.name: SEND_MESSAGE_SCHEMA
key_constructs.purpose: JSON schema describing the send_message tool parameters for model-facing tool calling.

key_constructs.name: send_message_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level handler that dispatches 'list' or 'send' actions for the tool.

key_constructs.name: _send_to_platform
key_constructs.purpose: Routes and chunks messages to platform-specific sender functions and handles platform-level constraints.

key_constructs.name: _send_telegram
key_constructs.purpose: Sends messages and media via Telegram Bot API with formatting fallbacks and attachment handling.

key_constructs.name: _parse_target_ref
key_constructs.purpose: Parse various platform-specific target reference formats into chat/thread IDs and explicitness flag.

key_behaviors: list available messaging targets across platforms
resolve human-friendly channel names to platform IDs
send text and media to platform-specific APIs with chunking and fallbacks
mirror sent messages back into gateway sessions

insights.title: Redact query secrets in send_message errors and unify error payload format
insights.problem: Error messages propagated to users/models could leak secrets present in URLs or error texts (access tokens, api_key, signature in query or assignment forms).
insights.root_cause: Exception messages and HTTP responses were serialized directly into return payloads and logs without redaction.
insights.solution: Introduced redact_sensitive_text usage and additional regex-based sanitization (_URL_SECRET_QUERY_RE and _GENERIC_SECRET_ASSIGN_RE) to mask secrets in error strings; introduced helper _sanitize_error_text and _error returning standardized redacted error payload; applied to many send_* functions and top-level error handling.
insights.lesson_learned: Sanitize all user-visible error strings and logs that may include remote URLs or provider debug messages; centralize sanitization to avoid miss sites.

tools/skill_manager_tool.py.short_description: Agent-managed skill creation, editing and file management
tools/skill_manager_tool.py.description: Provides functions the agent uses to create, edit, patch, delete, and manage files for user skills stored under the skills directory. Includes validation for names, frontmatter, size limits, atomic writes, and integrates an optional security scanner to block or warn on unsafe generated skills.

key_constructs.name: _create_skill
key_constructs.purpose: Validate inputs and create a new skill directory with SKILL.md, performing atomic writes and security scanning.

key_constructs.name: _edit_skill
key_constructs.purpose: Replace SKILL.md for an existing skill with validation, atomic write and rollback on scan failure.

key_constructs.name: _patch_skill
key_constructs.purpose: Perform fuzzy find-and-replace in SKILL.md or supporting files with size and structure validation.

callers.context: result = _patch_skill("my-skill", "Do the thing.", "Do the new thing.")

key_constructs.name: _write_file
key_constructs.purpose: Add or overwrite supporting files (references/templates/scripts/assets) within a skill with atomic writes and size checks.

key_constructs.name: _validate_frontmatter
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure SKILL.md contains YAML frontmatter with required fields and non-empty body.

callers.context: assert _validate_frontmatter(content) == "SKILL.md frontmatter is not closed. Ensure you have a clos
assert _validate_frontmatter(content) == "SKILL.md must have content after the frontmatter (instruct

key_behaviors: create and persist new agent skills with proper frontmatter
edit or patch skill content safely with rollback on security issues
add or remove supporting files for skills

insights.title: Invalidate skills prompt cache on skill mutations
insights.problem: Adding/removing/modifying skills did not invalidate the skills prompt snapshot/cache, causing stale prompt data.
insights.root_cause: Cache invalidation hooks were missing in skill management paths.
insights.solution: Call clear_skills_system_prompt_cache(clear_snapshot=True) after successful create/edit/patch/delete/write_file/remove_file operations to force rebuild.
insights.lesson_learned: Any mutation path that affects cached data must explicitly invalidate caches; centralize invalidation to avoid stale state.

agent/auxiliary_client.py.short_description: Resolver and adapters for auxiliary (side-task) LLM/vision clients
agent/auxiliary_client.py.description: Centralized resolver and adapter layer that selects the best available backend for auxiliary tasks (context compression, web extraction, vision, etc.) and exposes OpenAI-style client shims. Implements adapters for Codex Responses, Anthropic, and handles credential pool selection, base_url resolution, streaming translation and usage shaping so callers use a uniform client API regardless of backend.

key_constructs.name: _select_pool_entry
key_constructs.purpose: Selects and validates a credential pool entry for a given provider.

key_constructs.name: _convert_content_for_responses
key_constructs.purpose: Convert chat.completions-style content blocks into Codex Responses API input format.

key_constructs.name: _CodexCompletionsAdapter
key_constructs.purpose: Adapter that sends chat-style messages through the Codex Responses API and synthesizes a chat.completions-like response.

key_constructs.name: CodexAuxiliaryClient
key_constructs.purpose: OpenAI-client-compatible wrapper exposing chat.completions.create() backed by Codex Responses.

key_constructs.name: _AnthropicCompletionsAdapter
key_constructs.purpose: Adapter that translates chat.completions calls into native Anthropic Messages API calls and normalizes responses.

key_constructs.name: AnthropicAuxiliaryClient
key_constructs.purpose: Wrapper exposing an OpenAI-style chat interface backed by a native Anthropic client.

key_behaviors: resolve and select best available auxiliary LLM/vision provider
translate chat-style calls to provider-specific APIs (Codex/Anthropic)
fallback across providers on payment/credit errors

insights.title: Use alternative Nous free-tier vision model when appropriate
insights.problem: Free-tier Nous accounts couldn't use paid auxiliary vision models; vision features would fail for free users.
insights.root_cause: Auxiliary model selection always picked the default paid model unless source==pool, without checking account tier.
insights.solution: Call check_nous_free_tier() and switch to _NOUS_FREE_TIER_VISION_MODEL when user is on free tier.
insights.lesson: Auxiliary client should adapt model choices to account capabilities (tier) to preserve functionality across free/paid accounts.

insights.title: Harden codex adapter to handle dict-shaped streamed items and function_call guarding
insights.problem: Streamed responses from Codex could include dict-shaped items instead of SDK objects; synthesis logic created plain-text outputs even when function_call events were present (collapsing tool-calls into fake text).
insights.root_cause: Extraction loop assumed attributes (getattr) and didn't account for dict entries; also didn't track presence of function_call events during streaming.
insights.solution: Add helper _item_get() that supports getattr or dict.get() for fields; track has_function_calls during streaming and skip text-delta synthesis if function_call events were streamed to avoid collapsing tool-call responses into plain text.
insights.lesson_learned: When consuming streamed SDK events, normalize item shapes (dict vs object) and track semantic event types (tool calls) to decide whether to synthesize text output; avoid producing misleading plain-text when actual tool calls occurred.

insights.title: Replace stale hermes login text with auth
insights.problem: Help messages referenced deprecated 'hermes login' command.
insights.root_cause: Previously deprecated command remained in messaging.
insights.solution: Replace references to 'hermes login' with 'hermes auth' in warning messages.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep user-visible messages and docs in-sync with CLI commands to avoid confusion.

insights.title: Update default auxiliary model mappings
insights.problem: Auxiliary client used older/less appropriate auxiliary model choices for some providers.
insights.root_cause: Static mapping _API_KEY_PROVIDER_AUX_MODELS had stale entries.
insights.solution: Set default gemini auxiliary model to gemini-3-flash-preview (and later updated in other commit to gemini-3-flash-preview after catalog update).
insights.lesson_learned: Keep auxiliary model defaults aligned with primary model catalog; when introducing a new provider, ensure aux mappings are reviewed.

insights.title: Configurable per-task timeouts from config.yaml
insights.problem: Timeouts for auxiliary model calls were hardcoded; users with slow local models (Ollama, llama.cpp) could not easily extend timeouts.
insights.root_cause: Timeout values were embedded as default parameters in call_llm/async_call_llm and specific callers.
insights.solution: Introduce _get_task_timeout(task) to read auxiliary.{task}.timeout from configuration and make call_llm/async_call_llm default timeout None so they auto-resolve from config when not explicitly set.
insights.lesson_learned: When a subsystem uses multiple logical tasks with different operational characteristics, move magic numbers to config with sensible defaults and allow explicit override at call sites.

insights.title: Guard LLM content access and add extract_content_or_reasoning
insights.problem: Multiple auxiliary LLM call sites assumed response.choices[0].message.content was non-null and used .strip(), causing AttributeError when content is None (reasoning-only models).
insights.root_cause: Some LLMs return content=None and provide reasoning in structured fields; call sites did .content.strip() unguarded.
insights.solution: Add extract_content_or_reasoning() to centralize content extraction and fallback to structured reasoning fields (reasoning, reasoning_content, reasoning_details). Replace .content.strip() uses with extractor and add retry logic when empty.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize non-obvious API normalization (content vs structured reasoning) into a single helper to avoid duplicated bug-prone code. Always guard against None for provider-variant responses.

insights.title: Neuter OpenAI SDK AsyncHttpxClientWrapper.__del__ and cleanup stale clients
insights.problem: Prompt-toolkit idle loop saw 'Event loop is closed' errors when AsyncOpenAI client wrappers scheduled aclose() onto a different/closed loop during GC, causing disruptive 'Press ENTER to continue' messages.
insights.root_cause: SDK __del__ scheduled aclose() on asyncio.get_running_loop(), which could be prompt_toolkit's loop while transports belonged to closed worker loops.
insights.solution: Monkey-patch AsyncHttpxClientWrapper.__del__ to no-op at CLI startup (neuter_async_httpx_del), install a custom asyncio exception handler suppressing 'Event loop is closed', and add cleanup_stale_async_clients() to proactively close cached async clients whose loop is closed.
insights.lesson_learned: When third-party SDK destructors interact with event loops, provide defensive measures (neuter or explicit cleanup) and add proactive evictions. Document the assumption and keep workarounds encapsulated.

agent/builtin_memory_provider.py.short_description: Adapter exposing MEMORY.md/USER.md as the builtin memory provider
agent/builtin_memory_provider.py.description: Wraps the file-backed MemoryStore (MEMORY.md and USER.md) as a MemoryProvider so it integrates with MemoryManager. Provides system prompt injection and compatibility accessors while delegating actual persistence and writes to the existing memory tool implementation.

key_constructs.name: BuiltinMemoryProvider
key_constructs.purpose: MemoryProvider implementation that exposes MEMORY.md and USER.md content for system prompts and integrates with the memory tool pipeline.

key_constructs.name: system_prompt_block
key_constructs.purpose: Return combined MEMORY.md and USER.md formatted blocks to include in the system prompt.

key_constructs.name: get_tool_schemas
key_constructs.purpose: Return an empty list because the memory tool is intercepted by run_agent.py.

provide compatibility layer for legacy memory store access

agent/memory_manager.py.short_description: Orchestrates builtin and one external memory provider
agent/memory_manager.py.description: Central manager that registers the builtin memory provider plus at most one external provider, aggregates system prompt blocks, performs prefetch and sync across providers, routes memory tool calls, and exposes lifecycle hooks. Ensures failures in one provider don't block others and prevents multiple external providers from being registered to avoid conflicts.

key_constructs.name: MemoryManager
key_constructs.purpose: Coordinates providers, combines system prompt blocks, handles prefetch/sync, tool routing and lifecycle events.

key_constructs.name: sanitize_context
key_constructs.purpose: Strip memory-context fence tags from provider output to avoid nested fencing.

key_constructs.name: build_memory_context_block
key_constructs.purpose: Wrap prefetched memory in a fenced block with a system note for injection into model prompts.

key_behaviors: aggregate system-level memory blocks for the system prompt
prefetch relevant memory context before a turn
sync completed turns to registered memory providers
route memory tool calls to the appropriate provider

insights.title: Memory prefetch context fencing to prevent prompt injection
insights.problem: Prefetched memory content could include sequences that break fences or cause the model to treat recalled context as user input, enabling prompt-injection style issues.
insights.root_cause: External memory providers might return content containing <memory-context> tags or similar markers that could escape fences when injected into prompts.
insights.solution: Added sanitize_context() to strip fence-escape sequences and build_memory_context_block() to wrap prefetched text in a <memory-context> fenced block with an explicit system note preventing the model from treating it as new user input. run_agent now injects fenced blocks rather than raw strings.
insights.lesson_learned: Always fence and sanitize externally-sourced context inserted into prompts to avoid prompt-injection and preserve prompt cache stability; treat recalled memory as background data, not as user messages.

agent/memory_provider.py.short_description: Abstract base class and lifecycle for memory provider plugins
agent/memory_provider.py.description: Defines the MemoryProvider interface used by MemoryManager: lifecycle methods (initialize, shutdown), recall hooks (prefetch, queue_prefetch), sync, tool schema exposure, and optional hooks for turn/session events. Plugins implement this abstract class to integrate external memory backends while the builtin provider remains always active.

key_behaviors: define contract for memory providers to implement prefetch and persistence
expose provider-specific tools to the agent via schemas
provide lifecycle hooks for integration

insights.title: Add CLI for viewing Hermes logs with filters

insights.solution: New hermes_cli.logs with tail_log, _read_tail, follow, filters by level/session/since, list_logs. Integrates with hermes CLI as 'hermes logs'.

insights.title: Completion queue for notify_on_complete
insights.problem: There was no lightweight mechanism to allow the CLI or gateway to be notified when a background process that requested an agent notification completed.
insights.root_cause: Process sessions could be persisted and recovered but there was no in-memory queue for completed sessions that should trigger an agent turn.
insights.solution: Added completion_queue (queue.Queue) to ProcessRegistry; when _move_to_finished runs, if session.notify_on_complete is True the method enqueues a sanitized completion dict including truncated output. Checkpoint persistence includes notify_on_complete for crash recovery.
insights.lesson: For cross-component notifications, enqueue compact completion events at the source so front-ends can react without polling internal registries.

hermes_cli/gateway.py.short_description: CLI gateway subcommand: manage and install the messaging gateway
hermes_cli/gateway.py.description: Provides the hermes gateway CLI surface for run/start/stop/install/uninstall/setup/status operations and service integration (systemd/launchd), plus helpers to find, kill, and manage gateway processes. It includes logic for service naming, systemd unit path resolution, linger guidance, PID management, and cross-platform process enumeration to support gateway lifecycle management.
key_behaviors: discovers and kills stale gateway processes
installs/uninstalls gateway system services
starts, stops, and reports gateway status across platforms

insights.title: Avoid killing freshly-restarted service-managed gateway processes
insights.problem: After restarting a gateway via systemd/launchd, the stale-process sweep would list all gateway PIDs (including the newly spawned service PID) and kill them, leaving the gateway stopped.
insights.root_cause: find_gateway_pids enumerated all gateway processes (ps/ps aux style) with no awareness of which PIDs are managed by system services. There was no way to exclude service-managed PIDs during the manual sweep.
insights.solution: Add _get_service_pids() which queries systemd MainPID and launchd list to collect service-managed PIDs; extend find_gateway_pids(exclude_pids=None) and kill_gateway_processes(exclude_pids=None) to accept an exclude set and skip those PIDs. In cmd_update, call _get_service_pids() and exclude those PIDs when killing manual processes.
insights.lesson_learned: When interacting with system service managers, prefer querying the manager for authoritative PIDs rather than inferring from process lists; provide exclusion hooks to protect service-managed processes from manual sweeps.

insights.title: Harden launchd parsing and dedupe systemd scopes
insights.problem: Launchd parsing could accept malformed output; systemd handling had duplicated blocks for user/system scope.
insights.root_cause: Code duplicated systemctl handling and did not validate launchctl output structure.
insights.solution: Loop over user/system scope args to dedupe logic, call get_launchd_label() directly, and validate launchctl output by checking parts[2] == expected label (skip header), avoiding mis-parsing.
insights.lesson_learned: Consolidate duplicate logic and validate external command output structure before trusting parts.

insights.title: Add timeouts to subprocess.run calls to avoid hangs
insights.problem: gateway CLI used many subprocess.run calls with no timeout; blocked processes (systemctl, launchctl, wmic) could hang hermes gateway commands indefinitely.
insights.root_cause: Subprocess calls lacked timeout arguments; platform-specific system tools can block (waiting on IPC or locks).
insights.solution: Added timeouts (tiered: 10s/30s/90s) to subprocess.run calls and handled TimeoutExpired for status checks to treat as not-running; updated tests to accept kwargs.
insights.lesson_learned: Always set a reasonable timeout for external system calls invoked by CLI tools and handle TimeoutExpired explicitly; tests should mock subprocess.run to accept **kwargs.

insights.title: Bridge agent.gateway_timeout -> HERMES_AGENT_TIMEOUT
insights.problem: Gateway runtime needed to read configured gateway_timeout and map to the agent timeout env var.
insights.root_cause: Multiple timeout controls existed; unify config-driven mapping.
insights.solution: When loading gateway config, set HERMES_AGENT_TIMEOUT env var from gateway config if not already set by .env.
insights.lesson_learned: Bridge config values into runtime env early so lower-level code reading env vars sees the intended values; maintain precedence (existing env vars keep priority).

insights.title: Replace wall-clock execution timeout with inactivity-based timeout
insights.problem: A hard wall-clock asyncio.wait_for timeout killed legitimate long-running but active agents (delegation, streaming, reasoning).
insights.root_cause: Timeout logic used wall-clock only, not taking into account agent activity (tool calls/stream woken) so long-running active agents were erroneously killed.
insights.solution: Replaced wait_for with a poll loop that monitors agent.get_activity_summary() every few seconds and only terminates when the agent has been idle for the configured gateway_timeout. Also updated stale-agent eviction to consider idle time.
insights.lesson_learned: For long-running tasks, prefer inactivity-based timeouts over wall-clock to avoid terminating actively-working processes; provide diagnostics showing last activity and iteration counts to help debugging.

insights.title: Shared thread sessions by default with sender attribution
insights.problem: In threaded group channels, previously sessions might be per-user causing confusion in communal threads.
insights.root_cause: Earlier default isolated threads per user; UX expectation is shared thread session for collaborative threads.
insights.solution: Default thread_sessions_per_user to False; when thread_id present and sharing enabled, the gateway prefixes messages with [sender name] so the agent can distinguish participants and avoids inserting a per-turn pinned user into system prompt (to keep prompt cache stable).
insights.lesson_learned: Session keying semantics have UX implications; default sharing for threaded conversations aligns with collaboration expectations, but make it configurable for per-user isolation when required.

insights.title: Self-heal missing launchd plist on start
insights.problem: If the macOS launchd plist was manually removed or lost during an upgrade, launchd_start would fail (or take a different error path) rather than regenerating the plist and starting the service.
insights.root_cause: launchd_start assumed the plist existed and only refreshed it; it did not handle the case where the plist file was entirely missing.
insights.solution: If the plist path does not exist, generate and write the plist file, load it with launchctl and start the service, then return early. Simplified subsequent error handling because plist existence is guaranteed after this path.
insights.lesson_learned: Service install artifacts (launchd/systemd units) can be removed or corrupted outside the program. Commands that start services should tolerate and regenerate missing service definitions to avoid hard failures.

insights.title: Include PATH, VIRTUAL_ENV, and HERMES_HOME in generated launchd plist
insights.problem: launchd provides a minimal default PATH; gateway subprocesses (node, ffmpeg, npm binaries) were not found when run as launchd agents because PATH, VIRTUAL_ENV and HERMES_HOME were not present in the plist environment.
insights.root_cause: Generated plist previously lacked environment variables that mirror the interactive shell environment and virtualenv layout.
insights.solution: Capture a sane PATH (prepend venv/bin and node_modules/.bin, include resolved node dir and the user's PATH) and inject PATH, VIRTUAL_ENV and HERMES_HOME into the plist's EnvironmentVariables.
insights.lesson_learned: Service metadata must include the runtime environment required by subprocesses; snapshotting PATH and virtualenv info at install time avoids 'binary not found' errors for services launched outside an interactive shell.

insights.title: Profile-scoped service names and plist paths (HERMES_HOME-aware)
insights.problem: Multiple HERMES_HOME profiles collided on the same macOS launchd plist and service label; hardcoded paths/labels prevented profile isolation.
insights.root_cause: Service label and plist path were hardcoded to ai.hermes.gateway and ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.hermes.gateway.plist, ignoring HERMES_HOME/profile semantics.
insights.solution: Introduce _profile_suffix(), get_service_name(), get_launchd_plist_path(), and get_launchd_label() which use get_hermes_home() to scope service names and plist paths per profile (profile name or short hash). Update all launchctl command usage to use get_launchd_label().
insights.lesson_learned: When supporting multiple data directories/profiles, service names and OS-level artifacts must be namespaced to avoid collisions; centralize logic that computes labels/paths so all callers remain consistent.

insights.title: Simplified launchctl error path after ensuring plist exists
insights.problem: Complex error branching attempted to handle returncode 3 (service not found) together with plist existence checks across two paths.
insights.root_cause: The code checked for both return code and file presence in two places because the file might be missing; after adding self-heal, that complexity was reduced.
insights.solution: After regen path ensures plist existence, the error branch only checks the called process return code (e.returncode != 3) and raises otherwise; the reload path handles reloading when job was unloaded.
insights.lesson_learned: When an earlier step guarantees a precondition (file exists), simplify later error branches to be clearer and less brittle.

insights.title: Include user-local bin paths in generated systemd unit PATH
insights.problem: systemd unit PATH omitted user-local bins like ~/.local/bin, ~/.cargo/bin, ~/go/bin and others, causing uv/pipx/cargo-installed tools to be undiscoverable when running as service.
insights.root_cause: Systemd unit generation only appended common system paths, not user-local ones; for system services home resolution needed handling.
insights.solution: Added _build_user_local_paths(home, path_entries) to detect existing user-local dirs and include them. For system units resolve appropriate home dir and include candidate paths only if present. Updated tests.

insights.title: Do not force temperature during compaction calls and add cooldown on summary failures
insights.problem: Compaction summary calls previously forced temperature which could be incompatible with temperature-restricted providers; repeated summary failures retried continuously increasing load and noise.
insights.root_cause: Temperature hard-coded for summary calls and no retry-backoff or cooldown after failure.
insights.solution: Removed forced temperature parameter when calling call_llm (do not inject 'temperature'); added _SUMMARY_FAILURE_COOLDOWN_SECONDS and logic to skip summary attempts during cooldown after RuntimeError or other exceptions; added _summary_failure_cooldown_until tracking.
insights.lesson_learned: Respect provider constraints (like disallowing temperature) and back off on repeated summary failures to avoid wasting provider capacity and generating noise.

insights.title: Lower severity log when no summary model is available
insights.problem: Logging a warning when no summary model is available was noisy; this is expected operationally.
insights.root_cause: Overly aggressive logging level for a non-fatal operational condition.
insights.solution: Change from logger.warning to logger.debug when no summary model is available; keep behavior of dropping middle turns.
insights.lesson_learned: Use appropriate log levels to avoid alert-fatigue for expected, non-fatal conditions.

insights.title: Remove hardcoded compression timeout; rely on config-driven default
insights.problem: Compression used a hardcoded 45s timeout that might be insufficient for slow local models.
insights.root_cause: Timeout for compression was embedded in the compressor code.
insights.solution: Remove explicit timeout in compressor body and let call_llm resolve timeout from auxiliary.compression.timeout config.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep per-task defaults centralized; avoid duplicating timing constants across modules.

agent/copilot_acp_client.py.description: Provides a CopilotACPClient exposing a minimal OpenAI-like chat interface by running the copilot CLI with ACP, sending prompt text, collecting streamed JSON-RPC responses, extracting tool_call blocks, and returning a completion-shaped object Hermes expects. Handles subprocess lifecycle, JSON-RPC request/response loop, stdout/stderr parsing, and tool-call extraction/cleaning for function-call workflow compatibility.

insights.title: Bridge tool-calls from ACP backend into Hermes tool execution
insights.problem: ACP backend could emit tool call blocks (<tool_call>{...}</tool_call>) or bare JSON describing function calls; Hermes wasn't reliably extracting these and sometimes duplicated extraction or left tool-call markup in text.
insights.root_cause: No robust parsing for ACP XML-wrapped tool call blocks and bare JSON fallback; finish_reason and assistant message content were not updated accordingly.
insights.solution: Enhance _format_messages_as_prompt to include OpenAI function schema for tools and tool_choice hints, implement _extract_tool_calls_from_text() that: prefers XML <tool_call> blocks, falls back to JSON regex only if no XML, parses into SimpleNamespace tool_call objects with function.name and arguments, removes consumed spans from the response text, sets finish_reason='tool_calls' when tool calls found.
insights.lesson_learned: When bridging two incompatible protocols (ACP and Hermes function schema), make the contract explicit: instruct backend to emit machine-readable blocks and implement robust parsing that prefers structured blocks over heuristics. Ensure finish_reason and message content are consistent with extracted calls.

insights.title: Auto-repair jobs.json with invalid control characters
insights.problem: json.load() failed on jobs.json containing bare control characters in string values (e.g. literal newlines in prompts), causing load_jobs() to return empty list and stop all scheduled jobs silently.
insights.root_cause: Strict JSON parsing rejects unescaped control characters; no fallback or logging previously.
insights.solution: On JSONDecodeError, retry with json.loads(..., strict=False); if recovered, rewrite file via save_jobs() (auto-repair) and log a warning. Only return empty list if unrecoverable.

insights.title: Advance recurring job next_run_at before execution to avoid duplicate runs on crash/restart
insights.problem: If the gateway crashed mid-job before mark_job_run persisted the next_run_at, the job would be seen as due on restart and could re-fire repeatedly while the gateway restarted rapidly.
insights.root_cause: run_job was executed before persisting next occurrence; failure left next_run_at unchanged (in the past) so restarts retriggered the job in the grace window.
insights.solution: Add advance_next_run(job_id) that computes and persists the next occurrence for cron/interval jobs and call it before run_job() in the scheduler tick. One-shot jobs left unchanged.
insights.lesson_learned: For recurring scheduled work, persist the next occurrence before executing to achieve at-most-once semantics under restart/crash conditions (preferred over at-least-once multiplicative resends).

gateway/pairing.py.short_description: Code-based DM pairing approval flow and approved user store
gateway/pairing.py.description: Implements a one-time code pairing system to approve unknown messaging users: generates cryptographically-random codes, enforces rate limits and lockouts, persists pending and approved lists per platform with secure file writes, and exposes helpers to list/approve/revoke pairings. Designed to replace static allowlists and provide an owner-reviewed onboarding flow for messaging adapters.

key_constructs.name: PairingStore
key_constructs.purpose: Main store class managing pending pairing codes, approved users, rate limits, lockouts, and file-backed persistence per platform.

key_constructs.name: generate_code
key_constructs.purpose: Create a crypto-random pairing code for a user if not rate-limited, not locked out, and pending capacity exists.

key_constructs.name: approve_code
key_constructs.purpose: Approve a pending code: move the entry to the approved list and return user info, increments fail counters on invalid attempts.

key_constructs.name: _secure_write
key_constructs.purpose: Atomically write JSON files with restrictive permissions (tempfile + rename) to avoid partial writes and ensure owner-only access.

key_constructs.name: is_approved
key_constructs.purpose: Check if a given user_id is in the approved list for a platform.

key_behaviors: generates one-time pairing codes for new messaging users
approves codes to add users to approved lists
enforces rate limits and lockout on failed approvals
persists pending and approved state securely

insights.title: Atomic writes and locking for PairingStore
insights.problem: Concurrent platform adapters writing/reading pairing files could observe partial writes or race conditions, leading to corrupted pairing state.
insights.root_cause: Writes used Path.write_text directly and read-modify-write cycles lacked inter-thread locking.
insights.solution: Implemented _secure_write using tempfile.mkstemp + os.replace + fsync to atomically replace files and added a threading.RLock to PairingStore to protect read-modify-write cycles; wrapped public mutation methods with the lock.
insights.lesson_learned: For shared file-backed stores accessed by multiple threads/processes, use atomic replace patterns and explicit locking for read-modify-write sequences to avoid races and partial-file reads.
