tools/vision_tools.py.short_description: Vision analysis tool: downloads images, encodes, and calls vision LLMs
tools/vision_tools.py.description: Provides async vision analysis utilities that validate and download images (with SSRF redirect guarding and site policy checks), detect MIME types, convert images to base64 data URLs, and forward structured multimodal requests to an auxiliary LLM vision router. Handles retries, temporary file cleanup, capability error mapping, and returns JSON analysis results for use by the agent.

key_constructs.name: vision_analyze_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Main async entry that accepts an image URL or local path plus a user prompt, prepares base64 image payloads, calls the vision LLM router, and returns a JSON result with analysis or error.

key_constructs.name: _download_image
key_constructs.purpose: Async downloader with redirect guards, retry/backoff, site policy checks, and temporary file write for remote images.

key_constructs.name: _image_to_base64_data_url
key_constructs.purpose: Convert a local image file to a base64 data URL suitable for embedding in multimodal LLM requests.

key_constructs.name: _validate_image_url
key_constructs.purpose: Sanity checks for HTTP(S) URLs, network location, and blocks private/internal addresses using url_safety policies.

key_behaviors: download and validate images safely
encode images to base64 data URLs for LLM calls
call the vision auxiliary client and return analysis results
retry on empty reasoning and map capability/billing errors to user-friendly messages

insights.title: Guard vision LLM analysis extraction and retry on reasoning-only responses
insights.problem: Vision analysis extracted response.choices[0].message.content.strip() which failed when content was None (reasoning-only) or empty.
insights.root_cause: Same root cause as other tools: provider variant returning structured reasoning instead of content.
insights.solution: Use extract_content_or_reasoning() and retry once on empty content; log a warning before retry.
insights.lesson_learned: Apply a single extraction strategy across tools feeding auxiliary LLMs to handle diverse provider responses.

tools/website_policy.py.short_description: Load, cache and enforce website blocklist policy for URL tools
tools/website_policy.py.description: Provides lightweight helpers to load a user-managed website blocklist from config and optional shared files, normalize rules, cache the parsed policy with a short TTL, and check URLs against the blocklist. It is intentionally minimal so web/browser tools can apply policy without importing the full CLI stack and will fail-open on errors by default.

key_constructs.name: load_website_blocklist
key_constructs.purpose: Load and parse website_blocklist from config and shared files, caching results.

callers.context: from tools.website_policy import WebsitePolicyError, check_website_access, load_website_blocklist

key_constructs.name: check_website_access
key_constructs.purpose: Determine whether a URL is allowed or blocked; returns block metadata when blocked.

callers.context: from tools.website_policy import WebsitePolicyError, check_website_access, load_website_blocklist
check_website_access = lambda url: None  # noqa: E731 — fail-open if policy module unavailable

key_constructs.name: invalidate_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Clear the in-memory cached policy forcing next check to re-read config.

key_constructs.name: _normalize_rule
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize raw rule strings or URLs into host/domain patterns.

key_constructs.name: _iter_blocklist_file_rules
key_constructs.purpose: Parse shared blocklist file lines into normalized rules while tolerating IO errors.

key_behaviors: loads website blocklist from ~/.hermes/config.yaml and shared files
caches parsed policy with short TTL to avoid repeated reads
checks URLs and returns blocking metadata when a rule matches

hermes_state.py.short_description: SQLite session store with FTS and concurrency handling
hermes_state.py.description: Implements a WAL-mode SQLite-backed session and message store (SessionDB) with FTS5 full-text search, migrations, token/accounting counters, and write retry logic to avoid writer convoying. The module stores session metadata, message history, token/cost accounting and supports safe concurrent readers with a single writer pattern for CLI and gateway usage.

key_constructs.name: SessionDB
key_constructs.purpose: Primary SQLite-backed session and message persistence class with FTS, migrations, and concurrency-safe write helper.

key_constructs.name: SCHEMA_SQL
key_constructs.purpose: SQL DDL used to create the core sessions and messages tables.

key_constructs.name: FTS_SQL
key_constructs.purpose: FTS5 virtual-table and triggers for full-text search on messages.

key_constructs.name: _execute_write
key_constructs.purpose: Internal helper to run write transactions with BEGIN IMMEDIATE and jittered retry to mitigate SQLite lock contention.

key_constructs.name: create_session
key_constructs.purpose: Create a new session record in the database.

key_constructs.name: update_token_counts
key_constructs.purpose: Atomically update or set session token/cost counters (supports absolute and incremental modes).

key_behaviors: creates and updates persistent sessions
stores message history and exposes FTS search
maintains token and cost counters with safe concurrent writes

insights.title: Eliminate long SQLite WAL contention freezes with jittered app-level retries
insights.problem: Multiple hermes processes sharing state.db caused WAL write-lock convoy effects and 15-20s TUI freezes; long SQLite timeout and deterministic backoff synchronized writers.
insights.root_cause: SQLite's busy handler with long timeout (30s) and deterministic sleep schedule caused convoys when many writers contested the WAL; WAL not checkpointed due to reader locks and bloated WAL.
insights.solution: Shorten SQLite timeout to 1s, set isolation_level=None, implement _execute_write wrapper that does BEGIN IMMEDIATE with app-level randomized jitter retries (20-150ms) up to 15 attempts, passive WAL checkpoints every N writes, and centralize write methods to use helper.
insights.lesson_learned: Under multi-process contention, prefer short DB timeouts plus randomized application-side retry to avoid synchronized backoffs; centralize write logic and perform non-blocking WAL checkpointing to keep WAL size under control.

gateway/platforms/mattermost.py.short_description: Mattermost gateway adapter for sending/receiving messages
gateway/platforms/mattermost.py.description: An aiohttp-based adapter that integrates Hermes with Mattermost via its REST API and WebSocket events. It authenticates the bot, sends/edits posts, uploads files, sends typing indicators, and runs a WebSocket loop to process inbound events while handling reconnection and rate/error cases.

key_constructs.name: MattermostAdapter
key_constructs.purpose: Platform adapter implementing BasePlatformAdapter methods to connect, send messages, upload files and listen for events via WebSocket.

key_constructs.name: check_mattermost_requirements
key_constructs.purpose: Validate environment variables and aiohttp availability to determine if the adapter can run.

key_constructs.name: MAX_POST_LENGTH
key_constructs.purpose: Practical message chunking threshold for posts to avoid oversized Mattermost messages.

key_constructs.name: _api_get
key_constructs.purpose: HTTP GET helper for Mattermost API with error logging.

key_constructs.name: _upload_file
key_constructs.purpose: Upload binary content to Mattermost and return the file id for attachment posts.

key_behaviors: connects to Mattermost and authenticates bot identity
sends, edits, and chunks long messages into posts
downloads remote URLs and uploads them as attachments

insights.title: Set message type to DOCUMENT when file attachments exist
insights.problem: Mattermost adapter downloaded file attachments but left msg_type as TEXT, preventing document enrichment and causing agent to miss attached files.
insights.root_cause: Missing update of msg_type when file_ids present — inconsistent with Telegram/Discord behavior.
insights.solution: Set msg_type = MessageType.DOCUMENT when file_ids is non-empty so downstream document-inlining executes.
insights.lesson_learned: Ensure adapters update MessageType consistently when media/files are present to enable enrichment pipelines.
constructs: MessageType handling at inbound message processing

insights.title: Detect PHOTO/VOICE/DOCUMENT from file mime types instead of blanket DOCUMENT
insights.problem: Previously the adapter treated all attachments as DOCUMENT, which prevented vision/STT pipelines from triggering for images/audio.
insights.root_cause: Message type was set to DOCUMENT unconditionally for attachments, ignoring mime types.
insights.solution: After downloading attachments, inspect media_types and set msg_type to PHOTO if any image/*, VOICE if audio/*, else DOCUMENT.
insights.lesson_learned: Use mime type inspection to select correct MessageType so downstream capabilities (vision, STT) are correctly invoked.
constructs: download flow and media type dispatch

tools/environments/docker.py.short_description: Hardened Docker-based execution environment for sandboxed tasks
tools/environments/docker.py.description: Provides DockerEnvironment, a security-hardened wrapper around docker run/exec that enforces capability drops, no-new-privileges, tmpfs limits, resource constraints, optional persistence via bind mounts, and controlled environment forwarding. It locates the docker CLI robustly, validates daemon availability, prepares volumes/env, starts containers, executes commands and manages disk quota support probing.

key_constructs.purpose: Executes commands inside a hardened Docker container with configurable CPU/memory/disk limits, mounts, and persistence options.

key_constructs.name: find_docker
key_constructs.purpose: Locate the docker CLI via PATH or common non-PATH install locations.

key_constructs.name: _ensure_docker_available
key_constructs.purpose: Preflight check that the docker executable and daemon are available and responsive.

key_constructs.name: _SECURITY_ARGS
key_constructs.purpose: Default docker run flags to drop capabilities, set no-new-privileges and PID/tmpfs constraints.

key_constructs.name: _storage_opt_supported
key_constructs.purpose: Probe whether the host supports per-container --storage-opt disk quotas.

key_behaviors: starts hardened containers for task execution
mounts credential and skill directories into containers read-only
executes commands inside containers with timeouts and stdin handling

insights.title: Sanitize workdir interpolation to prevent shell injection for docker backend
insights.problem: Workdir values containing shell metacharacters could inject arbitrary shell commands when interpolated into 'cd {workdir} && ...' wrappers.
insights.root_cause: Unquoted interpolation of user-supplied workdir into shell commands (docker exec path prepends).
insights.solution: Use shlex.quote for the subpath while preserving leading '~' expansion semantics; special-case '~' and '~/...' to allow shell tilde expansion while quoting the remainder.
insights.lesson_learned: Always quote/sanitize path components that will be interpolated into shell commands. Preserve expected shell behaviors (like ~ expansion) carefully.

acp_adapter/auth.py.short_description: Detect configured Hermes runtime provider for ACP adapter

acp_adapter/permissions.py.description: Creates a Hermes-compatible approval callback that forwards permission checks to an ACP connection's request_permission coroutine and waits for the decision, mapping ACP option kinds to Hermes approval strings. It runs the coroutine on a provided event loop and enforces a timeout, returning deny on failure or timeout.

agent/title_generator.py.short_description: Asynchronous session title auto-generation from first exchange
agent/title_generator.py.description: Provides utilities to generate a short descriptive session title from the first user/assistant exchange using a cheap auxiliary LLM call. Title generation runs asynchronously in a background thread so it doesn't add latency to the user response and will store the title in the session DB if one is not already set.

insights.title: Increase default title generation timeout to match config-driven policy
insights.problem: Title generation had a short hardcoded timeout (15s) that could fail with slower models.
insights.root_cause: Default was embedded in function signature.
insights.solution: Increase default to 30s and rely on config-based mechanism for overrides.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep user-visible defaults balanced for local and cloud models; prefer config-driven values where possible.

agent/usage_pricing.py.short_description: Normalize usage and estimate token-based costs across providers
agent/usage_pricing.py.description: Dataclasses and functions to canonicalize API usage payloads from different providers into a common token bucket model and estimate USD costs using configured/official pricing or provider metadata. environments/agent_loop.py.short_description: Reusable multi-turn agent loop with tool-calling dispatch

insights.title: Avoid blocking input() inside prompt_toolkit TUI; always skip confirmation for slash commands
insights.problem: Slash commands invoked inside prompt_toolkit TUI hung because input() blocks the event loop.
insights.root_cause: Slash handler used input() to confirm installs/uninstalls; prompt_toolkit environment caused input() to hang.
insights.solution: Always skip confirmation in slash-command path (implicit consent). Keep CLI argparse path unchanged. Add skip_confirm behavior and clarifying messages.
insights.lesson_learned: Interactive TUI environments and raw input() are incompatible; avoid blocking input calls inside event loops and provide alternate flows (implicit consent or non-blocking confirmation).

insights.title: Defer prompt cache invalidation by default for /skills install/uninstall
insights.problem: Unconditional cache invalidation when installing/uninstalling skills mid-session broke prompt cache and increased model costs.
insights.root_cause: Cache invalidation happened immediately, affecting running session state.
insights.solution: Add invalidate_cache parameter to do_install/do_uninstall; slash commands default to invalidate_cache=False (defer to next session). Provide --now to force immediate invalidation with explicit messaging.
insights.lesson_learned: Hot changes to runtime-config (skills) can invalidate caches and increase external costs. Provide a safe default (defer) and explicit opt-in for immediate activation.

insights.title: Invalidate skills prompt cache on CLI install/uninstall
insights.problem: Installing or uninstalling skills via CLI hub didn't invalidate prompt cache so new/removed skills didn't show until restart.
insights.root_cause: Cache invalidation not included in hub flows.
insights.solution: Call clear_skills_system_prompt_cache(clear_snapshot=True) after success in do_install and do_uninstall.
insights.lesson_learned: CLI operations that change on-disk content should invalidate both in-memory and persisted caches.

insights.title: Use extractor for reasoning-only LLM responses and retry loop
insights.problem: Reference and aggregator model calls used response.choices[0].message.content.strip() which crashed on None or produced empty text for reasoning-only responses.
insights.root_cause: Unprotected .content.strip() and no consistent fallback to structured reasoning.
insights.solution: Call extract_content_or_reasoning(response) and retry on empty content with backoff where appropriate.
insights.lesson_learned: Tools that call external LLMs must tolerate provider-specific response formats; share extraction logic and implement limited retries for reasoning-only outputs.

insights.title: Reuse shared check functions to avoid duplicate availability checks
insights.problem: Multiple tools registered with the same check_fn caused repeated calls to the same expensive check, incurring unnecessary cost.
insights.root_cause: Check logic invoked per-tool without memoizing per-call shared check_fn results.
insights.solution: Cache check_fn results in a local dict per get_definitions call so each unique check_fn runs at most once per invocation; store boolean result and reuse.
insights.lesson_learned: When multiple items share identical predicate functions, memoize results per invocation to prevent repeated expensive checks.

key_constructs.purpose: JSON-schema declaration used to register the session_search tool.

key_behaviors: searches past sessions by keyword using FTS5
returns recent session metadata without LLM cost
summarizes matching sessions via auxiliary LLM in parallel

insights.title: Session search should not drop sessions when summarizer fails
insights.problem: When auxiliary summarizer failed or returned None the session_search tool would silently drop matched sessions and return an empty list, causing false-negative search results.
insights.root_cause: Loop assumed summarization result truthiness and continued on exceptions or empty results without producing a fallback preview; also some call sites used .content.strip() without guarding against None.
insights.solution: Catch summarization exceptions, nullify result, build a single entry structure and if summary is missing produce a raw preview fallback (first ~500 chars or 'No preview available'). Also guarded LLM content extraction in related call sites by using extract_content_or_reasoning() and or-guards.
insights.lesson_learned: Never drop user-visible search hits just because an auxiliary summarizer is unavailable; always provide a sensible fallback preview. Guard all .content accessors against None or use a shared extractor for reasoning-only models.

tools/skills_guard.py.short_description: Regex-based security scanner and install policy for externally sourced skills
tools/skills_guard.py.description: Performs static analysis over skill directories using a comprehensive set of regex threat patterns (exfiltration, injection, destructive actions, persistence, obfuscation, supply-chain risks) to produce findings and a categorized verdict. It enforces a trust-aware install policy that decides whether a skill should be allowed, blocked, or prompted based on the skill's source trust level.

key_constructs.name: Finding
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing one detected issue with metadata like severity, file, line, and description.

key_constructs.name: ScanResult
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass summarizing the scan of a skill, its verdict, findings, and summary metadata.

key_constructs.name: THREAT_PATTERNS
key_constructs.purpose: List of regex-based threat patterns with identifiers, severities, categories and human descriptions used to detect dangerous constructs.

key_constructs.name: INSTALL_POLICY
key_constructs.purpose: Hardcoded mapping of trust levels to allowed actions for safe/caution/dangerous verdicts.

key_behaviors: scans skill directories for dangerous patterns
classifies findings and emits a verdict
applies a trust-based install policy to allow/block installs

insights.title: Guard LLM audit call for None content and retry once
insights.problem: LLM-based audit code used .message.content.strip() causing AttributeError when content is None.
insights.root_cause: Ungarded access to message.content across tools; lack of centralized extraction.
insights.solution: Use call_llm plus extract_content_or_reasoning() and retry once if the content is empty.
insights.lesson_learned: Audit and security tooling that calls LLMs must be resilient to reasoning-only outputs and should be best-effort (don't block install on LLM failure).

insights.title: Prefer gateway user_id for Mem0 scoping
insights.problem: Mem0 used config.user_id default ('hermes-user') for all sessions, sharing memory between users when running as gateway.
insights.root_cause: Initialization only read persisted config and environment variables, ignoring user_id passed by the gateway runtime.
insights.solution: Set self._user_id = kwargs.get('user_id') or config default; gateway now injects user_id via AIAgent init kwargs.
insights.lesson: Memory providers should accept runtime-scoped identifiers (kwargs) to operate correctly in multi-user gateway mode; keep config defaults for CLI single-user.

insights.title: Mem0 API v2 compatibility: use filters= and unwrap dict responses
insights.problem: Mem0 SDK v2 changed call signatures to accept filters={} and returns dict responses {results: [...]}. Existing code used user_id= and assumed list returns, breaking compatibility.
insights.root_cause: API breaking change in Mem0 SDK between versions (new filters param and wrapped response shape).
insights.solution: Added _read_filters() (user-scoped) and _write_filters() (user+agent) helpers, switched calls to pass filters=self._read_filters() for reads and **self._write_filters() for writes, and added _unwrap_results() to normalize responses whether they are dicts or lists.
insights.lesson_learned: When integrating external APIs, centralize parameter shaping and response normalization to isolate version changes behind small helpers; use read-vs-write scoping to preserve semantics (cross-session read vs attribution on write).

plugins/memory/retaindb/__init__.py.short_description: RetainDB memory provider plugin with durable write-behind queue and file store
plugins/memory/retaindb/__init__.py.description: MemoryProvider for RetainDB that implements HTTP client interactions, a durable SQLite-backed write-behind queue for crash-safe async ingestion, semantic search/context queries, file upload/ingest APIs, and overlay formatting for synthesized context. It exposes many tool schemas (profile/search/context/remember/forget and file tools) used by the agent.

key_constructs.name: _Client
key_constructs.purpose: HTTP client encapsulating RetainDB REST calls for memory and file operations with consistent headers and error handling.

key_constructs.name: _WriteQueue
key_constructs.purpose: SQLite-backed durable async queue that persists pending ingests and retries on failure in a background thread.

key_constructs.name: RetainDBMemoryProvider
key_constructs.purpose: MemoryProvider implementation coordinating the client, write queue, prefetch caches, and exposing tool handlers for memory and file APIs.

key_constructs.name: _build_overlay
key_constructs.purpose: Construct a compact overlay of profile and query results to include in context synthesis while avoiding duplicates.

key_behaviors: queues and ingests session data to RetainDB reliably
performs semantic search and context queries
uploads and ingests files into shared memory store

insights.title: Use thread-local SQLite connections and join prefetch threads
insights.problem: Previously the write queue created and closed a new sqlite3 connection per operation, and prefetch threads could accumulate under load; shutdown didn't join threads, causing resource leaks and race conditions.
insights.root_cause: SQLite connections were not reused per thread (expensive) and prefetch spawns did not wait/join previous threads, allowing unbounded thread creation.
insights.solution: Introduce threading.local() per _WriteQueue to cache one connection per thread via _get_conn(); reuse connections for all DB operations. Track prefetch threads, join previous batch before spawning new threads, and join on shutdown.
insights.lesson_learned: SQLite connections are not necessarily safe to share across threads; use one connection per thread and reuse it. Always join background threads during shutdown and limit concurrent prefetches to avoid runaway thread creation.

insights.title: Make RETAINDB project optional and default to 'default'
insights.problem: Code assumed RETAINDB_PROJECT defaulted to 'hermes' and required it; API expected default project behavior when unset.
insights.root_cause: Project resolution used hermes/ profile fallback but did not honor 'default' project semantics.
insights.solution: Make project optional (default ''), and resolve to hermes-<profile> when profile present or 'default' otherwise; document that API will auto-create/use 'default' project when unset.
insights.lesson_learned: Respect provider API semantics (auto-created default projects); prefer leaving optional fields empty so APIs can apply server-side defaults.

insights.title: Fix RetainDB API routes and add write-behind queue + file tools
insights.problem: Prior implementation called incorrect RetainDB endpoints (404s) and was synchronous, blocking the hot path.
insights.root_cause: Wrong endpoint paths used and no durable write-behind queue.
insights.solution: Correct endpoints (context/query, memory/ingest/session, memory, memory/search, memory/profile), implement durable SQLite spool write-behind queue, automatic replay after crash, per-row error marking and retry, prefetch background tasks and file storage tools.
insights.lesson_learned: When integrating with external APIs, validate endpoints against provider docs and add durable write-behind to tolerate transient failures; prefer async offload for IO-heavy operations.

hermes_cli/tools_config.py.short_description: Interactive toolset and provider configuration UI used by Hermes CLI
hermes_cli/tools_config.py.description: TUI/CLI helpers for selecting platforms, enabling/disabling toolsets, prompting for provider-specific credentials, running post-setup hooks, and persisting per-platform tool configuration into ~/.hermes/config.yaml. It centralizes provider options and interactive flows used by `hermes tools` and setup tooling.
key_behaviors: lets users select and configure toolsets per platform
prompts for API keys and runs provider-aware post-setup steps
persists platform tool configuration to the Hermes config

insights.title: Add missing mattermost entry to tools_config.PLATFORMS
insights.problem: Mattermost toolset was not referenced in tools_config.PLATFORMS.
insights.solution: Add mattermost entry with default_toolset hermes-mattermost.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep PLATFORMS and TOOLSETS in sync; missing mapping can hide toolsets from installers.

insights.title: Ensure plugins are discovered before reading plugin toolsets
insights.problem: Plugin toolsets were missing in some flows because discover_plugins() was not run before queries for plugin-provided toolsets, causing plugin toolsets to be invisible to the TUI and in standalone processes.
insights.root_cause: discover_plugins() only ran as a side-effect of importing model_tools.py; some code paths never imported it.
insights.solution: Call discover_plugins() (idempotent) before invoking get_plugin_toolsets() in the helper functions.
insights.lesson_learned: Explicitly initialize plugin discovery where plugin state is read; don't rely on import side-effects for initialization.

insights.title: Add Matrix platform to PLATFORMS dict
insights.problem: Matrix platform entry missing led to KeyError when referencing platform tools for Matrix.
insights.root_cause: Matrix entry was overlooked while listing platform defaults.
insights.solution: Add 'matrix' entry with label and default_toolset to PLATFORMS mapping.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep platform registry consistent with code that expects platform keys; add tests for platform enumerations.

It manages bridge lifecycle, session locking, message polling, mention/mention-pattern processing, media caching, and decision logic for which group messages to process. The adapter exposes normalized MessageEvent objects to the gateway and handles sending/delivery through the bridge.

insights.title: Accept metadata kwargs in media send methods

insights.solution: Added **kwargs to the three media sending method signatures so additional metadata (like thread metadata) is accepted without error.

insights.title: Sanitize workdir interpolation for apptainer/singularity backend
insights.problem: Similar shell injection risk as docker backend when prepending cd for ~ expansion.
insights.root_cause: Unquoted workdir interpolation into shell wrapper for apptainer exec.
insights.solution: Use shlex.quote on subpath while leaving leading ~ unquoted to allow shell expansion; set safe fallback working dir.
insights.lesson_learned: Normalize quoting behavior across container backends and preserve user expectations for ~ expansion.

It normalizes events into MessageEvent objects, escapes/strips MarkdownV2, and supports advanced features like model picker state, approval flows, and persistent topic thread id persistence.

key_behaviors: connects to Telegram via polling or webhook
receives and batches incoming messages and media
creates and persists DM forum topics

insights.title: Inline approval buttons via Telegram InlineKeyboard
insights.problem: Approval requests previously relied on text commands which are clunky for users and limited by callback_data size.
insights.root_cause: Text-based approval flow constrained UX and had to parse free-form responses.
insights.solution: Add send_exec_approval() that sends an inline keyboard with compact approval IDs, store mapping in _approval_state keyed by monotonic counter, handle ea:* callback_data in _handle_callback_query to resolve approvals.
insights.lesson: For platforms with button/callback primitives, use short opaque IDs and a server-side map to avoid callback_data length limits and to make actions idempotent and trackable.

insights.title: Interactive paginated model picker for Telegram
insights.problem: Large provider model lists were presented as a long text list; navigation and UX were poor for providers with many curated models.
insights.root_cause: Previous implementation built all model buttons at once (max_models=8) and duplicated logic in-place without pagination or state tracking.
insights.solution: Introduce _build_model_keyboard() for 8-per-page pagination, add stateful _model_picker_state keyed by chat_id, add mg:<page> callbacks for page navigation, increment max_models usage, and add noop catch-all answers for non-actionable buttons.
insights.lesson_learned: Interactive adapters need explicit state per-chat for multi-step flows; build small helpers to encapsulate pagination and ensure callback_data stays compact (Telegram 64-byte limit). Always answer callback queries to avoid UI hangs.

insights.title: Picker expiry / no-state handling
insights.problem: Callback queries could arrive after picker state expired, causing confusing errors or uncaught cases.
insights.root_cause: Model picker state was stored transiently and not checked for expiration when callbacks were processed.
insights.solution: Add explicit state checks in _handle_model_picker_callback to return friendly messages like 'Picker expired — use /model again.' and catch-all query.answer() for noop buttons.
insights.lesson_learned: Interactive flows must defensively handle missing/expired server-side state and provide user-friendly fallbacks.

insights.title: Increase max_models for provider listing
insights.problem: Previously only a small subset of curated models reached the picker.
insights.root_cause: Hardcoded max_models value (8) limited provider model discovery.
insights.solution: Raise max_models to 50 in gateway/run.py (used by telegram send_model_picker) so more curated models are surfaced.
insights.lesson_learned: Provider lists should be sized according to curated provider sizes; expose sensible limits as constants rather than magic numbers.

insights.title: Respect get_hermes_home() when persisting thread IDs and hot-reload config
insights.problem: Code used Path.home()/.hermes/config.yaml rather than HERMES_HOME; with profiles this saved and read config from wrong location.
insights.root_cause: Hardcoded path assumptions rather than using get_hermes_home().
insights.solution: Use get_hermes_home() to locate config.yaml for persisting thread ids and for hot-reload checks.
insights.lesson_learned: Use the canonical HERMES_HOME accessor consistently; mismatches cause state leakage between profiles.

insights.title: Fallback to no message_thread_id on 'Message thread not found' BadRequest
insights.problem: python-telegram-bot's BadRequest inherits from NetworkError, so 'Message thread not found' was treated as transient and retried; repeated retries resulted in silent failures and lost streaming/tool messages.
insights.root_cause: Retry loop didn't special-case BadRequest; it retried identical failing sends and swallowed errors.
insights.solution: Detect BadRequest inside NetworkError handling; if error message indicates 'thread not found' and thread_id was set, clear thread_id and retry once; other BadRequest errors raised immediately.
insights.lesson_learned: Differentiate transient network errors from permanent API errors even when exception hierarchy groups them; implement specific recovery strategies (clear thread id) for recoverable permanent errors.

plugins/memory/honcho/__init__.py.short_description: Honcho memory provider plugin integrating Honcho SDK
plugins/memory/honcho/__init__.py.description: MemoryProvider implementation that integrates with Honcho for AI-native cross-session memory, offering profile, semantic search, dialectic context, and persistent conclusions. It exposes tool schemas (profile, search, context, conclude), manages session lifecycle (lazy/eager), prefetching and cadence controls, and writes configuration to hermes home when requested.

key_constructs.name: HonchoMemoryProvider
key_constructs.purpose: MemoryProvider implementation that wraps Honcho session manager, prefetching, dialectic queries, and tool handlers.

key_behaviors: initializes Honcho sessions for agent sessions

insights.title: Use gateway user_id to scope Honcho peer_name when present
insights.problem: Honcho memory plugin used a static peer_name from config, causing gateway users to share Honcho memory unless configured per-user.
insights.root_cause: Plugin relied only on static client config; gateway sessions did not inject user_id into plugin config.
insights.solution: When initialize() receives kwargs user_id, override cfg.peer_name to that user ID so memories are scoped per gateway user.
insights.lesson: Plugins must prefer runtime-supplied session-scoped identifiers over global config defaults for per-user isolation in multi-tenant gateway contexts.

gateway/platforms/webhook.py.description: Runs an aiohttp server to accept webhook POSTs, validates HMAC signatures, rate-limits and deduplicates deliveries, renders prompts from templates, optionally loads skills, and triggers non-blocking agent runs. The adapter also supports configurable delivery targets (log, GitHub comment, or cross-platform delivery) and dynamic route reloading from disk.

insights.title: Add {__raw__} template token to dump full JSON payload
insights.problem: Webhook prompt templates lacked an easy way to surface the entire JSON payload to the agent; existing template expansion only supported dot-notation access.
insights.root_cause: No built-in token to dump raw JSON payload; large payloads also risked being unbounded.
insights.solution: Implemented a special {__raw__} token in _render_prompt that dumps the entire payload as pretty JSON and truncates to 4000 characters.
insights.lesson_learned: Provide explicit template tokens for common debugging/monitoring use-cases and enforce a safe truncation boundary to avoid huge prompt injections.

insights.title: Pass thread_id/message_thread_id through to adapter.send for forum topics
insights.problem: When performing cross-platform delivery, Telegram forum topic messages (which require message_thread_id/thread_id) were not delivered correctly because thread identifiers were not forwarded as adapter metadata.
insights.root_cause: deliver_extra contained thread ids but _deliver_cross_platform did not convert them into adapter metadata.
insights.solution: Extract thread_id or message_thread_id from deliver_extra and pass metadata={'thread_id': thread_id} into adapter.send so target adapters can deliver to forum topics.
insights.lesson_learned: Cross-platform bridging must propagate platform-specific metadata; adapters should accept normalized metadata for platform features like forum threads.

insights.title: Dynamic webhook subscriptions hot-reload from disk
insights.problem: Agent-created webhook subscriptions needed persistence and near-instant activation without gateway restarts.
insights.root_cause: Static config.yaml routes were the only supported route source.
insights.solution: Add a dynamic subscriptions file (~/.hermes/webhook_subscriptions.json) with _reload_dynamic_routes() that mtime-gates reloads, merges dynamic routes with static ones (static precedence), updates self._routes and logs reloads, and call reload on connect and each incoming request.
insights.lesson_learned: For user-driven dynamic configuration files, mtime-gated hot-reload is a low-overhead pattern. Always ensure static config takes precedence to avoid accidental overrides.

insights.title: Merge dynamic and static routes with static precedence
insights.problem: Dynamic subscriptions could conflict with static config routes.
insights.root_cause: Without explicit merging rules, dynamic routes might silently override critical static settings.
insights.solution: When reloading dynamic routes, filter out any dynamic keys that conflict with static routes; build _routes so static routes win.
insights.lesson_learned: When combining configuration sources, define and enforce precedence explicitly; tests should cover conflict cases.

tools/environments/modal.py.short_description: Modal SDK execution environment with snapshot persistence
tools/environments/modal.py.description: Executes commands inside Modal cloud sandboxes using Modal SDK's Sandbox APIs, manages mounting credentials and skill/cache files, and persists filesystem snapshots for later restoration. It uses a background AsyncWorker for Modal async calls, synchronizes files into the sandbox, supports executing commands with timeouts/interrupts, and records/restores snapshot IDs per task.

key_constructs.name: ModalEnvironment
key_constructs.purpose: Execution environment that creates Modal sandboxes, syncs files, runs commands, snapshots filesystem, and handles cleanup.

key_constructs.name: _AsyncWorker
key_constructs.purpose: Background thread with its own asyncio loop used to run Modal SDK coroutines safely from synchronous code.

key_constructs.name: _get_snapshot_restore_candidate
key_constructs.purpose: Resolves snapshot ids for a task and indicates legacy key presence for migration.

key_behaviors: creates and manages Modal sandboxes
executes commands and returns outputs
snapshots and restores persistent filesystem state

insights.title: Replace swe-rex wrapper with native Modal SDK
insights.problem: swe-rex dependency caused AsyncUsageWarning (sync App.lookup used in async context) and unencrypted tunnel deprecations; complexity from HTTP runtime was unnecessary.
insights.root_cause: Relying on swe-rex wrapper introduced extra HTTP/tunnel layer and async/sync mismatch.
insights.solution: Rewrote ModalEnvironment to use Modal SDK async API (App.lookup.aio, Sandbox.create.aio, Sandbox.exec.aio). Kept _AsyncWorker background thread pattern so synchronous caller code remains safe; implemented persistent snapshots via sandbox.snapshot_filesystem.aio(); adapted command execution to read stdout/stderr and exit codes via SDK. Updated cleanup/terminate behavior.

tools/environments/ssh.py.short_description: SSH remote execution environment with ControlMaster persistence
tools/environments/ssh.py.description: Provides remote command execution over SSH using ControlMaster for connection persistence, supports syncing of skills and credential files via rsync, and offers both oneshot and persistent-shell execution modes with interrupt and timeout handling. It detects remote home, manages control sockets, and cleans up connections and temp files on cleanup.

key_constructs.name: _ensure_ssh_available
key_constructs.purpose: Raises a clear error if ssh client is not found in PATH.

key_constructs.name: SSHEnvironment
key_constructs.purpose: PersistentShellMixin/BaseEnvironment subclass that runs commands over SSH with ControlMaster, syncs files, and supports both oneshot and persistent execution.

key_behaviors: establishes persistent SSH connections
syncs skills and credentials to remote host
executes commands remotely with interruption support

insights.title: Sanitize remote workdir interpolation for SSH backend
insights.problem: Remote workdir could contain shell metacharacters and cause injection when building 'cd {workdir} && ...' wrapper.
insights.root_cause: Unquoted remote workdir interpolation.
insights.solution: Quote the workdir with shlex.quote except keep '~' unquoted and handle ~/ specially.
insights.lesson_learned: Sanitize remote path interpolation for SSH commands just like local shell wrappers.

gateway/platforms/base.py.short_description: Common base classes, cache utilities, and message normalization for platforms
gateway/platforms/base.py.description: Defines the base adapter interface BasePlatformAdapter, MessageEvent/SendResult dataclasses, media/document/audio caching utilities, and helper functions used by all platform adapters. It centralizes sending/receiving semantics, retryable error detection, cache directories, and basic URL/log sanitization to ensure consistent platform behavior across Telegram, WhatsApp, Discord, etc.

key_constructs.purpose: Abstract base class that platform adapters inherit for lifecycle, message handling, background task tracking, and error management.

callers.context: from gateway.platforms.base import BasePlatformAdapter, MessageEvent, MessageType

key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing a normalized incoming platform message with media, reply context, and metadata.

from gateway.platforms.base import MessageEvent as _ME, MessageType as _MT

key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing send outcome including retryable flag and error details.

key_constructs.name: cache_image_from_url
key_constructs.purpose: Downloads and caches images with retries and returns a local file path.

key_constructs.name: cache_document_from_bytes
key_constructs.purpose: Stores uploaded document bytes in a sanitized cache path and returns file path.

caches media and documents locally for tools

insights.title: Bypass Level 1 active-session guard for control commands (/stop, /new, /reset, /approve, /deny, /status)
insights.problem: While an agent was running, base adapter queued all incoming messages; control commands like /stop and /new were queued and sometimes leaked into the conversation or deadlocked approval flows.
insights.root_cause: The Level 1 guard intercepted everything and did not include several administrative commands in the bypass set.
insights.solution: Consolidate bypass logic and add /stop, /new, /reset to bypass set so they are dispatched inline to gateway runner's handler. Also catch dispatch errors and log them.
insights.lesson: Guard layers must allow control commands to bypass normal message queuing; treat admin commands as a separate class that must be processed immediately to avoid leakage or deadlocks.

insights.title: Pause typing indicator support
insights.problem: Typing indicator (_keep_typing) kept refreshing while the agent waited for approval; on Slack this disables the compose box via assistant_threads_setStatus preventing users from typing /approve or /deny.
insights.root_cause: Typing refresh ran regardless of waiting-for-input state.
insights.solution: Add _typing_paused set and pause_typing_for_chat/resume_typing_for_chat methods; _keep_typing skips send_typing for paused chats. Pause before sending approval prompt, resume after resolution.
insights.lesson: Long-running platform indicators should be pausable when awaiting user input to avoid interfering with platform UX (e.g., blocking compose box).

insights.title: Sanitize media URLs in logs to avoid leaking query secrets
insights.problem: Logs printed full media URLs (including query params and userinfo) which could leak secrets (e.g., presigned S3 query tokens).
insights.root_cause: Directly logging raw URL substrings without sanitization.
insights.solution: Added _safe_url_for_log(url, max_len) that strips query, fragment and userinfo and truncates long values; used it in image/audio cache retry logs and in BasePlatformAdapter sending logs.
insights.lesson_learned: Never log raw URLs that may contain credentials; provide centralized sanitization and use it consistently.

insights.title: Strip @botname suffix from slash commands
insights.problem: Commands sent in group chats include an @botname suffix (e.g., /compress@TigerNanoBot) and were not recognized.
insights.root_cause: get_command() returned the raw command without normalizing away the @mention suffix.
insights.solution: Strip the '@...' part from the command token before lowercasing/returning.
insights.lesson_learned: Platform-specific message forms (mentions, suffixed commands) must be normalized so command dispatch logic remains robust across contexts.

plugins/memory/holographic/__init__.py.short_description: Holographic structured memory plugin with fact store and retrieval
plugins/memory/holographic/__init__.py.description: Implements a MemoryProvider backed by a structured SQLite fact store with HRR-based retrieval, entity resolution, trust scoring, and auto-extraction heuristics. It exposes tool schemas for fact storage and feedback, provides prefetch hooks for relevant facts, and integrates with session lifecycle to optionally auto-extract facts on session end.

key_constructs.name: HolographicMemoryProvider

key_constructs.name: register

performs semantic and algebraic retrieval
auto-extracts facts from conversation on session end

Provides get_honcho_client() to lazily create a Honcho client singleton and helpers to resolve active host and session naming strategies used by the Honcho memory provider.

plugins/memory/openviking/__init__.py.short_description: OpenViking memory plugin integrating with an OpenViking server
plugins/memory/openviking/__init__.py.description: Provides a MemoryProvider implementation that interacts with an OpenViking REST API for tiered contextual retrieval, semantic search, browsing, and explicit memory writes. It implements a thin HTTP client, background prefetch/sync threads, session commit on end, and exposes tool schemas for search/read/browse/remember/add_resource functionality.

key_constructs.name: _VikingClient
key_constructs.purpose: Lightweight httpx-based client encapsulating OpenViking API calls with headers and basic helpers.

key_constructs.name: OpenVikingMemoryProvider
key_constructs.purpose: MemoryProvider that records turns, prefetches context, commits sessions, and implements tool handlers for OpenViking features.

key_constructs.name: SEARCH_SCHEMA
key_constructs.purpose: Tool schema for performing semantic searches against OpenViking.

key_behaviors: records conversation turns to OpenViking
prefetches search results for context injection
commits sessions to trigger extraction and allows explicit remembers

insights.title: Ensure pending OpenViking sessions are committed on process exit and avoid double-commit
insights.problem: Pending session messages could be lost if shutdown_memory_provider was never called (gateway crash, exceptions in async flush) and the final turn's messages could be skipped because turn_count was checked before waiting for the pending sync thread.
insights.root_cause: No process-level safety net to commit sessions on process exit; ordering bug where turn_count check happened before waiting for sync thread; a global last-provider reference could cause double-commits on normal shutdown.
insights.solution: Register an atexit handler that calls on_session_end for the last active provider; set a global _last_active_provider in provider __init__; reorder on_session_end to join pending sync thread before checking _turn_count so the last turn's messages are flushed; clear the atexit global on normal shutdown to avoid double-commits.
insights.lesson_learned: Long-running providers that maintain in-memory pending state must register a best-effort process-exit safety net; joins should be performed before turn-count (or other short-circuit) checks to avoid missing the last in-flight work; clear global shutdown references to avoid duplicate work.

agent/anthropic_adapter.py.short_description: Anthropic Messages API client and auth helpers
agent/anthropic_adapter.py.description: Translates Hermes internal messages to Anthropic's Messages API, builds Anthropic client instances handling multiple auth types (x-api-key, Bearer/OAuth, Claude Code credentials), and exposes helpers for token refresh, credential discovery, and model capability metadata. It centralizes provider-specific headers, beta flags, and model output limits for safe usage across the agent.

key_constructs.name: build_anthropic_client
key_constructs.purpose: Creates an anthropic.Anthropic client and configures auth mode and beta headers for API keys, OAuth tokens, and third-party endpoints.

key_constructs.name: read_claude_code_credentials
key_constructs.purpose: Reads refreshable Claude Code OAuth credentials from ~/.codex/.credentials.json for reuse and refresh.

key_constructs.name: _is_oauth_token
key_constructs.purpose: Heuristic to determine whether a provided Anthropic key is an OAuth/setup-token vs a regular API key.

key_behaviors: constructs Anthropic SDK clients with correct auth and headers
auto-detects and refreshes Claude Code OAuth credentials
provides model-specific output limits and feature capability checks

insights.title: Remove unused OAuth wrapper and helper conversions
insights.problem: Several Hermes-specific OAuth wrapper functions and a private conversion helper had no callers in the codebase.
insights.root_cause: These functions became unused after refactors that moved to pure functions or changed the login/refresh flow; references were removed but the old wrappers remained.
insights.solution: Deleted the unused run_hermes_oauth_login, refresh_hermes_oauth_token wrappers and a private _convert_user_content_part_to_anthropic helper.
insights.lesson: When refactoring public vs. pure helpers ensure wrappers are removed or migrated and run a full-code reference search; unused wrappers can mislead about runtime behavior.

insights.title: Use model-native output limits for Anthropic instead of hardcoded 16K
insights.problem: Anthropic adapter defaulted to 16384 max_tokens when none configured, starving models that support much larger output (e.g., Claude Opus 4.6 at 128K).
insights.root_cause: Hardcoded fallback of 16K ignored model-specific native output limits; thinking tokens counted against max_tokens causing exhaustion and truncated/empty responses.
insights.solution: Introduce _ANTHROPIC_OUTPUT_LIMITS map and _get_anthropic_max_output() with substring longest-prefix match; default to highest known limit for unknown models; clamp max_tokens to context_length-1 when provided.
insights.lesson_learned: Adapter defaults must reflect model capabilities; prefer model-native limits and clamp to user-configured context length to avoid exceeding windows.

tools/terminal_tool.py.short_description: Terminal tool that runs commands across multiple execution backends
tools/terminal_tool.py.description: High-level terminal tool implementation that routes shell commands to local, Docker, Modal, Singularity, and SSH environments, enforcing safety guards (sudo handling, dangerous-command approval), workdir validation, and environment lifecycle management. It provides helpers to prompt for sudo (interactive or cached), transform sudo invocations for stdin piping, disk usage warnings, environment override registration, and manages global environment caches and cleanup.

key_constructs.name: _transform_sudo_command
key_constructs.purpose: Transforms sudo invocations to use -S and returns stdin password to allow non-interactive sudo with provided credentials.

key_constructs.name: set_sudo_password_callback
key_constructs.purpose: Registers a CLI callback to collect sudo password via UI integration.

key_constructs.name: register_task_env_overrides
key_constructs.purpose: Registers per-task environment overrides (e.g., custom images) used when creating environments for a task.

key_behaviors: executes shell commands across multiple backends
prompts and manages sudo credentials for command execution
applies approval and dangerous-command guards before running

insights.title: notify_on_complete: automatic agent notification for background processes
insights.problem: Users running long background commands wanted automatic notification when the process finished without manual polling.
insights.root_cause: Background processes previously required manual polling or wait calls to notify the agent.
insights.solution: Added notify_on_complete boolean to terminal tool schema and handler; ProcessSession.notify_on_complete set when used; ProcessRegistry.completion_queue populated on process finish; gateway/CLI use queue to trigger synthesized messages and agent turns.
insights.lesson: Design background execution APIs with a lightweight notification option that enqueues completion details; ensure checks and persistence include the new flag for crash recovery.

insights.title: Consolidate dangerous-command guard usage
insights.problem: A helper _check_dangerous_command wrapper was a thin pass-through to a consolidated approval module and was unused.
insights.root_cause: Approval/guard logic was centralized; wrapper no longer needed.
insights.solution: Removed the wrapper and rely on the _check_all_guards consolidated impl.
insights.lesson: Prefer single consolidated guard helpers and avoid multiple thin wrappers that drift out of sync.

insights.title: Allowlist validation for workdir before executing terminal commands
insights.problem: Workdir values could include shell metacharacters and cause command injection when passed to backends.
insights.root_cause: No defensive validation of workdir strings before they are used by backends.
insights.solution: Added _WORKDIR_SAFE_RE allowlist (alphanumeric, separators, tilde, dot, hyphen, underscore, space, plus, at, equals, comma) and _validate_workdir() returning an error string when dangerous; terminal_tool early-returns a blocked status payload for unsafe workdir.
insights.lesson_learned: Use allowlists for file/path validation rather than deny-lists to reduce risk of novel shell metacharacters slipping through; perform defense-in-depth by validating at the tool boundary and quoting at backend boundaries.

insights.title: Terminal modal backend now checks for modal SDK instead of swe-rex
insights.problem: Health-check/install guidance referenced swe-rex which is no longer required after refactor.
insights.root_cause: Dependency change to Modal SDK made swe-rex checks obsolete.
insights.solution: Changed importlib.spec check and error messages to reference 'modal' instead of 'swerex'/'swe-rex'.

trajectory_compressor.py.short_description: Compresses agent trajectories into token budgets using summarization
trajectory_compressor.py.description: Post-process tool to compress completed agent trajectories to a target token budget by preserving protected head/tail turns and summarizing middle regions via an LLM summarizer. It counts tokens using a tokenizer, batches summarization requests (supports provider routing or direct endpoints), collects per-trajectory and aggregate metrics, and writes compressed outputs and metrics files.

key_constructs.name: CompressionConfig
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass capturing tokenizer, summarization, and processing configuration for compression jobs.

key_constructs.name: TrajectoryCompressor
key_constructs.purpose: Orchestrates token counting, protected-region detection, summarization calls, and writing compressed trajectories and metrics.

key_constructs.name: TrajectoryMetrics
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass that records detailed per-trajectory compression statistics and diagnostic counters.

key_behaviors: measures tokens for trajectories
selectively summarizes middle regions to meet token targets
emits compressed trajectories and aggregate metrics

hermes_cli/setup.py.short_description: Interactive setup wizard for Hermes Agent configuration
hermes_cli/setup.py.description: Provides an interactive, modular setup wizard to configure providers, models, terminal backends, messaging platforms, and tools. It reads/writes Hermes config and environment helpers and offers non-interactive guidance when stdin isn't a TTY.

insights.root_cause: Catalog changes from providers were not mirrored in defaults used during initial setup.
insights.solution: Update _DEFAULT_PROVIDER_MODELS with Gemini 3.x entries and Gemma variants; remove deprecated Gemini 2.0 references.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep setup defaults synchronized with supported provider catalogs; fallback values help offline installs but must be periodically reviewed.

insights.title: Installer now installs modal SDK (not swe-rex)
insights.problem: Setup flow and messaging still referenced swe-rex during Modal backend setup.
insights.root_cause: Dependency replaced; packaging messages required update.
insights.solution: Adjusted messaging and pip install target to 'modal'.

insights.title: Add HF provider setup flow to interactive setup
insights.problem: Interactive setup did not present Hugging Face option or token prompt.
insights.root_cause: Hugging Face provider newly registered but setup flow not updated.
insights.solution: Add provider list entry and interactive token prompt/save in setup for huggingface.
insights.lesson_learned: Interactive setup should be updated in lockstep when adding providers; ensure environment variables are cleared/updated to prevent routing confusion.

gateway/platforms/signal.py.short_description: Signal messenger adapter using signal-cli HTTP daemon
gateway/platforms/signal.py.description: Adapter that connects to a signal-cli HTTP daemon to receive inbound messages via SSE and send outbound messages via JSON-RPC. It handles streaming reconnects, health monitoring, attachments fetching/caching, mention rendering, group allowlists, and self-message filtering before routing messages into Hermes.

key_constructs.purpose: Main platform adapter that manages connection, SSE listener, health monitor, message parsing, and send operations

insights.title: Implement proper attachment sending methods for Signal adapter
insights.problem: Signal adapter inherited base methods for send_image_file/send_voice/send_video which only sent file path as text (no attachment), so MEDIA: tag delivery resulted in literal paths instead of attachments.
insights.root_cause: Adapter did not override attachment methods; inconsistent behavior with other platform adapters.
insights.solution: Added a shared _send_attachment helper that validates file existence and size (single stat call, handles FileNotFoundError), routes group vs DM properly, and performs RPC send; implemented send_document/send_image_file/send_voice/send_video delegating to _send_attachment; added size checks and improved error messages.
insights.lesson_learned: Ensure platform adapters override all media delivery entry points used by the gateway media pipeline; use a single validation syscall to avoid TOCTOU and provide clear error diagnostics including file paths.

gateway/platforms/telegram_network.py.short_description: Transport that retries Telegram API requests via fallback IPs preserving TLS/SNI
gateway/platforms/telegram_network.py.description: Provides an httpx Async transport which preserves api.telegram.org hostname/TLS SNI while retrying TCP connects against discovered fallback IPv4 addresses. It can discover fallback IPs using DNS-over-HTTPS providers and falls back to a seed list, enabling operation on networks with broken DNS routing.

key_constructs.name: TelegramFallbackTransport
key_constructs.purpose: httpx AsyncBaseTransport that retries connections against fallback IPs while preserving host and TLS SNI

key_constructs.name: discover_fallback_ips
key_constructs.purpose: Discover Telegram API IPv4 addresses via DoH providers and exclude system-resolved IPs

key_constructs.name: _rewrite_request_for_ip
key_constructs.purpose: Clone an httpx.Request targeting a fallback IP but keep host header and sni_hostname

key_constructs.name: _normalize_fallback_ips
key_constructs.purpose: Validate and normalize candidate IPv4 addresses

key_behaviors: retry Telegram Bot API requests against fallback IPs preserving hostname
discover fallback IPs via DoH and filter out system DNS results
maintain sticky fallback selection to avoid repeated failures

insights.title: Honor proxy env vars for fallback transport
insights.problem: Telegram fallback transport did not inherit proxy configuration from environment, causing fallback requests to bypass proxy when primary transport used one.
insights.root_cause: Fallback transport constructor did not check HTTP(S)_PROXY/ALL_PROXY environment variables and therefore didn't set proxy kwargs for transport creation.
insights.solution: Added _resolve_proxy_url() helper that reads typical proxy env vars and, if present, injects 'proxy' into transport_kwargs when creating primary and fallback httpx transports.
insights.lesson_learned: Network transports must consistently apply proxy configuration across primary and fallback paths; reading common env var keys ensures consistency without requiring explicit config.

tools/browser_providers/browser_use.py.short_description: Provider for Browser Use cloud browser sessions
tools/browser_providers/browser_use.py.description: Implements a CloudBrowserProvider for Browser Use (browser-use.com), supporting direct API key or managed gateway modes. It creates, closes, and emergency-cleans browser sessions, using idempotency keys when managed and exposing session metadata like CDP URL and feature flags.

key_constructs.name: BrowserUseProvider
key_constructs.purpose: Provider implementation to create and manage Browser Use sessions (create/close/emergency cleanup)

key_constructs.name: _get_or_create_pending_create_key
key_constructs.purpose: Manage in-memory idempotency keys used when creating sessions via a managed gateway

key_constructs.purpose: Determine if a failed create response should keep the pending idempotency key

key_behaviors: create and close Browser Use sessions
support managed-gateway idempotency and short managed timeouts
handle API errors and perform emergency cleanup

insights.title: Add Browser Use provider with managed-gateway support and idempotency handling
insights.problem: Nous-managed browser sessions previously routed through Browserbase; switching to Browser Use required managed-mode behavior, idempotency and header fixes.
insights.root_cause: Managed provider expectations differ (headers, endpoints, payload), and idempotency handling was necessary to avoid race conditions on create_session.
insights.solution: Implemented BrowserUseProvider supporting direct API key or resolve_managed_tool_gateway() for managed mode, _get_or_create_pending_create_key() for idempotency, _should_preserve_pending_create_key() to decide whether pending key should be preserved on 409/500, and use X-Browser-Use-API-Key header. Normalized cdp_url handling and returned external_call_id for managed responses.
insights.lesson: Managed cloud providers require idempotency keys and careful handling of conflict/timeouts; header names and payload expectations can differ across vendors — centralize provider config resolution.

tools/browser_providers/browserbase.py.short_description: Provider for Browserbase cloud browser sessions
tools/browser_providers/browserbase.py.description: CloudBrowserProvider implementation for Browserbase that requires direct API credentials. It creates sessions with optional features (proxies, advanced stealth, keep-alive), handles 402 fallbacks for paid features, and closes or force-releases sessions.

key_constructs.name: BrowserbaseProvider
key_constructs.purpose: Provider that creates, closes, and emergency-cleans Browserbase sessions

key_constructs.name: _get_config_or_none

insights.title: Browserbase becomes direct-only (managed Nous changes move to Browser Use)
insights.problem: Managed Nous gateway now uses Browser Use; Browserbase should no longer attempt to support managed mode and had previously mixed direct and managed behavior.
insights.root_cause: Switching managed gateway vendor required making Browserbase direct-only to avoid misrouting managed requests.
insights.solution: Remove managed-mode resolution from BrowserbaseProvider; require BROWSERBASE_API_KEY & PROJECT_ID for configuration and raise informative errors when missing. Cleaned up idempotency/pending-key logic (moved to browser_use).
insights.lesson: When changing a managed backend vendor, retain direct credentials support for existing users but remove managed fallback to prevent ambiguous routing.

tools/browser_providers/firecrawl.py.short_description: Provider for Firecrawl cloud browser sessions
tools/browser_providers/firecrawl.py.description: Implements CloudBrowserProvider for Firecrawl, creating short-lived browser sessions via the Firecrawl API and providing close and emergency cleanup primitives. It wraps HTTP requests and returns CDP/connect URLs for sessions.

insights.title: Add Firecrawl cloud browser provider
insights.problem: No Firecrawl provider existed; browser tool lacked integration with Firecrawl cloud browser service.
insights.root_cause: New cloud provider support required implementing create/close/emergency cleanup and integrating TTL/config variables.
insights.solution: Added FirecrawlProvider implementing provider_name, is_configured, create_session (POST), close_session (DELETE), emergency_cleanup, and uses FIRECRAWL_API_KEY and optional FIRECRAWL_API_URL and FIRECRAWL_BROWSER_TTL.
insights.lesson_learned: Providers should centralize header and URL construction and reuse config TTL; document env vars and add to onboarding options so users can configure provider credentials.

insights.title: Add Supermemory memory provider
insights.problem: Need for a semantic long-term memory provider with search and profile recall.
insights.root_cause: New provider implementation required SDK integration, config, tools, and tests.
insights.solution: Introduce SupermemoryMemoryProvider with config read/write, prefetch/ingest behaviors, explicit tools (store, search, forget, profile), tests for provider behavior, and plugin.yaml registration.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding a new plugin/provider, include comprehensive unit tests for availability, config round-trip, prefetch behavior, sync/ingest and tools; obey HERMES_HOME and get_hermes_home() conventions for profile-scoped storage.

insights.title: Use get_hermes_home() for profile-scoped storage
insights.problem: Provider used hardcoded ~/.hermes path, which breaks profile isolation.
insights.root_cause: Code used os.path.expanduser('~/.hermes') instead of centralized get_hermes_home() utility.
insights.solution: Switch to get_hermes_home() in initialize when hermes_home not provided and fix README example.
insights.lesson_learned: Use centralized constants/utilities for paths to maintain profile isolation and avoid hardcoded home paths.

insights.title: Prevent unbounded growth of _seen_uids set in EmailAdapter
insights.problem: EmailAdapter._seen_uids accumulated every IMAP UID seen, never pruning, leading to unbounded memory growth in long-running high-volume inboxes.
insights.root_cause: No eviction/trimming policy for the UID tracking set.
insights.solution: Added _seen_uids_max and _trim_seen_uids() that keeps only the most recent half of the cap (keeps newest 1000 when cap 2000). Call _trim_seen_uids() during connect and after fetch cycles.

insights.title: Interactive model picker via discord.ui.View
insights.problem: Switching models via text is clumsy in Discord; needed an interactive, discoverable picker with dropdowns.
insights.root_cause: No prior interactive UI for model browsing; Discord supports Select menus and Views enabling a two-step drilldown.
insights.solution: Add send_model_picker() to send an embed and a ModelPickerView class that presents a provider select and then a model select, with back/cancel, auth checks, and in-place edits.
insights.lesson_learned: When building multi-step UIs on platforms with richer interaction primitives, centralize the logic in a View to preserve state and lifecycle (timeouts), and include auth gating.

insights.title: Remove default selection on provider dropdown
insights.problem: Discord didn't fire the select callback when clicking an already-selected default option, preventing users from (re)selecting the current provider to browse models.
insights.root_cause: SelectOption was being created with default=True for the current provider, which caused Discord to treat selecting it again as no change and thus not emit an event.
insights.solution: Remove default=bool(p.get('is_current')) from SelectOption so selection always triggers callback.
insights.lesson_learned: Platform-specific UI behavior (like 'no callback if selection doesn't change') can break expected flows; avoid relying on UI widget 'default' selection when you need a callback on re-selection.

insights.title: Register skills as native Discord slash commands using shared gateway collection logic
insights.problem: Discord had its own registration logic and diverged from Telegram's behavior; command caps and filtering were duplicated.
insights.root_cause: Different code paths for different platforms led to inconsistent command registration, cap enforcement, and name handling.
insights.solution: Call hermes_cli.commands.discord_skill_commands() to obtain skill entries (name, description, cmd_key) and register them as app commands, preserving reserved names and logging skipped skills when Discord's 100-command cap is reached. Use a closure factory to bind cmd_key to handler.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize platform-agnostic collection/filtering logic for cross-platform features (slash command registration) to ensure consistent behavior across adapters and easier maintenance.

insights.title: Register native Discord slash commands for queue/background/btw
insights.problem: Commands /queue, /background, /btw were present in the central registry but not registered as native Discord slash commands, so they didn't appear in Discord's slash picker.
insights.root_cause: Gateway adapter created handlers at runtime but did not call @tree.command() for these commands.
insights.solution: Add @tree.command decorated async handlers (slash_queue, slash_background, slash_btw) which call existing _run_simple_slash helper to integrate with command handling.
insights.lesson_learned: When exposing cross-platform commands, ensure each platform adapter registers native UI affordances (slash commands) to give users consistent experiences. Keep names/descriptions aligned with central registry.

gateway/platforms/matrix.py.short_description: Matrix (matrix-nio) gateway adapter with optional E2EE
gateway/platforms/matrix.py.description: Connects to a Matrix homeserver using matrix-nio, supports optional end-to-end encryption, subscribes to room events, handles media and reactions, and converts room messages into MessageEvent objects. The adapter manages sync loops, E2EE key handling, sending (with HTML formatting and threads), deduplication, and robust error/retry behavior for encrypted sends.

key_constructs.name: MatrixAdapter
key_constructs.purpose: Adapter that connects to Matrix, handles sync loop, E2EE, event callbacks and room sends

key_constructs.name: check_matrix_requirements
key_constructs.purpose: Validates matrix-nio availability, homeserver and auth; ensures E2EE deps when requested

key_constructs.name: _format_state_change
key_constructs.purpose: Helper (not in file root but conceptually) used for formatting messages — file contains markdown-to-html conversion and formatting helpers

key_constructs.name: _STORE_DIR
key_constructs.purpose: Filesystem path used to persist E2EE store and sync state

key_behaviors: syncs with Matrix homeserver and forwards room messages to the agent
sends Matrix messages with optional encryption and HTML formatting
manages E2EE keys, pending encrypted events, and deduplication

insights.title: Hard-fail when Matrix E2EE enabled but python-olm missing
insights.problem: When matrix-nio is installed without the e2e extras (python-olm/libolm missing), ENCRYPTION_ENABLED is False; adapter logged warnings but returned True from connect(), causing the bot to appear online but fail to decrypt messages in E2EE rooms.
insights.root_cause: Silent degradation to plaintext mode when E2EE was requested resulted in false-positive successful connect and broken behavior at runtime.
insights.solution: Added _check_e2ee_deps() to assert python-olm availability (nio.crypto.ENCRYPTION_ENABLED). Modified check_matrix_requirements() and MatrixAdapter.connect() to hard-fail and return False with clear error messages when MATRIX_ENCRYPTION=true but E2EE deps are missing; ensure client creation failures propagate as errors.
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer failing fast with clear, actionable messages when required crypto dependencies are missing instead of silently degrading; validate environment proactively during startup.

insights.title: Stable MATRIX_DEVICE_ID support for E2EE persistence
insights.problem: Without a stable device_id the bot would present a new device on each restart, causing other clients to refuse to share Megolm keys and preventing E2EE from functioning reliably.
insights.root_cause: Device identity was not plumbed through config/env and not passed to nio.AsyncClient or restore_login/restore paths.
insights.solution: Read MATRIX_DEVICE_ID from env and platform extras, prefer config extra over env; pass device_id to nio.AsyncClient constructor and use effective_device_id in restore_login/restore paths; log guidance messages; fail connect when crypto store not available.
insights.lesson_learned: When dealing with persistent crypto stores and device identity, allow users to pin device_id for stability across restarts and ensure it's passed to client constructors and restoration functions.

insights.title: Use get_hermes_home() for Matrix E2EE store path
insights.problem: Matrix E2EE store directory was hardcoded to ~/.hermes/... which broke per-profile HERMES_HOME usage.
insights.root_cause: Paths used Path.home() and literal ~/.hermes instead of centralized get_hermes_home().
insights.solution: Use hermes_constants.get_hermes_home() to compute store directory so each profile has an isolated store.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize home/profile resolution in a single helper and use it everywhere to support profiles/alternate HERMES_HOME.

insights.title: Harden E2EE access-token handling and device restore
insights.problem: Using access_token auth without resolving device_id and restoring login could leave E2EE crypto state uninitialized, causing failure to decrypt inbound events or encrypt outbound messages.
insights.root_cause: Code didn't always call restore_login or set device_id correctly when access_token present; logging and behavior assumed a user_id-only path.
insights.solution: Always call whoami and extract user_id/device_id. If device_id present and client supports restore_login(), call restore_login(user_id, device_id, access_token) to bind crypto store. Warn when encryption requested but crypto store not restored. Improved logging.

insights.title: Retry encrypted send after E2EE maintenance when unverified device errors/timeouts occur
insights.problem: Initial encrypted send could fail with OlmUnverifiedDeviceError or Timeout; previously this may not have been retried with ignore_unverified_devices=True.
insights.root_cause: Single-path send didn't attempt a retry after running E2EE maintenance and ignoring unverified devices, leading to permanent send failures on common E2EE races.
insights.solution: Wrap room_send with helper that supports ignore_unverified_devices. On retryable exceptions (timeout, OlmUnverifiedDeviceError, SendRetryError), run _run_e2ee_maintenance() and retry with ignore_unverified_devices=True. Log appropriately.

It handles insertion, update, deletion, feedback, entity extraction/linking, and rebuilding category memory banks for holographic retrieval.
