gateway/platforms/feishu.py.description: Implements an adapter bridging Hermes to Feishu/Lark via WebSocket and webhook transports, parsing rich post payloads, caching media, enforcing group policies, batching messages, handling deduplication and interactive card events. It normalizes Feishu messages into internal MessageEvent structures and provides sending, batching, and media upload logic with rate and security controls.

key_constructs.name: FeishuAdapterSettings
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass holding adapter runtime configuration like credentials, batching, dedup and webhook settings.

key_constructs.name: FeishuPostParseResult
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing parsed post content including text, image keys, media refs and mentions.

key_constructs.name: parse_feishu_post_content
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level parser converting Feishu post JSON into normalized text and media references.

key_constructs.name: _build_markdown_post_payload
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to build Feishu Markdown post payloads for sending messages.

key_constructs.name: _escape_markdown_text
key_constructs.purpose: Utility for escaping special characters when converting rich content to Markdown.

key_behaviors: receives and normalizes Feishu inbound messages and events
uploads and caches images/files/audio from Feishu
enforces allowlist/group policies and deduplication
sends batched and formatted responses with fallback handling

insights.title: Per-group access control for Feishu with global admins and group rules
insights.problem: Feishu adapter lacked fine-grained per-group authorization policies.
insights.root_cause: Only global FEISHU_GROUP_POLICY and FEISHU_ALLOWED_USERS were supported; couldn't express per-chat allowlist/blacklist/admin-only/disabled rules.
insights.solution: Add FeishuAdapterSettings.admins, default_group_policy and group_rules mapping. Implement FeishuGroupRule with policies, parse config.extra.group_rules, thread chat_id through group gating, and change _allow_group_message / _should_accept_group_message to accept chat_id and apply per-group rules; allow global admins to bypass rules.
insights.lesson_learned: Group chat platforms need per-chat policy overrides and global admin bypass. Thread chat identifiers through gate checks and normalize both open_id and user_id for backward compatibility.

insights.title: Configurable Feishu websocket reconnect and ping timing
insights.problem: Feishu SDK default reconnect jitter and interval (first reconnect random 0-30s, subsequent 120s) and unreachable ping timing made reconnections slow and detection of disconnects slow on unstable networks.
insights.root_cause: Hard-coded reliance on SDK defaults for reconnect nonce/interval and no way to set websocket ping_interval/ping_timeout from platform extras.
insights.solution: Added FeishuAdapterSettings fields ws_reconnect_nonce, ws_reconnect_interval, ws_ping_interval, ws_ping_timeout; introduced coercion helpers to validate config and applied these values when connecting by wrapping the SDK websockets.connect to inject ping settings and by setting internal ws client attributes for reconnect timing.
insights.lesson_learned: Expose SDK tunables via adapter config with input coercion; keep SDK defaults when values are invalid; ensure runtime application of overrides if SDK refreshes config.

insights.title: Fix message drops caused by stale event handler / loop references
insights.problem: Inbound messages were intermittently dropped after reconnect with warnings like "Dropping inbound message before adapter loop is ready".
insights.root_cause: Event handler instance was created once in __init__ and reused across reconnects; callbacks captured stale adapter loop references after reconnect, causing run_coroutine_threadsafe to target an invalid/closed loop.
insights.solution: Rebuild event handler on every connect, clear handler on disconnect, and change runtime callbacks to check loop readiness (using getattr(loop, 'is_closed', ...)) before scheduling coroutine threadsafe; unify webhook dispatch path to use same checks.
insights.lesson_learned: When platform adapters use background threads and SDK callbacks, ensure callbacks capture current live event loop and rebuild resources on reconnect to avoid stale closures capturing old state.

insights.title: Fix shutdown hang by stopping websocket thread loop and cancelling tasks
insights.problem: Process could hang on Ctrl+C or SIGTERM because the Feishu SDK runs a background thread with an infinite select loop that never exited, and the thread wasn't joined on disconnect.
insights.root_cause: Thread-local event loop used by SDK thread wasn't stopped/cleaned properly; pending tasks and the thread were left running preventing process exit.
insights.solution: Store reference to thread-local event loop (_ws_thread_loop), on disconnect cancel tasks in that loop, call loop.stop via call_soon_threadsafe, await the thread future with a timeout, and clean up pending tasks in the thread's finally block prior to closing the loop.
insights.lesson_learned: When integrating third-party SDKs that spawn their own threads/event loops, ensure adapter tracks and cleanly stops/join threads and cancels tasks to avoid process hangs.

insights.title: Reapply websocket runtime overrides after SDK reconfigures client
insights.problem: User-supplied websocket tuning (reconnect/ping settings) were not reliably applied because the Feishu SDK refreshed its client config after our initial overrides.
insights.root_cause: The SDK's client configuration path could call back into its own configure() which replaced client attributes we had set earlier.
insights.solution: Wrap both websockets.connect and the client's _configure method to reapply runtime overrides after the SDK configure() runs; assert original_configure presence and restore it in finally block.
insights.lesson_learned: When third-party SDKs provide configuration extension points, intercept both connection and configuration callbacks to ensure runtime overrides persist across SDK-initiated reconfiguration.

insights.title: Consolidate coercion helpers and add loop readiness check helper
insights.problem: Multiple nearly-identical coercion functions for integer config parsing and repeated loop readiness checks scattered in code.
insights.root_cause: Code duplication made maintenance harder and risked inconsistent behavior when validating extra config values.
insights.solution: Consolidated coercion functions into _coerce_int and _coerce_required_int, added _is_loop_ready helper that uses getattr to be test-friendly, and replaced duplicates accordingly.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize small coercion and predicate helpers to reduce duplication and to make unit-testing easier (e.g., graceful handling of missing is_closed attribute).

related_files.relationship: frequently changed together (from commit history)
related_files.reason_to_check: historically modified together with this file

It loads/refreshes credentials from a token file and maps subcommands to Google API calls returning JSON results.

insights.title: Allow direct execution of google-workspace skill scripts
insights.problem: Directly running scripts like setup.py or google_api.py from the repo failed to import hermes_constants.
insights.root_cause: Scripts assumed package-installed import paths and did not fall back to repo root path when executed directly.
insights.solution: Wrap import of hermes_constants in try/except ModuleNotFoundError and push repo root into sys.path when hermes_constants not importable.
insights.lesson_learned: Skill scripts should support direct execution from the checkout by adding a repo-root fallback to sys.path to make developer workflows smoother.

plugins/memory/holographic/holographic.p.short_description: HRR phase-vector utilities for holographic memory
plugins/memory/holographic/holographic.p.description: Implements holographic reduced representation (HRR) operations using phase-encoded vectors: deterministic atom encoding, bind/unbind, bundle, text/fact encoding, serialization and similarity metrics. Designed to create reproducible, cross-process phase vectors for storing and comparing structured memory representations.

key_constructs.name: encode_atom
key_constructs.purpose: Deterministically produce a phase vector for a string via SHA-256 blocks.

key_constructs.name: bind
key_constructs.purpose: Circular convolution implemented as element-wise phase addition to associate two vectors.

key_constructs.name: unbind
key_constructs.purpose: Circular correlation implemented as elemental phase subtraction to retrieve bound components.

key_constructs.name: bundle
key_constructs.purpose: Superpose vectors via circular mean (phase of summed complex exponentials).

key_constructs.name: encode_fact
key_constructs.purpose: Create a structured fact vector by binding content and entity role vectors and bundling them.

key_behaviors: generate deterministic phase vectors for tokens and entities
bind/unbind and bundle vectors to form compositional embeddings
serialize/deserialize vectors to bytes for storage

plugins/memory/holographic/retrieval.py.short_description: Hybrid retrieval strategies combining FTS, Jaccard and HRR
plugins/memory/holographic/retrieval.py.description: Provides FactRetriever which runs a hybrid retrieval pipeline: FTS5 candidate retrieval, Jaccard reranking, optional HRR similarity scoring, trust-weighting and temporal decay, plus compositional probes (probe/related/reason) using HRR algebra. It enables semantic and structural memory queries against the MemoryStore and special contradiction detection across facts.

key_constructs.name: FactRetriever
key_constructs.purpose: Main retrieval class implementing search, probe, related, reason, contradict and vector scoring using combined strategies.

key_constructs.name: _fts_candidates
key_constructs.purpose: Private helper to fetch raw FTS5 candidates from the store and normalize ranks.

key_constructs.name: search
key_constructs.purpose: Combined FTS+Jaccard+HRR retrieval pipeline that applies trust weighting and optional temporal decay.

key_constructs.name: probe
key_constructs.purpose: Compositional entity query that unbinds an entity key to extract associated content structurally.

key_constructs.name: contradict
key_constructs.purpose: Detects potentially contradictory fact pairs by entity overlap and low HRR content similarity.

key_behaviors: retrieve relevant facts combining text and vector similarity
perform compositional HRR probes and multi-entity reasoning
detect contradictions among stored facts

It includes secure stdio env filtering, reconnection/backoff, credential sanitization, sampling handlers and a background event loop hosting server tasks.

insights.title: Pass oauth config dict through to build_oauth_auth
insights.problem: mcp_tool only passed server_name and url to build_oauth_auth; OAuth-related config (client_id, secret, scope) was ignored.
insights.root_cause: Signature mismatch / incomplete parameter forwarding.
insights.solution: Pass config.get('oauth') through to build_oauth_auth so pre-registered client info and scope are honored.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding a new helper that accepts richer config, update call sites to forward the richer dict (avoid implicit reliance on global config inside helper).

Handles backend selection, client initialization, result normalization, optional LLM-based content summarization via auxiliary clients, and safety/policy checks.

insights.title: Guard and fallback on empty/None LLM content in web synthesis
insights.problem: Synthesis and content processing used .message.content.strip() which raised when content was None or returned empty for reasoning-only responses.
insights.root_cause: Assumed content always present; no fallback to reasoning fields.
insights.solution: Use extract_content_or_reasoning() and add retry logic on empty content; return whatever is available after retries.
insights.lesson_learned: For content-synthesis tools, handle reasoning-only LLM outputs, retry once or twice, and avoid crashes from None content.

gateway/platforms/api_server.py.short_description: OpenAI-compatible HTTP API adapter exposing Hermes via REST
gateway/platforms/api_server.py.description: Runs an aiohttp-based OpenAI-compatible API that proxies chat/completions and responses requests to Hermes AIAgent, stores response state, supports SSE run event streams, session continuity, idempotency caching, CORS, and API key auth. Enables integration with Open WebUI and other OpenAI-compatible frontends by mapping requests into agent sessions and returning streamed or stored results.

key_constructs.name: APIServerAdapter
key_constructs.purpose: Adapter that runs the aiohttp server, handles auth, request parsing, agent creation and streaming of results.

key_constructs.name: ResponseStore
key_constructs.purpose: SQLite-backed LRU storage of Responses API state to support previous_response_id and persistence.

key_constructs.name: cors_middleware
key_constructs.purpose: Middleware to enforce CORS origin allowlist and handle preflight requests.

callers.context: mws = [mw for mw in (cors_middleware, security_headers_middleware) if mw is not None]

key_constructs.name: _IdempotencyCache
key_constructs.purpose: In-memory idempotency cache with TTL used to deduplicate identical requests.

key_constructs.name: _handle_chat_completions
key_constructs.purpose: HTTP handler that maps OpenAI chat/completions requests to Hermes agent runs (including streaming).

key_behaviors: exposes OpenAI-compatible endpoints to drive Hermes agent
stores and retrieves Responses API state for session continuity
streams run lifecycle events via SSE and enforces CORS and API key auth
provides idempotency and request body size limits

insights.title: Add basic security headers to all API responses
insights.problem: API server responses lacked basic security headers (nosniff, no-referrer).
insights.root_cause: No global middleware existed to set security headers.
insights.solution: Add security_headers_middleware to include X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff and Referrer-Policy: no-referrer on all responses; wire into app middlewares list.
insights.lesson_learned: Apply basic security headers at middleware level so all routes and error responses are covered.

insights.title: Set Access-Control-Max-Age for preflight responses
insights.problem: Browsers repeatedly issued OPTIONS preflight requests causing latency and load.
insights.root_cause: CORS preflight responses lacked max-age header.
insights.solution: Add 'Access-Control-Max-Age': '600' to the CORS header set returned for preflight responses and CORS-per-origin responses.
insights.lesson_learned: Tuning CORS caching can reduce redundant preflight traffic for browser-based clients.

insights.title: Ensure CORS headers are present on SSE streaming responses
insights.problem: StreamResponse.prepare() flushes headers before middleware runs, so SSE responses lacked CORS and other middleware-injected headers.
insights.root_cause: Middleware cannot modify headers after prepare(); SSE handler created StreamResponse without precomputed CORS headers.
insights.solution: Compute CORS headers up front via _cors_headers_for_origin() and include them in StreamResponse headers before prepare().
insights.lesson_learned: For streaming responses that call prepare(), resolve middleware-determined headers ahead of time and include them in the initial response.

insights.title: /v1/health alias added for OpenAI compatibility
insights.problem: OpenAI-compatible clients expect /v1/health; only /health existed causing integration surprises.
insights.root_cause: Missing endpoint alias for OpenAI-style health checks.
insights.solution: Added router.add_get('/v1/health', self._handle_health) so /v1/health mirrors /health.

insights.title: Allow Idempotency-Key in CORS preflight
insights.problem: Browser clients using Idempotency-Key header were blocked by CORS preflight due to header not included in Access-Control-Allow-Headers.
insights.root_cause: _CORS_HEADERS omitted Idempotency-Key.
insights.solution: Added 'Idempotency-Key' to _CORS_HEADERS Access-Control-Allow-Headers.

insights.title: Interrupt agent and cancel task on SSE client disconnect
insights.problem: When a streaming SSE client disconnected mid-stream, agent LLM calls continued and background task could become orphaned, wasting resources.
insights.root_cause: SSE writer previously cancelled only the task wrapper without interrupting the agent; agent continued making LLM API calls.
insights.solution: Pass mutable agent_ref to agent run wrapper, call agent.interrupt('SSE client disconnected') on disconnect, then cancel the agent task; ensure graceful await and log.
insights.lesson_learned: When user disconnects mid-stream, actively interrupt in-progress work (not just cancel wrapper task) to stop external API calls and resource usage.

insights.title: Thread engagement — auto-respond in bot-started and mentioned threads
insights.problem: Bot would only respond when explicitly @mentioned in thread replies; conversations where the bot already participated required repeated mentions.
insights.root_cause: No tracking of bot-sent message timestamps or previously-mentioned threads; gating relied solely on mention detection.
insights.solution: Track _bot_message_ts (timestamps of messages bot sent) and _mentioned_threads (threads where bot was mentioned). Use these sets to allow auto-response in threads; bound both sets to 5000 entries and prune oldest when exceeding cap. Also fetch thread context on first entry not just when mentioned.
insights.lesson: Maintain lightweight provenance/participation sets with bounded memory to allow context-aware auto-responses in threaded platforms; ensure caps and prune strategies to avoid unbounded growth.

insights.title: Prevent Block Kit approval double-click race
insights.problem: Clicks on Slack approval buttons could be processed multiple times leading to double resolution of approval flows.
insights.root_cause: No per-message resolved-state tracked for approval messages.
insights.solution: Track _approval_resolved[msg_ts] flag when sending approval messages; prevent duplicate resolutions; update message blocks to show decision and remove buttons.
insights.lesson: Interactive button handlers must be idempotent and guard against duplicate events, especially when the underlying action unblocks threads or performs side effects.

insights.title: Apply markdown-to-mrkdwn conversion in edit_message
insights.problem: Slack edits were sending raw markdown directly to chat_update, producing malformed links and broken formatting.
insights.root_cause: edit_message bypassed format_message conversion that send() used.
insights.solution: Call self.format_message(content) in edit_message and pass formatted text to chat_update.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep send and edit flows consistent wrt formatting conversions; avoid duplicating logic — centralize formatting.

insights.solution: Add tests for skipping continuation when reasoning exhausted and for empty-content detection; update timeout default expectation to 1800s.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding early-exit cases and changing runtime defaults, update unit tests to cover both normal and edge-case flows.

insights.title: Add tests to ensure service-managed PIDs are excluded from manual sweeps
insights.problem: No tests existed to protect against the 'restart then immediate kill' regression.
insights.root_cause: Lack of integration tests for service restart flows allowed regression to slip in.
insights.solution: Add tests simulating systemd and launchd behavior, test exclusion logic, ensure manual PIDs are killed while service PIDs are preserved.
insights.lesson_learned: Critical system interactions need integration-style tests that simulate platform service managers.

insights.title: Verify self-heal and environment capture behavior for launchd plist
insights.problem: Need to assert the CLI regenerates the plist when missing and that generated plist contains PATH/VIRTUAL_ENV/HERMES_HOME and expected ordering/deduplication.
insights.root_cause: New behaviors around automatic plist regen and PATH/VIRTUAL_ENV injection require explicit tests.
insights.solution: Added tests covering missing-plist self-heal and multiple assertions on PATH contents and environment variables in generated plist.
insights.lesson_learned: Whenever service install/start behavior is changed to regenerate or snapshot environment, add tests that assert both action invocation (launchctl calls) and content of generated service definition.

insights.title: Add comprehensive tests for Gemini provider integration and models.dev filtering
insights.problem: Provider integration (Gemini) and models.dev filtering behavior lacked coverage.
insights.root_cause: New provider features had no corresponding tests to validate aliases, auth env var resolution, model normalization, and models.dev noise filter.
insights.solution: Add tests verifying PROVIDER_REGISTRY entries, env var resolution, model catalog entries, normalization behavior (passthrough/aggregator), context lengths, models.dev noise filters and list_agentic_models with mocked fetch responses.

insights.title: Update streaming callback expectations for suppressed content routing change
insights.problem: PR #3566 routes suppressed content to stream_delta_callback (for tag extraction) when tool calls are present; tests expected suppressed content to be omitted from callback.
insights.solution: Update assertions to expect that content after tool calls is delivered to stream_delta_callback (for reasoning tag extraction) while display-level suppression is handled later.
insights.lesson_learned: When streaming API behavior changes to support additional downstream processing (like tag extraction), tests must be updated to assert the new responsibilities between streaming callback and display layers.

insights.title: Add repo-root sys.path fallback for direct execution
insights.problem: setup.py could not import hermes_constants when executed directly.
insights.root_cause: Same as google_api.py — import path not configured for direct script runs.
insights.solution: Add try/except to insert HERMES_AGENT_ROOT into sys.path when hermes_constants not found.
insights.lesson_learned: Consistent direct-run fallback across similar scripts improves developer ergonomics.

cli.py.short_description: Interactive terminal CLI for Hermes Agent, TUI, config bridging and browser/CDP auto-launch helpers
cli.py.description: This module implements the interactive Hermes Agent command-line interface (TUI) and a large set of helper functions used during CLI startup. It composes prompts, bridges config.yaml → environment variables, loads .env, provides platform probing for browser/CDP auto-launch, and contains the HermesCLI class (the core TUI/REPL). The file exists to provide a single interactive entrypoint for running the agent locally, handling environment/config translation so other modules (e.g., terminal tools, browser tooling) can read behavior via environment variables, and to present a polished REPL with streaming/rendering logic and tool orchestration. It also contains utility functions used by startup and tests (e.g. load_cli_config, _get_chrome_debug_candidates, _load_prefill_messages, _parse_reasoning_config).

key_constructs.name: HermesCLI
key_constructs.purpose: Main interactive CLI class implementing the text UI, REPL loop, streaming UI coordination, and platform integration points.
key_constructs.reasoning: Most edits that change CLI behavior, streaming, reasoning box coordination, or browser auto-launch will involve this class. Tests (unit tests in tests/test_cli_browser_connect.py and status-bar tests) import HermesCLI and exercise methods on it; ensure any method/attribute signatures called by tests remain compatible.

key_constructs.name: _get_chrome_debug_candidates
key_constructs.purpose: Return a list of likely Chrome/Chromium/Edge/Brave executable paths to try for CDP auto-launch, platform-aware.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function centralizes platform-specific probing and is used by the browser auto-launch logic. When changing platform probing or test behavior, respect its contract: returns paths in OS-native format and must deduplicate candidates. Tests mock os.path.isfile and shutil.which to assert discovery behavior.

key_constructs.name: _load_prefill_messages
key_constructs.purpose: Load ephemeral prefill messages from a JSON file (resolved relative to HERMES_HOME), returning a list of {role, content} dicts or an empty list on errors.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to inject ephemeral few-shot messages into prompt building. Changes must preserve error handling (warnings on malformed JSON, non-array content, missing file) and path resolution semantics (resolve relative to HERMES_HOME unless path is absolute).

key_constructs.name: _parse_reasoning_config
key_constructs.purpose: Translate a reasoning-effort string into an OpenRouter reasoning config via parse_reasoning_effort from hermes_constants; warn on unknown values.
key_constructs.reasoning: Small helper that normalizes user-facing reasoning effort strings. Keep warnings and None semantics for unknown/empty input because other code expects None meaning default.

key_constructs.name: load_cli_config
key_constructs.purpose: Load and merge CLI configuration from ~/.hermes/config.yaml (preferred) or project cli-config.yaml, expand env var tokens, and bridge selected values into environment variables.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function is the canonical bridge between config.yaml and the environment read by various tools (terminal toolset, browser tooling, auxiliary models). It contains non-obvious rules: user config wins for terminal settings, legacy root-level provider/base_url fallbacks are accepted but only as fallbacks, and terminal cwd resolution differs for local vs remote backends. Edits must preserve env bridging behavior and the rules governing when .env values are overridden vs preserved. Tests and other components assume these env var mappings.

key_constructs.name: _COMMAND_SPINNER_FRAMES
key_constructs.purpose: Tuple of characters used to render an animated spinner in the CLI.
key_constructs.reasoning: UI cosmetic constant used for streaming displays; changing it only affects display but could be referenced in tests or UI snapshots.

key_behaviors: load CLI and project config and translate selected values into env vars
discover local browser executables to auto-launch CDP debug ports
load ephemeral prefill messages for prompt priming
coordinate streaming output and reasoning-box rendering in the TUI

pitfalls.mistake: Changing env-bridging precedence (which keys overwrite .env) without preserving the 'only override when config file explicitly sets terminal' semantics
pitfalls.consequence: Could unintentionally overwrite user .env settings, causing terminal tools to pick wrong backends or credentials at runtime
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the _file_has_terminal_config gating logic and the mapping rules in load_cli_config when modifying config-to-env behavior

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying platform probing paths in _get_chrome_debug_candidates but not updating tests that mock shutil.which/os.path.isfile
pitfalls.consequence: Unit tests that assert specific discovery behaviors will fail; Windows fallback paths use OS-specific environment variables
pitfalls.prevention: When changing candidate ordering or names, update tests to reflect the new order and ensure cross-platform path joining (os.path.join) is respected

pitfalls.mistake: Altering reasoning/streaming buffering state (e.g. _deferred_content) without adjusting initialization/reset hooks
pitfalls.consequence: Streaming output may appear out of order (reasoning box vs content), causing UI inversion bugs covered by commit history
pitfalls.prevention: Respect streaming coordination invariants: buffer content while reasoning box is open and flush on _close_reasoning_box; ensure state is initialized in reset paths

key_sections: load_cli_config: loads and merges config.yaml, expands ${ENV_VAR}, and maps terminal/browser/auxiliary settings into environment variables
_get_chrome_debug_candidates: platform-specific browser executable discovery logic used by browser auto-launch
_load_prefill_messages: semantics for prefill message path resolution and error handling
skip_unless_needed: Large TUI rendering and streaming helper functions (if your change is only related to config or browser discovery)
ASCII-art/banner code and long constant personality lists

relevant_snippets.description: Mocks shutil.which, os.path.isfile, and subprocess.Popen and asserts HermesCLI._try_launch_chrome_debug invokes the discovered path with --remote-debugging-port and OS-specific Popen kwargs

related_files.relationship: load_cli_config mirrors and overlaps behavior with hermes_cli.config's load_config/DEFAULT_CONFIG; both influence how config.yaml is interpreted and which env vars get set at startup
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing mapping of config keys → TERMINAL_* env vars or auxiliary model env vars, update corresponding logic in hermes_cli/config.py and gateway/run.py which also bridges config into env

related_files.relationship: gateway.run performs a similar config bridging and must remain consistent with CLI's load_cli_config semantics
related_files.reason_to_check: When altering which keys or defaults are bridged to environment variables (agent.gateway_timeout, TERMINAL_*), gateway startup and agent runtime semantics may diverge

data_constants_to_check: DEFAULT terminal/docker images and docker_mount_cwd_to_workspace flags
auxiliary env mappings (AUXILIARY_* env vars) in load_cli_config

insights.title: Defer response content until reasoning block completes
insights.problem: With show_reasoning + streaming the response content could arrive while the reasoning box is still rendering and the response box would appear before reasoning finished (visual ordering inversion).
insights.root_cause: Streaming path emitted content tokens immediately even when a reasoning UI box was open; interleaved writes caused the response to render early.
insights.solution: Buffer content in _deferred_content when reasoning box is open and flush it when the reasoning box closes (_close_reasoning_box). Also initialize _deferred_content in reset.
insights.lesson: UI streaming must respect ordered rendering semantics; when two logical channels (reasoning vs content) exist, buffer lower-priority output until higher-priority display completes.

insights.title: Inject background-process completion events into CLI input loop
insights.problem: Background processes with notify_on_complete needed to trigger agent turns; CLI needed to drain completion queue while idle or after agent turns.
insights.root_cause: Process completion notifications were previously only handled by gateway; CLI didn't check the process registry completion_queue regularly.
insights.solution: CLI idle loop and end-of-turn logic now drain ProcessRegistry.completion_queue and inject synthesized system messages into pending input.
insights.lesson: When adding cross-cutting notification features, ensure all front-ends (CLI and gateway) drain the same completion queue so behavior is consistent.

insights.title: Remove unused HermesCLI methods
insights.problem: Methods reset_conversation and _clear_current_input were unused after refactors.
insights.root_cause: The CLI session management API evolved; callers were replaced by new_session and other code paths, leaving these methods unused.
insights.solution: Removed the unused methods.
insights.lesson: Clean up unused public-facing methods in interactive components to avoid confusion about supported operations.

insights.title: Extract browser discovery logic for Chrome/CDP auto-launch
insights.problem: Browser discovery logic was duplicated/inlined in _try_launch_chrome_debug and differed per platform.
insights.root_cause: Monolithic function did platform-specific path probing inline, making it harder to extend/maintain.
insights.solution: Introduced _get_chrome_debug_candidates(system) that centralizes candidate paths and platform-specific probing; simplified _try_launch_chrome_debug to call it.
insights.lesson_learned: Encapsulate platform probing logic to make behavior consistent and testable across OS variants.

insights.title: Handle CJK wide characters in TUI input height calculation
insights.problem: TUI input height calculation counted characters by code points; CJK wide characters occupy two terminal cells and caused incorrect wrapping/height calculation.
insights.root_cause: Used len() for width estimation instead of terminal cell width functions.
insights.solution: Use prompt_toolkit.utils.get_cwidth and prompt_toolkit.application.get_app().output.get_size() when available; fall back to shutil.get_terminal_size if not.
insights.lesson_learned: Terminal display calculations must use cell width semantics to handle wide characters; use prompt_toolkit helpers where available and provide safe fallbacks for tests and non-ptk environments.

insights.title: /model --global writes model.default instead of model.name
insights.problem: CLI's global model switch persisted value to model.name while consumers read model.default; this made global switches appear to revert after restart.
insights.root_cause: Inconsistent config key used by CLI when saving the global model.
insights.solution: Change save_config_value to write model.default instead of model.name; also set provider keys appropriately.
insights.lesson_learned: Carefully maintain canonical config keys across CLI and runtime code; add tests or constants to avoid divergent keys.

insights.title: Prevent duplicate reasoning box renders and persistent show flag
insights.problem: Reasoning box rendered three times during tool-calling loops due to multiple code paths firing reasoning display callbacks and a state flag being reset incorrectly between intermediate boundaries.
insights.root_cause: Reasoning callback fired from _build_assistant_message even when streaming had already displayed reasoning; _reasoning_stream_started got reset by _reset_stream_state during intermediate tool loops.
insights.solution: Skip firing reasoning callback when streaming is active; introduce _reasoning_shown_this_turn flag that persists across tool-calling loops and is only reset at start of user turn; set flag when streaming shows reasoning.
insights.lesson_learned: Streaming and post-stream display paths must coordinate via persistent state across intermediate steps; distinct flags for per-call vs per-turn visibility avoid duplicate UI side-effects.

insights.title: Neuter SDK destructor and suppress 'Event loop is closed' in prompt loop
insights.problem: Prompt-toolkit reported 'Event loop is closed' caused by SDK destructor scheduling close on different loop; surfaced as disruptive UI prompt.
insights.root_cause: SDK destructor behavior not compatible with prompt-toolkit's idle loop.
insights.solution: Call neuter_async_httpx_del at CLI startup, install custom asyncio exception handler suppressing 'Event loop is closed', and call cleanup_stale_async_clients after agent thread joins.
insights.lesson_learned: Apply defensive measures in CLI startup to prevent third-party SDK resource cleanup from impacting interactive loops; proactively clean stale resources after background work.

hermes_cli/config.py.short_description: Configuration management and defaults for Hermes Agent (HERMES_HOME config and .env handling)
hermes_cli/config.py.description: This module centralizes configuration paths, secure file setup for HERMES_HOME, DEFAULT_CONFIG schema, and helpers for managed installs (NixOS/Homebrew). It provides routines used by both CLI and gateway to read/write/validate configuration and to seed default artifacts (SOUL.md) into the user's hermes home. The file exists to provide a single authoritative DEFAULT_CONFIG used by CLI flows, to enforce secure permissions for config files and dirs, and to encapsulate managed-install behaviors (blocking edits, providing update commands). It also exposes validation utilities (validate_config_structure and ConfigIssue) used by tests and startup diagnostics.

key_constructs.name: DEFAULT_CONFIG
key_constructs.purpose: Canonical default configuration dictionary used to populate missing keys when loading user config.
key_constructs.reasoning: DEFAULT_CONFIG is the single source of truth for default values referenced by many subsystems (terminal defaults, auxiliary timeouts, memory defaults). Changes to these defaults affect runtime behavior and tests; keep the shape and keys stable or increment config versioning where appropriate.

key_constructs.name: ensure_hermes_home
key_constructs.purpose: Create ~/.hermes directory structure, set secure permissions for directories and files, and seed default SOUL.md.
key_constructs.reasoning: Called during setup and CLI startup; it enforces 0700/0600 permissions on non-Windows systems. When editing file creation behavior, respect idempotency and permission-setting conventions; tests expect these directories to exist and have owner-only permissions where applicable.

key_constructs.name: get_config_path
key_constructs.purpose: Return the canonical path to the user's config.yaml (HERMES_HOME/config.yaml).
key_constructs.reasoning: Other modules import this to find the primary config. Avoid changing its resolution semantics without coordinating consumers.

callers.context: from hermes_cli.config import get_hermes_home, get_config_path, load_config, save_config

key_constructs.name: get_env_path
key_constructs.purpose: Return the path to the user's .env (HERMES_HOME/.env).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by startup loaders to populate environment variables; keep path stable to avoid double-looking .env in different places.

key_constructs.name: get_managed_system
key_constructs.purpose: Detect whether Hermes is managed by a package manager (NixOS/Homebrew) via env var or marker file and return a normalized name or None.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to block configuration edits in managed installs and to provide user-friendly messages. Tests and user interactions depend on the exact behavior (HERMES_MANAGED precedence then .managed file).

key_constructs.name: validate_config_structure
key_constructs.purpose: Validate structure of user-supplied config.yaml and return ConfigIssue items describing errors/warnings (used for diagnostics and tests).
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests in tests/hermes_cli/test_config_validation.py exercise this function. Its output is used to provide actionable diagnostics on startup. Keep validation logic and ConfigIssue shape stable as tests assert specific message substrings.

key_constructs.name: ConfigIssue
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass used to represent validation issues (severity, message, hint).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by validate_config_structure and test assertions compare equality and fields; preserve the dataclass fields and equality semantics.

callers.context: issue = ConfigIssue(severity="error", message="test msg", hint="test hint")

key_behaviors: ensure hermes home directory and permissions are set
expose DEFAULT_CONFIG used across CLI/gateway/run_agent
validate config structure and surface programmatic ConfigIssue items
provide managed-install detection and user-facing messages

pitfalls.mistake: Changing DEFAULT_CONFIG keys or shapes without migration/validation updates
pitfalls.consequence: Users' existing config.yaml may be silently misinterpreted, and validation tests will fail
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve key names or update validate/migrate logic and bump config version metadata where appropriate

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying permission helpers (_secure_dir/_secure_file) to be stricter or to run on Windows
pitfalls.consequence: Cross-platform tests and Windows behavior may break; attempting to chmod on Windows can raise NotImplementedError
pitfalls.prevention: Keep current try/except swallowing OSError/NotImplementedError; only alter with cross-platform considerations and tests

pitfalls.mistake: Changing managed-install detection heuristics (HERMES_MANAGED env var or .managed marker)
pitfalls.consequence: Automated packaging/systemd behavior and messages for NixOS/Homebrew flows could become inconsistent, causing user confusion or CI failures
pitfalls.prevention: Keep detection order and semantics or coordinate with packaging/systemd scripts and tests

key_sections: DEFAULT_CONFIG: authoritative defaults for many subsystems
ensure_hermes_home: directory seeding and secure permission behavior
get_managed_system / format_managed_message: managed-install detection and user-facing guidance
validate_config_structure: shape validation used by tests
skip_unless_needed: Long OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS metadata (UI prompts), CLI wizard flows and user interactive editors

relevant_snippets.description: Multiple test cases call validate_config_structure with malformed and valid configs to assert returned ConfigIssue items and messages

related_files.relationship: CLI startup and load_cli_config perform similar env bridging and rely on DEFAULT_CONFIG shapes and validation utilities
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing DEFAULT_CONFIG structure, ensure load_cli_config and gateway bridging logic remain consistent

related_files.relationship: gateway.run performs its own config bridging and relies on the same conventions for HERMES_HOME and env mapping
related_files.reason_to_check: When mapping agent.gateway_timeout, TERMINAL_* env vars, or auxiliary env vars, keep both bridging implementations aligned

insights.title: Expose MATRIX_DEVICE_ID in CLI optional env var prompts
insights.problem: Users had no convenient way to set MATRIX_DEVICE_ID from the Hermes CLI setup flows.
insights.root_cause: OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS did not include MATRIX_DEVICE_ID.
insights.solution: Added MATRIX_DEVICE_ID with metadata to OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS and included key in the _EXTRA_ENV_KEYS set.
insights.lesson_learned: CLI setup should surface new environment-backed options for user convenience when security-sensitive but useful features (like stable device id) are introduced.

insights.title: Add FIRECRAWL_BROWSER_TTL optional env var and skill-config migration hooks
insights.problem: New Firecrawl provider requires TTL config; skill-config feature requires new migrate/show behaviors.
insights.root_cause: New provider and skills config feature introduced new configuration fields.
insights.solution: Added FIRECRAWL_BROWSER_TTL to OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS and introduced get_missing_skill_config_vars and UI prompts in migrate_config + show_config integration for skill-defined settings.
insights.lesson_learned: Central config registry should be extended when new optional env vars or new features (skill config) are added so setup flows and config display expose them.

insights.title: Add GEMINI env vars and logging config defaults
insights.problem: Config schema needed entries for Google/Gemini API keys and logging defaults.
insights.root_cause: New provider and centralized logging feature required config exposure.
insights.solution: Add GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, GEMINI_BASE_URL optional entries and add logging section with level/max_size_mb/backup_count defaults.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding new auth or infra features, update configuration schema and examples to guide users; making logging configurable aids deployability.

insights.title: Add config structure validation and user diagnostics
insights.problem: Malformed config.yaml produced confusing runtime errors like 'Unknown provider' with no guidance.
insights.root_cause: YAML mistakes (e.g., custom_providers as dict instead of list, misplaced fallback_model) lead to subtle lookup failures with poor user-facing diagnostics.
insights.solution: Implemented validate_config_structure() returning ConfigIssue dataclass items, print_config_warnings() for startup diagnostics, and integrated into doctor and error messages; added explicit checks for custom_providers, fallback_model, and misplaced root keys.
insights.lesson_learned: Validate user configuration structure early and provide actionable hints; surface problems at startup and in error messages to reduce user confusion.

insights.title: New default agent.gateway_timeout config option and mapping
insights.problem: Gateway previously used hard-coded wall-clock timeouts; new config introduced to control inactivity timeouts.
insights.root_cause: Need for configurable inactivity timeout in gateway (mapped to HERMES_AGENT_TIMEOUT env var).
insights.solution: Added agent.gateway_timeout default (1800s) and mapping to env var when gateway loads config.
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer explicit config options (and documentation) for behavior that previously relied only on env vars; ensure env var precedence is respected.

insights.title: Add auxiliary.{task}.timeout defaults to DEFAULT_CONFIG
insights.problem: No per-task timeout defaults present in config; previously hardcoded values lived in code.
insights.root_cause: Config schema lacked timeout entries for the auxiliary providers.
insights.solution: Populate DEFAULT_CONFIG with timeout entries (compression:120, vision:30, other tasks:30).
insights.lesson_learned: When adding configurable behavior, include sane defaults in the central config model so users see expected behavior without manual editing.

insights.title: Add agent.tool_use_enforcement default and docstring
insights.problem: Tool-use enforcement behavior needed to be configurable and have a default in config merging.
insights.root_cause: Previously enforcement was implicit in code; no default config key existed.
insights.solution: Add agent.tool_use_enforcement: 'auto' default with docs in DEFAULT_CONFIG so deep-merge provides behavior for existing installs.

insights.title: Update DASHSCOPE/DashScope descriptions for Alibaba provider default change
insights.problem: Provider docs and optional env var descriptions needed to reflect default endpoint shifts.
insights.root_cause: Default inference_base_url changed to coding-intl /v1 endpoint.
insights.solution: Updated OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS descriptions accordingly.

insights.title: Add HF_TOKEN optional env var guidance
insights.problem: Setup lacked prompts/documentation for HF_TOKEN and HF_BASE_URL during interactive provider configuration.
insights.root_cause: Hugging Face provider addition required documented environment and setup guidance.
insights.solution: Add HF_TOKEN and HF_BASE_URL to OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS with prompt/url metadata.
insights.lesson_learned: Add provider-specific env guidance in config to aid users during interactive setup.

tests/test_cli_browser_connect.py.short_description: Unit tests for CLI browser/CDP auto-launch discovery and Popen invocation
tests/test_cli_browser_connect.py.description: This test module exercises HermesCLI's Chrome/CDP auto-launch behavior on Windows. It focuses on two behaviors: (1) when an executable is found via PATH (shutil.which), the CLI should invoke it with the correct --remote-debugging-port and subprocess.Popen kwargs appropriate for Windows; (2) when PATH lookup fails, the discovery should fall back to scanning common installation directories (ProgramFiles etc.) and still invoke the discovered binary. The tests mock shutil.which, os.path.isfile and subprocess.Popen to isolate discovery and invocation logic and assert the exact command array and Popen kwargs passed.

key_constructs.name: TestChromeDebugLaunch
key_constructs.purpose: Test class grouping Windows browser discovery/invocation unit tests.
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains two tests that mock system-level functions to validate HermesCLI._try_launch_chrome_debug behavior; when editing CLI auto-launch logic, run these tests to verify Windows path and fallback behaviors remain intact.

key_constructs.name: test_windows_launch_uses_browser_found_on_path
key_constructs.purpose: Validate that when shutil.which returns a path and os.path.isfile confirms it, subprocess.Popen is invoked with that path and the correct debugging port flag.
key_constructs.reasoning: Asserts exact args and ensures start_new_session kwarg is True on Windows runs

key_constructs.name: test_windows_launch_falls_back_to_common_install_dirs
key_constructs.purpose: Validate fallback behavior: when PATH yields nothing, code inspects ProgramFiles-style install locations and invokes the discovered binary with correct args.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses monkeypatch to set ProgramFiles env and assembles expected installed path using os.path.join to ensure cross-platform test stability

key_behaviors: assert that browser discovery uses PATH first, then installation directories
assert subprocess.Popen is called with '--remote-debugging-port=<port>' and Windows-specific kwarg start_new_session

pitfalls.mistake: Hardcoding Windows-style paths in expected values using literal backslashes or not using os.path.join in test setup
pitfalls.consequence: Tests become flaky on non-Windows runners or when path normalization differs; commit history shows a fix to use os.path.join
pitfalls.prevention: Use os.path.join to construct expected install paths and ensure shutil.which/os.path.isfile are mocked consistently

pitfalls.mistake: Not mocking subprocess.Popen and letting real Popen run during tests
pitfalls.consequence: Tests may attempt to launch a real browser on CI/machines, causing hangs or side effects
pitfalls.prevention: Always patch subprocess.Popen in these tests (the tests do so)

key_sections: test_windows_launch_uses_browser_found_on_path: PATH discovery + Popen kwarg assertions
test_windows_launch_falls_back_to_common_install_dirs: ProgramFiles fallback behavior and cross-platform path construction
skip_unless_needed: test scaffolding; focus on the two tests which directly document the expected behavior

related_files.relationship: Directly tests HermesCLI._try_launch_chrome_debug and relies on _get_chrome_debug_candidates behavior
related_files.reason_to_check: If cli.py's browser discovery or Popen invocation signature changes, update these tests accordingly

insights.title: Cross-platform path separators in browser tests
insights.problem: Test used hardcoded Windows path string which mismatched os.path joining rules on different platforms causing flaky test failure.
insights.root_cause: Test set environment ProgramFiles path with hardcoded separators instead of os.path.join.
insights.solution: Use os.path.join for Windows install path to ensure test passes cross-platform and monkeypatch env accordingly.
insights.lesson_learned: Tests that deal with filesystem paths should use os.path.join and not assume path separators.

insights.title: Test Windows browser discovery and launch behavior
insights.problem: Need to validate path discovery (PATH vs ProgramFiles) and Popen args for Windows.
insights.root_cause: Platform-specific behavior needs unit tests.
insights.solution: Added tests that mock shutil.which, os.path.isfile and subprocess.Popen to assert expected command invocation.
insights.lesson_learned: When probing platform-specific locations, add unit tests covering both PATH and installation-dir fallbacks.

cron/scheduler.py.short_description: Schedules and executes due cron jobs, runs optional scripts, and delivers outputs to platforms.
cron/scheduler.py.description:
This module implements the cron job scheduler used by the Hermes Agent. It provides the logic to resolve job origins and delivery targets, run pre-run data-collection scripts (safely constrained within HERMES_HOME/scripts), build prompts (including loading referenced skills), execute a job via the core AIAgent runner (run_job), and deliver results to messaging platforms either via a running gateway adapter or a standalone send helper. The module uses file-based locking to prevent concurrent ticks across processes and contains logic to wrap/format delivery content, extract media attachments, and respect user configuration (cron.wrap_response).

The file exists to centralize scheduled-job execution and delivery semantics so that scheduled runs behave consistently with interactive/gateway flows (media extraction, platform routing, origin fallbacks). It enforces security constraints on script execution (path containment), validates delivery platform names against a whitelist, and attempts to use a live adapter when available for E2EE support before falling back to the standalone send helper. The module is mindful of concurrency (locks, asyncio loop detection) and environment management: it temporarily sets environment variables for session context and delivery routing and ensures they are cleaned up.

Design choices visible in the implementation: (1) strict script execution containment to HERMES_HOME/scripts/ to avoid path traversal risks, (2) dual delivery path (live adapter versus standalone) for compatibility with gateway runtime, (3) prompt composition that prepends a system-level cron hint (including the [SILENT] sentinel) and optionally loads skill content into the prompt, and (4) defensive error handling and logging for config and delivery failures. The file integrates with other subsystems: tools.send_message_tool for sends and parsing targets, gateway.channel_directory for resolving human-friendly channel labels, Gateway config loader for platform enablement, and hermes_state.SessionDB for session persistence.

key_constructs.name: SILENT_MARKER
key_constructs.purpose: Sentinel string used to indicate the agent should suppress delivery of a cron job result.
key_constructs.reasoning: This marker controls whether the scheduler should deliver a final response. Code that checks delivery suppression must treat this exact constant consistently and case-insensitively.

callers.context: assert any(SILENT_MARKER in r.message for r in caplog.records)

key_constructs.name: _SCRIPT_TIMEOUT
key_constructs.purpose: Maximum allowed seconds for script subprocess execution.
key_constructs.reasoning: Subprocesses launched by _run_job_script use this wall-clock timeout; callers must be aware of the timeout semantics and error messages this produces.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_origin
key_constructs.purpose: Extract and validate origin routing context from a job dict (platform and chat_id required).
key_constructs.reasoning: Other parts of the scheduler depend on a normalized origin dict when delivering back to the source chat or when setting session env vars; maintain the expected keys and types (platform, chat_id, optional thread_id).

key_constructs.name: _resolve_delivery_target
key_constructs.purpose: Convert job.deliver into a concrete platform/chat_id/thread_id target or None for local-only delivery.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function enforces platform whitelisting, resolves 'origin' and fallback home channels, parses explicit targets like 'telegram:chat:thread', and uses gateway.channel_directory to translate human-friendly names. It is security-sensitive (prevents env enumeration by limiting platform names).

key_constructs.name: _deliver_result
key_constructs.purpose: Send job output to a resolved delivery target using the live adapter (if available) or the standalone _send_to_platform helper; handles wrapping and media extraction.
key_constructs.reasoning: Delivery must prefer the runtime adapter for E2EE rooms, gracefully fallback on failures, respect cron.wrap_response config, and separate MEDIA: tags into attachments. Call sites must supply adapters and loop when calling from a running gateway to enable the live path.

key_constructs.name: _run_job_script
key_constructs.purpose: Execute an allowed Python script under HERMES_HOME/scripts/ and capture/redact its output for injection into prompts.
key_constructs.reasoning: Scripts are powerful but dangerous; this function enforces containment within the scripts directory, returns structured (success, output) results, and redacts sensitive output when possible. Editors must preserve path containment checks and error strings used in LLM-visible messages.

key_constructs.name: _build_job_prompt
key_constructs.purpose: Compose the final prompt for the agent from the job prompt, optional script output, and optionally attached skills; also prepends cron guidance.
key_constructs.reasoning: The system-level cron hint explains auto-delivery and the SILENT_MARKER semantics and instructs the agent not to call send_message. Skills are loaded via tools.skills_tool.skill_view and merged in a way that preserves diagnostic notices about missing skills.

key_constructs.name: run_job
key_constructs.purpose: The top-level routine to execute a single job: prepares session/store, composes prompt, runs the agent (via run_agent.AIAgent), captures output, persists run results, and triggers delivery.
key_constructs.reasoning: run_job coordinates many subsystems (SessionDB, runtime_provider, AIAgent, cron.jobs persistence, delivery helper). It sets and later cleans temporary env vars (HERMES_SESSION_* and HERMES_CRON_AUTO_DELIVER_*); callers/editors must preserve env cleanup semantics and SessionDB lifecycle calls (end_session, close) that tests assert.

key_behaviors: resolves job origin for routing back to the source
resolves and validates delivery targets (origin/home channel/explicit)
executes pre-run scripts within HERMES_HOME/scripts securely
builds LLM prompts including skills and cron guidance
delivers outputs via live gateway adapters or standalone send helper
logs configuration and delivery errors for observability

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the HERMES_HOME/scripts containment checks in _run_job_script
pitfalls.consequence: Could allow arbitrary script execution outside the designated scripts directory (path traversal or absolute path injection), posing a security risk.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the relative_to() containment assertion and symlink-safe resolution logic when editing script execution behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing how environment variables (HERMES_SESSION_*, HERMES_CRON_AUTO_DELIVER_*) are set or cleaned up in run_job
pitfalls.consequence: Could leak session/delivery state into subsequent runs or other processes, causing incorrect routing or test failures (the test suite asserts env vars are unset after run_job).
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the try/finally cleanup pattern and ensure env vars are restored/removed as originally implemented.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying delivery flow so BasePlatformAdapter.extract_media is not used
pitfalls.consequence: Media attachments could be sent as raw MEDIA: text lines instead of forwarded as attachments; tests expect media_files to be passed separately.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the media extraction step and ensure the cleaned text + media_files are passed to _send_to_platform.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the platform whitelist (_KNOWN_DELIVERY_PLATFORMS) or loosening validation without tests
pitfalls.consequence: Could open an avenue for environment variable enumeration or unexpected behavior when resolving home channels from environment variables.
pitfalls.prevention: Any change to platform validation should be accompanied by tests and a security review to ensure env var enumeration protections remain.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the live-adapter preference or breaking the asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe fallback
pitfalls.consequence: E2EE-capable deliveries (e.g., Matrix) may fail to encrypt; runtime adapter errors may be swallowed or cause incorrect fallbacks.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the logic that tries the runtime adapter on a running event loop and falls back to the standalone coroutine via asyncio.run or a thread executor if needed.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering SILENT_MARKER handling or only checking startswith
pitfalls.consequence: Delivery suppression behavior could regress (previous bugs used startswith and missed markers not at the start); tests expect containment checks that are case-insensitive and position-agnostic.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure delivery suppression logic searches for the marker anywhere in the final response (case-insensitively) as covered by tests and commit history.

key_sections: _resolve_delivery_target: resolves and validates the concrete delivery target (security- and env-dependent)
_run_job_script: safe script execution and redaction behavior
_deliver_result: live adapter vs standalone delivery, wrapping, and media extraction
_build_job_prompt: cron_hint and skill-loading behavior
skip_unless_needed: top-of-file constants for file locking (_LOCK_DIR/_LOCK_FILE) if you are not modifying concurrency
detailed logging lines except when debugging specific errors

relevant_snippets.description: Unit tests for _resolve_origin, _resolve_delivery_target, and several deliver_result wrapping behaviors (header/footer and MEDIA: extraction).
Tests that run_job persists sessions, sets and cleans environment variables, and logs malformed config.yaml errors.

related_files.relationship: Uses _send_to_platform and _parse_target_ref from this module to parse targets and perform standalone sends.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to argument order, media_files semantics, or _send_to_platform's signature requires changes here.

related_files.relationship: Uses BasePlatformAdapter.extract_media to split attachments from text before delivery.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying extract_media behavior affects cron media extraction and tests that assert expectations about media_files.

related_files.relationship: Optional resolution of human-friendly channel names into concrete ids via resolve_channel_name, used by _resolve_delivery_target.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to channel name resolution semantics will affect how human labels are interpreted by cron deliveries.

related_files.relationship: SessionDB is instantiated and used to persist cron run messages; run_job interacts with end_session/close.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to SessionDB API or side-effects could require run_job updates to maintain session lifecycle behavior.

insights.title: Suppress delivery when [SILENT] appears anywhere in response
insights.problem: Earlier code only checked startswith('[SILENT]') so responses that appended [SILENT] (or had it later) still triggered delivery.
insights.root_cause: Use of startswith prevented detection of marker if not at start.
insights.solution: Change check to use substring membership (SILENT_MARKER in deliver_content.strip().upper()).
insights.lesson_learned: Marker detection should account for marker appearing anywhere; choose containment over startswith when semantics require anywhere-in-string matching.

insights.title: Extract MEDIA: tags from cron deliveries before sending
insights.problem: Cron scheduler passed raw text including MEDIA: tags to the send helper, so receivers saw literal MEDIA: lines instead of actual attached files.
insights.root_cause: Cron delivery did not call BasePlatformAdapter.extract_media like the gateway message flow does.
insights.solution: Call BasePlatformAdapter.extract_media to split media files and cleaned text, then pass media_files to _send_to_platform so attachments are delivered properly.
insights.lesson_learned: All message delivery codepaths (gateway, cron, scheduled) must share the same media extraction logic to behave consistently across send entrypoints.

insights.title: Add delivery guidance to cron job prompts to avoid send_message thrashing
insights.problem: Cron-run agents attempted to call send_message even though delivery was handled by the scheduler, causing wasted iterations and failures because send_message might be disabled.
insights.root_cause: Prompt guidance lacked explicit instruction that final response is auto-delivered and that agents must not call send_message.
insights.solution: Replace the old [SILENT] hint with a cron_hint that explains DELIVERY semantics (auto-delivered), instructs 'do NOT use send_message', and documents how to use [SILENT] to suppress delivery.
insights.lesson_learned: Explicitly document at-system-prompt level how platform behaviors (delivery, suppressed actions) work to prevent agents from attempting unsupported actions.

insights.title: Replace wall-clock timeout with inactivity-based timeout
insights.problem: Cron jobs that were actively working (tool calls, streaming tokens) hit a hard wall-clock timeout and were killed even though they were active.
insights.root_cause: Using future.result(timeout=N) enforced a maximum wall-clock duration regardless of activity.
insights.solution: Implement polling loop that checks agent.get_activity_summary() at intervals (5s). Only trigger timeout if idle duration exceeds HERMES_CRON_TIMEOUT; HERMES_CRON_TIMEOUT=0 disables timeout. Add diagnostic info on timeout (last activity description, idle duration, current tool, iteration count).
insights.lesson_learned: For long-running agents that perform I/O/streaming, prefer inactivity-based timeouts over wall-clock limits; expose diagnostics for timeouts.

insights.title: Ensure sys.path insertion before repo-level imports
insights.problem: Standalone cron invocations (e.g. after hermes update reload) could raise ModuleNotFoundError for in-repo modules because sys.path modification happened after imports.
insights.root_cause: sys.path.insert was placed after some repo-level imports.
insights.solution: Move sys.path.insert(0, parent) before repo-level imports so local modules (hermes_time etc.) are importable.
insights.lesson_learned: When writing scripts that may be executed in varied contexts, ensure local package import paths are configured before importing project modules.

insights.title: Call advance_next_run before running job in tick()
insights.problem: Scheduler called run_job before persisting next_run_at, enabling duplicate execution on crash/restart.
insights.root_cause: Order of operations in tick() didn't persist next occurrence prior to execution.
insights.solution: Advance next_run_at for recurring jobs (cron/interval) before calling run_job(), so a crash during run won't re-queue the same occurrence on restart.
insights.lesson_learned: Atomic scheduling semantics require persisting schedule advancement before executing to avoid duplicate runs under restart/crash loops.

tests/cron/test_scheduler.py.short_description: Unit tests for cron scheduler: origin/delivery resolution, delivery wrapping, media extraction, and run_job lifecycle.
tests/cron/test_scheduler.py.description:
This test module verifies core behaviors of cron/scheduler.py. It focuses on origin resolution, delivery target parsing (explicit IDs, topic/thread parsing for Telegram, and human-friendly name resolution via gateway.channel_directory), the delivery wrapper behavior (header/footer insertion, wrap disabling via config, MEDIA: extraction to attachments), and run_job session lifecycle and environment handling (SessionDB injection, setting/cleaning HERMES_CRON_AUTO_DELIVER environment variables, and logging of malformed config files).

Tests use pytest and unittest.mock extensively to isolate external dependencies: gateway.config.load_gateway_config, tools.send_message_tool._send_to_platform, gateway.channel_directory.resolve_channel_name, hermes_state.SessionDB, run_agent.AIAgent, and dotenv.load_dotenv are patched to create controlled conditions. Several tests assert logging behavior via caplog to ensure that config parsing errors or missing skills produce visible warnings rather than silent failures. The suite emphasizes security and environment hygiene (e.g., env var cleanup) and reproduces historical edge cases related to SILENT_MARKER placement and media extraction.

key_constructs.name: TestResolveOrigin
key_constructs.purpose: Grouping of tests asserting _resolve_origin behavior for fully specified, missing, and partially missing origin dicts.
key_constructs.reasoning: These tests codify expected validation rules: both 'platform' and 'chat_id' must exist for origin to be considered valid.

key_constructs.name: TestResolveDeliveryTarget
key_constructs.purpose: Tests for _resolve_delivery_target: origin delivery, explicit targets with/without thread_id, label resolution through channel_directory, and home-channel fallbacks.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates parsing logic and fallback rules that are security-sensitive (platform whitelist and env-based home channels).

key_constructs.name: TestDeliverResultWrapping
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts wrapping of cron output, respect for cron.wrap_response config, MEDIA: extraction, and that cron deliveries do not mirror to gateway session.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures cron deliveries are formatted for human consumption and attachments are forwarded as files, matching gateway behavior.

key_constructs.name: TestRunJobSessionPersistence
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies run_job sets up SessionDB, passes it to AIAgent, cleans env vars, and logs or preserves final_response semantics as required by delivery logic.
key_constructs.reasoning: These tests ensure session lifecycle and environment side-effects are correct (tests assert end_session calls and env cleanup).

key_constructs.name: TestRunJobConfigLogging
key_constructs.purpose: Checks that malformed config.yaml and bad prefill messages are logged as warnings rather than silently swallowed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures gateway operators can diagnose broken configuration via logs.

key_behaviors: asserts origin validation rules
validates delivery target parsing and fallback logic
confirms wrapped delivery content and media extraction
ensures run_job sets up and tears down session and env state
verifies malformed config logging

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying scheduler side-effects (env vars, SessionDB lifecycle) without updating tests
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will fail because they assert exact env cleanup and calls to SessionDB.end_session/close.
pitfalls.prevention: Run the specific tests that assert these behaviors after edits and update mocks accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the signature/order of tools.send_message_tool._send_to_platform
pitfalls.consequence: Many tests patch this helper and depend on argument positions/keyword usage; mismatches will break widespread tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Update test patches and assertions if the send helper signature is changed.

key_sections: TestDeliverResultWrapping: demonstrates expected payload wrapping, MEDIA: extraction and non-mirroring
TestRunJobSessionPersistence: important for env/session lifecycle expectations and run_job contract
skip_unless_needed: smaller helper test cases that only verify trivial input validation

related_files.relationship: Primary system under test — test cases map directly to functions in this file.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any code edits to scheduler.py must be validated by running this test module, which asserts behavioral contracts and side-effects.

related_files.relationship: Mocked by tests to observe how _send_to_platform is called; changes there affect test expectations.
related_files.reason_to_check: If send helper signature or semantics change, update test patches accordingly.

insights.title: Add test for trailing [SILENT] marker
insights.problem: Need to ensure [SILENT] anywhere in response prevents delivery.
insights.root_cause: Existing tests covered prefix and case-insensitive checks but not trailing marker.
insights.solution: Added test_silent_trailing_suppresses_delivery to assert delivery suppressed when [SILENT] appended after explanation.
insights.lesson_learned: Test all reasonable positions for a sentinel marker used to control behavior.

insights.title: Test that cron delivery strips MEDIA: tags and passes attachments separately
insights.problem: Ensure cron path uses same media extraction as gateway so attachments are not sent as raw text.
insights.root_cause: Cron path previously didn't extract media.
insights.solution: Add test verifying cleaned content and forwarded media_files list passed to send helper.
insights.lesson_learned: Add tests for cross-cutting behavior (media extraction) to ensure consistency across multiple delivery flows.

insights.title: Add tests for cron prompt delivery guidance
insights.problem: No tests validated that cron hints include delivery guidance and ordering.
insights.root_cause: Missing unit tests for system prompt content.
insights.solution: Add tests ensuring 'do NOT use send_message' and that the guidance precedes user prompt.
insights.lesson_learned: System prompt changes should have tests to avoid regressions that cause agents to use forbidden tools.

insights.title: Add tests for inactivity-based cron timeout behavior
insights.problem: Timeout behavior was untested for activity-aware logic.
insights.root_cause: No unit tests for get_activity_summary-based timeout polling loop.
insights.solution: Add tests simulating active/idle/unlimited/env-var scenarios using fake agents exposing get_activity_summary.
insights.lesson_learned: Activity-based control flow needs tests that simulate both active and idle conditions to validate timeout semantics.

hermes_cli/main.py.short_description: Top-level CLI entrypoint and orchestration for the hermes command-line tool
hermes_cli/main.py.description: This module is the top-level command-line entry point for the Hermes Agent. It initializes environment and logging, applies a profile override prior to importing other hermes modules, loads .env files, and defines numerous CLI helpers and commands for interactive flows (chat, setup, gateway, update, etc.). The early portion of the file is focused on environment bootstrapping: inserting the project root on sys.path and implementing a pre-import profile resolution that sets HERMES_HOME and strips the --profile/-p flag from sys.argv so that downstream modules that cache HERMES_HOME at import-time see the correct value. Main runtime commands (e.g., cmd_update) and interactive helpers are implemented later in the file (file is large and truncated in the excerpt).

key_constructs.name: _require_tty
key_constructs.purpose: Guard that exits with a clear error when a command requiring a terminal is invoked without an interactive TTY.
key_constructs.reasoning: Must be used for interactive commands that rely on curses or input() to avoid running in non-interactive contexts (pipes/cron) that hang or spin at 100% CPU. Edits must preserve both the user-facing error message and exit behavior.

key_constructs.name: _apply_profile_override
key_constructs.purpose: Pre-parse argv for --profile/-p and set HERMES_HOME before other hermes modules are imported.
key_constructs.reasoning: This runs before other hermes imports because many modules read and cache HERMES_HOME at import time. Changing its semantics, order, or the argv-stripping behavior will affect many modules' initialization and tests that rely on profile override behavior.

key_constructs.name: PROJECT_ROOT
key_constructs.purpose: Filesystem path to repository root used for loading project .env and ensuring imports work from project source.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to load a project-level .env fallback and to prepend to sys.path; changing it can alter import resolution and environment loading.

key_constructs.name: _relative_time
key_constructs.purpose: Format timestamps into relative human-readable strings (e.g., '2h ago', 'yesterday').
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by interactive session browser and status displays; behavior should remain stable for consistent UI output and tests that assert substrings like 'yesterday' or timestamps.

key_constructs.name: _has_any_provider_configured
key_constructs.purpose: Heuristic check to determine whether any inference provider credentials/configuration exist.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to decide whether to prompt for provider credentials during setup flows. It inspects env vars, .env file, provider-specific auth fallbacks, config.yaml model entries and provider-specific credential files (e.g., Claude Code). It intentionally errs on the side of permissiveness (returns True if any usable provider found) and must continue to be tolerant of file read errors and missing keys.

key_constructs.name: _session_browse_picker
key_constructs.purpose: Interactive curses-based session browser with fallback numbered picker for non-curses environments.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a curses UI (with careful handling of curses.error) and a fallback for Windows/terminals without curses. The function includes adaptive column widths, search/filtering logic, key handling (Esc, backspace, printable chars) and returns a selected session ID or None. Edits must preserve both curses and fallback behaviors and not make the UI blocking in automated tests.

key_constructs.name: cmd_update
key_constructs.purpose: CLI command implementing the 'hermes update' workflow (git fetch/pull/update logic).
key_constructs.reasoning: Referenced by tests. Its behavior includes branch fallback to main when the current branch lacks an origin counterpart, non-interactive guard for prompts, and careful stash/restore and reset semantics. Historical commits indicate it has numerous edge-case fixes; modifications should respect those behaviors (ff-only fallback, stash restore behavior, non-interactive prompt skipping).

key_behaviors: apply profile override before module imports
load project and user .env files
initialize logging early for CLI mode
provide interactive session browsing and selection
guard interactive commands against non-TTY invocation

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or reordering the pre-import profile override (_apply_profile_override).
pitfalls.consequence: Modules that cache HERMES_HOME at import time will see the wrong path, causing incorrect data directories, config reads, and test failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Always ensure profile parsing runs before any hermes_* imports and that argv stripping preserves other CLI args.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing how argv is mutated when stripping --profile/-p (e.g., wrong indices).
pitfalls.consequence: Argparse later in the CLI may see incorrect arguments or lose flags, breaking CLI parsing and tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain careful indexing logic and include tests that exercise --profile both as separate args and --profile=value forms.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying _has_any_provider_configured to be strict on errors or raise exceptions on missing files.
pitfalls.consequence: Setup flows or automated tests may be blocked; the function is intentionally tolerant and must default to not blocking users.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve try/except guards and default-to-safe behavior (assume not configured only when clearly absent).

pitfalls.mistake: Altering curses UI input handling or key codes without preserving fallback behavior.
pitfalls.consequence: Interactive navigation may break in real terminals; tests that don't use curses expect fallback picking to work.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the two-tier approach (curses first, fallback numbered list) and ensure the fallback remains non-blocking for tests by using isatty guards.

key_sections: _apply_profile_override: sets HERMES_HOME before other imports (critical for init)
_has_any_provider_configured: determines whether setup prompts are needed
cmd_update: complex git update flow with many edge cases and test coverage
skip_unless_needed: curses UI internals in _session_browse_picker (read only when modifying session picking)
help text block at top (usage) which is reference-only
