relevant_snippets.description: Mocks subprocess.run to simulate git and asserts cmd_update picks origin/main when current branch lacks remote counterpart and that non-interactive sessions skip prompts.

related_files.relationship: setup flows & model/provider selection are invoked by CLI commands defined here; docs/flows interoperate strongly
related_files.reason_to_check: Editing provider-setup flags or _has_any_provider_configured behavior should be cross-checked with setup.py logic.

related_files.relationship: provider auth detection and credential resolution are used by CLI setup and provider heuristics
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to provider detection or environment-based checks must be consistent with auth resolution semantics.

related_files.relationship: gateway-related CLI commands (hermes gateway ...) interact with gateway runner; profile/HERMES_HOME changes affect runtime behavior
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change how HERMES_HOME or env bridging works, gateway startup may be affected.

related_files.relationship: model/provider selection UI and gating call into models.py helpers (Nous gating, pricing); main wires the model flows
related_files.reason_to_check: Altering model-selection or free-tier checks impacts the CLI's provider/model UX.

insights.title: Sync bundled skills to default profile when updating from named profile
insights.problem: Running 'hermes update' from a named profile did not sync bundled skills to the default profile because the filter excluded default profiles.
insights.root_cause: The list_profiles filter incorrectly excluded default profile (is_default) from the cross-profile sync loop.
insights.solution: Remove the 'not p.is_default' condition so the default profile is included in sync.
insights.lesson: Cross-profile operations should be explicit about inclusion/exclusion; failing to sync the canonical default profile is a break in the update workflow.

insights.title: Nous model selection uses free-tier gating and unavailable model presentation
insights.problem: Model selection flow needed to dim/unavailable models for free-tier Nous users and provide upgrade links.
insights.root_cause: Previous prompt flow did not handle unavailable models or portal upgrade UX.
insights.solution: Integrate filter_nous_free_models and partition_nous_models_by_tier into NOSU model flow; pass unavailable_models and portal_url into _prompt_model_selection to render grayed-out block and upgrade link.
insights.lesson: UI prompt helpers should accept both selectable and unavailable lists plus an upgrade URL to present an accurate, actionable menu.

insights.title: Prefer credential pool tokens for Codex model flow
insights.problem: Model flow for Codex could miss tokens stored in credential pool and instead consult legacy state only.
insights.root_cause: Model flow used resolve_codex_runtime_credentials exclusively and didn't consult get_codex_auth_status.
insights.solution: Query get_codex_auth_status() first and prefer its api_key if logged_in; otherwise fall back to resolve_codex_runtime_credentials().
insights.lesson_learned: Credential pools can be the single source-of-truth for OAuth-managed providers; flow code should consult pool-aware status helpers before legacy storage.

insights.title: Wire models.dev into model selection flow and update Gemini handling
insights.problem: Model selection relied too heavily on static curated lists or live probes, leading to outdated or overwhelming model lists.
insights.root_cause: No primary use of models.dev filtered registry in selection flow.
insights.solution: Try models.dev (list_agentic_models) first, fall back to curated static list for offline, else probe live. Print informative messages about which list is used.
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer filtered live registries over static lists for up-to-date catalogs, but keep curated offline fallback for reliability.

insights.title: Provider selection UI improvements and getpass for secrets
insights.problem: Secret prompts used input() which echoes sensitive values; provider menu ordering and defaults were not optimal.
insights.root_cause: Various interactive prompts used input() and provider ordering didn't emphasize common choices.
insights.solution: Use getpass.getpass() for API key and token prompts to hide input; rework provider menu into top_providers and extended_providers with a 'More...' flow and improved default selection and curses-aware prompt helper.
insights.lesson_learned: Use secure input for secrets and design CLI menus to surface common options first while allowing access to advanced ones; ensure fallback behavior for non-TTY environments.

insights.title: Skip config migration prompt in non-interactive sessions
insights.problem: hermes update blocked/hung when run from cron or non-interactive contexts due to input() call.
insights.root_cause: Only checked stdin.isatty() previously; input() still used and could raise/hang in piped contexts.
insights.solution: Require both stdin and stdout to be TTY before prompting; if not interactive, print guidance and skip prompting. Catch EOFError as fallback.
insights.lesson_learned: CLI actions invoked in non-interactive contexts must avoid input() and detect both stdin and stdout TTY state. Provide clear guidance for manual follow-up actions.

insights.title: Add 'webhook' subcommand wiring
insights.problem: Main CLI needed to expose webhook command.
insights.root_cause: New webhook CLI added; main parser not wired.
insights.solution: Add cmd_webhook and webhook subparser entries to CLI.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding a new CLI module, wire subcommands into the top-level parser and provide consistent help text.

insights.title: Use profile-aware launchd label in update flow
insights.problem: Update logic used hardcoded launchctl label; with profile-scoped labels this must use get_launchd_label().
insights.root_cause: Launchd label became profile-dependent; update flow still referenced the hardcoded label.
insights.solution: Use get_launchd_label() when running launchctl list/stop/start in cmd_update and restart logic.
insights.lesson_learned: When refactoring global naming to be context-scoped, update all call sites to reference the new helpers to avoid mismatches.

insights.title: Guard .strip() against None values from YAML model config
insights.problem: YAML key present with no value becomes None; .get(...).strip() crashed with AttributeError.
insights.root_cause: Used entry.get('name', '').strip() but when YAML had 'name:' with no value get returns None (not using default), so .strip() failed.
insights.solution: Use (entry.get('name') or '').strip() and similarly for base_url to handle missing/null values.

insights.title: Harden hermes update flow against diverged history and non-main branches
insights.problem: hermes update could fail on diverged history (ff-only), on non-main branches, could leave stash unstashed/restored incorrectly, and could exit in gateway non-interactive flows.
insights.root_cause: Previous logic assumed simple fast-forward and didn't handle fetch failures, detached HEAD or branch switching, or stash restore semantics on early-return.
insights.solution: Improved fetch error reporting, auto-switch to main when updating (stash before checkout), fallback to git reset --hard origin/main when ff-only fails, avoid restoring stash when reset fails, restore stash and original branch on early returns, and treat stash conflicts as non-fatal in non-interactive mode (return False instead of exit). Added many tests.

insights.title: Hugging Face provider integration and HF curated model list
insights.problem: Users needed first-class support for Hugging Face Inference Providers and curated HF models.
insights.root_cause: HF provider not previously registered; model picker would show overly large live catalogs.
insights.solution: Register 'huggingface' provider, add HF_TOKEN support, curated HF model lists and UI options, and prefer curated lists over live probes for large known provider catalogs.
insights.lesson_learned: Introduce first-class provider mappings and curated model lists to improve UX; keep a custom model entry point for arbitrary choices.

hermes_cli/models.py.short_description: Model/provider catalogs and helpers for Nous Portal free-tier gating and pricing
hermes_cli/models.py.description: This module centralizes curated model lists, provider-to-model mappings, and lightweight helpers used by the interactive setup and provider selection flows. It contains hard-coded catalogs (OPENROUTER_MODELS, _PROVIDER_MODELS, curated HF lists) used as offline fallbacks, plus logic for Nous Portal pricing/tier handling: fetching account tier, determining whether a model is free, filtering Nous model lists according to allowlists, and partitioning models into selectable/unavailable groups for free-tier users. It also implements a small TTL cache for free-tier checks to avoid hitting the Nous Portal API repeatedly while still picking up upgrades quickly.

key_constructs.name: OPENROUTER_MODELS
key_constructs.purpose: Curated list of model id and display description pairs used as a default catalog for OpenRouter-like endpoints.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is authoritative offline catalog data exposed to setup UI and model pickers when live /models endpoints are unavailable. Edits should remain compatible with UI expectations (tuple entries).

key_constructs.name: _PROVIDER_MODELS
key_constructs.purpose: Mapping of provider id → list of curated model identifiers as a fallback catalog per provider.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by setup.py and model pickers to present sane defaults. Keep in sync with other places that add providers.

key_constructs.name: _NOUS_ALLOWED_FREE_MODELS
key_constructs.purpose: Allowlist of Nous models that are permitted to appear when priced as free.
key_constructs.reasoning: Policy: only allow certain models to be visible when free; other free models are hidden to keep UI tidy. Mutating this set changes what users on Nous free tiers see.

key_constructs.name: _is_model_free
key_constructs.purpose: Return True if model has zero prompt and completion cost according to pricing map.
key_constructs.reasoning: Helper used by higher-level filtering/partitioning functions. It must parse pricing values defensively (returns False on missing/unparseable values).

key_constructs.name: filter_nous_free_models
key_constructs.purpose: Filter Nous model IDs according to free-model policy and the allowlist.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates the policy rules: allowlist models only when free; other models are shown only when not free. Must be tolerant of missing pricing data (returns original list when pricing missing).

key_constructs.name: fetch_nous_account_tier
key_constructs.purpose: Call Nous Portal /api/oauth/account to fetch subscription info and return parsed JSON or empty dict on error.
key_constructs.reasoning: Network helper used by check_nous_free_tier; returns {} on any failure so callers default to not blocking paid users.

key_constructs.name: is_nous_free_tier
key_constructs.purpose: Return True when account_info indicates monthly_charge == 0 (free tier).
key_constructs.reasoning: Explicit parsing with safe fallbacks; returns False on missing/unparseable fields so paying users are never blocked.

key_constructs.name: partition_nous_models_by_tier
key_constructs.purpose: Split Nous model list into (selectable, unavailable) based on free_tier boolean and pricing dict.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to present grayed-out/disabled models to free-tier users; must return (all, []) when not free or when pricing unknown.

key_constructs.name: _FREE_TIER_CACHE_TTL
key_constructs.purpose: Time-to-live (seconds) for caching Nous free-tier detection.
key_constructs.reasoning: Short TTL (3 minutes) balances network load and UX responsiveness to account upgrades; editing value affects frequency of API calls.

key_constructs.name: check_nous_free_tier
key_constructs.purpose: Return whether the current Nous user is on a free tier, using cached result and making API calls if needed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Caches results in _free_tier_cache and uses provider auth helpers to obtain access tokens. It must default to False on error (assume paid) to avoid blocking paying users.

key_constructs.name: clear_nous_free_tier_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Invalidate the cached free-tier result, typically called on login/logout.
key_constructs.reasoning: Simple cache invalidation helper that must be called when provider auth state changes so UI reflects new account tier quickly.

key_behaviors: provide curated model lists per provider
filter Nous models based on pricing and allowlist policy
fetch Nous account tier and cache the result for a short duration
partition Nous models into selectable/unavailable buckets for UI

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming network calls always succeed and returning None or raising on failures.
pitfalls.consequence: CLI setup could block or provide empty menus; tests expect graceful fallback to curated lists and defaults.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep try/except in fetch_nous_account_tier and default behaviors (return {} and treat as paid).

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or extending TTL semantics of _free_tier_cache without considering UX.
pitfalls.consequence: Setting TTL too long could delay reflecting user upgrades; too short increases portal API calls and rate limits.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve semantics (currently 180s) or update tests and related call sites when changing.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the allowlist logic in filter_nous_free_models (e.g., reversing inclusion rules).
pitfalls.consequence: Free-tier users may see paid models or promotional free models unexpectedly; UX and tests rely on explicit policy.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the documented rules: allowlist models shown only when free; other free models hidden.

key_sections: _NOUS_ALLOWED_FREE_MODELS: policy allowlist
filter_nous_free_models: implements allowlist and policy rules
fetch_nous_account_tier & is_nous_free_tier: network and parsing helpers
check_nous_free_tier: cache-aware entrypoint used by UI
skip_unless_needed: large curated _PROVIDER_MODELS mappings (read only when adding providers)
miscellaneous provider alias maps and label dictionaries

related_files.relationship: setup wizard and provider/model selection use these catalogs and gating helpers
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing provider lists, allowlist, or free-tier behavior affects setup UI and must be coordinated.

related_files.relationship: check_nous_free_tier resolves Nous auth state and uses auth helpers to obtain tokens
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to how tokens are resolved or auth state represented will impact check_nous_free_tier.

related_files.relationship: gateway may present provider/model options (pricing) and relies on consistent env/config bridging
related_files.reason_to_check: If altering pricing fetching behavior or caching, consider gateway UX.

insights.title: Nous free-tier model gating and pricing features
insights.problem: Nous Portal returns a mix of free and paid models; users on free tiers should only see free models and paid models should appear dimmed or unavailable.
insights.root_cause: No gating logic existed to hide non-allowlisted free models or to partition models by account tier.
insights.solution: Added functions: filter_nous_free_models (apply allowlist policy), fetch_nous_account_tier, is_nous_free_tier, partition_nous_models_by_tier. Also added a 3-minute TTL cache (check_nous_free_tier() + clear_nous_free_tier_cache()) to avoid repeated portal API calls.
insights.lesson: When integrating marketplace-like providers with variable pricing/tiering, implement deterministic filtering and a short TTL cache to avoid repeated network calls while still picking up user upgrades quickly.

insights.title: Model list update: replace z-ai/glm-5 with z-ai/glm-5.1
insights.problem: Model list needed updating to newer model id.
insights.root_cause: Provider model naming/version bump.
insights.solution: Replaced 'z-ai/glm-5' with 'z-ai/glm-5.1' in curated lists.
insights.lesson: Keep curated provider lists updated; small change but prevents user selection mismatch.

insights.title: Add Gemini model catalog entries and provider labels/aliases
insights.problem: Gemini provider lacked entries in model catalog and provider labels/aliases.
insights.root_cause: New provider integration needed corresponding model lists and alias mapping.
insights.solution: Add gemini models to _PROVIDER_MODELS, add 'gemini' label to _PROVIDER_LABELS and alias mappings for google → gemini; add gemini to _PROVIDER_ORDER.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding a provider, update model lists, labels, alias maps, and ordering so the CLI and selection flows present them correctly.

insights.title: Fetch and display live model pricing (including cache pricing) with caching
insights.problem: Users needed live per-million-token pricing for providers (OpenRouter, Nous Portal) visible in model pickers and provider listings.
insights.root_cause: Pricing information is available from /v1/models but not consumed or displayed by the CLI.
insights.solution: Added fetch_models_with_pricing() with a per-base_url cache, formatting helpers _format_price_per_mtok and format_model_pricing_table, and included cache read pricing (input_cache_read) when present. get_pricing_for_provider wraps provider-specific credential resolution.
insights.lesson_learned: Network-backed UI additions should be cached per endpoint and fail gracefully (return empty dict) to avoid blocking interactive flows; include column alignment utilities for readable terminal tables.

insights.title: Curated Hugging Face model list and provider labels
insights.problem: Lack of HF provider mapping and consistent curated model lists across modules.
insights.root_cause: HF provider support introduced but required curated lists in multiple places.
insights.solution: Add curated HF list and provider alias/labels to hermes_cli.models and expose to UI.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep curated provider-model lists synchronized across modules; add tests to detect divergence.

tests/hermes_cli/test_webhook_cli.py.short_description: Unit tests for the webhook CLI (subscribe/list/remove) and webhook gating
tests/hermes_cli/test_webhook_cli.py.description: This test module covers the hermes_cli.webhook CLI surface: subscribing, listing, updating, removing webhook subscriptions, persistence of subscription files under HERMES_HOME, corrupted-file handling, and gating when webhook support is disabled. The tests use pytest fixtures and monkeypatching heavily: an autouse fixture isolates HERMES_HOME to a tmp_path and patches hermes_cli.webhook._is_webhook_enabled to True by default. Tests exercise both normal flows and failure/corruption paths, and assert printed output using capsys. They also test gating behavior by monkeypatching _is_webhook_enabled and _get_webhook_config to emulate enabled/disabled states.

key_constructs.name: _isolate
key_constructs.purpose: pytest autouse fixture that isolates tests by setting HERMES_HOME to a temporary directory and patches webhook enabled state to True
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures webhook CLI tests operate in an isolated filesystem and avoid touching real user data; editing tests or code must preserve compatibility with HERMES_HOME reads/writes.

key_constructs.name: _make_args
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that builds an argparse.Namespace with default CLI argument values for passing into webhook_command
key_constructs.reasoning: Used in almost every test to simulate CLI invocation; test author and editing agents must keep the expected argument names and defaults aligned with hermes_cli.webhook.webhook_command signature.

key_constructs.name: TestSubscribe
key_constructs.purpose: Test cases covering subscribe behavior: creation, options parsing, auto-secret generation, updates and input validation
key_constructs.reasoning: Covers many success and edge cases for subscription creation and mutation; changes to webhook_command semantics must be validated against these tests.

key_constructs.name: TestList
key_constructs.purpose: Test cases for listing subscriptions both when empty and when entries exist
key_constructs.reasoning: Asserts human-readable output and counts; output substrings are asserted so formatting changes may break tests.

key_constructs.name: TestRemove
key_constructs.purpose: Tests removing existing and non-existent subscriptions and selective removal behavior
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures persistence and correct deletion semantics via _load_subscriptions/_save_subscriptions helpers.

key_constructs.name: TestPersistence
key_constructs.purpose: Tests that subscription file is written and that corrupted files are handled gracefully
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures _load_subscriptions returns {} on corrupted JSON to avoid crashing CLI and guide user remediation.

key_constructs.name: TestWebhookEnabledGate
key_constructs.purpose: Tests that webhook CLI commands are blocked or allowed depending on _is_webhook_enabled
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates gating behavior and helpful user message when gateway/webhooks are disabled.

key_behaviors: create webhook subscription entries on subscribe
list all subscriptions and show counts
remove subscriptions and persist changes
gracefully handle corrupted subscription file
block CLI actions when webhooks are disabled and instruct setup

pitfalls.mistake: Not isolating HERMES_HOME in tests
pitfalls.consequence: Tests could read/write the user's real ~/.hermes data and produce flaky results or pollution.
pitfalls.prevention: Use the autouse _isolate fixture or ensure tests set HERMES_HOME and monkeypatch webhook gating helpers.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming _is_webhook_enabled is always True
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that assert blocked behavior will not exercise gating branches.
pitfalls.prevention: Explicitly monkeypatch _is_webhook_enabled in tests needing disabled behavior (as this test suite does).

pitfalls.mistake: Changing names/keys of CLI args expected by webhook_command (e.g., deliver_chat_id/deliver_extra)
pitfalls.consequence: Many tests construct Namespace with specific keys; mismatches cause AttributeError or silent failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep function signature and arg names stable or update all tests when refactoring.

key_sections: _isolate: ensures HERMES_HOME isolation and default gating
TestSubscribe: exercises most creation/update edge cases
TestWebhookEnabledGate: demonstrates gate behavior under monkeypatch
skip_unless_needed: print/capsys assertions for formatting (read only when adjusting CLI output)

relevant_snippets.description: Fixture isolation and _make_args helper used by most tests.
Subscribe/list/remove test cases asserting persistence and gating behavior.

related_files.relationship: implementation under test (webhook_command, persistence helpers, gating functions)
related_files.reason_to_check: Any edits to hermes_cli.webhook (argument names, file locations, gating checks) must be reflected here.

related_files.relationship: provides get_env_path and HERMES_HOME helpers used to locate subscription JSON
related_files.reason_to_check: If config file paths or HERMES_HOME resolution changes, tests must update how they set HERMES_HOME.

data_constants_to_check: hermes_cli.webhook: subscription json schema keys (events, prompt, deliver, deliver_extra, secret)

gateway/run.py.short_description: Gateway lifecycle controller: load gateway config, start adapters, route messages and manage platform reconnection/exit.
gateway/run.py.description:
This module is the entrypoint and central orchestrator for the messaging gateway portion of Hermes. It bootstraps environment/config (including special SSL cert detection), bridges select config.yaml settings into environment variables, loads the GatewayConfig, and exposes the GatewayRunner class which manages adapter lifecycle, session routing, delivery, reconnection/backoff, and shutdown semantics. The module contains many helper utilities for platform-specific normalization (WhatsApp identifiers), config/model resolution, and adapter error handling. GatewayRunner is the operational core used by tests to exercise startup, adapter connect/disconnect, fatal-error handling, and session-level commands.

The file exists to separate gateway-specific lifecycle concerns from the agent runtime (run_agent.py). It must run very early and do some environment fiddling before other libraries load (notably the SSL cert auto-detection that must run before HTTP libs are imported). It also intentionally maps values from config.yaml into os.environ for the rest of the process to read, and it sets several gateway-specific env flags like HERMES_QUIET and HERMES_EXEC_ASK. The runner uses SessionStore, DeliveryRouter and BasePlatformAdapter abstractions from gateway.* (imported later) to create adapters for each configured platform and maintain the adapter registry and failure queues. Several design decisions reflect operating as a long-lived multi-session server: per-session running-agent cache, sentinel objects to mark pending agent setup, eviction/watchers for stale agents, and logic that treats retryable fatal errors differently (queue for reconnection) versus non-retryable fatal errors (cause clean or failed exit so supervisors can restart).

key_constructs.name: _ensure_ssl_certs
key_constructs.purpose: Detect a local CA cert bundle and set SSL_CERT_FILE before HTTP libs import.
key_constructs.reasoning: Must run early to ensure downstream libraries (aiohttp, discord, etc.) pick up a usable CA bundle, especially on non-standard systems (NixOS, macOS Homebrew, Alpine). When editing import order or adding network libraries, preserve this early call.

key_constructs.name: _AGENT_PENDING_SENTINEL
key_constructs.purpose: Sentinel object placed into _running_agents when a session starts processing before actual agent instantiation completes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to avoid duplicate concurrent agent creation for the same session. Edits interacting with _running_agents or eviction logic must special-case this sentinel to avoid races; tests exercise sentinel-related race guards.

key_constructs.name: _normalize_whatsapp_identifier
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize WhatsApp JID/LID strings to a stable numeric identifier.
key_constructs.reasoning: Multiple parts of the gateway rely on stable WhatsApp ids; changes that affect WhatsApp bridging or filename mapping must preserve normalization semantics.

key_constructs.name: _expand_whatsapp_auth_aliases
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve cross-referenced WhatsApp LID mappings by reading session mapping JSONs under HERMES_HOME/whatsapp/session.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses on-disk mapping files to find alternate identifiers (including _reverse suffix). Tests or changes that alter session file layout must be kept in sync.

key_constructs.name: _load_gateway_config
key_constructs.purpose: Load and parse ~/.hermes/config.yaml returning {} on error.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is a tolerant loader used to obtain values needed early; callers should assume it may return empty dict and handle defaults accordingly.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_gateway_model
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve the gateway/global model name from config.yaml, handling either string or dict representations.
key_constructs.reasoning: Gateway-created transient agents must pick the same model the CLI persists. Code that creates temporary agents (compress, memory flush helpers) should call this to avoid falling back to hardcoded defaults.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_hermes_bin
key_constructs.purpose: Find a command/argv list that invokes the 'hermes' CLI (shutil.which or python -m hermes_cli.main fallback).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for self-update or restart commands; must return argv parts or None. Changes here affect how gateway spawns Hermes update invocations.

key_constructs.name: GatewayRunner
key_constructs.purpose: Manage lifecycle of platform adapters, session store, delivery routing, reconnection watchers, and adapter fatal-error handling.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the central gateway controller used by unit tests to exercise startup/reconnect/fatal handling. Many internal attributes (e.g., _failed_platforms, _running_agents, _session_model_overrides) are mutated by command handlers; keep attribute names/semantics stable if tests or other modules reference them.

key_behaviors: load and bridge config.yaml values into environment variables
detect and set SSL cert location before network libs import
create and manage BasePlatformAdapter instances for each configured platform
queue failed adapters for reconnection or request clean/failed process exit for non-retryable mass-failures

pitfalls.mistake: Reordering or removing early bootstrap (e.g., moving _ensure_ssl_certs after imports).
pitfalls.consequence: HTTP libraries (aiohttp, discord, etc.) may initialize without a valid CA bundle, causing TLS verification failures on NixOS/other systems.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep SSL cert detection executed before importing any network/HTTP libraries; avoid introducing network module imports at module top-level before this call.

pitfalls.mistake: Treating the _AGENT_PENDING_SENTINEL as a real agent instance in eviction/idle checks.
pitfalls.consequence: Sentinel could be evicted immediately by stale detection logic, causing premature concurrent agent creation races and lost messages.
pitfalls.prevention: Special-case the sentinel in eviction/health-check code paths; skip calling agent methods or computing idle times when the sentinel is present.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming config.yaml keys always exist or are of expected types when bridging to environment variables.
pitfalls.consequence: KeyErrors or invalid env values could be injected; behavior may differ between CLI and gateway because gateway expects config to be authoritative for some terminal env vars.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve current defensive patterns: safe-loading YAML, type checks before setting env vars, and swallowing non-fatal errors.

key_sections: _ensure_ssl_certs: early bootstrap that must remain before HTTP imports
Config bridging block: how config.yaml maps to env vars (terminal, auxiliary, agent)
Adapter error handling and reconnection logic: _failed_platforms, _platform_reconnect_watcher, _handle_adapter_fatal_error
_AGENT_PENDING_SENTINEL handling: places where _running_agents is set/checked
skip_unless_needed: Large command/CLI helper functions in file header that only build small text placeholders
Platform-normalization helpers not relevant to current platform work

relevant_snippets.description: Creates a GatewayRunner and monkeypatches _create_adapter to return a fatal adapter that should cause a clean exit flag.
Multiple tests for reconnection watcher behavior and failed_platforms handling; these exercise _platform_reconnect_watcher logic.

related_files.relationship: Defines BasePlatformAdapter and MessageEvent used by GatewayRunner to interact with platform adapters.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to adapter lifecycle APIs (connect/disconnect/send/_set_fatal_error) require coordinated updates here and tests.

related_files.relationship: Manages session keys, SessionStore and SessionEntry referenced by GatewayRunner for session lifecycle and transcript management.
related_files.reason_to_check: Session reset, rewrite_transcript, or session_key generation changes affect GatewayRunner flows (e.g., /new, /resume).

related_files.relationship: GatewayRunner spawns transient agent instances and relies on run_agent AIAgent behavior/kwargs; changes to agent init args (provider/api_mode/user_id) should be synced.
related_files.reason_to_check: Gateway creates temporary agents for background tasks (compress, /compress, memory flush) and passes provider/runtime kwargs resolved in _resolve_runtime_agent_kwargs.

insights.title: Sentinel eviction race and pending-command safety net
insights.problem: Pending sentinel objects used during async setup were evicted prematurely due to stale-detection logic, and leaked slash-command text in the pending queue could be passed to the agent as user input.
insights.root_cause: Eviction logic assumed agent objects had get_activity_summary(); sentinels lack it (idle treated as infinite). Additionally, pending fallback processing passed through raw pending text without discarding known slash commands.
insights.solution: Guarded eviction to skip the pending sentinel when checking idle time. Added a safety net in the pending-message processing to discard known slash commands (resolve_command) so commands don't leak into agent input.
insights.lesson: Concurrent lifecycle placeholders need explicit handling in eviction/health checks; text-based commands should be filtered before being handed to the agent to avoid accidental interpretation.

insights.title: Agent notification injection for process completion
insights.problem: Gateway watchers needed to trigger agent turns when a background process with notify_on_complete finished.
insights.root_cause: Previously gateway sent only text notifications or status updates; it didn't inject a synthetic MessageEvent to the agent when agent_notify was requested.
insights.solution: When a watched session exits with agent_notify, create a synthetic MessageEvent with platform/session context and call adapter.handle_message to trigger a full agent turn. Gateway also respects notify_on_complete when deciding whether to send periodic updates.
insights.lesson: For cross-component notifications, synthesize platform-typed events with correct SessionSource so the normal agent processing pipeline handles the notification uniformly.

insights.title: Platform-aware interactive model picker integration
insights.problem: Gateway needed to leverage adapter-specific picking UI when available instead of falling back to text.
insights.root_cause: Generic model command always showed text output; adapters with interactive pickers weren't being used.
insights.solution: Detect adapter capability via getattr(type(adapter), 'send_model_picker', None), call send_model_picker and supply an async callback closure (_on_model_selected) which performs model switch, updates cached agents, and sets pending session overrides.
insights.lesson_learned: When offering cross-platform commands, detect per-adapter capabilities and use them; provide a closure to capture contextual state for callbacks and update local caches safely (with locks).

insights.title: Increase model discovery depth for picker
insights.problem: Picker showed too few models for curated providers.
insights.root_cause: Hardcoded max_models used when building provider lists was low.
insights.solution: Raise max_models from 8 to 50 when calling list_authenticated_providers.
insights.lesson_learned: Tune discovery limits and keep them configurable; increase limits when adding pagination so UI can surface more items.

insights.title: Fix silent TypeError on memory flush during /new and /resume commands
insights.problem: Memory flush helper _async_flush_memories accepted (session_id) but /new and /resume handlers passed two args (session_id, session_key). The TypeError was swallowed at debug level, so flush never ran.
insights.root_cause: Argument mismatch between caller sites and target function; silent swallowing hid the failure.
insights.solution: Removed stray session_key argument when creating background task for memory flush in /new and /resume handlers (now pass only session_id).
insights.lesson_learned: Keep call sites aligned with helper signatures; avoid swallowing TypeError silently in production code as it hides broken behavior. When helper signature changes, update all callers.

insights.title: Clear session-scoped model overrides on /new (session reset)
insights.problem: Session-scoped model overrides persisted across /new (reset) calls, causing users to keep using a previously switched model even after creating a new session.
insights.root_cause: Session reset flow did not remove entries from _session_model_overrides; ordering of reset/cleanup could leave stale state.
insights.solution: Pop the session_key from _session_model_overrides during reset; multiple commits added this removal and ensured it occurs before reset and agent creation so next agent picks up default model.
insights.lesson_learned: Session-scoped mutable registries must be cleared on session reset; explicitly remove overrides (don't rely on agent restart alone).

insights.title: Isolate session-scoped env/credential registries using ContextVars and clear them on session reset
insights.problem: Skill-registered credential files and env passthrough sets were process-global and could leak between sessions handled in the gateway pipeline, causing cross-session credential/data bleed.
insights.root_cause: Registries for credential files and env passthrough used module-level mutable structures; gateway handles multiple sessions concurrently in a single process.
insights.solution: Call clear_env_passthrough() and clear_credential_files() before resetting session; credential and env modules were refactored to back their session-scoped registries with ContextVar so each logical session/context has isolated state.
insights.lesson_learned: In a long-lived multi-session gateway, session-scoped state must be context-isolated (ContextVars or explicit context objects). Always clear per-session registries on reset to avoid leaking secrets between sessions.

insights.title: Gateway model resolution now uses model.default config key
insights.problem: The CLI/gateway /model --global flow wrote model.name while other parts of code read model.default, leading to apparent reversion of the configured global model after restart.
insights.root_cause: Inconsistent config key usage (model.name vs model.default) across CLI and gateway code paths.
insights.solution: Changed write/read sites to use model.default consistently so persistent global model changes are read by daemon restarts and CLI tooling.
insights.lesson_learned: Pick canonical config keys and use them consistently across CLI, gateway, and setup code; inconsistent keys will cause surprising reverts.

insights.title: Batch and reduce noisy session expiry logs and fix async flush call signature
insights.problem: Session expiry loop logged many lines per cycle and called _async_flush_memories with wrong argument count causing flushes to never run correctly.
insights.root_cause: Incorrect arity when calling _async_flush_memories and unbatched logging for each session.
insights.solution: Collect expired sessions first and log a compact summary (platform counts), call _async_flush_memories(entry.session_id) with correct signature, change some log levels (debug vs info) for per-session messages, and log a consolidated summary after flush attempts.
insights.lesson_learned: Batch background operations and log summaries rather than per-item noise; ensure function call signatures across async boundaries are correct so background tasks actually run.

insights.title: Instrument inbound message and response timings
insights.problem: Lack of consistent inbound/response logging made it harder to diagnose latency and API call volume.
insights.root_cause: No standardized inbound/response logging was emitted for each message handling run.
insights.solution: Log inbound message (platform, user, chat, preview) at handler start and log response ready with timing, api_calls and response length. These metrics are useful for monitoring.
insights.lesson_learned: Add lightweight structured logs around request lifecycle boundaries (start/finish) with key metrics (latency, api_calls) to aid debugging and performance monitoring.

insights.title: Exit process with failure when all platforms fail with retryable errors
insights.problem: If all messaging platforms exhausted retries and were queued for background reconnection, the gateway stayed alive with no connected platforms and exit code 0, preventing systemd from restarting the service.
insights.root_cause: Runner did not distinguish the case where all adapters failed with retryable/fatal errors and did not set an exit-with-failure flag.
insights.solution: When adapters are all down and queued, if the adapter's fatal_error_retryable is True, set _exit_with_failure = True, log an error, await stop() so the process exits non-zero so systemd Restart=on-failure can restart it. Keep previous behavior (stay alive) if errors are not retryable.
insights.lesson_learned: Background reconnection is not always preferable to letting an external supervisor (systemd) perform a service restart; surface retryable mass-failure as a process failure so external supervisors can manage recovery.

insights.title: Preserve full transcript when compressing (/compress and hygiene compression)
insights.problem: /compress handler and hygiene compression were overwriting the OLD session transcript after compressing and creating a new session_id — destroying historical messages and making session_search lose data.
insights.root_cause: _compress_context created a new session_id but code wrote compressed messages back into the old session_id (rewrite_transcript called with old id).
insights.solution: After _compress_context, detect the new session_id, update session_entry.session_id and session_store, and write compressed messages into the NEW session_id. Adjust hygiene path similarly. Updated tests and mocks to simulate session_id changes.

insights.title: Scope progress thread fallback to Slack only
insights.problem: Progress-thread fallback used event_message_id for multiple platforms; for Telegram DM this caused failures since Telegram uses message_thread_id for forum topics only and using event_message_id as thread_id produced invalid metadata.
insights.root_cause: Generic fallback logic used event_message_id for all platforms regardless of platform-specific threading semantics.
insights.solution: Only use event_message_id fallback for Slack DM; for other platforms (Telegram etc.) use explicit source.thread_id only.

run_agent.py.short_description: Standalone AIAgent implementation: conversation loop, tool-calling, streaming, and tool result handling.
run_agent.py.description:
This module implements a self-contained AIAgent used by the CLI and gateway to interact with LLM providers and tools. It centralizes core agent responsibilities: building prompts (via agent.prompt_builder imports), executing API calls (OpenAI/OpenRouter/Anthropic variants), supporting streaming responses, accumulating streamed tool-calls, invoking tools (function-calling) and managing tool execution concurrency and results. The class AIAgent encapsulates runtime options (model, provider, api_mode), iteration budgeting for preventing runaway loops, and large-result handling (saving to HERMES_HOME/cache/tool_responses). It also provides safety wrappers around stdio to avoid crashes in headless environments and functions to sanitize surrogate characters that can crash provider SDK JSON serialization.

Why it exists: to provide a single authoritative implementation of the agent's run loop and tool-calling semantics shared by interactive CLI and gateway modes. The file purposely includes provider-specific fallbacks and streaming accumulator logic because providers differ in how they stream text and tool-call deltas. The code is heavily tested in unit tests around streaming, tool invocation, context-pressure display, and plugin hook invocation. Many helper constructs (IterationBudget, _SafeWriter, surrogate sanitizers, and path-scoping checks for parallel tool execution) are written to support robust long-running operation and safe concurrency.

key_constructs.name: _SafeWriter
key_constructs.purpose: Wrap stdout/stderr streams to swallow OSError/ValueError from broken pipes in headless/service contexts.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to avoid agent crashes when stdout/stderr become unavailable (systemd, Docker). If editing output code or tests, ensure _SafeWriter continues to guard writes/flush/isatty.

key_constructs.name: IterationBudget
key_constructs.purpose: Thread-safe per-agent iteration counter enforcing max tool-calling iterations and supporting refunds for programmatic turns.
key_constructs.reasoning: A central safety mechanism to cap parent and subagent loops; modifications to agent delegation or subagent budgeting must respect this interface.

key_constructs.name: _NEVER_PARALLEL_TOOLS
key_constructs.purpose: Set of tools that must not run concurrently (e.g., interactive/clarify).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by concurrency heuristics to decide whether a batch of tool calls can be parallelized. Preserving this list or its semantics is required when changing parallel execution logic.

key_constructs.name: _should_parallelize_tool_batch
key_constructs.purpose: Heuristic to decide whether a batch of tool calls is safe for concurrent execution, considering read-only tool sets and path-scoped conflicts.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function contains path normalization and reserved-path overlap checks; changes can alter concurrency safety and cause race conditions for file-targeting tools.

key_constructs.name: _sanitize_surrogates
key_constructs.purpose: Replace lone UTF-16 surrogate code points with U+FFFD to avoid json.dumps() failures in provider SDKs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Sanitization is important before sending provider API payloads; tests validate this behavior and it must be applied consistently to messages and streaming deltas.

key_constructs.name: _strip_budget_warnings_from_history
key_constructs.purpose: Remove budget-warning markers from 'tool' role messages before hydrating history to prevent leaking ephemeral warnings into model context.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures tool-injected budget warnings don't alter future model behavior (e.g., blocking tool use). Tests exercise this function.

key_constructs.name: _save_oversized_tool_result
key_constructs.purpose: If tool returns very large text, save it to HERMES_HOME/cache and replace content with a preview and file path pointer.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents context explosion by persisting large outputs to disk; editing the path, threshold, or preview size affects on-disk behavior and tests expecting file creation.

key_constructs.name: AIAgent
key_constructs.purpose: Main agent implementation: manages conversation loop, tool calls, streaming accumulation, plugin hook invocation, prompt building and compression integration.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the primary run-time object instantiated by CLI and GatewayRunner. It has many responsibilities and integration points (prompt_builder, memory_manager, ContextCompressor, plugins). Keep __init__ signature and public methods stable because gateway.run and tests create agents with specific init args (user_id, api_key, model, quiet_mode, skip_memory, etc.).

key_behaviors: run provider API calls with streaming or non-streaming paths and accumulate deltas
invoke function-calling tools and manage parallel/sequential execution heuristics
protect stdio to avoid crashes in headless environments
save oversized tool results to disk and insert compact preview references

pitfalls.mistake: Altering AIAgent.__init__ signature or _init_kwargs keys without updating callers (gateway.run, tests).
pitfalls.consequence: Runtime TypeErrors or missing provider credentials during agent instantiation, and many tests will fail that construct AIAgent with specific kwargs.
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying constructor args, update all call-sites (gateway.run._resolve_runtime_agent_kwargs and tests) and adjust tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing surrogate sanitization or _sanitize_messages_surrogates calls prior to json.dumps.
pitfalls.consequence: json.dumps()/provider SDK calls can raise or produce corrupted payloads for messages containing lone surrogate characters; tests assert sanitization behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep surrogate replacement logic intact and ensure any new message shaping code calls the sanitizer.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing parallelization heuristics (_should_parallelize_tool_batch/_PATH_SCOPED_TOOLS) without considering reserved-path overlap logic.
pitfalls.consequence: File-targeted tools may run concurrently on overlapping paths, causing race conditions or corrupt filesystem writes.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect reserved path extraction and overlap checks; update tests that depend on concurrency decisions when changing behavior.

key_sections: AIAgent.__init__: how agent runtime options are wired (model, api_mode, user_id, toolsets)
_interruptible_streaming_api_call / streaming accumulator: provider streaming behavior and backfill logic
_invoke_tool / handle_function_call integration: tool-call dispatch and plugin hooks
IterationBudget: iteration capping and refund behavior for subagents and execute_code
skip_unless_needed: _SafeWriter internals (boilerplate stream guards)
Some provider-specific edge-case logging blocks unless debugging providers

relevant_snippets.description: Constructs streaming chunks and asserts _interruptible_streaming_api_call accumulates text and tool-call deltas; relevant to streaming accumulator logic.
Fixtures and helper functions used to mock tools/OpenAI client; tests instantiate AIAgent and validate behaviors like logging handlers and sanitation.

related_files.relationship: Provides prompt building and system/context guidance used by AIAgent to construct LLM inputs.
related_files.reason_to_check: If prompt structure or guidance constants change, the agent run loop and tests expecting injected guidance must be updated.

related_files.relationship: Function-call dispatch surface used by AIAgent to execute tools; handle_function_call and tool metadata are tightly coupled to agent tool invocation.
related_files.reason_to_check: Tool invocation payloads, hook shapes, and call ids must be consistent between agent and model_tools.

related_files.relationship: GatewayRunner spawns AIAgent instances and depends on constructor arg semantics and provider-runtime kwargs resolved here.
related_files.reason_to_check: If AIAgent constructor or runtime kwargs change, gateway.run must be updated to supply matching args.

insights.title: Thread gateway user_id into memory provider initialization
insights.problem: Gateway sessions all used static identifiers for memory providers, causing different users to share a single memory bucket.
insights.root_cause: Memory plugins read defaults from plugin configs rather than receiving the per-gateway user_id from AIAgent.
insights.solution: AIAgent.__init__ accepts and stores a user_id; the _init_kwargs used to initialize memory providers include user_id when present. Mem0 and Honcho providers prefer kwargs-provided user_id over the config default.
insights.lesson: Plugin initialization must respect per-session context provided by the runtime (gateway) and not rely solely on static plugin config for identity scoping.

insights.title: Remove dead helpers and attributes
insights.problem: There were several write-only or unused attributes and long unused helper methods left in run_agent.py.
insights.root_cause: Iterative refactors left unused methods and attributes (e.g. _is_anthropic_url, _classify_empty_content_response, _last_reported_tool) behind.
insights.solution: Removed those dead methods/attributes to reduce maintenance surface.
insights.lesson: Keep core runner lean; remove feature stubs and maintain a single source of truth for identical logic to avoid divergence.

insights.title: Sentinel eviction race fix in GatewayRunner
insights.problem: A pending sentinel placeholder added during async setup could be immediately evicted by stale-detection logic because the sentinel lacked get_activity_summary(), resulting in premature eviction/race with setup.
insights.root_cause: _AGENT_PENDING_SENTINEL objects did not implement the same interface expected by the eviction checker which assumed get_activity_summary() existed; the idle calculation returned inf and met eviction criteria.
insights.solution: Guard eviction logic to skip eviction when _stale_agent is _AGENT_PENDING_SENTINEL.
insights.lesson: When using sentinel placeholders in concurrent initialization flows, ensure eviction/health checks special-case sentinels to avoid races.

insights.title: Backfill empty Codex output from streamed events
insights.problem: Codex backend (chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex) sometimes streams output via response.output_item.done or output_text.delta events but get_final_response() / final_response.output was empty, causing responses to be rejected.
insights.root_cause: The streaming path ignored non-terminal events and relied on final_response.output; when SDK returned empty output after streaming, no recovery was performed.
insights.solution: Collect output_item.done events and text deltas during streaming (both primary and fallback stream paths), backfill final_response.output from collected items or synthesize from deltas (only when no tool calls). Add diagnostics logging when output remains empty.
insights.lesson_learned: Streaming APIs can surface content via events rather than final objects; consumers must capture and backfill streamed content to be robust to SDK/backend shape changes. Ensure synthesized fallbacks do not misrepresent tool-invocation content.

insights.title: Improve logging for terminal stream events
insights.problem: response.incomplete/failed events lacked useful diagnostics leading to hard-to-debug empty outputs.
insights.root_cause: Terminal events weren't logged with status and incomplete_details.
insights.solution: Log status, incomplete_details, streamed_chars count and client context when terminal events occur to aid debugging.
insights.lesson_learned: Clear logging for terminal streaming events is essential when backfilling or creating synthesized responses.

insights.title: Provider-specific actionable guidance for Codex OAuth 401
insights.problem: Users received a generic 'check API key' message when Codex OAuth returned 401, which didn't help when the issue was a reused refresh token by another client.
insights.root_cause: Generic handling of HTTP 401/403 errors across providers without customizing messages for known provider-specific recovery flows.
insights.solution: When provider is openai-codex and status_code == 401, print Codex-specific recovery steps telling user to run codex then hermes login --provider openai-codex.
insights.lesson_learned: When surfaced errors can have provider-specific remedies, tailor guidance to be actionable; this complements server-side detection of specific error codes.

insights.title: xAI prompt caching: attach x-grok-conv-id header to route requests to same server
insights.problem: xAI's distributed endpoints can fail to hit prompt caches unless requests are routed to the same backend server.
insights.root_cause: No header tying session/request to a deterministic server instance.
insights.solution: If base_url contains x.ai and session_id exists, set extra_headers {'x-grok-conv-id': session_id} for model API calls to improve prompt cache hits.
insights.lesson_learned: Provider-specific advanced features (prompt caching) can be leveraged client-side by adding required routing headers; guard these with base_url checks and only set when session_id is available.

insights.title: Preserve dots for certain providers and include OpenCode Go
insights.problem: Model names needed to be preserved/dotted for some endpoints (Alibaba/DashScope, now OpenCode Go).
insights.root_cause: Provider detection and base_url checks didn't include 'opencode-go' or opencode.ai/zen/go URL pattern.
insights.solution: Extend _anthropic_preserve_dots() to return True for provider in {'alibaba','opencode-go'} and check base_url for opencode.ai/zen/go.
insights.lesson_learned: Normalization decisions must mirror provider semantics; include explicit provider checks and base_url fallbacks for new providers.

insights.title: Centralize logging setup via hermes_logging
insights.problem: Error/agent file handlers were being added ad-hoc and could be duplicated when many AIAgent instances were created (gateway mode).
insights.root_cause: run_agent previously added its own RotatingFileHandler for errors.log; idempotence and centralization were lacking.
insights.solution: Use hermes_logging.setup_logging() (idempotent) for centralized setup, and setup_verbose_logging() for debug mode.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize logging configuration to avoid duplicated handlers and inconsistent log formats. Make logging setup idempotent because multiple components may initialize it.

insights.title: Remove redundant local import
insights.problem: normalize_usage was being imported redundantly inside a function.
insights.root_cause: Duplicate import introduced by previous refactor.
insights.solution: Remove the redundant local import (already module-level).
insights.lesson_learned: Avoid unnecessary local imports when already imported at module level; prefer single canonical import to reduce clutter.

insights.title: Introduce per-API-request usage summarization helper
insights.problem: Plugins listening for request-scoped events needed token usage info but giving raw response objects leaks implementation and increases coupling.
insights.root_cause: Previously post_llm_request passed full response objects to plugins; that exposes provider internals and could change across providers.
insights.solution: Added _usage_summary_for_api_request_hook(response) to normalize and emit a compact usage dict (prompt_tokens, total_tokens, etc.) and pass usage into post_api_request.
insights.lesson_learned: Provide adapter-level normalization of provider-specific fields before exposing them to plugin ecosystem; keep plugin payloads stable and minimal.

insights.title: Switched plugin hook invocations from pre_llm_request/post_llm_request to pre_api_request/post_api_request
insights.problem: Plugins expecting prior hook names/payloads would miss events or get unexpected kwargs.
insights.root_cause: Hook rename plus payload shape changes (messages -> message_count, tools -> tool_count, response->response_model/usage) changed plugin contract.
insights.solution: Updated internal invocations to new hook names and new narrow kwargs; added env_var_enabled helper to gate debug dumping.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing plugin hooks, coordinate a migration path and keep hooks tolerant (use **_ to absorb extra kwargs) and document the change; tests should validate the new small payload shape.

insights.title: Pass session_id and tool_call_id through tool invocation
insights.problem: Tool handlers and plugin hooks lacked tool_call_id and session_id, hindering attribution and multi-tenant reasoning.
insights.root_cause: handle_function_call previously accepted task_id but not tool_call_id/session_id; callers didn't propagate those IDs.
insights.solution: Augmented _invoke_tool and calls to handle_function_call to include tool_call_id and session_id; plugin hooks pre_tool_call/post_tool_call now receive these fields.
insights.lesson_learned: Propagate contextual identifiers (session_id, tool_call_id) through tool dispatch path to allow plugins, tracing, and memory providers to attribute results correctly.

insights.title: Guard plugin invocations with try/except
insights.problem: Plugin code exceptions should not crash the agent loop.
insights.root_cause: Plugins run arbitrary code; if a plugin raised, it could bubble up.
insights.solution: Invoke plugin hooks inside try/except around each hook call (already present but reinforced during hook changes).
insights.lesson_learned: Plugin system must always be safe-fail: plugin exceptions should be contained and logged, not allowed to abort core flows.

insights.title: Handle Ollama-style reused tool-call index in streaming accumulator
insights.problem: Ollama-compatible endpoints reused index 0 for each tool call in a parallel batch, causing the streaming accumulator to concatenate names/arguments for multiple tool calls into a single slot.
insights.root_cause: Accumulator keyed solely by reported index; when endpoints reuse the same index with different ids, the code assumed index uniqueness.
insights.solution: Track last seen id per raw_index and an active slot mapping per raw_index. When a new non-empty id appears at an index that previously held a different id, allocate a fresh slot and redirect subsequent deltas for that raw index to the new slot. Preserve behavior for providers that use incrementing indices.
insights.lesson_learned: Streaming protocols can vary in semantics; use stable identifiers (ids) rather than assumed sequence indices. Accumulators must support reused indices by disambiguating via ids.

insights.title: Show reasoning before response when tool calls suppress content
insights.problem: When tool call chunks arrived, suppressed content deltas (including reasoning tags) were not routed to the reasoning display during streaming; reasoning then appeared after streamed content, reversing expected order.
insights.root_cause: Suppressed content branch didn't route suppressed deltas to the stream delta callback that extracts reasoning tags.
insights.solution: When content is suppressed due to tool calls, still call stream_delta_callback(delta.content) so tag extraction runs and reasoning is displayed before tool execution and final response.

insights.title: Make tool-use enforcement configurable
insights.problem: Tool-use enforcement was hardcoded to gpt/codex model substrings only; users couldn't opt in/out or customize targets.
insights.root_cause: Previously injected TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_GUIDANCE only when model matched hardcoded substrings.
insights.solution: Added agent.tool_use_enforcement config handling (auto|true|false|list). Implemented logic to interpret booleans/strings/lists and decide injection; tests added.

insights.title: Strip budget warning markers from history
insights.problem: Budget-warning text embedded in tool-result messages leaked into replayed history and prevented models (esp. GPT-family) from calling tools in subsequent turns.
insights.root_cause: Tool-injected budget warning content remained in stored conversation history.
insights.solution: Added _strip_budget_warnings_from_history(messages) that removes _budget_warning JSON keys and text-pattern [BUDGET ...] from 'tool' role messages before hydrating history; invoked at start of run_conversation.

insights.title: Enable fine-grained tool streaming for Claude/OpenRouter and retry SSE errors
insights.problem: Anthropic buffered entire tool call arguments causing long silence; OpenRouter or proxies dropped idle SSE connections (Network connection lost), causing streaming failures for very large tool outputs.
insights.root_cause: Lack of Anthropic beta header to enable token-level streaming and SSE error events were not classified as transient connection errors by retry logic.
insights.solution: If using OpenRouter + Claude model, inject x-anthropic-beta: fine-grained-tool-streaming-2025-05-14 header. Treat OpenAI APIError without status_code and with common connection phrases as retryable in the streaming retry loop. Also send explicit max_tokens for Claude via OpenRouter.

insights.title: Activate plugin lifecycle hooks (pre/post_llm_call, session start/end)
insights.problem: Plugin system had hook registration but majority of lifecycle hooks were never invoked, limiting plugin integration points.
insights.root_cause: Only pre_tool_call and post_tool_call were used; other hooks not called by core agent loop.
insights.solution: Invoke on_session_start at session creation, pre_llm_call before each LLM turn (collect non-None returns for ephemeral context injection), post_llm_call after a completed turn, and on_session_end at end of run_conversation. Updated PluginManager.invoke_hook to return non-None results list. Tests added.

insights.title: Use real token counts for compression decisions and cap context pressure display
insights.problem: Previously used rough char/3 estimates for next prompt token count; context pressure percentage display could exceed 100% causing confusing UI.
insights.root_cause: Heuristic (chars/3) produced imprecise estimates and pct display integer was not capped.
insights.solution: Replace heuristic with provider-reported prompt_tokens + completion_tokens as the basis for compression decisions; cap displayed percentage at 100%.

insights.title: Detect thinking-budget exhaustion and skip useless continuation retries
insights.problem: When finish_reason='length' and the response contained only internal reasoning blocks or empty content, the agent retried uselessly (wasted API calls) or raised unclear errors.
insights.root_cause: Code did not detect 'think-only' or null content caused by model exhausting output tokens on reasoning, so it attempted continuations or surfaced cryptic errors.
insights.solution: Detect thinking-only length truncation early by inspecting response content (chat and anthropic variants), and return a targeted user-facing error and partial response, skipping continuation retries and cleaning up resources.
insights.lesson_learned: Differentiate truncation with real content from truncation where the model used up tokens on internal reasoning; provide actionable user guidance and avoid retrying identical failing calls.

insights.title: Increase API default timeout to support slow-thinking models
insights.problem: Some models perform long reasoning (>15 minutes) and were being killed by default 900s API timeout resulting in failed long-running requests.
insights.root_cause: Default HERMES_API_TIMEOUT was 900s; slow-thinking models like GLM-5/5.1 need longer windows.
insights.solution: Raise default HERMES_API_TIMEOUT to 1800s (30 minutes) in build and streaming timeouts.
insights.lesson_learned: Timeout defaults should reflect real model behavior; keep streaming chunk timeouts separate from total request timeout.

insights.title: Use get_toolset_for_tool to derive available toolsets for skills prompt
insights.problem: build_skills_system_prompt called check_toolset_requirements() redundantly to compute available toolsets; duplicated checks were expensive.
insights.root_cause: Code re-queried toolset availability rather than reusing already-loaded tool definitions.
insights.solution: Derive toolsets from loaded tool definitions using get_toolset_for_tool to avoid duplicated availability checks.
insights.lesson_learned: Reuse existing loaded metadata (tool definitions) rather than re-running availability checks; prefer light-weight mapping functions.

tests/gateway/test_runner_fatal_adapter..short_description: Unit tests for GatewayRunner fatal-adapter behavior and exit/reconnect semantics.
tests/gateway/test_runner_fatal_adapter..description:
This test module validates GatewayRunner's handling of fatal adapter startup/runtime errors. It defines two small BasePlatformAdapter subclasses used as fakes: _FatalAdapter (connect() sets a non-retryable fatal error) and _RuntimeRetryableAdapter (normal connect but can be injected with a retryable runtime fatal_error). The tests assert that a non-retryable fatal during startup causes the runner to request a clean exit and set a user-friendly exit_reason, while a retryable runtime fatal is enqueued for reconnection and triggers stop() with _exit_with_failure True so an external supervisor (systemd) can restart the service.

Why it exists: to ensure the gateway distinguishes between retryable and non-retryable fatal adapter errors and surfaces the correct process-level signaling (clean exit vs failed exit or reconnection attempts). The tests exercise GatewayRunner.start() via monkeypatching _create_adapter and call _handle_adapter_fatal_error directly for runtime error handling.

key_constructs.name: _FatalAdapter
key_constructs.purpose: Test adapter that sets a non-retryable fatal error during connect().
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to simulate startup conflicts (e.g., token in use) and assert GatewayRunner asks to exit cleanly.

key_constructs.name: _RuntimeRetryableAdapter
key_constructs.purpose: Test adapter that connects but can be given a retryable runtime fatal error, used to exercise reconnection/failure-queue handling.
key_constructs.reasoning: Simulates runtime bridge crash scenarios where retrying/reconnection is appropriate; tests ensure runner queues and schedules reconnection attempts and sets exit flags appropriately.

key_constructs.name: test_runner_requests_clean_exit_for_nonretryable_startup_conflict
key_constructs.purpose: Assert that non-retryable startup fatal errors set should_exit_cleanly and descriptive exit_reason.

model_tools.py.short_description: Tool discovery and dispatch layer that exposes tool schemas and runs tool handlers (sync↔async bridging).
model_tools.py.description:
This module is a thin orchestration layer over the tools registry and serves as the single public API surface for other agent components to discover available tools and dispatch tool calls. It exists to: (1) import tool modules so those modules can self-register their schemas/handlers with tools.registry, (2) produce filtered OpenAI-style tool/function definitions for model calls (get_tool_definitions), (3) coerce/validate tool arguments against tool JSON Schemas, and (4) route actual tool invocations to the registry while providing lifecycle hooks and a stable sync→async bridging mechanism.

Key design decisions visible in the file: persistent event loops (per-main-thread and per-worker-thread) are created and reused to avoid “Event loop is closed” errors from cached async clients (httpx/AsyncOpenAI). Tool discovery happens eagerly via importing a curated list of tools; discovery is wrapped to swallow import errors from optional dependencies. Toolset filtering supports both new toolset names (resolve_toolset/validate_toolset) and legacy _tools-suffixed names via _LEGACY_TOOLSET_MAP. The module also performs dynamic schema adjustments (e.g., rebuild execute_code schema and modify browser_navigate description) to avoid presenting the model with tool names that are not actually available at runtime.

The dispatcher (handle_function_call) integrates argument coercion, read-loop tracking, pre/post plugin hook invocation, agent-loop interception for tools that need higher-level state, and JSON-serialised error handling. The file is intentionally careful about context propagation: task_id, tool_call_id, and session_id are accepted and passed through to plugin hooks (commit history confirms the signature was expanded to add these IDs). Because this module is used by multiple runtime paths (CLI, gateway, RL, batch_runner), it focuses on robust, backward-compatible behavior: it stores the last resolved tool list in _last_resolved_tool_names for callers that rely on process-global state.

key_constructs.name: _get_tool_loop
key_constructs.purpose: Return or create a persistent event loop for the main/CLI thread.
key_constructs.reasoning: Modifications that affect how the main persistent loop is created or closed will impact cached async client cleanup and can reintroduce 'Event loop is closed' errors; maintain loop lifetime semantics when editing.

key_constructs.name: _get_worker_loop
key_constructs.purpose: Return or create a persistent event loop bound to the current worker thread (stored in thread-local).
key_constructs.reasoning: Worker threads use per-thread loops to avoid contention and closed-loop GC errors; changing storage or lifecycle here affects multithreaded tool executions.

key_constructs.name: _run_async
key_constructs.purpose: Bridge sync callers to async tool handlers using context-aware strategies (new thread for running loop present, per-thread loop for worker threads, persistent main loop otherwise).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the single source of truth for sync→async bridging in the codebase; altering its behavior changes how all async tool handlers are executed across CLI, gateway, and RL paths.

key_constructs.name: _discover_tools
key_constructs.purpose: Import a curated list of tool modules to trigger their registry.register() calls while isolating import failures.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tool discovery order and the list of modules influence which tools register; optional imports are guarded to keep the system resilient to missing optional deps.

key_constructs.name: TOOL_TO_TOOLSET_MAP
key_constructs.purpose: Mapping of tool name -> toolset name (populated from the registry after discovery).
key_constructs.reasoning: Consumers (e.g., batch_runner.py) rely on this map for grouping tools by toolset; it's built after discovery so edits affecting discovery must preserve its initialization timing.

key_constructs.name: TOOLSET_REQUIREMENTS
key_constructs.purpose: Toolset requirement metadata from registry (used for CLI checks and doctor.py).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by UI/doctor tooling to decide whether toolsets need additional credentials or services; its value is registry-driven and assumed stable post-discovery.

key_constructs.name: _LEGACY_TOOLSET_MAP
key_constructs.purpose: Backward-compatible mapping from old _tools-suffixed names to concrete tool name lists.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by get_tool_definitions to accept legacy toolset names; changing this affects backwards compatibility with user configs that still use legacy names.

key_constructs.name: get_tool_definitions
key_constructs.purpose: Return a filtered list of tool definitions (OpenAI function schema style) governed by enabled/disabled toolsets and runtime availability.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the public API used by prompt-building and model calls; its behavior (filtering, quiet_mode prints, dynamic schema modifications) must be preserved for model correctness and to avoid hallucinated tool invocations.

key_constructs.name: coerce_tool_args
key_constructs.purpose: Coerce string-valued tool arguments (from the LLM) into types expected by the tool's JSON Schema.
key_constructs.reasoning: Helps robustness against LLM type mismatches; tests depend on safe coercion behavior and its handling of union types and failure cases.

key_constructs.name: _coerce_value
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that attempts coercion of a string to a schema-declared type (supports union types).
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains the logic that decides coercion order for union types; changes can affect whether a value becomes int/float/bool or remains string.

key_constructs.name: _coerce_number
key_constructs.purpose: Parse string to float or int with additional guards for inf/nan and integer-only enforcement.
key_constructs.reasoning: Edge-case guards (nan/inf) and integer-only handling are relied upon by callers; altering behavior will affect numeric coercion semantics.

key_constructs.name: _coerce_boolean
key_constructs.purpose: Parse boolean-like strings ('true'/'false') to Python booleans, else return original string.
key_constructs.reasoning: A small but important function to avoid mis-parsing other string values as booleans.

key_constructs.name: handle_function_call
key_constructs.purpose: Main dispatcher for invoking a tool by name: argument coercion, lifecycle hooks, agent-loop interception, registry.dispatch invocation, and JSON-serialized result/error.
key_constructs.reasoning: Central dispatch function used throughout the system. Tests exercise error paths, hook propagation (task_id, session_id, tool_call_id), and agent-loop interception. Its signature was expanded to include tool_call_id and session_id, so callers and plugin hooks depend on these parameters.

key_constructs.name: _AGENT_LOOP_TOOLS
key_constructs.purpose: Set of tool names that must be handled by the agent loop (not dispatched via registry) because they require agent-level state.
key_constructs.reasoning: This set determines interception behavior — adding/removing items affects where calls are handled and tests assume certain names exist in the set.

key_constructs.name: _READ_SEARCH_TOOLS
key_constructs.purpose: Set of read/search tool names used to track read-loop behavior (resets/consecutive read tracking).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to notify notify_other_tool_call in file_tools when a non-read/search tool runs; altering it changes read-loop tracking semantics.

key_constructs.name: _last_resolved_tool_names
key_constructs.purpose: Process-global list of tool names resolved in the most recent get_tool_definitions call.
key_constructs.reasoning: Backwards-compat consumers (e.g., code_execution_tool fallback) may rely on this global; its update timing and contents are important.

key_behaviors: discovers and registers tools by importing tool modules
produces filtered OpenAI function-style tool schemas
routes tool invocations to registry.dispatch with lifecycle hooks
coerces LLM-returned string arguments to schema types

pitfalls.mistake: Close or recreate the main persistent event loop arbitrarily.
pitfalls.consequence: Cached async clients (httpx/AsyncOpenAI) tied to that loop will raise 'Event loop is closed' during garbage collection or later use.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the persistent loop lifecycle and use _get_tool_loop/_get_worker_loop/_run_async helpers to run async handlers.

pitfalls.mistake: Change the discovery module list or discovery timing without updating TOOL_TO_TOOLSET_MAP initialization.
pitfalls.consequence: TOOL_TO_TOOLSET_MAP and TOOLSET_REQUIREMENTS might be stale or incomplete leading to incorrect toolset metadata for consumers.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep discovery and the post-discovery constants initialization coupled; ensure registry-based maps are computed after imports.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify get_tool_definitions output shape or omit dynamic schema rewrites (execute_code/browser_navigate).
pitfalls.consequence: Models may hallucinate tool names that are not actually available, leading to incorrect tool invocation attempts in production.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the filtered_tools list semantics and the post-processing patches that remove references to unavailable tools.

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or alter signature parameters (tool_call_id, session_id) from handle_function_call.
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks plugin hook context propagation and any telemetry or downstream code that expects these IDs (tests assert they are passed).
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the expanded signature and propagate IDs to plugin hooks and any relevant calls.

key_sections: get_tool_definitions: how toolset filtering and dynamic schema adjustments happen
_run_async: authoritative sync→async bridging strategy and thread/loop behavior
_discover_tools: which tool modules are auto-imported and how import errors are handled
coerce_tool_args/_coerce_value/_coerce_number: arg type coercion edge cases
skip_unless_needed: the large hardcoded _modules list in _discover_tools (only change if adding/removing tools)
logging/print statements used for quiet_mode output

related_files.reason_to_check: Changing how dispatch or schema lookup works requires updating call sites in model_tools (e.g., coercion relies on schema shape returned by registry.get_schema).

related_files.relationship: Responsible for validate_toolset/resolve_toolset/get_all_toolsets used in get_tool_definitions.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes in toolset resolution semantics or API will affect filtering logic in get_tool_definitions.

related_files.reason_to_check: Plugin hook argument contract (names and kw args) must remain compatible with handle_function_call's propagation.
