related_files.reason_to_check: If notify_other_tool_call signature changes, model_tools must adapt call semantics.

insights.title: Expand handle_function_call signature to include tool_call_id and session_id
insights.problem: Tool lifecycle hooks and downstream handlers lacked sufficient context to correlate a tool result with a session or call.
insights.root_cause: Only task_id was propagated, leaving plugin authors and telemetry unable to attribute tool calls to a particular session or tool invocation.
insights.solution: Added optional tool_call_id and session_id parameters and propagated them into plugin hook invocations (pre_tool_call/post_tool_call).
insights.lesson_learned: Explicitly propagate contextual IDs through call stacks; prefer named params over packing into opaque dicts.

tests/test_model_tools.py.short_description: Unit tests for model_tools: dispatch, coercion/edge error handling, legacy toolsets and backward-compat wrappers.
tests/test_model_tools.py.description:
This test module verifies crucial behaviors of model_tools: that tools meant to be intercepted by the agent loop produce an error message, unknown tools produce informative JSON errors, tool dispatcher returns JSON even when exceptions occur, plugin lifecycle hooks receive contextual IDs (task_id, session_id, tool_call_id), legacy toolset mapping shape, and backward-compat wrapper functions such as get_all_tool_names and get_toolset_for_tool. The tests use unittest.mock.patch to isolate registry.dispatch and plugin invocations where needed. The coverage focuses on contract-level guarantees that other components rely upon.

Tests are deliberately small and assert on string content/JSON shape rather than on internal implementation details. The test that asserts hook propagation inspects the exact call_args_list invoked on hermes_cli.plugins.invoke_hook, so the order and kwarg names are part of the expected behavior. Several tests expect the presence of specific tool names (web_search, terminal) in the global lists, tying test expectations to the tool discovery logic.

key_constructs.name: TestHandleFunctionCall
key_constructs.purpose: Group of tests exercising handle_function_call behavior and plugin hook propagation.
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains tests that verify error formats and the invocation of pre/post plugin hooks with contextual IDs; important when changing handle_function_call signature or hook invocation semantics.

key_constructs.name: TestAgentLoopTools
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that expected tools are present in the agent-loop interception set and that regular tools are not.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates the composition of _AGENT_LOOP_TOOLS which gates whether calls are dispatched or left to the agent loop.

key_constructs.name: TestLegacyToolsetMap
key_constructs.purpose: Checks that legacy toolset mapping keys exist and that values are lists of strings.
key_constructs.reasoning: Protects backward compatibility by ensuring legacy names remain available and well-formed.

key_constructs.name: TestBackwardCompat
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies wrapper helper functions (get_all_tool_names, get_toolset_for_tool) and the TOOL_TO_TOOLSET_MAP constant.
key_constructs.reasoning: These tests assert the public API surface for tooling and batch runners; changes to these helpers can break consumers.

key_behaviors: asserts agent-loop tools return an agent-loop error
asserts unknown tools return JSON error strings
validates plugin hook propagation of task/session/tool_call ids

pitfalls.mistake: Rely on side effects from real registry.dispatch during tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests may call networked or optional-dependency-backed tool handlers leading to flaky failures or long-running tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Tests patch registry.dispatch as shown in test_tool_hooks_receive_session_and_tool_call_ids to isolate behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Change the order or kwarg names passed to plugin hooks.
pitfalls.consequence: The test test_tool_hooks_receive_session_and_tool_call_ids will fail because it asserts exact call_args_list.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve hook invocation order and kwarg names or update the test when intentionally changing hook contract.

pitfalls.mistake: Remove expected tools like 'web_search' or 'terminal' from discovery used in backward-compat tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Backward compatibility tests will fail, indicating broader breakage for consumers expecting these tools.
pitfalls.prevention: When altering discovery or default toolsets, update tests and dependent consumers simultaneously.

TestBackwardCompat: exercises public helper wrappers and map constants

agent/credential_pool.py.short_description: Credential pool implementation managing multiple provider credentials, rotation, refreshing, seeding and soft leasing.
agent/credential_pool.py.description:
This module implements a persistent credential pool used for provider-level failover and runtime key rotation. It is responsible for reading/writing the credential pool stored in the user's auth.json, seeding credentials from environment variables or legacy provider state, selecting an appropriate credential according to configurable strategies (fill_first, round_robin, random, least_used), marking credentials exhausted (with reason and cooldown), rotating priorities, refreshing OAuth credentials, and providing a simple soft-leasing API for concurrent use. The core data representation is the PooledCredential dataclass which models a single credential record (access/refresh tokens, metadata, status) and is round-tripped to/from JSON.

Why it exists: multi-credential providers (multiple API keys, OAuth tokens that can rotate) require a central mechanism to pick usable keys, avoid recently exhausted keys, recover when tokens are refreshed externally (e.g., Codex or Claude Code CLI), and to let concurrent child agents avoid clobbering a single credential. The pool centralizes selection/rotation logic, persists state, and exposes higher-level operations such as select(), mark_exhausted_and_rotate(), try_refresh_current(), and a lease API (acquire_lease/release_lease) to limit concurrent usage per credential.

How it fits in the system: the agent code (AIAgent, model adapters, and tools that spawn child agents) call into this module to get a runtime credential and base_url for provider API calls. The gateway and tools that run subagents (like delegate_task) rely on selecting and leasing credentials via this module. The module also interfaces with hermes_cli.auth helpers (read_credential_pool, write_credential_pool, _import_codex_cli_tokens, _resolve_zai_base_url etc.) to persist state and to import tokens from other CLIs or provider-specific files. The module contains provider-specific sync logic (e.g., Claude Code and Codex syncs) and special handling for custom provider endpoints (custom:* pool keys).

Design decisions reflected: (1) PooledCredential separates fields persisted in JSON from transient extra keys via extra + __getattr__ to keep backward/forward compatibility; (2) selection strategy is configurable via config.yaml (credential_pool_strategies); (3) exhaustion cooldowns vary by HTTP status (429 vs others), and the pool can respect explicit reset timestamps provided by providers; (4) leasing API enforces a soft cap on concurrent usage per credential and uses locks to be thread-safe.

Note: file contains complex parsing and normalization helpers for timestamps and provider error messages which drive exhausted cooldown behaviour and explicit reset handling.

key_constructs.name: STATUS_OK
key_constructs.purpose: Status constant representing a credential that is usable.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used throughout the pool to mark and check availability of credentials; do not change string value without checking persistence compatibility.

key_constructs.name: STATUS_EXHAUSTED
key_constructs.purpose: Status constant indicating a credential is in cooldown/exhausted state.
key_constructs.reasoning: Persistent status that affects selection and TTL calculations; tests depend on this string.

key_constructs.name: AUTH_TYPE_OAUTH
key_constructs.purpose: Indicates credential uses OAuth (refreshable) flow.
key_constructs.reasoning: Controls refresh behavior and logic paths that attempt refreshing via provider-specific routines.

key_constructs.name: CUSTOM_POOL_PREFIX
key_constructs.purpose: Prefix used in pool keys for custom OpenAI-compatible endpoints (custom:<name>).
key_constructs.reasoning: Custom endpoints share provider='custom' but distinct pool keys; this prefix must remain stable.

key_constructs.name: _EXTRA_KEYS
key_constructs.purpose: Set of keys carried through JSON roundtrips via PooledCredential.extra but not used as explicit attributes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Keeps optional provider-specific token fields accessible without changing the dataclass; modifications affect JSON schema handling.

key_constructs.name: PooledCredential
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing a single credential entry in the pool (fields, JSON round-trip helpers, runtime accessors).
key_constructs.reasoning: Central structure consumed by selection, rotation, persistence and refresh logic; preserves 'extra' provider-specific fields and defines runtime_api_key/runtime_base_url behavior for special providers (nous).

key_constructs.name: label_from_token
key_constructs.purpose: Derive a human-friendly label from a JWT access token by decoding claims.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when seeding/creating credentials from env or OAuth flows; relies on hermes_cli.auth._decode_jwt_claims.

key_constructs.name: _parse_absolute_timestamp
key_constructs.purpose: Best-effort parser for provider reset timestamps (epoch s/ms or ISO-8601).
key_constructs.reasoning: Normalizes diverse provider timestamp representations into epoch seconds used for exhaustion reset logic.

key_constructs.name: _normalize_error_context
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize provider error payloads to extract reason, message, and reset_at in seconds.
key_constructs.reasoning: Feeds into _mark_exhausted to persist a reset timestamp when available; robust parsing prevents misinterpreting error payloads.

key_constructs.name: get_pool_strategy
key_constructs.purpose: Return configured selection strategy for a provider from config.yaml, defaulting to fill_first.
key_constructs.reasoning: Selection behavior is driven by YAML config; changes to this function alter how providers choose keys at runtime.

key_constructs.name: DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENT_PER_CREDENTIAL
key_constructs.purpose: Default soft cap for concurrent leases (used by acquire_lease).
key_constructs.reasoning: Controls default concurrency; tests and lease logic expect this default to be present.

key_constructs.name: CredentialPool
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates a list of PooledCredential entries and operations: select, mark_exhausted_and_rotate, persist, refresh, and leasing.
key_constructs.reasoning: Acts as the in-memory plus persisted authority for credential selection/rotation and thread-safe leasing; callers (agents/tools) depend on its semantics.

key_constructs.name: load_pool
key_constructs.purpose: Factory that loads credential_pool for a provider from persistent auth.json and seeds from environment/legacy state when appropriate.
key_constructs.reasoning: Called by callers (tests, delegate tool) to obtain a ready-to-use CredentialPool; loads and migrates provider-specific state and removes stale seeded entries.

key_constructs.name: mark_exhausted_and_rotate
key_constructs.purpose: Mark the current credential as exhausted, persist the status, and rotate to the next credential according to strategy.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when an API call receives a failure (billing/ratelimit) to move to a different key and to persist the exhausted state.

key_constructs.name: acquire_lease
key_constructs.purpose: Attempt to obtain a lease for a credential (optionally by id) and increment active lease counters safely.
key_constructs.reasoning: Newer concurrency API that prevents multiple concurrent users from overflowing a single credential; uses internal locking and counters.

key_constructs.name: release_lease
key_constructs.purpose: Release a previously-acquired lease, decrementing the active counter and unlocking potential selection.
key_constructs.reasoning: Must be called on all code paths (including failure) to avoid leaked leases; tests cover release correctness.

key_constructs.name: try_refresh_current
key_constructs.purpose: Attempt to refresh OAuth credentials for the currently selected entry and persist updates when successful.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tries provider-specific refresh logic (e.g., refresh_codex_oauth_pure) and is used by callers to recover credentials before rotation.

key_behaviors: loads and persists the credential pool from auth.json
selects a credential using configurable strategies
marks credentials exhausted and rotates to next
attempts provider-specific OAuth refreshes
provides a soft lease API for concurrent usage

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying the string values of persisted status constants (e.g., STATUS_EXHAUSTED) or changing JSON field names without migration.
pitfalls.consequence: Existing auth.json entries will no longer be recognized or handled correctly, breaking selection and exhaustion logic.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect current field names and status strings; if changing, add migration code and update tests that assert persisted values.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering timestamp parsing semantics in _parse_absolute_timestamp or _normalize_error_context without handling all provider formats (ints, ms, ISO strings, custom messages).
pitfalls.consequence: Cooldown/reset times might be miscomputed, causing credentials to remain exhausted longer or to be prematurely reused.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure robust parsing and add tests covering numeric ms vs s and ISO strings; reuse helpers when adding provider-specific reset fields.

pitfalls.mistake: Failing to call release_lease on all child-agent failure paths or exceptions.
pitfalls.consequence: Leases will leak, causing the pool to appear saturated and preventing future leases/selection; tests expect release behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing code that acquires leases, maintain try/finally semantics to always call release_lease and run unit tests simulating child failure.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing seeding behavior to ignore _resolve_zai_base_url when creating seeded Z.AI/openrouter entries.
pitfalls.consequence: Seeded entries may carry incorrect base_url and fail on first-call runtime errors (observed in commit history).
pitfalls.prevention: When touching seeding logic (_seed_from_env), reuse provider base_url resolution helpers like _resolve_zai_base_url and ensure tests for seeded env keys still pass.

key_sections: PooledCredential: data model and to_dict/from_dict behaviour (how JSON maps to objects)
CredentialPool.__init__: sort/priority and strategy initialization, lease state
selection and rotation methods (select, mark_exhausted_and_rotate): core runtime behaviour
_refresh_entry and try_refresh_current: provider-specific refresh and synchronization
_seed_from_env and related seeding code: environment-based population and migration rules
skip_unless_needed: detailed regex and timestamp parsing helpers (only change if debugging reset parsing)
extra provider-specific sync helpers unless working on that provider

relevant_snippets.description: Writes temporary auth.json with two anthropic entries and asserts selection skips exhausted entry.
Round-robin and random strategy behavior checks including config.yaml seeding.

related_files.relationship: reads/writes auth.json and provides token import/refresh helpers used by this module
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes in hermes_cli.auth's read/write functions, token refresh helpers, or JSON schema require adapting pool persistence and sync logic.

related_files.relationship: contains provider-specific helpers used for syncing/refreshing claude_code tokens
related_files.reason_to_check: CredentialPool calls read_claude_code_credentials and refresh_anthropic_oauth_pure from this adapter; API changes affect refresh paths.

related_files.relationship: delegation tool resolves child credential pools and uses leasing API
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to acquire_lease/release_lease semantics must be verified against delegate path which relies on leases being released on all child exit paths.

insights.title: Use resolved Z.AI base URL when seeding credentials from environment
insights.problem: Seeded Z.AI credentials used incorrect default base_url causing first-call failures for keys that belong to non-default endpoints.
insights.root_cause: Seeding path didn't probe or respect per-key endpoint resolution.
insights.solution: Call _resolve_zai_base_url during _seed_from_env() to pick correct base_url for zai entries.
insights.lesson: Credential seeders must perform the same base_url resolution logic as runtime resolvers to avoid inconsistencies between seed-time and run-time behavior.

insights.title: Credential pool leasing API and thread-safety
insights.problem: Concurrent child agents could all use the same credential leading to rate-limits and exhaustion; there was no soft leasing API.
insights.root_cause: CredentialPool lacked lease counting, priority-aware selection, and thread-safe counters for concurrent usage.
insights.solution: Add DEFAULT_MAX_CONCURRENT_PER_CREDENTIAL constant, _active_leases dict, _max_concurrent, and methods acquire_lease(credential_id=None), release_lease(credential_id), active_lease_count(credential_id). Use internal locking to guard state. acquire_lease prefers least-leased credentials, respects soft cap, and sets _current_id.
insights.lesson_learned: Credential pools used across threads/processes require explicit concurrency control and leasing semantics; selection should prefer least-loaded entries and be thread-safe.

insights.title: Sync Codex pool entry from ~/.codex/auth.json on exhaustion
insights.problem: Codex OAuth refresh tokens rotate externally (Codex CLI). Pool entries became stale and exhausted without a recovery path.
insights.root_cause: Pool entries stored refresh_token; when external client refreshed token, Hermes pool retained old refresh_token and could not refresh, entering exhaustion cooldown.
insights.solution: Add _sync_codex_entry_from_cli() to compare pool entry refresh_token against ~/.codex/auth.json and replace/update pool entry with fresh tokens if they differ; call this sync when entry.last_status == STATUS_EXHAUSTED.
insights.lesson_learned: When external tools can rotate credentials, implement an import/sync path to pick up new tokens and recover exhausted entries.

tests/tools/test_delegate.py.short_description: Unit tests validating the delegation/subagent tool behavior and its interactions with subagent creation, tooling, observability, and credential sharing.
tests/tools/test_delegate.py.description:
This test module exercises the delegate subagent tool (tools/delegate_tool.py) using mocked AIAgent instances to avoid real LLM calls. It verifies schema, requirement checks, child system prompt generation, blocked toolset stripping, single-task and batch modes, depth limits, active child tracking, inheritance of run-time credentials and print/callback hooks, tool name preservation across subagent runs, saved tool name propagation to child before run, and rich observability data emitted for subagent runs (tool_trace, tokens, model, exit_reason). The tests mock run_agent.AIAgent and provider interactions to assert that delegate_task and internal helpers (_build_child_agent, _run_single_child, etc.) behave correctly across success and failure paths.

Why it exists: the delegation tool spawns child agents that must inherit certain runtime credentials, callbacks, and must not inadvertently leak process-global state (like resolved tool names). The tests codify expected behaviour for both normal operation and failure scenarios, including concurrency/leasing semantics and observability data formatting.

How it fits in the system: these tests validate high-level behavior of a tool that orchestrates subagents and uses the credential pool and model tools subsystems. They ensure delegate tooling integrates correctly with run_agent.AIAgent and with module-global variables like model_tools._last_resolved_tool_names. They also verify that delegate_task returns a JSON-serializable result with expected metadata consumed elsewhere (e.g., telemetry or developer UI).

key_constructs.name: _make_mock_parent
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that builds a MagicMock parent agent used by delegate_task tests.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used extensively to simulate parent agent fields that delegate_task reads; preserves expected attributes and lock objects.

key_constructs.name: TestDelegateRequirements
key_constructs.purpose: TestCase verifying basic delegate_task availability and schema.
key_constructs.reasoning: Sanity checks that the delegate task schema and availability gate remain stable.

key_constructs.name: TestChildSystemPrompt
key_constructs.purpose: Tests that _build_child_system_prompt composes goal and context correctly.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures the child system prompt includes expected sections and omits empty context.

key_constructs.name: TestStripBlockedTools
key_constructs.purpose: Validates that _strip_blocked_tools removes disallowed toolsets.
key_constructs.reasoning: Toolset masking affects child agent capabilities; tests assert correct filtering behavior.

key_constructs.name: TestDelegateTask
key_constructs.purpose: Suite covering variations of delegate_task (no parent, depth limit, single vs batch, error propagation, credential inheritance).
key_constructs.reasoning: Covers the bulk of delegate_task public behavior and interactions with AIAgent.

key_constructs.name: TestToolNamePreservation
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures model_tools._last_resolved_tool_names is preserved across child agent runs (success or failure).
key_constructs.reasoning: Protects against regressions that leak or clobber process-global resolved tool names which affect imports and sandbox generation.

key_constructs.name: TestDelegateObservability
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that _run_single_child returns a rich observability dict including tool_trace, token counts, model and exit_reason.
key_constructs.reasoning: Observability data is used by calling code; structure/fields must be stable and instrumented.

key_behaviors: verifies delegate_task validates input and returns JSON error messages when invalid
validates single-task and batched delegation paths
ensures parent/child runtime credentials and callbacks are correctly inherited
asserts global model_tools tool name preservation across child runs
checks tool_trace pairing between tool_calls and tool results

pitfalls.mistake: Editing delegate tool internals without preserving try/finally release semantics for leases or not restoring process-global state.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that assert lease release, active_children cleanup, or tool name restoration will fail; orphaned state may also affect other tests.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing delegation flow, run this test file and ensure mocks assert both success and failure code paths (tests include child failure scenarios).

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming real network or provider tokens in these tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests are designed to use MagicMock and do not provide real API keys; injecting real network calls will break test hermeticity.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep AIAgent and provider interactions mocked as in existing tests when editing.

key_sections: TestDelegateTask: covers most delegate_task public behaviors (single, batch, depth, credential inheritance)
TestToolNamePreservation: important when changing global tool name handling
TestDelegateObservability: validates tool_trace pairing and token/exit metadata
skip_unless_needed: small schema check test methods and trivial assertions

related_files.relationship: the module under test; contains delegate_task and helpers used by these tests
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing delegate internals, returned JSON shape, or lease handling requires updating these tests.

related_files.relationship: AIAgent class is patched in many tests; run_agent.AIAgent signature/kwargs are validated by tests
related_files.reason_to_check: If AIAgent constructor signature or expected attributes change, these tests will need adjustment.

related_files.relationship: module-global _last_resolved_tool_names is referenced and restored by delegate code.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to global tooling resolution or name storage affect preservation/restoration assertions.

insights.title: Delegate tool tests for credential pool sharing and leasing
insights.problem: New pool-sharing behavior and leasing requires test coverage.
insights.root_cause: Tools.delegate_tool now resolves child credential pools and uses leases when running child agents.
insights.solution: Add TestChildCredentialPoolResolution and TestChildCredentialLeasing classes to validate sharing, loading, leasing, and proper release on failure.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing orchestration logic to use shared resources, provide tests for both success and failure paths to ensure leases are always released.

tests/test_credential_pool.py.short_description: Unit tests for credential pool selection, exhaustion cooldowns, seeding, migration and refresh behavior.
tests/test_credential_pool.py.description:
This test module verifies the credential pool's selection and rotation policies, exhaustion reset logic, seeding from environment variables, migration of legacy provider state (nous), removal of stale seeded entries, refresh behaviour for OAuth-backed providers, and persistence of state changes back to auth.json. It constructs temporary HERMES_HOME directories, writes sample auth.json payloads, sets or unsets environment variables to simulate seeded keys, and then loads pools via load_pool to observe runtime selection/rotation, mark_exhausted_and_rotate side-effects, and try_refresh_current behaviour.

Why it exists: to ensure the credential pool honors cooldowns and reset timestamps, persists exhausted flags, rotates priorities per strategy, cleans up stale seeded entries, and performs provider-specific migration (e.g., nous -> device_code seeding). These tests protect against regressions in complex provider interactions and persistence semantics.

How it fits: They are the primary unit tests for agent/credential_pool.py and exercise important edge cases used by runtime agent and delegation code. They are written to run without external network or provider credentials by stubbing provider refresh functions or manipulating environment variables and files. Many tests are used by CI to ensure the auth.json migration/seeding and leasing semantics remain stable.

key_constructs.name: _write_auth_store
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to write a temporary auth.json in a test HERMES_HOME folder.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to set up diverse pool states for each test scenario.

key_constructs.name: test_fill_first_selection_skips_recently_exhausted_entry
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts selection skips a recently exhausted credential in fill_first strategy.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates core exhaustion cooldown selection behaviour.

key_constructs.name: test_select_clears_expired_exhaustion
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures an exhausted credential whose TTL has elapsed is cleared and becomes usable again.
key_constructs.reasoning: Checks that exhaustion respects TTL computation and expiry.

key_constructs.name: test_round_robin_strategy_rotates_priorities
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies round_robin strategy rotates selection across calls and persists priority changes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests strategy-driven reordering/persistence between load_pool invocations.

key_constructs.name: test_random_strategy_uses_random_choice
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts random strategy delegates to random.choice (mocked) and returns chosen entry.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures provider-configured random strategy is honored.

key_constructs.name: test_try_refresh_current_updates_only_current_entry
key_constructs.purpose: Stubs refresh call and asserts only the selected/current credential is updated.
key_constructs.reasoning: Guards against accidentally refreshing unrelated entries.

key_constructs.name: test_load_pool_seeds_env_api_key
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures load_pool will seed credential from environment variables (e.g., OPENROUTER_API_KEY).
key_constructs.reasoning: Verifies runtime seeding behavior for env API keys.

key_behaviors: writes temporary auth.json and asserts load_pool produces expected in-memory pool
verifies exhaustion TTL logic and explicit reset timestamps
validates strategy-driven selection and persistence
tests environment seeding and stale seeded entry removal
mocks provider refresh helpers to assert try_refresh_current updates only current entry

pitfalls.mistake: Running tests without setting HERMES_HOME to a tmp_path.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will read/write real user auth.json and may corrupt state or fail unpredictably.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure monkeypatch.setenv('HERMES_HOME', ...) is used (tests do this) or run tests in isolated environment.

pitfalls.mistake: Allowing tests to contact real provider refresh endpoints (not using monkeypatch/mock).
pitfalls.consequence: Tests become flaky and require network/API keys; CI blanks provider env keys to avoid that.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep provider refresh functions stubbed as in tests (monkeypatch.setattr) and ensure CI environment variables remain unset.

key_sections: _write_auth_store: shows expected auth.json schema for tests
tests that seed env vars: illustrate how load_pool should seed from environment
tests that monkeypatch provider refresh: demonstrate expected refresh semantics
skip_unless_needed: tests that assert very specific persisted JSON layout beyond the obvious fields

related_files.relationship: under test; load_pool and pool methods are invoked by these tests
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to API, persistence schema, or seeding behavior require updating these tests.

related_files.relationship: read_credential_pool and write_credential_pool are used to persist state in auth.json
related_files.reason_to_check: If hermes_cli/auth.py changes its read/write format, tests will need to be updated.

insights.title: Tests for leasing behavior
insights.problem: Leasing behavior and counters needed coverage to prevent regressions.
insights.root_cause: New acquire_lease/release_lease API needed unit tests.
insights.solution: Add tests that verify lease selection prefers unleased entries and that release decrements counters.
insights.lesson_learned: Add tests for concurrent resource allocation primitives when introducing new concurrency APIs.

hermes_cli/commands.py.short_description: Central registry of slash commands, gateway helpers and CLI autocomplete/suggestion logic
hermes_cli/commands.py.description:
This module is the single source of truth for all slash commands used by the Hermes CLI and gateway platforms. It defines the CommandDef dataclass and COMMAND_REGISTRY (the list of built-in commands). From that registry it derives several lookup structures (COMMANDS, COMMANDS_BY_CATEGORY, SUBCOMMANDS, GATEWAY_KNOWN_COMMANDS) that are consumed by the CLI help, prompt autocompletion, platform integrations (Telegram, Slack, Discord) and gateway dispatch logic. The module also exports functions used by gateway/platform code to produce platform-specific command lists (telegram_bot_commands, telegram_menu_commands, slack_subcommand_map, discord_skill_commands) and enforces platform constraints (name length, allowed characters, truncation and collision avoidance).

Design decisions visible in the file: command metadata is immutable (frozen dataclass) and centrally declared so many consumers derive UI and platform behavior from the same source; lookups are built once at import-time but can be rebuilt via rebuild_lookups() to support plugin registration; platform-specific sanitization and clamping logic is centralized in helpers (_sanitize_telegram_name, _clamp_command_names) to avoid duplicate logic across callers. The module also contains interactive-autocomplete pieces (SlashCommandCompleter and SlashCommandAutoSuggest) implemented against prompt_toolkit APIs so the shell can provide completions and ghost-text suggestions for slash commands and subcommands.

Because several values (GATEWAY_KNOWN_COMMANDS, COMMANDS, SUBCOMMANDS) are derived at import-time and used across services (gateway, platform adapters), changes to COMMAND_REGISTRY must preserve the rebuild_lookups() behavior and semantics: plugin commands are intended to be appended by register_plugin_command() which calls rebuild_lookups() and updates all derived structures. Gateway-related helpers consider a config gate (gateway_config_gate) that can make a cli_only command available to the gateway only when a dotpath in config.yaml is truthy; the module reads a hermes_home-based config.yaml in a safe way (returns empty set on errors) to compute open gates for help/menu generation so tests and runtime behavior can diverge depending on the HERMES_HOME environment variable and config.yaml contents.

key_constructs.name: CommandDef
key_constructs.purpose: Immutable dataclass describing a single slash command's metadata (name, description, category, aliases, args_hint, subcommands, visibility flags, and optional gateway config gate).
key_constructs.reasoning: CommandDef centralizes structured metadata so multiple consumers (CLI help, gateway, platform menus, autocomplete) can rely on consistent, typed attributes rather than ad-hoc parsing of strings.

key_constructs.name: COMMAND_REGISTRY
key_constructs.purpose: List of built-in CommandDef entries that defines every canonical built-in slash command.
key_constructs.reasoning: This list is the authoritative source for commands and should be treated as the canonical dataset when adding/removing commands; derived lookups are built from it.

key_constructs.name: _build_command_lookup
key_constructs.purpose: Create a mapping from every canonical name and alias (without leading slash) to its CommandDef.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to establish fast resolution of name or alias to the canonical CommandDef; used when resolving user input and when rebuilding lookups.

key_constructs.name: resolve_command
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve a string (with or without leading slash, case-insensitive) to a CommandDef or None.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by CLI and gateway dispatch to map incoming textual commands to the metadata and ensure alias handling is uniform.

key_constructs.name: register_plugin_command
key_constructs.purpose: Append a plugin-provided CommandDef to COMMAND_REGISTRY and refresh derived lookups.
key_constructs.reasoning: Plugins call this to expose extra slash commands; it relies on rebuild_lookups() to make sure all derived dicts and platform lists are updated atomically.

key_constructs.name: rebuild_lookups
key_constructs.purpose: Recompute derived dictionaries and sets (COMMANDS, COMMANDS_BY_CATEGORY, SUBCOMMANDS, GATEWAY_KNOWN_COMMANDS, and the internal command lookup).
key_constructs.reasoning: Because plugin registration and runtime config gating can change what is visible, this function centralizes the logic for constructing all consumer-facing structures from COMMAND_REGISTRY.

key_constructs.name: _sanitize_telegram_name
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize a raw command/skill/plugin name into a Telegram-valid command name (lowercase, underscores, allowed chars, collapse underscores, trim).
key_constructs.reasoning: Telegram requires a restricted name character set; centralizing sanitization prevents runtime errors when calling Telegram APIs and ensures consistent menus across code paths.

key_constructs.name: _clamp_command_names
key_constructs.purpose: Enforce platform command name length limit (_CMD_NAME_LIMIT) while avoiding collisions by truncating and appending a digit if needed (dropping entries if all digit suffixes collide).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by platform menu generation to safely prepare a batch of command names that conform to third-party API length limits and prevent accidental overwrites/collisions.

key_constructs.name: _collect_gateway_skill_entries
key_constructs.purpose: Collect plugin and skill entries for gateway platforms, apply per-platform name sanitization and description truncation, and enforce a max slot cap (plugin commands first, then built-in skills).
key_constructs.reasoning: Abstracts the shared logic of preparing skill/plugin command entries for different platforms (Telegram/Discord/etc.) while allowing a sanitize_name hook so platform-specific naming rules are applied.

key_constructs.name: gateway_help_lines
key_constructs.purpose: Return a list of human-readable help lines for gateway surfaces, honoring cli_only and gateway_config_gate visibility rules.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by gateway help endpoints and messaging UIs; reads config-gate info via _resolve_config_gates() so help output can change based on config.yaml at runtime or in tests.

key_constructs.name: telegram_bot_commands
key_constructs.purpose: Produce (name, description) pairs suitable for Telegram setMyCommands API, applying sanitization and excluding entries that are not gateway-available.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates Telegram-specific formatting, ensures names obey Telegram constraints, and integrates config-gated visibility so the gateway can set menu commands safely.

key_constructs.name: SlashCommandCompleter
key_constructs.purpose: prompt_toolkit Completer implementation that provides slash-command and subcommand completions from the registry (and optional skill provider), used by the interactive CLI.
key_constructs.reasoning: Allows interactive tab completion in the CLI driven by the canonical COMMAND_REGISTRY and optional dynamic skill providers (plugin/hub skills).

key_constructs.name: SlashCommandAutoSuggest
key_constructs.purpose: prompt_toolkit AutoSuggest implementation that offers ghost-text command suggestions (single best completion) to the user.
key_constructs.reasoning: Improves UX by providing ghost-text suggestions from the same registry-based completion logic.

key_constructs.name: slack_subcommand_map
key_constructs.purpose: Return mapping of compact names to slash-prefixed commands for Slack integration, honoring cli_only and config-gate visibility.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides the bridge used by Slack adapter to translate its subcommand UI into Hermes slash commands.

key_constructs.name: discord_skill_commands
key_constructs.purpose: Prepare entries suitable for Discord registration by wrapping _collect_gateway_skill_entries with Discord-specific sanitization/limits.
key_constructs.reasoning: Facilitates reuse of the shared collection logic while exposing a small platform wrapper for Discord-specific growth and testing.

key_constructs.name: GATEWAY_KNOWN_COMMANDS
key_constructs.purpose: Frozenset of command names + aliases recognized by the gateway (includes config-gated commands so gateway can dispatch and let handlers decide).
key_constructs.reasoning: Snapshot of names the gateway should accept; used by gateway code to quickly validate or route incoming platform requests.

key_behaviors: exposes canonical slash command metadata to CLI and gateway
resolves names and aliases to canonical commands
generates platform-specific command menus (Telegram, Slack, Discord)
supplies prompt_toolkit completions and ghost-text suggestions
allows plugin registration to add commands at runtime

pitfalls.mistake: Modify COMMAND_REGISTRY entries at runtime without calling rebuild_lookups() or using register_plugin_command().
pitfalls.consequence: Derived lookups (COMMANDS, SUBCOMMANDS, GATEWAY_KNOWN_COMMANDS and the internal lookup) will be stale and UI/gateway code will not reflect the changes.
pitfalls.prevention: Always use register_plugin_command() for plugin additions or call rebuild_lookups() after changing COMMAND_REGISTRY.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume GATEWAY_KNOWN_COMMANDS will reflect config.yaml changes without rebuilding or re-importing.
pitfalls.consequence: Gateway visibility decisions (help lines, menus) may not honor current configuration; tests that monkeypatch HERMES_HOME must rely on functions that read config (gateway_help_lines, telegram_bot_commands) rather than the static set.
pitfalls.prevention: Use gateway_help_lines() or call functions that compute gates (they call _resolve_config_gates()) — avoid depending on the frozen set for dynamic visibility checks.

pitfalls.mistake: Bypass _sanitize_telegram_name when generating Telegram names (e.g., using raw names or simple replace operations).
pitfalls.consequence: Invalid names may be produced (illegal chars, double underscores, leading/trailing underscores) which can cause Telegram API failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Call _sanitize_telegram_name for any name destined for Telegram and respect empty-string return (skip entry when sanitized to empty).

pitfalls.mistake: Altering _CMD_NAME_LIMIT or _clamp_command_names semantics without updating tests and platform wrappers.
pitfalls.consequence: Name truncation collisions or dropped entries on platforms like Discord/Telegram; menus may silently omit commands.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing limits or collision logic, update all consumers and run the command registration/menu tests.

key_sections: COMMAND_REGISTRY: canonical list of commands and the metadata shape
rebuild_lookups: how derived structures are constructed and refreshed
_sanitize_telegram_name: Telegram-specific name rules (important for platform code)
_collect_gateway_skill_entries: shared skill/plugin to-platform transformation and cap logic
SlashCommandCompleter and SlashCommandAutoSuggest: prompt_toolkit integration for CLI
skip_unless_needed: low-level regex constants and trivial helpers (e.g. _TG_INVALID_CHARS) used only by sanitization code

relevant_snippets.description: Registry and basic resolve_command tests which exercise COMMAND_REGISTRY and resolve_command behavior.
Gateway help, Telegram/Slack menu generation and config-gate tests that exercise _resolve_config_gates and gateway helpers.

related_files.relationship: gateway uses these lists/menus to register and dispatch platform commands and to bridge config values into environment
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing the layout or visibility of commands or renaming command keys, ensure gateway adapters and menu registration calls are updated.

related_files.relationship: plugin manager exposes plugin command registrations that the commands module consumes; register_plugin_command is used by plugins
related_files.reason_to_check: Plugin commands require registration and name sanitization; changes to plugin command shape or registration lifecycle may require updates in both files.

related_files.relationship: get_hermes_home used by _resolve_config_gates to find config.yaml which gates some command visibility
related_files.reason_to_check: When altering config gating behavior or HERMES_HOME resolution semantics, adjust config gate reading logic accordingly.

data_constants_to_check: gateway_config_gate dotpath strings in CommandDef entries

insights.title: Centralize gateway skill/plugin collection and rename Telegram-specific clamps
insights.problem: Telegram-only name clamping and skill collection duplicated logic; discord and telegram had different limits and sanitization.
insights.root_cause: Platform-specific implementations duplicated plugin/skill collection and cap logic.
insights.solution: Refactored to _collect_gateway_skill_entries (shared plugin + skill collection, per-platform filtering, name sanitization, description truncation, cap enforcement), renamed _TG_NAME_LIMIT to _CMD_NAME_LIMIT and _clamp_telegram_names to _clamp_command_names with backward-compatible aliases, added discord_skill_commands wrapper.
insights.lesson_learned: Abstract out shared platform-agnostic business logic (collection, filtering, capping) and expose small platform-specific wrappers for sanitization to reduce duplication and inconsistencies.

insights.title: Centralize Telegram command name sanitization
insights.problem: Multiple code paths produced Telegram command names by ad-hoc replace operations (replace '-' with '_') and sometimes left invalid characters, causing Telegram API errors.
insights.root_cause: Sanitization was inconsistent across code paths (core commands, plugin commands, skill commands).
insights.solution: Add _sanitize_telegram_name() to normalize names (lowercase → hyphens→underscores → strip invalid chars → collapse underscores → trim), and use it in telegram_bot_commands, telegram_menu_commands and other sites. Skip empty sanitized names.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize platform-specific formatting constraints into a single helper and use it everywhere. Tests should assert platform format rules.

insights.title: Clamp/limit and description trimming for setMyCommands
insights.problem: Telegram setMyCommands has undocumented limits (description length and number of commands) that could make requests fail or be rejected.
insights.root_cause: Descriptions and counts were not consistently truncated/clamped before sending.
insights.solution: Keep descriptions short before calling setMyCommands and clamp names to known limit _TG_NAME_LIMIT. Central helper used to apply consistent truncation/rules.
insights.lesson_learned: When integrating with third-party APIs, document and enforce their limits centrally to avoid surprising runtime failures.

tests/hermes_cli/test_commands.py.short_description: Unit tests for the central command registry, gateway helpers, and CLI autocomplete
tests/hermes_cli/test_commands.py.description:
This test module validates the behavior and invariants of hermes_cli/commands.py. It covers the static registry integrity (no duplicate canonical names, categories validity, mutual exclusivity of cli_only/gateway_only), name resolution and aliasing, derived dictionary construction (COMMANDS, COMMANDS_BY_CATEGORY), gateway helper outputs (gateway_help_lines, telegram_bot_commands, slack_subcommand_map), and prompt_toolkit integration (SlashCommandCompleter, SlashCommandAutoSuggest). The tests exercise config-gating behavior by writing temporary config.yaml files and monkeypatching HERMES_HOME so the module's _resolve_config_gates logic can be validated in both on/off scenarios.

The test suite not only checks positive cases (expected entries present) but also negative cases (gateway-only excluded from COMMANDS, CLI-only commands omitted from gateway menus when gate is off). It also validates platform constraints (Telegram name character sets and hyphen removal), description truncation for skill commands, collision/cap logic via the clamping helpers, and resilience to broken skill providers (providers throwing exceptions should not crash autocompletion). These tests act as a behavior contract for the commands module — many functions and renaming/refactoring efforts in the history have relied on these tests to prevent regressions.

key_constructs.name: TestCommandRegistry
key_constructs.purpose: Group of tests that validate static COMMAND_REGISTRY integrity (non-empty, types, unique canonical names, categories, exclusive flags).
key_constructs.reasoning: Altering COMMAND_REGISTRY or CommandDef shape should preserve these invariants; these tests act as guardrails.

key_constructs.name: TestResolveCommand
key_constructs.purpose: Tests for resolve_command behavior: canonical resolves, aliases map to canonical, leading slash handling, unknown inputs returning None.
key_constructs.reasoning: resolve_command is used by many runtime paths; tests ensure alias semantics and input normalization remain stable.

key_constructs.name: TestGatewayConfigGate
key_constructs.purpose: Tests the gateway_config_gate mechanism by writing temporary config.yaml and monkeypatching HERMES_HOME to trigger inclusion/exclusion of commands from gateway lists.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures commands with gateway_config_gate appear only when the target config value is truthy; important for runtime gating of some CLI-only commands into gateway surfaces.

key_constructs.name: TestSlashCommandCompleter
key_constructs.purpose: Tests the SlashCommandCompleter behavior including prefix completion, exact-match trailing space, skill provider integration, and resilience to provider exceptions.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates prompt_toolkit completer UX requirements and the contract with optional dynamic skill providers.

key_constructs.name: TestTelegramBotCommands
key_constructs.purpose: Validates that telegram_bot_commands returns properly sanitized names and excludes CLI-only commands without config gates.
key_constructs.reasoning: Catches platform-compatibility regressions due to name sanitization and gating.

key_behaviors: verifies COMMAND_REGISTRY invariants
validates alias and canonical name resolution
checks platform menu generation and sanitization
exercises CLI completer and autosuggest behavior
tests config-gated command visibility

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying commands.py behavior or constants but not updating these tests' expectations (e.g., category names, inclusion/exclusion semantics).
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will fail, but diagnosing failures requires reconciling registry changes with test assertions rather than obvious runtime exceptions.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing CommandDef entries or gating behavior, update tests to match new intended semantics; run the test suite frequently.

pitfalls.mistake: Relying on global import-time computed values in commands.py for dynamic behavior in tests (e.g., assuming GATEWAY_KNOWN_COMMANDS will reflect a monkeypatched HERMES_HOME).
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that monkeypatch HERMES_HOME should call functions that recompute visibility (gateway_help_lines, telegram_bot_commands) — otherwise stale snapshots may be asserted against.
pitfalls.prevention: The tests already use gateway helper functions and tmp_path/monkeypatch fixtures; follow the same pattern when adding new tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming the skill commands provider is synchronous and always returns tidy data.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests mock providers to throw exceptions or return missing descriptions to ensure the completer swallows errors; new code must preserve that resilience.
pitfalls.prevention: When integrating skill providers, ensure exceptions are handled and missing metadata fallbacks are present.

reading_guide.start_here: the top-level Test* classes (TestCommandRegistry, TestResolveCommand, TestGatewayConfigGate)
key_sections: Tests validating COMMAND_REGISTRY integrity (early in file)
Gateway config gate tests (tmp_path/monkeypatch usage)
Autocomplete tests (SlashCommandCompleter provider scenarios)
skip_unless_needed: long lists of assertions for display/meta text formatting if you only care about registry invariants

relevant_snippets.description: Registry and canonical/alias resolution tests that exercise fundamental behavior of commands.py.
Gateway config gate and Telegram/Slack/Discord menu tests that validate platform generation logic and gating behavior.

related_files.relationship: This test file directly validates behavior implemented in hermes_cli/commands.py
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change in commands.py (registry shape, sanitization, clamping, completer behavior) requires updating this test file to reflect new behavior.

data_constants_to_check: gateway_config_gate dotpaths used in CommandDef entries

insights.title: Add tests for shared skill-to-command collection and Discord registration constraints
insights.problem: Refactor required updating and expanding tests to validate cross-platform command collection, sanitization, cap enforcement and backward-compat aliases.
insights.root_cause: Changing refactor touching public utility behavior needs verifying.
insights.solution: Added tests for Discord command entries, hyphen preservation, cap enforcement, per-platform disabled skills exclusion, reserved-name behavior, description truncation, and name-length enforcement.
insights.lesson_learned: When refactoring cross-platform features, expand unit tests to cover both platforms and edge cases (disabled skills, caps, truncation).

insights.title: Expand tests for Telegram name sanitization and menu behavior
insights.problem: Telegram name constraints were not previously asserted across the menu generation code paths.
insights.root_cause: Lack of unit tests for cross-system sanitization.
insights.solution: Add comprehensive tests for _sanitize_telegram_name, menu generation behavior (exclude empty sanitized names), and that generated menu names match Telegram's allowed character set.
