tools/delegate_tool.py.short_description: Tool for spawning focused subagents (child AIAgent instances) with isolated context, restricted tools, and credential leasing.
tools/delegate_tool.py.description:
This module implements the delegation/subagent architecture used by the agent to run isolated child AIAgent instances for delegated tasks. It exists so complex or multi-part user goals can be split into one or more focused subagents that run with their own conversation, tool restrictions, iteration budgets, and (when available) leased credentials from a shared pool. The parent agent blocks until children complete and only receives a concise summary per child; children do not inherit or expose the parent's conversation history or intermediate tool calls.

Design highlights: children are constructed on the main thread then executed on worker threads (ThreadPoolExecutor). The module enforces a delegation depth limit, strips blocked toolsets, and builds focused ephemeral system prompts that include an explicit workspace hint (when resolvable) to avoid teaching subagents to assume container paths like /workspace. It also includes logic to share a credential pool with children when provider matches (or to resolve a different provider's pool), acquire soft leases per child to avoid saturating a single key, and guarantee lease release in finally blocks. The code manipulates a global model_tools state during child construction/execution and therefore takes care to restore that state afterwards. The module also constructs callbacks to relay child tool usage to the parent UI (CLI spinner or gateway progress callback) and provides a flush mechanism to send any remaining batched messages when a child finishes.

How it fits into the system: delegate_tool is invoked as a tool by the parent AIAgent when the parent decides to split work. It references run_agent.AIAgent to instantiate children, model_tools for toolset resolution/global last-resolved names, agent.display for CLI emojis, and an internal/shared credential pool implementation. Tests mock AIAgent and credential pools to validate that children are created correctly, toolsets are stripped, workspace hints are injected, and credential leases are acquired and released.

key_constructs.name: DELEGATE_BLOCKED_TOOLS
key_constructs.purpose: Set of tool names that must never be available to child agents (e.g., delegation, memory writes, cross-platform side effects, code execution).
key_constructs.reasoning: This creates a whitelist/blacklist boundary so subagents cannot perform dangerous or uncontrolled actions (recursive delegation, memory writes, sending messages, executing code). Editors must preserve this set and its semantics to maintain safety/isolation guarantees.

key_constructs.name: MAX_CONCURRENT_CHILDREN
key_constructs.purpose: Maximum number of concurrent child agents to spawn in batch mode.
key_constructs.reasoning: Controls parallelism and interacts with credential leasing and resource usage tests; changing it affects concurrency behavior and tests that assert capping.

key_constructs.name: MAX_DEPTH
key_constructs.purpose: Maximum allowed delegation depth (parent=0, child=1).
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents recursive or runaway delegation. Code enforces this to reject attempts to create grandchildren; tests verify depth-limiting behavior.

key_constructs.name: DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS
key_constructs.purpose: Default per-subagent iteration budget when not otherwise specified.
key_constructs.reasoning: Determines how many reasoning iterations a child may perform; interacts with parent iteration budgets and user-configurable delegation settings.

key_constructs.name: DEFAULT_TOOLSETS
key_constructs.purpose: Default effective toolsets used when no parent/toolset info is present.
key_constructs.reasoning: Fallback to reasonable tool capabilities (terminal, file, web) for a child when parent capability information is missing.

key_constructs.name: check_delegate_requirements
key_constructs.purpose: Return whether delegation is available in the current environment.
key_constructs.reasoning: Currently implemented to always return True; callers use it to gate UI/tool availability. Edits should preserve its idempotent contract or update callers.

key_constructs.name: _build_child_system_prompt
key_constructs.purpose: Construct an ephemeral, focused system prompt for a subagent (goal, optional context, optional workspace path, and clear completion instructions).
key_constructs.reasoning: The prompt encodes safety guidance (don't assume /workspace), workspace hint usage, and the required summary format. Tests validate that context is included only when non-empty and that the goal is embedded; changes here affect child behavior and test expectations.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_workspace_hint
key_constructs.purpose: Best-effort resolution of a concrete absolute working directory from environment and parent_agent attributes to include in child prompts.
key_constructs.reasoning: Avoids teaching subagents fake container paths by only returning a concrete absolute directory that exists. It checks several parent_agent attributes and TERMINAL_CWD. Editors must preserve the conservative heuristic (only return valid absolute directories) to avoid introducing incorrect workspace instructions to children.

key_constructs.name: _strip_blocked_tools
key_constructs.purpose: Filter out toolset names that correspond to blocked capabilities so children don't receive restricted capabilities.
key_constructs.reasoning: Maps a list of requested toolsets to the reduced set that excludes forbidden toolset names. Tests assert exact output for inputs containing blocked names, so alterations can break test expectations and security constraints.

key_constructs.name: _build_child_progress_callback
key_constructs.purpose: Create an optional callback that relays child tool usage/progress to the parent's CLI spinner or gateway callback, including batching and flush semantics.
key_constructs.reasoning: Returns None if no display mechanisms exist; otherwise returns a function that handles events like tool.started, tool.completed, _thinking, and exposes a _flush method to send batched events. Important for UI responsiveness and tests that assert relaying behavior.

key_constructs.name: _build_child_agent
key_constructs.purpose: Construct an AIAgent instance configured as a child subagent, applying toolset filtering, credential overrides, workspace hints, callbacks, and delegation metadata.
key_constructs.reasoning: Central factory that derives child settings from the parent (enabled toolsets, credentials, model, acp overrides). It must be called on the main thread for thread-safety and mutates/reads global state like model_tools resolution. Tests use this to verify toolset inheritance and prompt building; edits must respect attribute mapping and locking/registration into parent's active children list.

key_constructs.name: _run_single_child
key_constructs.purpose: Execute a pre-built child agent in a worker thread, capture its result summary, tool trace, resource usage, and ensure credential leases are released and global tool name state is restored.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function performs the call to child.run_conversation(), builds structured result dictionaries, converts message traces into tool_trace entries, handles exceptions, and contains important finally-block logic that restores model_tools._last_resolved_tool_names and releases credential leases. Tests patch this function to verify batch execution and leasing behavior; edits must preserve resource cleanup and global state restoration.

key_constructs.name: DELEGATE_TASK_SCHEMA
key_constructs.purpose: JSON-schema-like tool parameter spec used by the tool registry to validate delegate_task inputs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests assert schema properties and names; designer code relies on this shape to validate incoming tool calls (goal, tasks, context, toolsets, max_iterations).

key_constructs.name: delegate_task
key_constructs.purpose: Public tool entrypoint that validates inputs, enforces depth limits, decides between single-task and batch modes, spawns child agents, aggregates results, and returns a JSON-encoded summary to the parent.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the function the parent AIAgent will call to perform delegation. It enforces error cases (missing parent_agent, depth limit, empty goal), uses ThreadPoolExecutor with MAX_CONCURRENT_CHILDREN for batch mode, and aggregates per-child metadata into the final result. Tests exercise the behavioural branches (no parent, depth limit, single vs batch, batching capped).

key_constructs.name: _resolve_child_credential_pool
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve an appropriate credential pool for a child agent given provider info and parent pools (shares pool when appropriate or loads a different pool).
key_constructs.reasoning: This function underpins credential leasing semantics: children acquire soft leases from the returned pool. Tests assert same-provider sharing vs different-provider behavior, so preserving logic is key to test stability and runtime correctness.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_delegation_credentials
key_constructs.purpose: Interpret delegation configuration entries that specify provider/model overrides, base URLs, or API keys for child agents.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used while parsing delegation config passed into delegate_task; tests reference it to ensure override semantics. Edits here change how credentials propagate to children and can affect security boundaries.

key_behaviors: spawns isolated child AIAgent instances for delegated tasks
constructs focused ephemeral system prompts with workspace hints
strips blocked toolsets to enforce child safety
shares and leases credentials from a pool to avoid key saturation
aggregates child summaries and tool traces to return to parent

pitfalls.mistake: Assume a workspace path without verifying it (e.g., hardcode /workspace).
pitfalls.consequence: Subagents may perform incorrect or destructive local file operations; tests and commit history explicitly guard against this.
pitfalls.prevention: Only include absolute, existing directories from the parent's environment (preserve the conservative behavior of _resolve_workspace_hint).

pitfalls.mistake: Forget to release a leased credential in all exit paths.
pitfalls.consequence: Leases remain held, causing credential starvation and unexpected rate-limiting for other subagents.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the finally block in _run_single_child that releases leases even on exceptions.

pitfalls.mistake: Neglect to restore the global model_tools._last_resolved_tool_names after child execution.
pitfalls.consequence: Subsequent parent operations or other threads can observe the child's tool resolution state, causing incorrect tool-to-toolset mappings or unexpected behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the existing saved/restore logic in the finally block that writes back model_tools._last_resolved_tool_names from child._delegate_saved_tool_names.

pitfalls.mistake: Allow children to gain toolsets the parent did not have (e.g., via unfiltered toolsets argument).
pitfalls.consequence: Subagents could perform capabilities the parent shouldn't allow, violating isolation and security assumptions.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the intersection logic in _build_child_agent and the filtering by _strip_blocked_tools to only allow subsets of the parent's capabilities.

pitfalls.mistake: Permit recursion beyond configured MAX_DEPTH.
pitfalls.consequence: Grandchildren might be spawned leading to uncontrolled resource usage and harder-to-track side effects.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve and enforce the _delegate_depth increment on child creation and the MAX_DEPTH checks in delegate_task.

key_sections: _build_child_agent: constructs the child AIAgent and maps parent attributes (toolsets, credentials, prompts)
_run_single_child: executes the child, builds result dicts, handles tool traces & cleanup
_build_child_progress_callback: how progress/events are relayed to parent UI (spinner/gateway) and batching behavior
_resolve_workspace_hint: heuristic to include a safe workspace path in child prompts
skip_unless_needed: detailed parsing of tool trace building (can be revisited only if modifying message/trace formats)
lower-level UI formatting (emoji/display) unless changing CLI behavior

relevant_snippets.description: Unit tests for the basic schema, prompt building, and strip-blocked-tools behavior; useful to validate prompt/context and toolset filtering.
Tests for delegate_task behavior: parent presence, depth limit, single vs batch execution, and capping of concurrent children.
Tests focusing on credential pool resolution and leasing semantics (acquire/release), and ensuring failures release leases.

related_files.relationship: Creates AIAgent instances; delegate_tool imports and constructs AIAgent from run_agent.AIAgent.
related_files.reason_to_check: If AIAgent constructor signature or public attributes change (enabled_toolsets, api_key, base_url, acp args, callbacks), delegate_tool._build_child_agent must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: Used to infer toolsets from tool names and contains the global _last_resolved_tool_names state mutated during child construction/execution.
related_files.reason_to_check: delegate_tool reads and restores model_tools._last_resolved_tool_names; changes to model_tools' API or global names require corresponding updates.

related_files.relationship: Provides CLI emojis and is referenced by _build_child_progress_callback to add emoji to spinner lines.
related_files.reason_to_check: If display.get_tool_emoji changes signature or behavior, child progress messages may break or format incorrectly.

related_files.relationship: Implied provider credential pool used for leasing credentials to child agents.
related_files.reason_to_check: delegate_tool expects methods like acquire_lease(), release_lease(), current(); changes in the pool API will break leasing logic and tests for lease acquire/release.

insights.title: Pass workspace hints to child agents and avoid assuming /workspace paths
insights.problem: Child agents assumed container-like workspace paths (e.g., /workspace/...), possibly causing incorrect file operations when parent agent's working dir differed.
insights.root_cause: Parent working directory hints were not propagated to child agent system prompts.
insights.solution: Add _resolve_workspace_hint() to detect parent's working directory from multiple heuristics (TERMINAL_CWD, parent_agent attributes) and inject WORKSPACE PATH into child system prompts via _build_child_system_prompt workspace_path param; add explicit rule in prompt instructing not to assume /workspace.
insights.lesson_learned: Child agents need concrete environment hints to avoid issuing destructive/incorrect local path operations; don't embed container-specific defaults into prompts.

insights.title: Share credential pools with subagents and per-task leasing
insights.problem: Subagents were pinned to fixed credentials or unaware of parent's credential pool, preventing rotation and leading to rate-limit hot-spotting and exhaustion.
insights.root_cause: No mechanism to share parent's credential pool with child agents and no leasing to distribute concurrent children across credentials.
insights.solution: Introduce _resolve_child_credential_pool() to share parent's pool when provider matches or load the child's provider pool when different. Update _build_child_agent to assign the resolved pool. Acquire soft leases via pool.acquire_lease() in _run_single_child before swapping credentials and release leases in finally. Ensure leases are released on failure.
insights.lesson_learned: When spawning parallel agents that make API calls, share credential pools and lease credentials to avoid saturating a single key; always release leases in finally blocks.

hermes_cli/profiles.py.short_description: Manage named Hermes profiles (isolated HERMES_HOME directories), aliases, cloning, and exports
hermes_cli/profiles.py.description:
This module implements management of multiple isolated Hermes instances (profiles). Each profile is a self-contained HERMES_HOME directory holding config.yaml, .env, memories, sessions, skills, gateway state, logs, etc. The module provides functionality to list existing profiles (including the legacy default ~/.hermes), create new profiles (empty or cloned from an existing profile), seed bundled skills into a profile via a subprocess, manage wrapper scripts (aliases in ~/.local/bin), and check runtime state such as whether a gateway is running for a profile.

Design-wise the module treats the legacy default (~/.hermes) specially: it's reported as the "default" profile and exported profile archives exclude a long list of infrastructure files that live in the default location. The implementation mixes filesystem operations (Path), subprocess invocations (for seeding skills and checking PATH collision with which), and light config parsing (yaml.safe_load when available) to reveal model/provider settings from a profile's config. Several constants at module top capture the list of directories to bootstrap per profile, files/subdirectory files that should be copied when cloning, and files to strip when doing a full clone_all. The module is written defensively for IO errors and missing optional deps (yaml import is inside try/except). It also includes name-validation logic and reserved-name/command collision checks to avoid creating aliases that would shadow shell commands or hermes subcommands.

key_constructs.name: _PROFILE_ID_RE
key_constructs.purpose: Regex pattern used to validate profile identifiers.
key_constructs.reasoning: Any code creating or resolving profile names must respect this constraint to avoid invalid directory names and ensure list_profiles and get_profile_dir behave predictably.

key_constructs.name: _PROFILE_DIRS
key_constructs.purpose: List of subdirectories bootstrapped inside every new profile.
key_constructs.reasoning: Profiles are expected to have these folders; code that inspects or creates profiles should preserve this layout.

key_constructs.name: _CLONE_CONFIG_FILES
key_constructs.purpose: Files copied from a source profile when cloning with --clone or clone_config.
key_constructs.reasoning: These are considered part of profile identity and continuity; cloning logic references them explicitly.

key_constructs.name: _CLONE_SUBDIR_FILES
key_constructs.purpose: Relative subpath files (e.g., memories/MEMORY.md) copied when cloning.
key_constructs.reasoning: Memory files are intentionally treated as part of identity and are included in partial clones.

key_constructs.name: _CLONE_ALL_STRIP
key_constructs.purpose: Runtime files removed after a full copytree clone (clone_all).
key_constructs.reasoning: When doing a full copy, some runtime state must not be carried over; this list enumerates that state.

key_constructs.name: _DEFAULT_EXPORT_EXCLUDE_ROOT
key_constructs.purpose: Root-level names excluded when exporting the default profile.
key_constructs.reasoning: Default profile (~/.hermes) contains repo-level infrastructure that should not be part of portable exports.

key_constructs.name: _RESERVED_NAMES
key_constructs.purpose: Profile names reserved and never allowed as aliases.
key_constructs.reasoning: Protects against creating confusing or privileged aliases.

key_constructs.name: _HERMES_SUBCOMMANDS
key_constructs.purpose: Set of hermes subcommands that cannot be used as profile aliases.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents alias conflict with built-in CLI subcommands.

key_constructs.name: _get_profiles_root
key_constructs.purpose: Return the canonical directory for named profiles (~/.hermes/profiles).
key_constructs.reasoning: Important for any operation that enumerates or resolves named profile directories; this is intentionally anchored to the user's home, not the active HERMES_HOME.

key_constructs.name: _get_default_hermes_home
key_constructs.purpose: Return the legacy default HERMES_HOME path (~/.hermes).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to treat the default profile specially in listing, exporting, and active profile handling.

key_constructs.name: _get_active_profile_path
key_constructs.purpose: Return path to the sticky active_profile file in the default home.
key_constructs.reasoning: Active profile persistence is stored here; creating or resolving active profile must reference this file.

key_constructs.name: _get_wrapper_dir
key_constructs.purpose: Return path to wrapper scripts directory (~/.local/bin).
key_constructs.reasoning: Wrapper creation/removal relies on this path; shell PATH checks also relate to it.

key_constructs.name: validate_profile_name
key_constructs.purpose: Raise ValueError if a profile name does not meet identifier constraints.
key_constructs.reasoning: All callers creating or accepting profile names should call this to enforce consistency and prevent invalid filesystem names.

key_constructs.name: get_profile_dir
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve a profile name into its HERMES_HOME directory (special-casing 'default').
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes the mapping between profile identifiers and filesystem locations.

key_constructs.name: profile_exists
key_constructs.purpose: Return whether a profile directory exists (always True for 'default').
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by CLI to validate operations like use/delete before performing filesystem actions.

key_constructs.name: check_alias_collision
key_constructs.purpose: Return a human-readable collision message if alias name conflicts with reserved names, hermes subcommands, or existing PATH commands.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to prevent creating wrapper scripts that would shadow system commands or collide with hermes internals; note it invokes the 'which' command.

key_constructs.name: _is_wrapper_dir_in_path
key_constructs.purpose: Check whether ~/.local/bin is present in the user's PATH environment variable.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to warn or determine if wrapper aliases will be effective in the user's shell environment.

key_constructs.name: create_wrapper_script
key_constructs.purpose: Create an executable shell wrapper at ~/.local/bin/<name> that invokes 'hermes -p <name>'.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides the user-visible alias functionality; creation uses specific file perms and returns the created Path or None on failure.

key_constructs.name: remove_wrapper_script
key_constructs.purpose: Remove a wrapper alias if it appears to be one created by this module.
key_constructs.reasoning: Safe removal requires verifying the file content contains 'hermes -p' to avoid deleting non-hermes binaries.

key_constructs.name: ProfileInfo
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass containing summary information about a profile (name, path, model/provider, gateway state, etc.).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to present profile lists and detailed info to the CLI/UI without loading heavy state.

key_constructs.name: _read_config_model
key_constructs.purpose: Read model/provider keys from a profile's config.yaml (robust to missing yaml/import errors).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is optional and best-effort; callers should treat its return values as advisory only (None when parsing fails).

key_constructs.name: _check_gateway_running
key_constructs.purpose: Determine whether the gateway process recorded in gateway.pid is currently alive.
key_constructs.reasoning: Handles both raw pid and JSON pid files; uses os.kill(pid, 0) as an existence check and handles many failure modes.

key_constructs.name: _count_skills
key_constructs.purpose: Count installed skills by searching for SKILL.md files under the profile's skills directory (excluding .hub/.git subpaths).
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a lightweight heuristic for reporting 'skill_count' without loading skill metadata or connecting to external services.

key_constructs.name: list_profiles
key_constructs.purpose: Return a list of ProfileInfo objects for the default and any named profiles on disk.
key_constructs.reasoning: Central listing routine used by CLI to display profiles; it reads model/provider, env presence, gateway state and alias existence.

key_constructs.name: create_profile
key_constructs.purpose: Create a new named profile directory; supports empty bootstrap, partial clone (config+memories) or full copytree clone.
key_constructs.reasoning: Core operation for provisioning isolated Hermes instances; it enforces name validation, prevents creating 'default', and performs copy/strip semantics for clone_all.

key_constructs.name: seed_profile_skills
key_constructs.purpose: Seed bundled skills into a profile by spawning a subprocess that calls tools.skills_sync.sync_skills with HERMES_HOME set.
key_constructs.reasoning: Runs in a subprocess to avoid module-level caching of HERMES_HOME in sync_skills; returns parsed JSON result or None if failed.

key_constructs.name: delete_profile
key_constructs.purpose: Delete a named profile and its related artifacts (wrapper script, gateway service).
key_constructs.reasoning: Handles lifecycle teardown; the implementation must be careful to stop/disable any running gateway and not allow deleting the default profile.

key_behaviors: list profiles with summary info
create new profile directories (bootstrap or clone)
seed bundled skills into a profile via subprocess
create and remove wrapper alias scripts
determine if gateway is running for a profile

pitfalls.mistake: Assume yaml is always available and config.yaml will parse.
pitfalls.consequence: Parsing errors or missing yaml module will be swallowed by the function and model/provider will be None — callers must handle None.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that _read_config_model is best-effort and guard downstream code against None values.

pitfalls.mistake: Overwrite an existing system command when creating a wrapper alias without checking PATH.
pitfalls.consequence: User's existing binaries could be shadowed causing unexpected behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: check_alias_collision calls out conflicts via 'which' and create_wrapper_script writes into ~/.local/bin only; preserve that checking logic and do not assume wrapper dir is in PATH.

pitfalls.mistake: Treat gateway.pid as always containing a plain pid integer.
pitfalls.consequence: Code may fail to detect running gateway if the pid file is JSON or empty; _check_gateway_running already handles both raw pid and JSON, so altering that logic risks regressions.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing gateway detection, preserve support for both legacy plain pids and JSON structures and the wide exception handling around os.kill.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing wrapper script without verifying it was created by Hermes.
pitfalls.consequence: Could delete unrelated user files/executables if names collide.
pitfalls.prevention: remove_wrapper_script checks file contents for 'hermes -p' before unlinking; do not remove or relax this safeguard.

pitfalls.mistake: Copytree-ing profile data and leaving runtime state files intact.
pitfalls.consequence: Service pids, runtime DBs, and credential files could be duplicated across profiles producing inconsistent behavior or security issues.
pitfalls.prevention: clone_all behavior strips _CLONE_ALL_STRIP items; respect that list and the logic that removes these runtime artifacts after full copy.

key_sections: validate_profile_name: canonical validation logic for profile identifiers
create_profile: bootstrap/clone logic and how clone_from/clone_all/clone_config are resolved
_check_gateway_running: pid/json parsing and os.kill-based liveness check
create_wrapper_script/remove_wrapper_script/check_alias_collision: wrapper script lifecycle and PATH collision checks
skip_unless_needed: module-level constant lists (unless changing cloning/strip/exclude behavior)
seed_profile_skills subprocess details unless debugging sync_skills interactions

related_files.relationship: Provides get_hermes_home() used as the default clone source when none is supplied.
related_files.reason_to_check: If HERMES_HOME location resolution changes, create_profile clone resolution and active profile handling must be updated.

related_files.relationship: sync_skills is invoked in a subprocess by seed_profile_skills to populate skills for a profile.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to tools.skills_sync.sync_skills API (signature, return format) affect seed_profile_skills parsing and error handling.

related_files.relationship: create_profile and functions that determine the default/active profile read/write a sticky active_profile file under default home.
related_files.reason_to_check: Editing how active profile is stored or formatted requires verifying read/write compatibility.

related_files.relationship: CLI commands that call into profiles.py (profile create/list/use/delete) will depend on these helpers for behavior and messages.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any signature change in profiles functions used by the CLI (e.g., create_profile) requires co-updating CLI call sites.

tests_to_run: Manual filesystem test: create a temporary HERMES_HOME and exercise create_profile/list_profiles/delete_profile flows

toolsets.py.short_description: Defines the canonical toolset manifest and utilities to resolve, validate, and create toolsets.
toolsets.py.description:
This module is the authoritative source of toolset (alias) definitions used by the CLI and all messaging platform adapters. It centralizes a static TOOLSETS mapping and a shared _HERMES_CORE_TOOLS list so that platform-specific toolsets can be declared concisely and consistently. The module provides runtime helpers to fetch a toolset by name, resolve composed toolsets (including recursive includes), work with plugin-registered toolsets, validate and create custom toolsets at runtime, and produce metadata about toolsets (counts, composite vs leaf, etc.).

Toolset composition is intentionally supported: toolset entries contain 'tools' (direct tool names) and 'includes' (other toolset keys). resolve_toolset performs depth-first resolution and uses a visited set to avoid infinite recursion and to handle diamond dependencies safely. The module also recognizes special aliases "all" and "*" which resolve to the union of tools from every known toolset (including plugin-provided toolsets). Interactions with the dynamic tool registry are guarded with try/except so failures in plugin discovery will fall back to static TOOLSETS.

Because toolset definitions are consumed by multiple subsystems (hermes_cli.tools_config, skills_config, platform adapters, gateway composition), the static data in this module is effectively authoritative for what tool names and platform toolsets exist. Tests exercise the module's resolution semantics, cycle detection, alias handling, runtime creation of custom toolsets, and structural integrity of TOOLSETS. A recent commit added several missing platform toolsets (e.g., hermes-mattermost, hermes-matrix, hermes-dingtalk) and updated hermes-gateway includes to ensure the gateway aggregates all messaging platforms, so the TOOLSETS contents are sensitive to cross-file consistency checks elsewhere in the repo.

key_constructs.name: _HERMES_CORE_TOOLS
key_constructs.purpose: Shared list of core tool names used by many built-in platform toolsets.
key_constructs.reasoning: This single list ensures multiple platform toolset entries remain identical without duplicating data; editing it affects many hermes-* toolsets.

key_constructs.name: TOOLSETS
key_constructs.purpose: Dictionary mapping toolset names to definitions (description, tools, includes).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the authoritative manifest for built-in toolsets; many modules read this directly to determine available functionality.

key_constructs.name: get_toolset
key_constructs.purpose: Return the raw toolset definition (dict) for a given name or None if missing.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by callers that need description/metadata rather than resolved tool lists; must preserve return shape (description/tools/includes).

key_constructs.name: resolve_toolset
key_constructs.purpose: Recursively resolve a toolset name into a flattened list of tool names, including included toolsets and plugin toolsets.
key_constructs.reasoning: Core function used by CLI and gateway code to calculate which tools to enable; it performs cycle detection via visited set and handles special aliases ('all', '*'). Ordering is not guaranteed because sets are used internally.

key_constructs.name: resolve_multiple_toolsets
key_constructs.purpose: Combine and deduplicate multiple toolset resolutions into a single list of tool names.
key_constructs.reasoning: Convenience wrapper used when multiple toolsets are selected for a platform; it calls resolve_toolset repeatedly and deduplicates results.

key_constructs.name: _get_plugin_toolset_names
key_constructs.purpose: Return a set of toolset names contributed by the tools registry (plugins) that do not appear in TOOLSETS.
key_constructs.reasoning: Enables dynamic extensions (plugins) to participate in 'all' resolution and to be discoverable without being statically declared; it imports tools.registry and must tolerate import/runtime errors.

key_constructs.name: get_all_toolsets
key_constructs.purpose: Return a mapping of all toolset definitions, merging static TOOLSETS and synthetic entries for plugin-provided toolsets.
key_constructs.reasoning: Consumers use this to display or persist available toolsets; it synthesizes definitions for plugin toolsets and thus depends on safe plugin registry inspection.

key_constructs.name: get_toolset_names
key_constructs.purpose: Return a list (or set) of all known toolset names (static + plugin-provided).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by 'all' alias resolution and by other code that must iterate over every toolset key.

key_constructs.name: validate_toolset
key_constructs.purpose: Check whether a toolset name is valid (either static, plugin-provided, or alias like 'all').
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by UI/CLI validation to accept or reject user-supplied toolset names.

key_constructs.name: create_custom_toolset
key_constructs.purpose: Add a toolset entry into TOOLSETS at runtime for ephemeral or user-created toolsets.
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposed to allow runtime extension without persisting to code; ensures created entries follow the required keys (description/tools/includes).

key_constructs.name: get_toolset_info
key_constructs.purpose: Return derived metadata for a toolset (name, description, is_composite, tool_count, direct_tools list, etc.).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by CLI listing and validation screens to display human-friendly information about toolsets.

key_behaviors: resolve toolset includes into a flattened tool list
support special aliases 'all' and '*' to aggregate every tool
inspect plugin registry for runtime toolsets

pitfalls.mistake: Assume resolve_toolset preserves a deterministic tool order.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests or callers that assert ordering may fail intermittently because internal sets are used and ordering is not guaranteed.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat results as a set of tool names; if order matters, callers should sort the returned list.

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or rename built-in platform toolset keys (e.g., hermes-cli, hermes-telegram) without updating PLATFORMS in hermes_cli.tools_config and skills_config.
pitfalls.consequence: Consistency tests will fail and platform behavior (default toolsets) will break; UI/config persistence might re-enable unexpected tools.
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying TOOLSETS, check platform mappings in hermes_cli.tools_config and skills_config and run related tests (see edit_checklist).

pitfalls.mistake: Assume plugin registry imports always succeed.
pitfalls.consequence: Import errors can cause discovery to fail; the module currently swallows exceptions and falls back to static TOOLSETS which can hide plugin issues.
pitfalls.prevention: Retain the try/except behavior around registry imports; if altering it, ensure failures are still handled gracefully to avoid startup crashes.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify cycle-detection semantics (visited set handling) naively.
pitfalls.consequence: Changing how visited is shared across branches can re-introduce infinite recursion or cause double-inclusion of tools.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the visited semantics: return [] on encountering a visited toolset and copy visited when enumerating 'all' branches to avoid cross-branch contamination.

key_sections: TOOLSETS: static manifest and why _HERMES_CORE_TOOLS is shared
resolve_toolset: cycle detection, plugin fallback, and 'all' alias handling
_get_plugin_toolset_names: how plugin toolsets are discovered and synthesized
get_all_toolsets/get_toolset_info: producing data for UI and persistence
skip_unless_needed: Lower-level helper utilities that purely format or display toolset metadata (if present)

tests/hermes_cli/test_tools_config.py.short_description: Unit tests for hermes_cli.tools_config platform tool persistence and platform/toolset consistency.
tests/hermes_cli/test_tools_config.py.description:
This test module verifies the behavior of hermes_cli.tools_config, focusing on persistence of platform tool selections, interaction with MCP server entries, provider visibility logic, and first-install behavior for managed Nous defaults. The tests exercise private helpers (_get_platform_tools, _save_platform_tools, _platform_toolset_summary, _toolset_has_keys, _configure_provider, _visible_providers) as well as the public tools_command entry for first-install flows. Many tests use monkeypatch to control environment variables and to stub functions from other modules (agent.auxiliary_client, hermes_cli.nous_subscription) and they patch save_config to avoid writing disk state during assertions.

Beyond pure unit behavior, the tests validate cross-module consistency: TestPlatformToolsetConsistency asserts that every platform declared in PLATFORMS has a corresponding TOOLSETS entry and that hermes-gateway includes all messaging platform toolsets. They also verify that skills_config.PLATFORMS covers gateway platforms. Several tests are regression tests for previously observed bugs (e.g., preserving MCP server names when saving tool selections, accidental persistence of 'hermes-cli' aggregate toolset) and therefore encode expectations about how configuration lists must be filtered and saved.

tests/test_toolsets.py.short_description: Unit tests for toolsets module: resolution, validation, creation, and integrity checks.
tests/test_toolsets.py.description:
This test suite verifies the core semantics of toolset resolution and the structural integrity of the static TOOLSETS mapping in toolsets.py. Tests cover basic lookup (get_toolset), resolution of leaf and composite toolsets (resolve_toolset), cycle detection (guarding against infinite recursion), support for the 'all' and '*' aliases, deduplication when combining multiple toolsets, and runtime creation of custom toolsets. There are also meta-tests ensuring each TOOLSETS entry has required keys and that includes reference existing toolset keys, and a test that asserts that all hermes-* platform toolsets share identical core tool lists.

The suite mutates TOOLSETS in some tests (temporary creation of cycle toolsets and runtime-created custom toolsets) and removes those entries in finally blocks to restore state. Tests implicitly rely on set semantics and deduplication: they assert membership and counts but do not depend on stable ordering. This test module is the primary unit test harness that protects resolution semantics and the static manifest consistency relied upon across the system.

key_constructs.name: TestGetToolset.test_known_toolset
key_constructs.purpose: Assert get_toolset returns a known toolset definition and that it contains expected tools.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates that get_toolset maps names to definitions; used by higher-level UI code.

key_constructs.name: TestResolveToolset.test_leaf_toolset
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure resolve_toolset returns the direct tools for a leaf toolset.
key_constructs.reasoning: Checks baseline resolution correctness.

key_constructs.name: TestResolveToolset.test_composite_toolset
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure composite toolsets include tools from included toolsets.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates include traversal logic.

key_constructs.name: TestResolveToolset.test_cycle_detection
key_constructs.purpose: Create a temporary cycle and verify resolve_toolset does not infinite-loop and still returns both tools.
key_constructs.reasoning: Protects against regressions in recursion/visited set handling.

key_constructs.name: TestResolveToolset.test_all_alias
key_constructs.purpose: Assert special alias 'all' resolves to many tools (aggregation across toolsets).
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures alias logic enumerates every known toolset including plugins.

key_constructs.name: TestCreateCustomToolset.test_runtime_creation
key_constructs.purpose: Create a toolset at runtime and ensure it is resolvable and valid.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates create_custom_toolset behavior and that runtime additions integrate with resolution logic.

key_constructs.name: TestToolsetConsistency.test_all_includes_reference_existing_toolsets
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure every includes entry references an existing static toolset.
key_constructs.reasoning: Structural integrity check to prevent misspellings and broken includes.

ensure cycle detection avoids infinite recursion

pitfalls.consequence: Subsequent tests (or test runs) may observe stale entries and fail unpredictably.
pitfalls.prevention: Tests here add and delete temporary TOOLSETS entries in try/finally blocks; preserve that pattern when editing tests or adding new ones.

pitfalls.mistake: Relying on ordering of resolve_toolset results.
pitfalls.consequence: Order-dependent assertions will be flaky; resolve_toolset returns a list constructed from sets.
pitfalls.prevention: Write assertions that check set membership and deduplication rather than sequence equality.

hermes_logging.py.short_description: Centralized, idempotent logging setup with redaction and rotating file handlers.
hermes_logging.py.description:
This module centralizes logging initialization for the Hermes Agent so all entrypoints (CLI, gateway, cron, tests) share consistent handlers, formats, and noise suppression. Its primary purpose is to create two disk-backed rotating logs under HERMES_HOME/logs (agent.log for INFO+ activity and errors.log for WARNING+ events) while ensuring secrets are redacted before being written using agent.redact.RedactingFormatter. The API is intentionally idempotent: calling setup_logging() multiple times will not produce duplicate handlers (unless forced). It also provides a separate setup_verbose_logging() helper to add a DEBUG-level console StreamHandler for interactive/verbose modes.

Design decisions reflected in the code: idempotency (module-level sentinel and handler-path deduplication via resolved paths), defensive lazy import of the RedactingFormatter to avoid circular imports, best-effort config reading from config.yaml (so logging can be configured before the rest of the app loads), and explicit suppression of noisy third-party loggers to keep logs readable. Handlers are created with RotatingFileHandler and a per-file size/backups policy; errors.log uses a smaller rotation size and stricter level. The module manipulates the root logger and third-party loggers, so callers must treat it as a global/early startup concern.

key_constructs.name: _logging_initialized
key_constructs.purpose: Module-level sentinel tracking whether setup_logging() has already run.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to make setup_logging idempotent across repeated calls; any editor must respect that tests and other code may toggle this sentinel for isolation.

key_constructs.name: _LOG_FORMAT
key_constructs.purpose: Default log message format for file handlers.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when building RedactingFormatter for file logs; changing it affects all persisted log formatting.

key_constructs.name: _LOG_FORMAT_VERBOSE
key_constructs.purpose: Log format used for verbose console logging.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by setup_verbose_logging() to present more verbose console output including logger name and level.

key_constructs.name: _NOISY_LOGGERS
key_constructs.purpose: Tuple of third-party logger names that should be bumped to WARNING to reduce noise.
key_constructs.reasoning: Suppresses verbose output from common libraries; changes here alter global logging levels for third-party modules.

key_constructs.name: setup_logging
key_constructs.purpose: Primary public entrypoint to configure file-based logging and noise suppression.
key_constructs.reasoning: Callers (CLI, gateway, cron runner) call this early. It returns the logs directory path and accepts overrides for hermes_home, log_level, rotation sizing, and force re-init semantics.

key_constructs.name: setup_verbose_logging
key_constructs.purpose: Add a DEBUG-level console StreamHandler marked with _hermes_verbose, idempotent per-process.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for interactive verbose modes; it avoids adding duplicate console handlers and lowers the root logger level to DEBUG when enabled.

key_constructs.name: _add_rotating_handler
key_constructs.purpose: Attach a RotatingFileHandler to a given logger for a file path, skipping duplicate attachments for the same resolved path.
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs path resolution and handler deduplication. Editors must preserve the duplicate-skip behavior to maintain idempotency.

key_constructs.name: _read_logging_config
key_constructs.purpose: Best-effort read of logging.* entries from HERMES_HOME/config.yaml and return level, max_size_mb, backup_count.
key_constructs.reasoning: Non-fatal attempt to load user preferences for logging so setup_logging can use config defaults if present; it intentionally swallows exceptions to avoid breaking early startup.

key_behaviors: creates logs/ directory under HERMES_HOME
adds rotating file handlers for agent.log and errors.log
ensures redaction formatter is used for persisted logs
suppresses noisy third-party loggers to WARNING
provides idempotent initialization and an optional force reinit

pitfalls.mistake: Importing RedactingFormatter at module import time instead of lazily.
pitfalls.consequence: Could create circular import errors if agent.redact imports code that imports this module during startup.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the lazy import pattern used in setup_logging() and setup_verbose_logging() (import inside functions).

pitfalls.mistake: Adding multiple handlers with the same path without checking resolved paths.
pitfalls.consequence: Duplicate records and inflated log files; tests and runtime behavior assume deduplication.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure _add_rotating_handler continues to check existing handlers by resolved baseFilename equality before adding a new handler.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing root logger level behavior without considering handler levels.
pitfalls.consequence: Handlers may not receive records if root level is higher than handler levels (or conversely, excessive noise).
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the existing logic that sets root.setLevel(level) only when necessary and keeps third-party loggers at WARNING.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming config.yaml is always parseable or present.
pitfalls.consequence: Early startup could fail if exceptions from config parsing are not swallowed; the current design swallows exceptions intentionally.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain try/except around config reads and keep _read_logging_config best-effort and non-fatal.

_add_rotating_handler: file handler deduplication and directory creation
_read_logging_config: how config.yaml is consulted (best-effort)
skip_unless_needed: constant definitions at top (read only if formatting needs change)
the exact list of _NOISY_LOGGERS unless changing suppression

insights.title: Centralized, idempotent logging setup with redact + rotation
insights.problem: Logging was duplicated across entrypoints with inconsistent handlers, risking duplicated records and secret leakage.
insights.root_cause: Each entrypoint added its own RotatingFileHandler and formatters without central coordination.
insights.solution: Add hermes_logging.setup_logging() (idempotent) to create agent.log and errors.log with RotatingFileHandler, RedactingFormatter and noise suppression for noisy third-party loggers. Add setup_verbose_logging() to enable debug console logging.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize logging early in startup, keep it idempotent, and use redaction for persisted logs. Provide both file and verbose console configs so callers can opt in.

tests/test_hermes_logging.py.short_description: Unit tests validating hermes_logging setup, idempotency, config reading, and handler behavior.
tests/test_hermes_logging.py.description: This test module exercises hermes_logging.py end-to-end behavior. It validates that setup_logging creates the logs directory and two rotating file handlers (agent.log and errors.log), enforces idempotency (no duplicate handlers on repeated calls), supports a force reinitialization path, respects explicit function parameters and config.yaml defaults, and that warning vs info filtering behaves as expected across both logs. It also tests the helper _add_rotating_handler (directory creation and deduplication) and _read_logging_config (absence of config and reading configured values). The tests rely on an autouse fixture to reset module-level state and to remove RotatingFileHandlers from the root logger for isolation, which is important because pytest-xdist can cause previous tests in the same worker to leave handlers attached.

key_constructs.reasoning: Covers most public behaviors and config precedence for file handler construction.

key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures console verbosity is added only once per process and marked with _hermes_verbose flag.

key_constructs.purpose: Tests _add_rotating_handler's directory creation and duplicate prevention for same path.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates the internal helper used by setup_logging for safe file handler creation.

key_constructs.purpose: Tests behavior of _read_logging_config with and without config.yaml and with missing sections.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures best-effort config reading is robust and returns expected tuples.

pitfalls.prevention: Keep the autouse fixture that strips RotatingFileHandlers and resets the sentinel; any edits should preserve test isolation logic.

agent/prompt_builder.py.short_description: Stateless utilities that assemble the agent's system prompt: identity, platform hints, context files, and skills index (with caching and snapshotting).
agent/prompt_builder.py.description:
This module provides stateless helper functions used by the agent to build the system-level prompt that is injected into model requests. It assembles several distinct pieces: the agent identity and guidance constants, platform-specific rendering hints, scanned project/context files (AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, SOUL.md), and a skills system prompt which indexes available skills. The file is intentionally lightweight and avoids importing heavy tooling registries; it relies on small parsing utilities in agent.skill_utils and on hermes_constants.get_hermes_home for locating user data.

Key design concerns reflected in the code are safety (prompt-injection scanning of context files), performance (in-process LRU cache plus a disk snapshot for skills index), and portability (platform-aware skill filtering). The skills cache is protected with a threading.Lock and persisted to a .skills_prompt_snapshot.json file validated using a manifest derived from file mtimes and sizes to allow fast cold starts without reparsing unchanged skill files. Context file content is sanitized and truncated to keep system prompts bounded in size. The module exposes many small functions (prefixed with _ where intended to be internal) that are composed by higher-level callers (e.g., AIAgent._build_system_prompt or run_agent entrypoints).

key_constructs.name: _scan_context_content
key_constructs.purpose: Scan a context file's text for prompt-injection indicators and invisible characters, returning either the original content or a BLOCKED message.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function centralizes detection of prompt-injection threats (regex patterns + invisible unicode) so any context file can be safely sanitized before being embedded into system prompts. Callers must respect that a BLOCKED message (string) is returned in place of content when threats are detected.

key_constructs.name: _find_git_root
key_constructs.purpose: Find the nearest parent directory containing a .git directory starting from a given Path.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to bound searches (e.g., for HERMES.md) so traversal stops at repo root rather than walking to filesystem root; editing must preserve that behavior or tests that rely on repository-bound discovery will break.

key_constructs.name: _find_hermes_md
key_constructs.purpose: Search cwd and parent directories up to the git root for .hermes.md or HERMES.md and return the first match.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to allow per-project or repo-level project context; its search ordering (cwd first, then parents until git root) is semantically important for which project context is loaded.

key_constructs.name: _strip_yaml_frontmatter
key_constructs.purpose: Remove YAML frontmatter delimited by --- from the start of a file, returning the human-readable body.
key_constructs.reasoning: Frontmatter may contain machine-parseable config that is handled elsewhere; this ensures only the descriptive body is inserted into system prompts.

key_constructs.name: DEFAULT_AGENT_IDENTITY
key_constructs.purpose: The default system identity text injected into prompts describing Hermes Agent.
key_constructs.reasoning: This string is the canonical agent identity used when building system prompts unless overridden; changes here alter agent persona globally.

key_constructs.name: MEMORY_GUIDANCE
key_constructs.purpose: Human-facing guidance about what should and should not be saved to persistent memory.
key_constructs.reasoning: Part of the system prompt guidance; tests assert presence of specific phrases, so wording changes may affect tests and agent behavior.

key_constructs.name: SESSION_SEARCH_GUIDANCE
key_constructs.purpose: Guidance instructing the agent to use session_search for cross-session recall.
key_constructs.reasoning: Injected into prompts to steer retrieval behavior; tests assert exact fragments from this constant.

key_constructs.name: TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_GUIDANCE
key_constructs.purpose: Strong instruction block requiring the model to use tools instead of only describing actions.
key_constructs.reasoning: This block is injected for certain model families to reduce models that describe actions without executing them; tests assume its existence and models list mapping matters.

key_constructs.name: TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_MODELS
key_constructs.purpose: Tuple of model name substrings which, when matched, should trigger tool-use enforcement guidance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to detect families (e.g., 'gpt', 'codex', 'gemini', 'grok') to decide whether to inject TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_GUIDANCE; changes must remain consistent with other routing and tests.

key_constructs.name: OPENAI_MODEL_EXECUTION_GUIDANCE
key_constructs.purpose: OpenAI GPT/Codex specific guidance addressing known failure modes (tool persistence, verification, missing context).
key_constructs.reasoning: Model-family specific instructions that the system injects for 'gpt'/'codex' models; tests and runtime behavior rely on this being present and formatted (XML-like tags are meaningful).

key_constructs.name: PLATFORM_HINTS
key_constructs.purpose: Mapping of platform keys to short guidance relevant to the platform (whatsapp, telegram, discord, etc.).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to adjust prompt wording for platform limitations (no markdown, media handling). Platform adapters expect these hints to exist and use them when building the system prompt.

key_constructs.name: CONTEXT_FILE_MAX_CHARS
key_constructs.purpose: Maximum allowed characters for a context file before it is truncated.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by _truncate_content to keep injected context bounded; tests assert behaviors at this limit so maintain numeric value and truncation semantics when editing.

key_constructs.name: CONTEXT_TRUNCATE_HEAD_RATIO
key_constructs.purpose: Fraction of the head to preserve when truncating a long context file.
key_constructs.reasoning: Together with CONTEXT_TRUNCATE_TAIL_RATIO these control how much of the start and end are kept; tests expect head and tail markers to be preserved.

key_constructs.name: clear_skills_system_prompt_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Clear the in-process skills prompt LRU cache and optionally remove the on-disk snapshot.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests call this to avoid cross-test cache leakage; editors should ensure locking is respected and snapshot removal handles OSErrors.

key_constructs.name: _build_skills_manifest
key_constructs.purpose: Return a mapping of skill index file relative paths to [mtime_ns, size] for snapshot validation.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to detect filesystem changes for snapshot validity. The manifest format is authoritative for snapshot invalidation — changing it requires snapshot version bumping and test updates.

key_constructs.name: _load_skills_snapshot
key_constructs.purpose: Load and validate a previously written snapshot from disk against the current manifest.
key_constructs.reasoning: Returns None on any mismatch or read error; callers rely on exact manifest equality and version matching to accept a snapshot.

key_constructs.name: _write_skills_snapshot
key_constructs.purpose: Persist parsed skills metadata and category descriptions to the snapshot file atomically.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses atomic_json_write; failures are logged at debug level; tests clear the snapshot path during setup/teardown.

key_constructs.name: _parse_skill_file
key_constructs.purpose: Read SKILL.md (once) and return (is_compatible, frontmatter, description) while handling parse errors gracefully.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is a single-pass lightweight parser that deliberately errs on showing a skill on parse failure (returns True, {}, ""); tests exercise many edge cases including read errors, platform filtering, truncated descriptions, and prerequisites extraction.

key_constructs.name: _skill_should_show
key_constructs.purpose: Decide whether a skill should be visible given conditional activation rules and available tools/toolsets.
key_constructs.reasoning: Implements visibility rules: fallback_for hides if primary is available; requires hides if required tools/toolsets are missing. Tests exercise gating via env vars and available toolsets; behavior must be preserved when changing skill visibility logic.

key_constructs.name: build_skills_system_prompt
key_constructs.purpose: High-level function that builds a textual skills index for inclusion in the system prompt, using caching and snapshotting for performance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposed API used by prompt assembly to include available skills. It deduplicates skills per category, honors platform compatibility, disabled-skills lists, and prerequisites handling (including probing only on localhost vs backend-only). Tests in TestBuildSkillsSystemPrompt exercise many code paths and caching invariants.

key_constructs.name: build_nous_subscription_prompt
key_constructs.purpose: Construct a short prompt explaining Nous-managed features and upgrade guidance based on subscription state.
key_constructs.reasoning: Depends on hermes_cli.nous_subscription.get_nous_subscription_features; tests monkeypatch that function to exercise different subscription states. Presence of HERMES_ENABLE_NOUS_MANAGED_TOOLS env var gates this function returning content.

key_constructs.name: build_context_files_prompt
key_constructs.purpose: Aggregate project context files (AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, SOUL.md) and produce a single prompt fragment describing project context.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function reads files from cwd and HERMES_HOME, sanitizes via _scan_context_content, strips frontmatter, truncates, and composes Project Context output; tests verify loading from workdir and HERMES_HOME, fallback seeded SOUL behavior, and handling of blocked content.

truncates oversized files keeping head and tail

pitfalls.mistake: Import heavy tool modules at module import time (e.g., tools.skills_tool).
pitfalls.consequence: Triggers expensive imports and tool registration during prompt_builder import, slowing cold starts and possibly causing import-time failures in contexts where optional tools are absent.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep prompt_builder lightweight and use agent.skill_utils for skill metadata; avoid importing optional heavy modules at top-level.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify the snapshot manifest format or snapshot version without coordinating snapshot validation logic.
pitfalls.consequence: Existing on-disk snapshots will be rejected or misinterpreted, causing unnecessary rebuilds or silent failures.
pitfalls.prevention: If manifest structure or snapshot contents change, increment _SKILLS_SNAPSHOT_VERSION and ensure _load_skills_snapshot/_write_skills_snapshot remain compatible with tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Change regex threat patterns or invisible character set without updating tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Prompt-injection tests (TestScanContextContent) may start failing or reduce protection coverage.
pitfalls.prevention: When altering patterns, run prompt_builder tests that assert blocking behavior for specific inputs.

pitfalls.mistake: Change CONTEXT_FILE_MAX_CHARS or truncation ratios without updating tests that assert truncation behavior.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that check preservation of head/tail markers or exact-length behavior may fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Run TestTruncateContent and related tests after modifications.

_truncate_content: truncation policy (CONTEXT_FILE_MAX_CHARS and head/tail ratios)
skill cache and snapshot (_SKILLS_PROMPT_CACHE, _build_skills_manifest, _load_skills_snapshot, _write_skills_snapshot)
skip_unless_needed: large constants blocks (GUIDANCE strings) — read only if changing wording

related_files.reason_to_check: If skill metadata parsing or file iteration semantics change, update mirror logic in prompt_builder (manifest building, snapshot structure, or compatibility checks).

related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to NousFeatureState or exposure of get_nous_subscription_features will affect build_nous_subscription_prompt unit tests and runtime behavior.

related_files.relationship: Contains heavy tool registration that prompt_builder intentionally avoids importing at module import time.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any editing that reintroduces eager import of this file must be avoided to preserve fast prompt_builder import and tests that simulate import failures.

insights.title: Include 'grok' family in tool-use enforcement heuristics
insights.problem: Some model families (e.g., grok) tended to describe actions instead of invoking tools. Tool-use enforcement guidance nudges models to call tools.
insights.root_cause: TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_MODELS tuple did not include 'grok'.
insights.solution: Add 'grok' to TOOL_USE_ENFORCEMENT_MODELS so prompt builder injects steering guidance for those families.
insights.lesson_learned: When applying behavioral steering by model family, keep the pattern list up-to-date as new model families are integrated; add unit tests to assert presence.

insights.title: Add OpenAI GPT/Codex execution discipline guidance
insights.problem: GPT/Codex models sometimes abandon tool persistence, skip prerequisite lookups, hallucinate, or don't verify before finalizing.
insights.root_cause: Different model families exhibit different failure modes; existing tool-use enforcement was generic and more targeted guidance was needed.
insights.solution: Added OPENAI_MODEL_EXECUTION_GUIDANCE (XML-tagged blocks) covering tool_persistence, prerequisite_checks, verification, and missing_context. run_agent adds this guidance for models with 'gpt' or 'codex' in model id.
insights.lesson_learned: Model-family specific prompting/instruction layers can materially change tool use behavior; keep guidance modular and testable.

insights.title: Two-layer caching for skills system prompt with shared utility module
insights.problem: Building skills system prompt was expensive and pulled heavy tool registry imports at prompt build time, causing slow cold starts.
insights.root_cause: Prompt builder imported tools.skills_tool which triggered tool registration and heavy imports; skills scanning was repeated and lacked caching across restarts.
insights.solution: Extract shared skill-parsing utilities to agent/skill_utils.py, implement in-process LRU cache (OrderedDict max 8) plus disk snapshot (.skills_prompt_snapshot.json) validated by mtime/size manifest; avoid importing tools at prompt build time.
insights.lesson_learned: Separate lightweight metadata parsing utilities from heavy tool registration to allow fast prompt building; use in-memory LRU plus persistent snapshot for cold-start performance.

tests/agent/test_prompt_builder.py.short_description: Pytest unit tests for prompt_builder: context scanning, truncation, skill parsing, skills prompt, and Nous subscription prompts.
tests/agent/test_prompt_builder.py.description:
This test module verifies the behavior of agent/prompt_builder.py across many areas: safety scanning (prompt injection patterns and invisible unicode), truncation logic for large context files, SKILL.md frontmatter parsing and error handling, skills index generation and caching, Nous subscription prompt generation, and context file collection from working directory and HERMES_HOME. Tests use pytest fixtures, monkeypatching of environment variables and imports, and temporary filesystem (tmp_path) to simulate realistic scenarios. The tests also assert that importing agent.prompt_builder does not eagerly import optional heavy modules (simulated via guarded builtins.__import__).

The test file contains many grouped test classes (TestGuidanceConstants, TestScanContextContent, TestTruncateContent, TestParseSkillFile, TestPromptBuilderImports, TestBuildSkillsSystemPrompt, TestBuildNousSubscriptionPrompt, TestBuildContextFilesPrompt). These classes contain focused tests asserting specific behaviors and edge cases; tests frequently manipulate environment variables (HERMES_HOME, HERMES_ENABLE_NOUS_MANAGED_TOOLS) and patch functions in hermes_cli.nous_subscription or agent.skill_utils.sys.platform to emulate different platforms and subscription states.

key_constructs.name: TestGuidanceConstants
key_constructs.purpose: Group of tests asserting the presence and wording of static guidance constants used in prompts.
key_constructs.reasoning: Wording and specific phrases are asserted so changing those constants in prompt_builder will break these tests.

key_constructs.name: TestScanContextContent
key_constructs.purpose: Tests that _scan_context_content correctly passes safe content and blocks malicious patterns and invisible unicode.
key_constructs.reasoning: Serves as the safety contract for context file scanning; many small regex cases are covered.

key_constructs.name: TestTruncateContent
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies _truncate_content behavior around short content, exact limit, truncated content, and head/tail preservation.
key_constructs.reasoning: Truncation semantics are asserted using CONTEXT_FILE_MAX_CHARS and head/tail markers; these are sensitive tests for prompt size control.

key_constructs.name: TestParseSkillFile
key_constructs.purpose: Tests _parse_skill_file for frontmatter extraction, missing descriptions, long description truncation, read errors, platform incompatibility, and prerequisites parsing.
key_constructs.reasoning: Covers multiple failure modes and ensures graceful defaults (True, {}, "") on errors; tests monkeypatch file.read_text to simulate failures.

key_constructs.name: TestPromptBuilderImports
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures importing agent.prompt_builder does not eagerly import optional heavy tools by monkeypatching builtins.__import__.
key_constructs.reasoning: Protects against regressions that would slow imports or cause import-time failures.

key_constructs.name: TestBuildSkillsSystemPrompt
key_constructs.purpose: Extensive tests for build_skills_system_prompt including empty skills dir behavior, index building, deduplication, platform filtering, disabled skills exclusion, prerequisite gating, and environment/backends differences.
key_constructs.reasoning: Exercises caching snapshot behavior (setup/teardown fixture clears snapshots) and various environment permutations.

key_constructs.name: TestBuildNousSubscriptionPrompt
key_constructs.purpose: Tests build_nous_subscription_prompt using monkeypatched Nous subscription feature feeders to assert prompts for subscribers and non-subscribers.
key_constructs.reasoning: Relies on hermes_cli.nous_subscription.get_nous_subscription_features being monkeypatched to deterministic values; environment flag HERMES_ENABLE_NOUS_MANAGED_TOOLS gates behavior.
