hermes_cli/model_switch.py.short_description: Shared CLI/gateway model selection and alias-resolution pipeline for provider/model switching
hermes_cli/model_switch.py.description:
This module centralizes the user-facing logic for switching models in both the interactive CLI and the gateway /model handlers. It implements the full resolution chain used when a user requests a model change: flag parsing, direct alias lookup (including user-configured model_aliases), catalog-based alias resolution against the models.dev catalog, provider resolution (including explicit --provider flows), credential detection/selection, provider-specific model name normalization, metadata lookup (ModelInfo, ModelCapabilities), and finally encapsulating the outcome in a ModelSwitchResult dataclass.

The file exists to ensure the same resolution semantics and error messages are used across different interfaces (CLI and gateway). It intentionally layers multiple sources of truth (user-provided direct aliases, built-in aliases, the live models.dev catalog) and enforces a deterministic precedence: direct exact aliases are consulted first (and can be extended via config.yaml), then short-name aliases resolved dynamically against MODEL_ALIASES and the provider's catalog, then provider detection heuristics. It also handles special-path behavior when --provider is specified (auto-detecting a model from a local endpoint, providing detailed error messages with config diagnostics when provider resolution fails).

Design decisions visible in the module: (1) Lazy-loading and merging of direct aliases from configuration into a runtime-global DIRECT_ALIASES dict so that user config can override/extend built-ins; (2) Explicit separation between alias resolution and provider/credential resolution steps so the caller (CLI or gateway) can pass current provider/context and get a comprehensive ModelSwitchResult; (3) Use of dataclasses (ModelSwitchResult, CustomAutoResult) to return structured results including warning and error messages so callers can choose whether to persist the switch or display messages to users. The module depends on other subsystems for provider resolution, normalization and catalog metadata (hermes_cli.providers, hermes_cli.model_normalize, agent.models_dev, hermes_cli.models, hermes_cli.runtime_provider, hermes_cli.config).

key_constructs.name: _HERMES_MODEL_WARNING
key_constructs.purpose: Pre-composed warning message about Nous Research Hermes models not being 'agentic'.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to surface a clear user-facing warning when a chosen model's name implies it's a Hermes LLM; callers should preserve this message where appropriate.

key_constructs.name: _check_hermes_model_warning
key_constructs.purpose: Return the Hermes-model warning string when the provided model_name resembles a Hermes LLM.
key_constructs.reasoning: Small helper encapsulating the heuristic check; callers should call this to produce consistent warnings.

key_constructs.name: ModelIdentity
key_constructs.purpose: NamedTuple holding vendor slug and family prefix used for catalog resolution for short aliases.
key_constructs.reasoning: Represents the canonical mapping for short aliases (e.g., 'sonnet' -> ('anthropic', 'claude-sonnet')). The structure matches how catalog entries are searched (vendor/family).

key_constructs.name: MODEL_ALIASES
key_constructs.purpose: Mapping of short alias -> ModelIdentity (vendor,family) without version numbers.
key_constructs.reasoning: This table provides the most common shorthand names users will type; resolution logic depends on this mapping being a family prefix rather than including version numbers.

key_constructs.name: DirectAlias
key_constructs.purpose: NamedTuple representing an exact model mapping that bypasses models.dev catalog resolution (model, provider, base_url).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for endpoints not in models.dev (e.g., local Ollama) where the full model id and base URL must be specified exactly.

key_constructs.name: _BUILTIN_DIRECT_ALIASES
key_constructs.purpose: Built-in direct alias mappings (initially empty in this codebase).
key_constructs.reasoning: Acts as a base that can be merged with user config-provided aliases; code expects a dict here even if empty.

key_constructs.name: DIRECT_ALIASES
key_constructs.purpose: Runtime merged dictionary of built-in + user-provided direct aliases.
key_constructs.reasoning: Lazily populated by _load_direct_aliases() and mutated globally; callers must use _ensure_direct_aliases() before reading.

key_constructs.name: _load_direct_aliases
key_constructs.purpose: Load user-configured model_aliases from config.yaml and merge with built-in aliases into a dict.
key_constructs.reasoning: Reads hermes_cli.config.load_config() and tolerates failures; returns merged mapping. Editors must respect that this function swallows exceptions and normalizes keys to lower-case.

key_constructs.name: _ensure_direct_aliases
key_constructs.purpose: Lazy initializer that populates the global DIRECT_ALIASES from config on first access.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to avoid loading config on module import; modifies a module-global variable — changes must keep lazy-load semantics and global mutation behavior in mind.

key_constructs.name: ModelSwitchResult
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass that conveys the detailed outcome of a model switch attempt (success, provider, model, credentials, messages, metadata).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by callers to decide whether to persist changes and what user-facing messages to show; contains both metadata and human-readable messages.

key_constructs.name: CustomAutoResult
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass used for auto-detect results when switching to a bare 'custom' provider with auto-detect behavior.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a compact structure for the special-case auto-detection path used when provider endpoints are queried for a default model.

key_constructs.name: parse_model_flags
key_constructs.purpose: Parse raw tokenized /model CLI args for --provider and --global flags and return (model_input, explicit_provider, is_global).
key_constructs.reasoning: Single place to parse flags from a raw string for both CLI and gateway commands; preserves exact parsing semantics (e.g., --global removed by simple replace).

key_constructs.name: resolve_alias
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve a short alias against the current provider's catalog; consults direct aliases first then MODEL_ALIASES + provider catalog.
key_constructs.reasoning: Core alias-to-model resolution routine. It enforces precedence: direct aliases (exact mapping) first, reverse-match direct-alias by model id second, then dynamic catalog search using MODEL_ALIASES against the provider's model list. The aggregator/non-aggregator branching is important — aggregators expose vendor/model-name formatted ids while non-aggregators use bare names.

key_constructs.name: get_authenticated_provider_slugs
key_constructs.purpose: Return slugs of providers that have credentials using list_authenticated_providers (cached).
key_constructs.reasoning: Wrapper used to determine which providers the user actually has configured credentials for; used by alias fallback logic to avoid resolving aliases to providers the user can't use.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_alias_fallback
key_constructs.purpose: Attempt resolving an alias across a sequence of providers (prefers authenticated providers), with a backwards-compatible fallback to ('openrouter','nous') if none supplied.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when an alias isn't found on the current provider — this function controls the order of providers tried and therefore which model the user ends up on. The commit history indicates this was changed to respect authenticated providers to avoid surprising resolutions.

key_constructs.name: switch_model
key_constructs.purpose: Entrypoint that implements the full model-switch pipeline and returns ModelSwitchResult.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the function callers (CLI / gateway) call to perform a model change. It coordinates provider resolution, alias resolution, auto-detection, normalization, credentials, metadata lookup and message composition. Any edits must preserve the resolution order and error/reporting semantics because interactive UX and persisted state rely on them.

key_behaviors: parses /model command flags (--provider, --global)
resolves short model aliases to concrete model IDs
loads and merges direct aliases from config.yaml
performs provider-aware model normalization and metadata lookup
returns structured result with warning/error messages

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming DIRECT_ALIASES is populated at import time
pitfalls.consequence: Direct alias lookups may miss user-configured aliases if _ensure_direct_aliases() is not called first, leading to unexpected catalog resolution or failure.
pitfalls.prevention: Always call _ensure_direct_aliases() or rely on resolve_alias which calls it.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing alias resolution precedence (e.g., checking catalog before direct aliases)
pitfalls.consequence: User-configured exact mappings or built-in direct aliases could be shadowed by catalog entries, yielding incorrect provider/base_url or unexpected model ids.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the existing precedence: DIRECT_ALIASES exact matches -> reverse direct alias model match -> MODEL_ALIASES/catalog search.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the aggregator vs non-aggregator branching in resolve_alias
pitfalls.consequence: Aliases that should match vendor/family prefixes on aggregator providers (vendor/model) will not resolve properly, or non-aggregator providers may incorrectly match vendor-prefixed ids.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep and test both code paths that check is_aggregator(current_provider).

pitfalls.mistake: Hardcoding fallback providers when alias resolution fails
pitfalls.consequence: May pick providers the user has no credentials for; this was a past bug and the code now prefers authenticated providers.
pitfalls.prevention: Use get_authenticated_provider_slugs() or accept a user_providers list argument instead of a fixed list.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering user-facing error messages without invoking validate_config_structure() diagnostics
pitfalls.consequence: Users lose actionable diagnostics about config issues when provider resolution fails (the module appends up to 3 config issues in Unknown provider errors).
pitfalls.prevention: When changing provider resolution failure messages, preserve the call to validate_config_structure() and appended details.

key_sections: resolve_alias: Understand alias precedence and aggregator vs non-aggregator behavior
parse_model_flags: Parsing semantics for --provider and --global options
_load_direct_aliases/_ensure_direct_aliases: How config.yaml model_aliases are merged
ModelSwitchResult: Fields produced by the pipeline that callers consume
skip_unless_needed: MODEL_ALIASES mapping listing many short names (useful for reference but static)
_HERMES_MODEL_WARNING constant (static message)

related_files.reason_to_check: If provider resolution behavior or provider metadata shapes change, switch_model's assumptions (pdef.id, pdef.name, pdef.base_url) must remain valid.

related_files.relationship: Catalog access functions (list_provider_models, get_model_info, get_model_capabilities, search_models_dev) provide the canonical model metadata used during resolution and for populating ModelSwitchResult.
related_files.reason_to_check: If models.dev API or cached function signatures change, this module will break; also affects catalog search semantics used by resolve_alias.

related_files.relationship: Contains detect_provider_for_model, validate_requested_model, and opencode_model_api_mode used by the pipeline for provider detection and model validation.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to provider detection heuristics or validation error shapes will alter switch_model outcomes and user-facing errors.

related_files.relationship: Provides resolve_runtime_provider and _auto_detect_local_model used in explicit --provider flows to auto-detect model on a local endpoint.
related_files.reason_to_check: Auto-detection changes may change the behavior when users switch to a provider with a base_url but no explicit model.

related_files.relationship: load_config and validate_config_structure are used to load model_aliases and to append config diagnostics to error messages.
related_files.reason_to_check: If config schema changes (e.g., model_aliases location/type), _load_direct_aliases must be updated; also, validate_config_structure output shape is included in error messages.

data_constants_to_check: agent/models_dev: list_provider_models return format (aggregator vs non-aggregator ids)

insights.title: Model alias fallback uses authenticated providers instead of hardcoded list
insights.problem: When an alias couldn't be resolved on the current provider, fallback searched a hardcoded provider list ('openrouter','nous'), which could pick providers the user doesn't have credentials for, leading to surprising resolutions.
insights.root_cause: Hardcoded fallback provider list ignored user credentials.
insights.solution: Introduce get_authenticated_provider_slugs() that uses list_authenticated_providers cache to determine provider slugs with valid credentials; _resolve_alias_fallback now accepts authenticated provider list and falls back to the old hardcoded tuple only when none are available.
insights.lesson_learned: Fallback logic that tries other providers should respect the set of authenticated/available providers for the user to avoid surprising credential-less resolutions.

insights.title: Augment Unknown provider messages with config diagnostics
insights.problem: Model switching error messages were unhelpful when caused by malformed config.yaml.
insights.root_cause: resolve_provider_full returned None without context; callers produced minimal messages.
insights.solution: When provider resolution fails, call validate_config_structure() and append up to 3 config issue messages to the error returned to the user.
insights.lesson_learned: Attach actionable diagnostics to user-facing errors when common misconfigurations can be detected programmatically.

tools/browser_camofox.py.short_description: HTTP client backend for a self‑hosted Camofox browser exposing browser tool primitives.
tools/browser_camofox.py.description:
This module implements a Camofox backend that proxies the project's browser tool interface to a self‑hosted Node.js Camofox server (configured via CAMOFOX_URL). It exists so the Hermes browser tools can operate against a local anti‑detection Firefox fork (Camoufox) exposed over a REST API instead of using the standalone agent‑browser CLI. The module centralizes session lifecycle, HTTP helpers, and the concrete browser operations (navigate, snapshot, click, type, scroll, back, press, close, get_images, vision, etc.) mapped 1:1 to REST endpoints on the Camofox server.

Design decisions visible in the code: the module keeps an in‑process session map protected by a threading.Lock so multiple tasks can use isolated sessions; it supports an optional "managed persistence" mode that uses deterministic identities (via tools.browser_camofox_state.get_camofox_identity) so the server can map to persistent profile directories; request timeouts are centralized with _DEFAULT_TIMEOUT; and every tool function returns a JSON string (json.dumps) describing success or error rather than raising errors or returning Python dicts. The module purposely handles some failures gracefully (for example navigation succeeds even if snapshot fails; camofox_close returns success True even when deletion fails), which affects observable behavior and must be preserved by edits. It also caches a VNC URL discovered from the /health endpoint once per process and exposes it in results when available.

How it fits into the larger system: browser_tool (tools/browser_tool.py) delegates to this module when CAMOFOX_URL is set; it interacts with config via hermes_cli.config.load_config (to read browser.camofox.managed_persistence) and with tools.browser_camofox_state.get_camofox_identity (to generate stable user/session ids for managed persistence). It also writes screenshot binaries into the Hermes data directory (get_hermes_home() / "browser_screenshots"). The HTTP helpers (_get/_post/_delete/_get_raw) are the central single point for networking logic to the Camofox server and are used throughout the tool functions.

key_constructs.name: _DEFAULT_TIMEOUT
key_constructs.purpose: Default timeout (seconds) used for HTTP requests to the Camofox server.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizing the request timeout ensures consistent network behavior and makes it easy to reason about request timing in all helper calls.

key_constructs.name: _SNAPSHOT_MAX_CHARS
key_constructs.purpose: Character threshold used to handle large snapshots (server pagination note).
key_constructs.reasoning: Defines an upper bound that is relevant to snapshot truncation/summarization logic; changing it can affect pagination or how snapshots are truncated/summarized.

key_constructs.name: _sessions
key_constructs.purpose: In‑process mapping of task_id -> session info dict (user_id, tab_id, session_key, managed).
key_constructs.reasoning: Holds per-task session state that must be consistent across calls; modifications must respect threading semantics and format of stored session dicts.

key_constructs.name: _sessions_lock
key_constructs.purpose: threading.Lock protecting _sessions.
key_constructs.reasoning: All accesses that mutate or read _sessions must respect this lock to avoid race conditions when used concurrently.

key_constructs.name: get_camofox_url
key_constructs.purpose: Return CAMOFOX_URL env var value with trailing slash trimmed (or empty string).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the single source for deriving the base URL used by all HTTP helpers; callers expect exact trimming behavior.

key_constructs.name: is_camofox_mode
key_constructs.purpose: Boolean helper that indicates whether CAMOFOX_URL is configured.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by higher layers to switch to this backend; must remain a simple check on the env var.

key_constructs.name: check_camofox_available
key_constructs.purpose: Probe /health endpoint to test server reachability and cache a VNC URL if present.
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs a network probe and sets module-level cache (_vnc_url and _vnc_url_checked). Changing caching semantics changes when the VNC URL is discovered/exposed.

key_constructs.name: get_vnc_url
key_constructs.purpose: Return cached VNC URL if found; triggers a probe if not yet checked.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides safe access to the VNC URL discovery behavior implemented by check_camofox_available.

key_constructs.name: _managed_persistence_enabled
key_constructs.purpose: Read config.browser.camofox.managed_persistence to determine whether to create deterministic identities.
key_constructs.reasoning: Controls whether sessions are ephemeral or managed/stable; it relies on load_config and must not raise on missing config.

key_constructs.name: _get_session
key_constructs.purpose: Get or create a session dict for the provided task_id (may be deterministic or ephemeral).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the authoritative session factory; it uses the lock, may call get_camofox_identity for managed mode, and must preserve the stored dict structure.

key_constructs.name: _ensure_tab
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure the session has a tab_id by POSTing to /tabs and storing the returned tabId.
key_constructs.reasoning: Creates tabs on the server; callers rely on it to create tabs with a starting URL and on returned session['tab_id'] being set.

key_constructs.name: _drop_session
key_constructs.purpose: Remove and return the session for a task (pop from _sessions).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by close logic; must be used under the lock to avoid leaking or double‑closing sessions.

key_constructs.name: _post
key_constructs.purpose: POST JSON to the Camofox server and return parsed JSON response; raises on non‑2xx.
key_constructs.reasoning: Unified HTTP POST helper used by many operations; relies on requests and raises for status so callers can handle HTTPError.

key_constructs.name: _get
key_constructs.purpose: GET JSON from the Camofox server and return parsed JSON response; raises on non‑2xx.
key_constructs.reasoning: Unified HTTP GET for JSON endpoints; callers assume resp.json() is returned.

key_constructs.name: _get_raw
key_constructs.purpose: GET from Camofox and return raw requests.Response (used for binary screenshot retrieval).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when binary payloads are required; callers handle response.content and are responsible for saving/encoding it.

key_constructs.name: _delete
key_constructs.purpose: DELETE to the Camofox server and return parsed JSON response; raises on non‑2xx.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for session deletion in camofox_close; behavior (raising on non‑2xx) is relied upon by callers' exception handling.

key_constructs.name: camofox_navigate
key_constructs.purpose: Navigate/create a tab to the given URL and optionally return an immediate snapshot and VNC hint.
key_constructs.reasoning: Primary entrypoint used by the browser tool for navigation; it both creates tabs and navigates existing ones and returns a JSON string describing the result (success/url/title/snapshot/element_count/vnc_url).

key_constructs.name: camofox_snapshot
key_constructs.purpose: Fetch and summarize/possibly truncate accessibility snapshot; returns JSON string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides the agent with a compact page representation; reuses summarization helpers from tools.browser_tool and may call an extraction path if user_task is provided.

key_constructs.name: camofox_click
key_constructs.purpose: Click an element by reference (ref) via POST to /tabs/<tab>/click; returns JSON string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Expects refs may include an '@' prefix and strips it; returns clicked ref and new URL from the server response.

key_constructs.name: camofox_type
key_constructs.purpose: Type text into an element by ref via /type endpoint; returns JSON string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Mirrors click function conventions (ref normalization) and returns a typed confirmation.

key_constructs.name: camofox_scroll
key_constructs.purpose: Scroll via /scroll endpoint with a direction; returns JSON string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Simple adapter for scrolling actions; callers expect 'scrolled' in the JSON response.

key_constructs.name: camofox_back
key_constructs.purpose: Navigate back via /back and return resulting URL as JSON string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Maps browser back functionality to the server; returns the new URL on success.

key_constructs.name: camofox_press
key_constructs.purpose: Simulate a key press via /press endpoint; returns JSON string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Keyboard action wrapper; preserves the contract of returning confirmation JSON.

key_constructs.name: camofox_close
key_constructs.purpose: Delete the session on the server and drop the in‑process session; returns JSON string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tries to delete server session but treats deletion failures as non‑fatal; returns closed:true even on exceptions (warning included).

key_constructs.name: camofox_get_images
key_constructs.purpose: Parse images from the accessibility snapshot text and return list of {src, alt} items as JSON string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Camofox lacks a dedicated images endpoint; this function parses the snapshot format (img "alt" and /url: lines) and must preserve parsing heuristics used by consumers.

key_constructs.name: camofox_vision
key_constructs.purpose: Take a screenshot (binary) from the server, save it locally, base64 encode it, and optionally annotate with snapshot context for vision analysis.
key_constructs.reasoning: Handles binary payload via _get_raw and writes to hermes_home/browser_screenshots. It must preserve file path handling, base64 encoding, and error behavior; callers expect a JSON string response including image data or a saved path.

key_behaviors: probes Camofox health and caches VNC URL
creates and manages per-task browser sessions
performs browser actions (navigate, click, type, scroll, back, press)
fetches and summarizes accessibility snapshots and images
fetches screenshots and encodes them for vision analysis

pitfalls.mistake: Changing return types of the camofox_* functions from JSON strings to Python dicts.
pitfalls.consequence: Callers (browser tool layer) expect stringified JSON and will break if given dicts or exceptions instead.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the json.dumps(...) return contract and existing keys in the returned JSON payloads.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the _sessions_lock or changing how _sessions is mutated without preserving the lock protocol.
pitfalls.consequence: Race conditions, lost or corrupted session state, intermittent failures when used concurrently from multiple threads.
pitfalls.prevention: Always use _sessions_lock when reading or writing _sessions; preserve the lock acquisition patterns used by _get_session and _drop_session.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering check_camofox_available caching (the _vnc_url/_vnc_url_checked flags) without understanding side effects.
pitfalls.consequence: VNC discovery may be repeated unnecessarily or not discovered at all; user‑visible vnc_url exposure timing will change.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the one‑time probe behavior or intentionally update callers if caching semantics are changed.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing network timeouts (DEFAULT_TIMEOUT) or request behavior without considering retries/raise_for_status semantics.
pitfalls.consequence: Operations may block longer or raise differently; callers rely on HTTPError/ConnectionError handling patterns.
pitfalls.prevention: If you must change timeouts or error handling, validate all callers and preserve expected exception types/handling.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying how screenshots are saved or their directory path without verifying hermes_home usage and directory creation.
pitfalls.consequence: File write failures or unexpected file locations; downstream vision consumers may not find saved files.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the get_hermes_home() path + browser_screenshots subdir usage and ensure mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) is preserved.

key_sections: _get_session: session identity creation and managed persistence (affects all calls)
_ensure_tab: how tabs are created and tab_id is set on session
HTTP helpers (_get/_post/_get_raw/_delete): single point of change for network behavior
camofox_snapshot: snapshot truncation/summarization integration with tools.browser_tool
skip_unless_needed: Per-action wrappers (camofox_click/camofox_type/camofox_scroll/camofox_back/camofox_press) — these are thin adapters
Top-of-file constants if only minor behavioral edits are planned

related_files.relationship: Primary caller / shared snapshot summarization helpers (SNAPSHOT_SUMMARIZE_THRESHOLD, _truncate_snapshot, _extract_relevant_content). Edits here may require coordinating changes to the browser tool.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing snapshot format, truncation behavior, or the JSON schema returned by camofox_* functions will require updates in browser_tool consumers.

related_files.relationship: Provides get_camofox_identity used for managed persistence to derive deterministic user_id and session_key.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change the expected identity dict shape or keys ('user_id','session_key'), _get_session and server mapping will break.

related_files.relationship: load_config is used for reading browser.camofox.managed_persistence flag.
related_files.reason_to_check: If config schema changes for browser.camofox.* keys, _managed_persistence_enabled must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: get_hermes_home is used to persist screenshots; file I/O and storage path semantics rely on this.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing hermes_home location or semantics will affect where screenshots are written and may require migration or cleanup.

tests_to_run: pytest -q (no unit tests specifically for this file in provided sources)
Run any integration/manual checks against a running Camofox server if available

gateway/config.py.short_description: Load, validate, and represent gateway/platform configuration (env, yaml, legacy json).
gateway/config.py.description:
This module centralizes all gateway-related configuration for messaging platforms. It defines data model classes (HomeChannel, SessionResetPolicy, PlatformConfig, StreamingConfig, GatewayConfig) and utility functions for coercing/normalizing config values and for loading configuration from layered sources: environment variables, a primary config.yaml, a legacy gateway.json, and built-in defaults. The file exists to present a single authoritative in-memory representation that platform adapters and the gateway runtime use to decide which platforms are connected, what delivery/home-channel settings to use, and session reset behavior.

Design choices: the configuration is modeled with dataclasses and explicit to_dict/from_dict methods to support round-trip serialization and backward compatibility. Environment overrides are applied via a separate pipeline (_apply_env_overrides) that can add platforms or mutate PlatformConfig.extra so runtime adapter code can pick up env-driven credentials and platform-specific options. The code contains platform-specific heuristics (e.g. WhatsApp considered connected when enabled, Matrix requires homeserver and token/password, SMS checks TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID env) so the GatewayConfig.get_connected_platforms method encodes platform auth semantics rather than a generic rule.

Interactions: platform adapters (gateway/platforms/*) read GatewayConfig.platforms entries and call methods such as get_home_channel, get_reset_policy, and get_unauthorized_dm_behavior. Tests exercise round-trip serialization (to_dict/from_dict), YAML bridging, and environment override plumbing (notably for Matrix/home channel overrides). The file must maintain stable keys and enum usage because other code and tests rely on Platform enum values and on exact keys being present in the serialized dicts.

key_constructs.name: _coerce_bool
key_constructs.purpose: Coerce arbitrary values (including strings) into booleans with a caller-provided default.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used throughout loading to interpret YAML and environment values consistently; callers expect specific default-preserving semantics when value is None or an unrecognized string.

key_constructs.name: _normalize_unauthorized_dm_behavior
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize unauthorized DM behavior strings to one of the supported values ('pair' or 'ignore').
key_constructs.reasoning: Normalization centralizes validation and prevents invalid values from propagating; callers expect a safe default when input is unrecognized.

key_constructs.name: Platform
key_constructs.purpose: Enum listing all supported platform identifiers (telegram, matrix, slack, etc.).
key_constructs.reasoning: Platform enum values are used as keys in GatewayConfig.platforms and are referenced across adapters and tests — changes to values or removal/addition must preserve compatibility (string values are serialized).

key_constructs.name: HomeChannel
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing a platform's default destination (platform, chat_id, name) used for cron and default delivery.
key_constructs.reasoning: Round-trip serialization via to_dict/from_dict is tested; environment overrides can replace or augment HomeChannel entries, so code that writes or reads this must match the dict schema exactly.

key_constructs.name: SessionResetPolicy
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass modeling when sessions auto-reset (modes: daily/idle/both/none and related parameters).
key_constructs.reasoning: from_dict explicitly treats None values as signals to use defaults (handles YAML nulls). Tests assert this behavior; editing this constructor or from_dict semantics will affect auto-reset logic and tests.

key_constructs.name: PlatformConfig
key_constructs.purpose: Per-platform configuration (enabled, token/api_key, home_channel, reply_to_mode, extra).
key_constructs.reasoning: PlatformConfig.extra is the extensible carrier for platform-specific options (e.g. Matrix homeserver, device_id). Other modules read extra keys directly; maintain stable structure and key names.

key_constructs.name: StreamingConfig
key_constructs.purpose: Controls streaming/delivery behaviour for token streaming to messaging clients (enabled, transport, edit interval).
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposed in GatewayConfig.to_dict/from_dict and read by gateway runtime; numeric coercions are applied in from_dict (float/int conversion).

key_constructs.name: GatewayConfig
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level configuration object aggregating platform configs, reset policies, quick commands, and runtime flags.
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains methods that runtime code and adapters call (get_connected_platforms, get_home_channel, get_reset_policy, get_unauthorized_dm_behavior). It is the single source of truth for gateway behavior at runtime and must preserve backwards-compatible serialization keys and default values.

key_constructs.name: load_gateway_config
key_constructs.purpose: Load and merge configuration from legacy gateway.json, primary config.yaml, environment variables, and defaults into a GatewayConfig instance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Handles layered precedence and YAML-to-schema mapping. Tests verify YAML bridging and environment-driven overrides, so this function must preserve reading order and mapping rules.

key_constructs.name: _apply_env_overrides
key_constructs.purpose: Apply environment variable-derived platform and home-channel overrides into an existing GatewayConfig object.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function mutates GatewayConfig.platforms (adding platform entries when credentials exist) and writes platform-specific extras. Tests (including Matrix and home-channel overrides) import and assert behavior of this helper — edits must maintain the same env var keys and the semantics of modifying or adding platform configs.

key_behaviors: load and merge gateway configuration from env, config.yaml, and legacy gateway.json
serialize/deserialize platform and reset policy data via to_dict/from_dict
determine connected platforms based on config and env heuristics
expose per-platform home channel and unauthorized DM behavior
coerce and normalize user-provided config values (booleans, behavior strings)

pitfalls.mistake: Change default values or from_dict handling for SessionResetPolicy without preserving None→default behavior.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that assert None in YAML yields defaults will fail; runtime may observe unexpected reset timings.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep from_dict logic that checks for None and substitutes default values.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify Platform enum string values or remove entries.
pitfalls.consequence: Serialization (to_dict/from_dict) and tests that look up Platform by string will break; persisted configs may become incompatible.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat Platform.value strings as stable identifiers; add new values instead of renaming existing ones.

pitfalls.mistake: Alter _apply_env_overrides to stop populating platform.extra keys (e.g. Matrix homeserver/device_id).
pitfalls.consequence: Platform adapters (MatrixAdapter) will miss required fields and may refuse to connect or behave incorrectly; tests expecting env-driven extras will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the existing env var keys and ensure extras like 'homeserver', 'user_id', 'device_id' remain present when env vars exist.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume get_connected_platforms is a generic check only for enabled flag.
pitfalls.consequence: Connected platforms may be reported when credentials are missing or required env vars (e.g. TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID for SMS) are not set, or conversely platforms may not appear when they should.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve platform-specific auth checks implemented in get_connected_platforms.

pitfalls.mistake: Treat 'unauthorized_dm_behavior' values without normalization.
pitfalls.consequence: Invalid strings may propagate into runtime logic that expects 'pair' or 'ignore'; tests check normalization behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Use _normalize_unauthorized_dm_behavior when accepting external inputs.

key_sections: load_gateway_config: main entrypoint for building GatewayConfig from env/YAML/legacy JSON
_apply_env_overrides: env-driven mutation of GatewayConfig (important for platform adapters)
GatewayConfig.get_connected_platforms: platform-specific logic determining if a platform is usable
SessionResetPolicy.from_dict: None handling for YAML nulls (important for defaults)
skip_unless_needed: StreamingConfig.from_dict: only relevant if streaming changes are required
PlatformConfig.reply_to_mode thin plumbing

relevant_snippets.description: Covers to_dict/from_dict roundtrips, load_gateway_config YAML bridging, and _apply_env_overrides home-channel overrides.

related_files.relationship: MatrixAdapter reads GatewayConfig.platforms[Platform.MATRIX].extra for homeserver, user_id, password, encryption and device_id; env overrides in _apply_env_overrides must produce those extras.
related_files.reason_to_check: When changing env var keys or extras mapping, ensure MatrixAdapter still finds required keys and check_matrix_requirements behavior remains correct.

related_files.relationship: BasePlatformAdapter subclasses expect PlatformConfig structure (token, api_key, extra, home_channel).
related_files.reason_to_check: If PlatformConfig schema changes (e.g., rename token→auth_token), adapters must be updated accordingly.

insights.title: Plumb MATRIX_DEVICE_ID from environment into platform extras
insights.problem: The gateway config did not include MATRIX_DEVICE_ID as an environment override, preventing stable device_id being surfaced to Matrix adapter.
insights.root_cause: Missing plumbing of new env var into GatewayConfig.platforms extras.
insights.solution: Read MATRIX_DEVICE_ID in _apply_env_overrides and set config.platforms[Platform.MATRIX].extra['device_id'] when present.
insights.lesson_learned: When introducing env-driven features, ensure the global config override pipeline passes them into platform configs.

insights.title: Add thread_sessions_per_user config option and expose to gateway extras
insights.problem: Need to control per-user isolation vs shared threads behavior via configuration.
insights.root_cause: New default for shared thread sessions requires explicit config to opt back into per-user isolation.
insights.solution: Added thread_sessions_per_user (default False) to GatewayConfig, preserved roundtrip in to_dict/from_dict, and bridged YAML key into config.extra.
insights.lesson_learned: Expose session-keying toggles in config with clear defaults, and ensure they flow through to adapters and runtime bridge code.

tests/gateway/test_config.py.short_description: Unit tests for gateway config serialization, YAML bridging, and env overrides.
tests/gateway/test_config.py.description:
This test module verifies the correctness and backward-compatibility of the gateway configuration layer. It verifies dataclass round-trips (to_dict/from_dict) for HomeChannel, PlatformConfig, SessionResetPolicy, and GatewayConfig; it tests load_gateway_config for YAML bridging (quick_commands, stt, session flags) and ensures environment-derived overrides (applied by _apply_env_overrides) correctly set per-platform home_channel entries. Tests focus on behavior-driven assertions (e.g., None in policy dict yields defaults) rather than implementation internals.

The tests set HERMES_HOME (monkeypatch.setenv) and create temporary config.yaml files to assert that load_gateway_config maps YAML keys into the GatewayConfig schema. The HomeChannelEnvOverrides test verifies that platform home-channel environment variables (for different platforms: Slack, Signal, Mattermost, Matrix, Email, SMS) are applied even when the platform already exists in the config (important for precedence semantics).

key_constructs.name: TestHomeChannelRoundtrip
key_constructs.purpose: Contains test(s) ensuring HomeChannel.to_dict/from_dict roundtrip works.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to validate serialization contract that other runtime code relies on.

key_constructs.name: TestPlatformConfigRoundtrip
key_constructs.purpose: Tests PlatformConfig round-trip serialization and default/disabled semantics.
key_constructs.reasoning: PlatformConfig is central for adapters; these tests catch regressions in schema or defaults.

key_constructs.name: TestGetConnectedPlatforms
key_constructs.purpose: Validates get_connected_platforms' platform heuristics (enabled + token etc.).
key_constructs.reasoning: Encodes expectations about what makes a platform 'connected'.

key_constructs.name: TestSessionResetPolicy
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures defaults and None→default behavior for reset policies.
key_constructs.reasoning: Confirms safe handling of YAML nulls and default parameters.

key_constructs.name: TestGatewayConfigRoundtrip
key_constructs.purpose: Round-trip test of full GatewayConfig serialization including unauthorized_dm_behavior.
key_constructs.reasoning: Catches serialization and preservation of platform.extra fields and global flags.

key_constructs.name: TestLoadGatewayConfig
key_constructs.purpose: Integration-style tests asserting load_gateway_config picks up YAML keys and maps them.
key_constructs.reasoning: Verifies correct mapping from user-facing config.yaml to internal GatewayConfig schema.

key_constructs.name: TestHomeChannelEnvOverrides
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures environment-provided home channel variables override or augment existing platform configs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests the env override precedence and the behavior of _apply_env_overrides for many platforms.

key_behaviors: assert serialization round-trips for config dataclasses
assert load_gateway_config maps config.yaml keys into GatewayConfig
assert _apply_env_overrides writes home_channel entries from env into existing platform configs

pitfalls.mistake: Changing to_dict/from_dict key names or removing platform.extra contents.
pitfalls.consequence: Round-trip tests will fail and adapters may not find expected keys.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve dict schema keys or update tests and adapters together.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the precedence order in load_gateway_config (e.g., reading gateway.json last).
pitfalls.consequence: User expectations around config.yaml winning over gateway.json will change and tests that assert YAML takes precedence will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep documented precedence or update tests and documentation when changing it.

pitfalls.mistake: Renaming env vars used by _apply_env_overrides without updating tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Env-driven tests will stop finding or creating platform configs.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing env var names, update tests and adapter code that depends on them.

key_sections: TestHomeChannelEnvOverrides: enumerates many platform-specific env var names that _apply_env_overrides must support
TestGatewayConfigRoundtrip: checks unauthorized_dm_behavior preservation
skip_unless_needed: Simple to_dict/from_dict tests — quick to scan and low complexity

relevant_snippets.description: YAML bridging and env override tests that validate load_gateway_config/_apply_env_overrides behavior.

related_files.relationship: Tests exercise the dataclasses and loader functions in this file.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to serialization keys, defaults, or env var mapping in gateway/config.py must be reflected in these tests.

related_files.relationship: HomeChannelEnvOverrides includes Matrix home room env vars; matrix adapter reads those extras.
related_files.reason_to_check: If tests change names of MATRIX_HOME_ROOM or how home_channel is used, examine matrix adapter code.

data_constants_to_check: env var names enumerated in TestHomeChannelEnvOverrides (SLACK_HOME_CHANNEL, MATRIX_HOME_ROOM, etc.)

tests/gateway/test_matrix.py.short_description: Unit tests for Matrix adapter behavior and Matrix-related gateway config env handling.
tests/gateway/test_matrix.py.description:
This test module focuses on Matrix-specific behavior: ensuring the Platform enum contains 'matrix', verifying that environment variables produce a usable PlatformConfig (via _apply_env_overrides), and exercising many MatrixAdapter helper functions (mxc→http conversion, DM detection/cache refresh, reply-fallback stripping, thread detection, message formatting, markdown→HTML conversion, and display name extraction). It also contains tests around requirements checking and end-to-end-encryption (E2EE) dependency handling; those tests simulate presence/absence of python-olm and matrix-nio by patching sys.modules or using lightweight fakes for nio types.

Tests use a combination of monkeypatch for environment variables and patch.dict/sys.modules to inject fake nio modules and to avoid importing heavy native dependencies during CI. Several tests are asynchronous (pytest.mark.asyncio) and use AsyncMock for mocking AsyncClient behaviors. This file therefore documents how MatrixAdapter is expected to accept env-driven config extras (homeserver, access token, password, device_id, encryption flag) and confirms transformation logic for Matrix-specific message/URL processing.

insights.title: Comprehensive E2EE tests and device_id tests for Matrix adapter
insights.problem: Matrix adapter had many edge cases around E2EE dependencies and device identity that lacked tests.
insights.root_cause: Complexity of nio interactions required many mocks to safely assert behavior.
insights.solution: Extended tests to cover check_matrix_requirements behavior when E2EE deps missing/available, connect() failing when deps missing, behavior when olm not loaded after login, device_id propagation from config/env and passed to AsyncClient constructor and restore_login, and constructor failure handling.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding crypto-dependent features, write tests that simulate both missing and present dependency situations and assert clear failures where required.

insights.title: Extensive tests for Matrix access token E2EE behavior and encrypted send retries
insights.problem: E2EE behavior and maintenance tasks require robust tests; matrix-nio mocks needed injecting into sys.modules.
insights.root_cause: Production code imports nio inside functions; tests must patch sys.modules to simulate nio classes/exceptions.
insights.solution: Added tests that patch sys.modules['nio'] with appropriate fakes, cover whoami-driven restore_login, maintenance tasks, and encrypted send retry logic.

hermes_constants.py.short_description: Import-safe, single-source constants and helpers for HERMES_HOME, optional-skills, legacy dirs, reasoning config, and provider URLs
hermes_constants.py.description:
This module centralizes small, widely-used constants and helper functions that are safe to import from anywhere in the codebase. It intentionally has no non-stdlib dependencies to avoid circular import problems and to serve as the authoritative source of truth for runtime directory locations (HERMES_HOME and optional-skills), legacy path compatibility, a human-friendly display of the current home path, parsing of a 'reasoning effort' configuration value, and several externally-used provider base/endpoint URLs. Because many subsystems (CLI, agent, gateway, persistence, packaging) need consistent directory resolution and small shared constants, this file exists to avoid drift and duplicated logic across the codebase.

The design decisions visible here favor small, pure helpers that return Path objects or simple dicts/constants. get_hermes_home reads HERMES_HOME from env and falls back to ~/.hermes; get_optional_skills_dir honors an HERMES_OPTIONAL_SKILLS override and also accepts an optional default Path parameter for packaged installs. get_hermes_dir encapsulates a backward-compatibility strategy: prefer a new consolidated path but return an existing legacy path (old_name) if it exists on disk. display_hermes_home produces a user-facing string using a ~/ shorthand when the home is under the user's home directory. parse_reasoning_effort normalizes and validates a small set of allowed reasoning effort levels and returns either None (unrecognized/empty), a disabled dict for "none", or an enabled dict with the chosen level. The module also declares constant base URLs and endpoint URLs for OpenRouter, AI Gateway, and NousResearch which are likely referenced by multiple model/provider adapters.

key_constructs.name: get_hermes_home
key_constructs.purpose: Return the configured HERMES_HOME as a Path, falling back to ~/.hermes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Many components require the canonical data directory — this single function is the canonical source of that value and must remain import-safe and stable. Callers expect a Path and that environment overrides work.

key_constructs.name: get_optional_skills_dir
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve the optional-skills directory, honoring HERMES_OPTIONAL_SKILLS or an explicit default, otherwise under HERMES_HOME.
key_constructs.reasoning: Packaged installs may put optional-skills outside the Python package tree; this function centralizes that policy. It accepts a default to support package installers that pass a packaged path.

key_constructs.name: get_hermes_dir
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve a Hermes subdirectory with backward compatibility between a new subpath and a legacy path name.
key_constructs.reasoning: Maintains on-disk compatibility: if an old legacy directory exists, return it so installs are not migrated unexpectedly. Callers expect deterministic behavior based on filesystem state.

key_constructs.name: display_hermes_home
key_constructs.purpose: Return a user-friendly string for HERMES_HOME using '~/...' when the home is under the current user's home.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for user-facing logs/messages; it intentionally returns a string rather than a Path so presentation logic is centralized.

key_constructs.name: VALID_REASONING_EFFORTS
key_constructs.purpose: Tuple of allowed reasoning effort strings (xhigh, high, medium, low, minimal).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by parse_reasoning_effort to validate input; keeping this constant in one place avoids mismatches across the codebase.

key_constructs.name: parse_reasoning_effort
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize and validate a reasoning effort string into a small config dict or None.
key_constructs.reasoning: Many parts of the agent accept a reasoning effort parameter; this function provides a consistent interpretation: None for empty/unrecognized, {'enabled': False} for 'none', or {'enabled': True, 'effort': level} for valid levels.

key_constructs.name: OPENROUTER_BASE_URL
key_constructs.purpose: Base URL for OpenRouter provider API.
key_constructs.reasoning: Other modules building OpenRouter endpoints should import this rather than hardcoding; several derived constants are declared for common endpoints.

key_constructs.name: OPENROUTER_MODELS_URL
key_constructs.purpose: Model list endpoint for OpenRouter.
key_constructs.reasoning: Convenience constant derived from OPENROUTER_BASE_URL; used by provider discovery code.

key_constructs.name: OPENROUTER_CHAT_URL
key_constructs.purpose: Chat completions endpoint for OpenRouter.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by OpenRouter chat adapter code to construct requests.

key_constructs.name: AI_GATEWAY_BASE_URL
key_constructs.purpose: Base URL for the ai-gateway proxy service.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralized constant for modules that may use the ai-gateway.

key_constructs.name: AI_GATEWAY_MODELS_URL
key_constructs.purpose: Models endpoint for ai-gateway.
key_constructs.reasoning: Derived convenience constant.

key_constructs.name: AI_GATEWAY_CHAT_URL
key_constructs.purpose: Chat completions endpoint for ai-gateway.
key_constructs.reasoning: Derived convenience constant.

key_constructs.name: NOUS_API_BASE_URL
key_constructs.purpose: Base URL for NousResearch inference API.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by the Nous provider adapter; declared centrally for consistency.

key_constructs.name: NOUS_API_CHAT_URL
key_constructs.purpose: Chat completions endpoint for NousResearch API.
key_constructs.reasoning: Derived convenience constant used by the Nous adapter code.

key_behaviors: return canonical HERMES_HOME path
resolve optional-skills directory honoring env override
choose legacy path if it exists to preserve on-disk compatibility
format a user-facing HERMES_HOME display string
parse and validate reasoning effort configuration
expose provider base and endpoint URLs

pitfalls.mistake: Importing heavyweight or application modules into this file (or adding dependencies that are non-stdlib).
pitfalls.consequence: May introduce circular imports or break modules that need a safe-to-import constants module; could make many modules fail to import early.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep this module dependency-free and only use stdlib; avoid importing application modules here.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing get_hermes_dir to unconditionally return the new path without checking the legacy path existence.
pitfalls.consequence: Existing installations that still use the legacy directory would be ignored and could exhibit data not found or duplicated data semantics; implicit migrations could surprise users.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the legacy-exists check behavior; if changing, coordinate with migration tooling and update callers that rely on the old path behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering parse_reasoning_effort return semantics (e.g., changing None vs an explicit dict for invalid inputs).
pitfalls.consequence: Callers that treat None as 'use default' or rely on the {'enabled': False} sentinel for 'none' may behave incorrectly, leading to unexpected agent behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the current contract: None for empty/unrecognized, {'enabled': False} for 'none', {'enabled': True, 'effort': <level>} for valid levels.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying the URL constants (base or endpoints) without updating provider adapters.
pitfalls.consequence: Network calls may break or point to incorrect endpoints, causing runtime failures in model/provider integrations.
pitfalls.prevention: If changing endpoints, search for usages in provider adapters (e.g., modules under agent/) and update them together; run integration tests that cover provider adapters if available.

hermes_time.py.short_description: Timezone-aware clock helper that returns a tz-aware datetime based on env/config or server-local time.
hermes_time.py.description:
This module centralizes timezone resolution and provides a single public helper now() to obtain a timezone-aware datetime for Hermes. It implements a deterministic resolution order (HERMES_TIMEZONE env var → timezone key in ~/.hermes/config.yaml → server local time) and caches the resolved ZoneInfo / name so subsequent calls are fast and avoid repeated I/O. The module intentionally never raises on invalid timezones: invalid values are logged as warnings and the code falls back to server local time.

Design decisions visible in the code: (1) a single simple public API (now()) keeps other modules from needing to duplicate timezone resolution logic; (2) caching of resolved timezone and its string name (module-level globals) reduces cost of reading config/yaml and instantiating ZoneInfo; (3) a reset_cache() function exists to allow tests or runtime config changes to force re-resolution; (4) ZoneInfo usage prefers the stdlib zoneinfo and falls back to backports.zoneinfo for older Python versions. The module uses broad exception handling around YAML/config reads and ZoneInfo construction so that a bad config or missing optional libs do not break the runtime.

How it fits in the larger system: other core components that need consistent wall-clock times (logging timestamps, scheduling, memory timestamps, cron jobs, message timestamps) should call hermes_time.now() to get an aware datetime in the user's configured timezone. The module depends on hermes_constants.get_hermes_home() to locate the config.yaml under the Hermes data directory, and it observes the HERMES_TIMEZONE environment variable which may be set by supervisor/system orchestration. reset_cache() is used to force re-evaluation after configuration changes or within tests.

Non-obvious behaviors / operational notes: the module returns server-local tz-aware datetimes when no valid timezone is configured; invalid timezone strings are logged and do not raise; _resolve_timezone_name performs file I/O and imports yaml lazily inside the function (so yaml may not be required unless config.yaml is present or env var is unset). The caching uses module-level globals which are not protected for concurrency; callers that mutate config at runtime must call reset_cache() to ensure the change is picked up.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_timezone_name
key_constructs.purpose: Read the configured IANA timezone string, checking env and config.yaml, returning an IANA name or empty string.
key_constructs.reasoning: This encapsulates the resolution order and handles file I/O and YAML parsing; callers should avoid calling it repeatedly because it does disk I/O and dynamic imports.

key_constructs.name: _get_zoneinfo
key_constructs.purpose: Validate a timezone name and return a ZoneInfo object, or None if invalid.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes ZoneInfo construction and logging on invalid values so callers get a single point of truth for validation behavior.

key_constructs.name: get_timezone
key_constructs.purpose: Return the cached ZoneInfo for the configured timezone, resolving and caching it on first access.
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposes the resolved zone to other code that may want direct ZoneInfo objects and ensures resolution happens once until reset_cache() is called.

key_constructs.name: get_timezone_name
key_constructs.purpose: Return the configured timezone IANA name (string) or empty string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides the canonical string name (or '') for display or config introspection without exposing internals of ZoneInfo.

key_constructs.name: now
key_constructs.purpose: Return the current time as a timezone-aware datetime using the configured timezone when available, otherwise server-local time.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the intended single-call API for the rest of the codebase to obtain consistent timezone-aware datetimes.

key_constructs.name: reset_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Clear the cached timezone state so the module will re-resolve timezone on next call.
key_constructs.reasoning: Allows tests and runtime config-change handlers to force a re-check of env/config without reloading the module.

key_constructs.name: _cached_tz
key_constructs.purpose: Module-level cached ZoneInfo or None.
key_constructs.reasoning: Holds the resolved ZoneInfo object between calls; edits must respect that this is global mutable state.

key_constructs.name: _cached_tz_name
key_constructs.purpose: Module-level cached timezone name string or None.
key_constructs.reasoning: Stores the resolved IANA name; useful for diagnostics and get_timezone_name().

key_constructs.name: _cache_resolved
key_constructs.purpose: Boolean flag indicating whether the timezone resolution has been performed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents repeated resolution and I/O; reset_cache() toggles this to force re-resolution.

key_behaviors: resolves timezone from environment or config file
returns timezone-aware current datetime
caches resolved timezone for reuse
logs and falls back to server-local time on invalid timezone

pitfalls.mistake: Calling _resolve_timezone_name() repeatedly from hot paths
pitfalls.consequence: Unnecessary file I/O and repeated YAML imports which can degrade performance.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the module-level caching and use get_timezone()/now() rather than calling resolver directly; use reset_cache() when configuration changes.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying config.yaml at runtime without calling reset_cache()
pitfalls.consequence: The module will continue to return the previously cached timezone, so config changes won't take effect until the process is restarted or reset_cache() is called.
pitfalls.prevention: Call reset_cache() after making runtime configuration updates that should be reflected immediately.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming thread-safety of module-level cache
pitfalls.consequence: Concurrent callers could race on resolution; while the worst case is duplicated work, it may cause non-deterministic logging or unexpected intermediate state.
pitfalls.prevention: Be aware of global mutable state; if code runs in a multi-threaded context and needs strict guarantees, orchestrate reset/resolution externally before concurrent access.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing ZoneInfo import strategy or exception handling casually
pitfalls.consequence: Altering how invalid timezones are handled or how backports are used may lead to unexpected exceptions or different fallback behavior across Python versions.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep explicit fallback to backports.zoneinfo and preserve broad exception handling/logging to avoid crashes caused by bad timezone strings or missing optional dependencies.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing logging of invalid timezone
pitfalls.consequence: Loss of operational visibility when operators supply a bad timezone, making debugging config issues harder.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the warning log that includes the invalid value and exception text.

key_sections: get_timezone: caching and how the ZoneInfo is exposed
_resolve_timezone_name: resolution order and file I/O behaviour
_get_zoneinfo: validation and warning logging for invalid zone names
reset_cache: how to force re-evaluation
skip_unless_needed: top-level imports and backport import handling (only relevant when targeting older Python versions)
module-level cache variable definitions (read but no complex logic)

related_files.reason_to_check: If hermes_constants.get_hermes_home changes its return type or location semantics, hermes_time._resolve_timezone_name path resolution may need adjustment.

related_files.relationship: Configuration file that may contain the timezone key consumed by this module.
related_files.reason_to_check: If config schema changes (key renamed or removed), the resolution logic here must be revisited; runtime edits should call reset_cache().

related_files.relationship: Consumers rely on now() for timezone-aware timestamps across the application (schedulers, logging, memory timestamps).
related_files.reason_to_check: If now() semantics change (e.g., return type or tz selection), dependent modules may require updates.

agent/model_metadata.py.short_description: Utilities for discovering and caching model metadata (context lengths, max tokens, pricing) and probing local model servers.
agent/model_metadata.py.description:
This module centralizes metadata about model capabilities (primarily context/window lengths and max completion tokens), provides utilities to infer provider names from URLs, probes local model servers to detect vendor types, and fetches model catalogs from remote sources (OpenRouter and OpenAI-compatible endpoints). It exists so higher-level agent code (ContextCompressor, run_agent pre-flight checks, model routing) can make informed decisions about prompt sizing, chunking and whether to attempt a given request size to a provider.

Design choices: metadata is obtained from two primary external sources — the OpenRouter catalog (OPENROUTER_MODELS_URL via requests) and arbitrary OpenAI-compatible base URLs (via /models). Results are cached in-memory with TTLs to avoid frequent network calls. The file contains thin fallback/default mappings (DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LENGTHS) for common model families so the system can operate even when remote lookups fail. It also implements logic to strip explicit provider prefixes (e.g., "openrouter:..." → "...") while intentionally preserving Ollama-style model:tag semantics, which is important for cache lookups and server queries. Local endpoint detection (is_local_endpoint, detect_local_server_type) uses URL parsing and short HTTP probes to identify common self-hosted stacks (ollama, lm-studio, vllm, llamacpp).

key_constructs.name: _PROVIDER_PREFIXES
key_constructs.purpose: Set of provider name prefixes recognized and removable from model IDs (e.g., 'openrouter:my-model').
key_constructs.reasoning: When editing, respect that only entries here are stripped by _strip_provider_prefix; changes alter how model IDs are normalized for cache and remote lookups.

key_constructs.name: _OLLAMA_TAG_PATTERN
key_constructs.purpose: Regex used to detect Ollama-style tags (e.g., '7b', 'latest', 'q4_0') to avoid stripping provider-like prefixes that are actually tags.
key_constructs.reasoning: This preserves model:tag semantics for Ollama; altering the pattern changes what is considered a tag and can affect whether prefixes are removed.

key_constructs.name: _strip_provider_prefix
key_constructs.purpose: Strip recognized provider prefix from a model string while preserving Ollama-style model:tag constructs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by cache lookups and queries; any changes must maintain the documented examples and ensure that 'http' prefixed models and Ollama tags remain unchanged.

key_constructs.name: CONTEXT_PROBE_TIERS
key_constructs.purpose: Descending list of candidate context sizes to probe when the model's context size is unknown.
key_constructs.reasoning: This list drives pre-flight probing behavior; editing it alters how the system attempts to find a working context length after failures.

key_constructs.name: DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LENGTHS
key_constructs.purpose: Fallback mapping of model name substrings to conservative default context lengths when no remote metadata is available.
key_constructs.reasoning: Authoritative defaults for many families; changes here directly affect the system's assumptions about model memory and may cause request failures or over-restriction if set incorrectly.

key_constructs.name: fetch_model_metadata
key_constructs.purpose: Fetch model metadata from OpenRouter, parse and cache it with a TTL, and expose entries keyed by model ID and aliases.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the primary remote source used in normal operation; network errors fall back to cached data. Respect cache TTL behavior when modifying.

key_constructs.name: fetch_endpoint_model_metadata
key_constructs.purpose: Fetch model metadata from a custom OpenAI-compatible /models endpoint, extract context length and token info, and cache results per normalized base URL.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for custom endpoints (self-hosted or vendor) and contains special-handling (llama.cpp /props probing). Changing logic here affects custom endpoint support and caching.

key_constructs.name: detect_local_server_type
key_constructs.purpose: Probe a local base_url to detect self-hosted server types (ollama, lm-studio, vllm, llamacpp) by requesting known endpoints.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to pick server-specific heuristics; network/timeouts matter. Tests or edits must preserve the specific endpoint probes and response checks to keep detection stable.

key_constructs.name: is_local_endpoint
key_constructs.purpose: Determine whether a base URL points to a local/private host (loopback or RFC-1918 ranges).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to treat local endpoints differently (e.g., trust or probe them). Editing IP detection or parsing could misclassify endpoints and change behavior.

key_constructs.name: _extract_pricing
key_constructs.purpose: Extract pricing-related fields from nested metadata payloads using an alias map for different naming conventions.
key_constructs.reasoning: Parses heterogenous payload shapes; altering the alias map or matching logic will affect pricing extraction correctness for diverse providers.

key_behaviors: fetches and caches model metadata from OpenRouter
probes /models endpoints on custom base URLs and caches results
detects whether a base_url is local and probes local server types
normalizes model IDs by stripping provider prefixes while preserving Ollama tags
provides fallback context length defaults for model families

pitfalls.mistake: Treating model IDs with provider-like prefixes as opaque without using _strip_provider_prefix
pitfalls.consequence: Cache misses and duplicate or inconsistent model lookups; remote queries may fail or miss correct entries.
pitfalls.prevention: Use _strip_provider_prefix where model normalization is expected; respect the Ollama tag preservation behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LENGTHS or CONTEXT_PROBE_TIERS without considering upstream provider capabilities and tests
pitfalls.consequence: Requests may be sized incorrectly: too large contexts can trigger model errors, too-small contexts reduce agent capability or split context unnecessarily.
pitfalls.prevention: Run tests that exercise provider-specific behavior (e.g., gemini-related tests) and be cautious altering these authoritative fallbacks.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying caching TTLs or cache keys in a non-backwards-compatible way
pitfalls.consequence: Increased network traffic, unexpected staleness, or inconsistent behavior across process restarts.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve TTL semantics (_MODEL_CACHE_TTL, _ENDPOINT_MODEL_CACHE_TTL) or ensure dependent modules are updated and tests run.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering detect_local_server_type probes (endpoints or response checks) without accounting for vendor variations
pitfalls.consequence: Local servers (ollama, lm-studio, vllm, llamacpp) may no longer be detected, breaking server-specific handling downstream.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the explicit endpoint checks and response-content tests; if adding new checks, ensure they don't overshadow existing ones.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming network calls always succeed in fetch functions
pitfalls.consequence: Unhandled exceptions or lack of fallback leading to crashes; silent returns of empty caches may degrade behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect existing try/except behavior; test offline and degraded conditions. Note functions already fall back to cached data.

related_files.reason_to_check: If the OpenRouter URL or its expected payload format changes, update constants and parse logic in this file.

related_files.relationship: Makes decisions about prompt composition and length; uses context window information to avoid exceeding provider limits.
related_files.reason_to_check: Adjusting default context lengths or detection behavior can alter prompt_builder's token budgeting; run related tests.

insights.title: Update default context lengths and provider prefixes for Gemini/Gemma
insights.problem: Gemini/Gemma models had outdated context-length defaults and provider prefix mappings.
insights.root_cause: Model catalog changes not reflected in DEFAULT_CONTEXT_LENGTHS and provider prefix lists.
insights.solution: Adjust gemini and gemma context lengths (gemini: 1,048,576; gemma-4 variants: 256,000), add 'gemini'/'google' aliases to _PROVIDER_PREFIXES and _URL_TO_PROVIDER mappings.
insights.lesson_learned: Model metadata (context length, provider prefixes) must be kept synchronized with upstream provider catalogs; add tests that assert expected context lengths to catch regressions.

mcp_serve.py.short_description: StdIO MCP server and EventBridge that exposes Hermes conversations and events to MCP clients.
mcp_serve.py.description:
This module implements a Hermes MCP (Messaging Channel Protocol) server and a background EventBridge that polls local session storage for new messages and approval events and exposes them via MCP tools. It exists to let MCP clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, etc.) list conversations, read history, send messages, poll live events, and manage approvals across all connected messaging platforms without importing the whole gateway stack. The module purposely performs lazy imports (mcp SDK, hermes_state, hermes_constants) so it can be executed in environments where full Hermes gateway dependencies are not available.

The EventBridge is a purpose-built, in-memory queue with cursor semantics, waiter support (blocking wait_for_event), an internal approval tracking map, and mtime-based caching to avoid unnecessary work during frequent polling. The EventBridge polls a SessionDB abstraction (if available) and sessions.json index to discover new messages. The create_mcp_server function (further down in the file) registers the MCP tools (conversations_list, conversation_get, messages_read, events_poll, events_wait, messages_send, permissions_list_open, permissions_respond and Hermes-specific channels_list) backed by EventBridge and the local session state. The server surface mirrors OpenClaw's 9-tool bridge plus an extra tool for channel directory listing.

Design choices visible in the file: lightweight helpers that read HERMES_HOME and sessions.json directly to avoid importing GatewayConfig, careful handling of message content formats (string or multipart list), extraction of non-text attachments from both multipart content blocks and MEDIA: tags embedded in text, and concurrency-safe queue operations with a fixed QUEUE_LIMIT. The module is an integration point between Hermes persistence (state.db / sessions.json), optional hermes_state SessionDB API, and the external MCP SDK (FastMCP).

key_constructs.name: _get_sessions_dir
key_constructs.purpose: Return the sessions directory path using hermes_constants.get_hermes_home or HERMES_HOME env fallback.
key_constructs.reasoning: Many operations read sessions.json directly; this helper centralizes the HERMES_HOME resolution and allows tests to monkeypatch hermes_constants.get_hermes_home or the environment.

key_constructs.name: _get_session_db
key_constructs.purpose: Return a SessionDB instance from hermes_state if available, or None on failure.
key_constructs.reasoning: EventBridge relies on this to read message transcripts; the lazy import lets MCP server run even if hermes_state isn't installed.

key_constructs.name: _load_sessions_index
key_constructs.purpose: Load and parse sessions.json into a dict mapping session_key to entry metadata.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by both EventBridge and MCP tools to enumerate conversations without importing the full SessionStore.

key_constructs.name: _load_channel_directory
key_constructs.purpose: Load cached channel_directory.json from HERMES_HOME or return empty dict if missing or unreadable.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides the extra "channels_list" MCP tool with persisted channel directory data.

key_constructs.name: _extract_message_content
key_constructs.purpose: Extract and normalize human-readable text content from a message; handles single string or multipart content list.
key_constructs.reasoning: Messages can be stored as plain text or lists of parts; this function ensures downstream tools/bridges present coherent textual content.

key_constructs.name: _extract_attachments
key_constructs.purpose: Find and normalize attachments (images, files, MEDIA: tags) from message content.
key_constructs.reasoning: Attachment detection is multi-strategy (multipart blocks, MEDIA: tags, image URLs) and must be consistent for MCP clients to fetch attachments.

key_constructs.name: QUEUE_LIMIT
key_constructs.purpose: Maximum number of events retained in the in-memory queue.
key_constructs.reasoning: Bound memory usage and provide predictable trimming behavior when events are produced faster than consumed.

key_constructs.name: POLL_INTERVAL
key_constructs.purpose: Polling interval (in seconds) between SessionDB polls for new messages.
key_constructs.reasoning: Controls EventBridge responsiveness and CPU usage; small value here (0.2s) aims to give near-real-time updates but relies on cheap mtime checks to be efficient.

key_constructs.name: QueueEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing an event in the EventBridge queue with cursor, type, session_key, and payload.
key_constructs.reasoning: Cursor-based events allow clients to request events after a given cursor and support wait semantics.

key_constructs.name: EventBridge
key_constructs.purpose: Background poller that watches SessionDB and sessions.json for changes and exposes a thread-safe in-memory event queue with waiter support.
key_constructs.reasoning: Decouples database polling and event delivery, provides cursor semantics, supports blocking waiters, approval lifecycle, and optimizes by using mtime caching to skip work if nothing changed.

key_constructs.name: create_mcp_server
key_constructs.purpose: Construct and return a FastMCP server instance with Hermes MCP tools registered, using an EventBridge instance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates tool registration logic and dependency on FastMCP; raises ImportError with guidance if mcp SDK is missing.

key_behaviors: polls SessionDB and sessions.json for new messages
enqueues message and approval events with cursor semantics
provides blocking wait_for_event for MCP clients
extracts message text and non-text attachments
exposes MCP tools to list conversations and read/send messages

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or simplifying the mtime-based checks in _poll_once
pitfalls.consequence: EventBridge will perform expensive work on every poll (200ms), leading to high CPU and possibly DB contention
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the mtime checks and cached_sessions_index usage when editing polling logic

pitfalls.mistake: Changing EventBridge locking or queue trimming without preserving thread-safety
pitfalls.consequence: Race conditions, lost events, or deadlocks under concurrent enqueue/waiter scenarios (tests assert concurrency behavior)
pitfalls.prevention: Respect self._lock usage around _queue, _cursor, and _pending_approvals; ensure self._new_event is signaled appropriately

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming message content is always a python dict/list vs JSON string from DB
pitfalls.consequence: Content extraction or attachment parsing may fail or return incorrect values when messages are stored as JSON strings in state.db
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain existing handling that accepts both string and list/dict content (see _extract_message_content and test DB creation in tests)

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or tightening the optional import fallbacks for hermes_constants or hermes_state
pitfalls.consequence: Module will raise ImportError in environments without gateway dependencies and break usage scenarios intended to work without full gateway
pitfalls.prevention: Keep lazy imports and environment fallbacks (HERMES_HOME) intact

pitfalls.mistake: Altering attachment detection regex or content-part handling carelessly
pitfalls.consequence: MEDIA: tags and multipart image blocks may not be recognized, breaking MCP clients' ability to fetch attachments
pitfalls.prevention: Retain the MEDIA: pattern and multipart part handling semantics or ensure tests for attachment extraction still pass

key_sections: EventBridge: core polling, mtime cache, queue mechanics and waiter semantics
helpers: _get_sessions_dir, _load_sessions_index, _get_session_db (used by EventBridge)
attachment helpers: _extract_message_content and _extract_attachments
