create_mcp_server: registers MCP tools and wires EventBridge into tool implementations
skip_unless_needed: top-of-file docstring and import try/except for FastMCP (boilerplate)
detailed MCP tool implementations if only inspecting EventBridge behavior

relevant_snippets.description: Fixtures: HERMES_HOME isolation, sessions_dir and sample_sessions which populate sessions.json used by the EventBridge and MCP tools.
Mock SessionDB creation (_create_test_db and mock_session_db) used to simulate state.db with messages stored as JSON strings or plain text.
EventBridge unit tests validating enqueue/poll/wait/queue-trimming and concurrency behavior.

related_files.relationship: Provides SessionDB implementation and schema for state.db which EventBridge polls. Edits to EventBridge assumptions about DB rows should be coordinated with hermes_state.
related_files.reason_to_check: If message schema or get_messages return shape changes, EventBridge parsing and tests must be updated.

related_files.relationship: get_hermes_home is used to resolve state.db and sessions.json locations; tests monkeypatch this function.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing HERMES_HOME resolution affects where sessions.json and state.db are read from; tests rely on monkeypatching this API.

related_files.relationship: FastMCP from mcp.server.fastmcp is required to create the MCP server; module gracefully fails with an ImportError if missing.
related_files.reason_to_check: MCP SDK API changes would require updates to create_mcp_server tool registration semantics.

tests/test_mcp_serve.py.short_description: Unit, EventBridge, and end-to-end tests for the Hermes MCP server and EventBridge.
tests/test_mcp_serve.py.description:
This test module verifies mcp_serve.py at three levels: pure unit tests for helper functions (content extraction and attachment parsing), unit/integration tests for the EventBridge queue and concurrency semantics, and end-to-end tests that exercise the MCP tools via FastMCP's tool manager using a real SQLite state.db and sessions.json. It contains fixtures that isolate HERMES_HOME to a temp directory, create a sample sessions.json, and create a test SQLite state.db with a messages table that mimics hermes_state's structure.

Tests intentionally use monkeypatching to override _get_sessions_dir, _get_session_db, and other internals to avoid requiring a running gateway. E2E tests are guarded by pytest.importorskip('mcp') so they run only when the MCP SDK is installed. The concurrency tests spawn threads to concurrently enqueue events into EventBridge to validate the locking and cursor increment behavior. Timing-sensitive tests use small sleeps and timeouts to ensure waiter semantics are exercised; these tests can be flaky if the environment is heavily loaded.

key_constructs.name: _isolate_hermes_home
key_constructs.purpose: Temporarily sets HERMES_HOME to a tmp_path and monkeypatches hermes_constants.get_hermes_home if available.
key_constructs.reasoning: All file-based helpers rely on HERMES_HOME; tests must isolate this to avoid reading real user data.

key_constructs.name: sessions_dir
key_constructs.purpose: Create and return a sessions directory (tmp_path/sessions) for tests to write sessions.json into.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by tests that read sessions.json directly; paired with monkeypatching in tests to point the code at this directory.

key_constructs.name: sample_sessions
key_constructs.purpose: Provide a sample sessions.json dict with three different platforms to test conversation listing and filtering.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to assert sorting and filtering behaviors of the conversations_list tool.

key_constructs.name: _create_test_db
key_constructs.purpose: Create a minimal SQLite state.db with sessions and messages tables and insert test messages.
key_constructs.reasoning: E2E tests need a realistic state.db; this function ensures consistent schema and stores message content as strings or JSON encoded blocks to match hermes_state behavior.

key_constructs.name: mock_session_db
key_constructs.purpose: Produces a TestSessionDB instance that reads from the test SQLite DB and provides get_messages(session_id).
key_constructs.reasoning: EventBridge._poll_once expects a SessionDB-like object; this fixture isolates DB reads to the temporary test DB.

key_constructs.name: _run_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that calls an MCP tool via FastMCP's tool manager and parses JSON result.
key_constructs.reasoning: E2E tests use this to exercise create_mcp_server tool implementations synchronously from tests.

key_behaviors: isolates HERMES_HOME for file-based tests
creates an ephemeral SQLite state.db for E2E tests
validates EventBridge queue/cursor semantics under concurrency
conditionally runs E2E tests only when mcp package is installed

pitfalls.mistake: Running E2E tests without the mcp SDK installed
pitfalls.consequence: E2E fixtures are skipped (importorskip), so tool registration and MCP tool assertions won't run; test coverage for MCP surface is lost
pitfalls.prevention: Install the mcp package in the test environment when E2E coverage is required; tests use pytest.importorskip for safety.

pitfalls.mistake: Not monkeypatching hermes_constants.get_hermes_home/HERMES_HOME
pitfalls.consequence: Tests may read or mutate a developer's real sessions.json/state.db and produce non-deterministic results
pitfalls.prevention: Use the provided _isolate_hermes_home fixture, which is autouse and ensures isolation.

pitfalls.mistake: Treating concurrency/time-dependent tests as strictly deterministic
pitfalls.consequence: Flaky tests in CI under heavy load or constrained CPU; wait-based tests may time out
pitfalls.prevention: When editing related code, run the concurrency and wait tests repeatedly and allow generous timeouts locally to verify stability.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the test DB schema or message storage format without updating _create_test_db or mock_session_db
pitfalls.consequence: E2E tests will fail because EventBridge expects particular columns and types returned by get_messages
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure test fixtures mirror any real hermes_state schema changes.

reading_guide.start_here: fixtures at top (_isolate_hermes_home, sessions_dir, sample_sessions, mock_session_db)
key_sections: Unit helper tests (TestHelpers, TestContentExtraction, TestAttachmentExtraction) that validate content/attachment parsing
EventBridge tests (TestEventBridge) that validate queue and concurrency behavior
E2E fixtures and tests that create the FastMCP server and call tools
skip_unless_needed: repetitive assertion blocks for list sorting or multiple similar cases — start with one and expand if debugging

relevant_snippets.description: Top-level docstring and imports; sets context for the three layers of tests.
Fixtures that prepare HERMES_HOME isolation, sample sessions.json, and sessions_dir.
Test DB creation helper (_create_test_db) and mock_session_db fixture which simulate hermes_state SessionDB returns.
EventBridge unit tests (enqueue/poll/wait/limits/concurrency/approvals).

related_files.relationship: Test target; the tests validate helpers, EventBridge, and MCP tool behaviors implemented in mcp_serve.py.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any behavioral changes in mcp_serve.py must be covered by updating tests or vice versa.

related_files.relationship: Monkeypatched by _isolate_hermes_home fixture to control get_hermes_home during tests.
related_files.reason_to_check: If hermes_constants.get_hermes_home API changes, tests must adapt their monkeypatches.

data_constants_to_check: POLL_INTERVAL and QUEUE_LIMIT if tests are timing/queue dependent

hermes_cli/plugins_cmd.py.short_description: CLI subcommand implementation for installing, updating, removing, enabling, disabling, and listing Hermes plugins.
hermes_cli/plugins_cmd.py.description:
This module implements the "hermes plugins" CLI subcommand and the core operations around plugin management. It exists to provide a user-facing, safe installer/updater/remover for plugins which are Git repositories cloned into the user's Hermes home under a plugins/ directory. The file centralises operations dealing with plugin filesystem layout, Git URL resolution, manifest reading, prompting for environment variables declared by a plugin, copying .example configuration files, and user-visible messages via Rich.

Design decisions reflected in the code: plugins are stored under get_hermes_home()/plugins to support profiles; installs are performed by cloning into a temporary directory then moved into place to allow manifest inspection and safe rollback; manifest_version compatibility is checked against a module-level _SUPPORTED_MANIFEST_VERSION; plugin names are validated and sanitized to strictly prevent path traversal and accidental resolution outside the plugins directory; environment variables required by plugins are surfaced and saved via hermes_cli.config helpers; interactive flows use getpass for secret values and respect KeyboardInterrupt/EOF to avoid blocking install. Subprocess interactions with git are used for clone and pull operations and are guarded for errors, timeouts, and missing git binaries.

key_constructs.name: _plugins_dir
key_constructs.purpose: Return/create the user's plugins directory under HERMES_HOME and ensure it exists.
key_constructs.reasoning: Other operations rely on a single canonical plugins directory; modifying this function changes where plugins are stored and should preserve directory creation semantics and use get_hermes_home().

key_constructs.name: _sanitize_plugin_name
key_constructs.purpose: Validate a user- or manifest-provided plugin name and return a safe Path inside the plugins directory.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function enforces critical path traversal protections and sanity checks (rejects '/', '\\', '..', '.', empty names). Changing it must maintain the same security guarantees and not allow names that resolve outside the plugins dir.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_git_url
key_constructs.purpose: Normalize an identifier into a cloneable Git URL (supports owner/repo shorthand and several URL schemes).
key_constructs.reasoning: Command dispatch uses this to accept both shorthand (owner/repo) and full URLs. Keep accepted schemes and error messaging consistent; tests rely on specific ValueError messages for invalid inputs.

key_constructs.name: _repo_name_from_url
key_constructs.purpose: Derive a repository directory name from a Git URL (strip .git and trailing slashes, handle ssh-style URLs).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to generate a default plugin name when plugin.yaml does not declare a name. Behavior around stripping '.git', trailing slashes, and ssh-style ':' separators matters for deterministic naming and is asserted in tests.

key_constructs.name: _read_manifest
key_constructs.purpose: Read plugin.yaml from an installed or downloaded plugin directory and return a dict, or an empty dict on missing/invalid YAML.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function is tolerant (returns {}) for missing or invalid manifests and logs a warning on parse errors. Tests assert that invalid YAML returns {} and logs a warning; any change must preserve that contract and logging level.

key_constructs.name: _copy_example_files
key_constructs.purpose: Copy any *.example files in a plugin to their non-.example counterparts if they don't already exist.
key_constructs.reasoning: This protects user config by skipping overwrites and prints status via the provided console. Tests patch shutil.copy2 to simulate errors and expect graceful handling; do not change overwrite semantics or exception swallowing.

key_constructs.name: _prompt_plugin_env_vars
key_constructs.purpose: Interactively prompt for environment variables declared in plugin.yaml (supports simple list and rich dict entries) and save them to the user's .env via hermes_cli.config helpers.
key_constructs.reasoning: This integrates with save_env_value and get_env_value and sets os.environ for immediate availability. It respects 'secret' flags by using getpass and handles EOF/KeyboardInterrupt by skipping further prompts. Tests verify multiple paths including secret input, skipping when already set, and graceful interrupt handling.

key_constructs.name: cmd_install
key_constructs.purpose: High-level install flow: resolve URL, clone to temp, read manifest, validate name/manifest_version, move into plugins dir, copy example files, prompt for env vars, and print after-install message.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the primary entrypoint for installing plugins and orchestrates subprocess calls to git. It performs safety checks (manifest_version vs _SUPPORTED_MANIFEST_VERSION), enforces --force semantics (removes existing plugin directory before moving), and prints user guidance about restarting gateway. Tests mock subprocess.run, filesystem helpers, and check sys.exit codes for failure modes; preserve exit paths and error messages.

key_constructs.name: cmd_update
key_constructs.purpose: Update an installed plugin by running 'git pull' in the plugin directory and copying new .example files.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates that the plugin exists and that it was installed from git (.git dir exists). Uses subprocess.run with cwd; tests mock run and assert behavior on success and failure paths (including sys.exit when plugin not found). Keep timeout and error semantics consistent for test expectations.

key_constructs.name: cmd_remove
key_constructs.purpose: Remove an installed plugin directory (calls shutil.rmtree) after verifying existence.
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs existence check and prints confirmation via _display_removed. Tests expect SystemExit with code 1 when plugin missing, and that rmtree is called with the sanitized Path on success.

key_constructs.name: cmd_list
key_constructs.purpose: List installed plugins and display manifest-supplied metadata if available.
key_constructs.reasoning: Iterates plugins_dir.iterdir(), filters directories, and uses _read_manifest for display. Tests cover empty directories and a single plugin with manifest present; display formatting is side-effect-driven and should remain stable for tests that assert no exceptions are raised.

key_constructs.name: plugins_command
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level dispatch that maps parsed CLI args (plugins_action etc.) to the appropriate cmd_* function.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the CLI dispatcher used by CLI entrypoints/tests: it accepts aliases (rm/uninstall → cmd_remove, ls → cmd_list, None → cmd_toggle). Tests patch internal cmd_* functions and assert correct dispatching calls; maintain argument names and alias mappings.

key_constructs.name: _get_disabled_set
key_constructs.purpose: Read and return the set of disabled plugins from hermes_cli.config load_config.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to maintain plugin enable/disable state in config.yaml. It must tolerate exceptions and return an empty set on failure (used by enable/disable flows).

key_constructs.name: _save_disabled_set
key_constructs.purpose: Persist a set/list of disabled plugins to config.yaml via save_config.
key_constructs.reasoning: Writes sorted list back into the config structure under plugins.disabled. Modifying storage keys or shape requires coordinating with other code that reads this config.

key_constructs.name: _require_installed_plugin
key_constructs.purpose: Verify plugin existence by sanitizing name and checking presence; on missing, prints installed list and exits with error.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralises the common existence check used by remove/update commands and emits consistent error messages and exit code; tests depend on this exit behavior and printed installed list formatting.

key_behaviors: installs plugin from git repository into plugins directory
validates and sanitizes plugin names to prevent path traversal
reads and tolerantly parses plugin manifests
prompts and saves environment variables declared by plugins
copies *.example configuration files without overwriting existing user files
updates and removes plugins via git operations or filesystem removal
enables/disables plugins by modifying CLI config

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying _sanitize_plugin_name to relax character checks or resolution checks
pitfalls.consequence: May allow path traversal or permit plugin names resolving outside the plugins directory, causing security issues or accidental file removal.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve existing checks against '/', '\\', '..', '.' and the relative_to validation against the plugins dir.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing subprocess.run invocation signatures, return-code handling, or exception types raised on failures
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that mock subprocess.run and assert sys.exit codes, stdout/stderr handling, and timeout behavior will break; user-visible error messaging may change.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep timeout, capture_output/text arguments, and returncode checks as-is or update tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering _read_manifest to raise on parse errors instead of returning {} and logging a warning
pitfalls.consequence: Install/update flow may abort unexpectedly and tests that expect graceful handling will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain tolerant behavior: return {} for missing/invalid YAML and log a warning at WARNING level.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing how environment variables are saved/displayed (not using display_hermes_home or save_env_value/get_env_value)
pitfalls.consequence: Could break profile-aware path displays and the tests which patch hermes_cli.config helpers; user .env may be written to wrong location.
pitfalls.prevention: Continue using hermes_cli.config.get_env_value and save_env_value and display_hermes_home for user-facing paths.

key_sections: cmd_install: main orchestration for clone -> manifest -> move -> env prompt -> display
_prompt_plugin_env_vars: interactive flow for required env vars (handles secret and interrupt)
_sanitize_plugin_name: security-critical name validation and path resolution
_read_manifest and _copy_example_files: manifest parsing and example config handling
skip_unless_needed: display helper functions (_display_after_install, _display_removed) — purely UI formatting
enable/disable toggle helper details if only working on install/remove flows

relevant_snippets.description: Sanitize, resolve URL, repo name, and dispatch tests (covers core validation and dispatch behavior).
Install/update/remove/list tests and interactive env-var prompting tests; mocks subprocess and filesystem operations.

related_files.relationship: Reads and writes CLI config and .env values; _prompt_plugin_env_vars depends on get_env_value and save_env_value and _get_disabled_set/_save_disabled_set use load_config/save_config.
related_files.reason_to_check: When modifying how env vars are saved or how disabled plugins are stored, ensure config.py's API and keys remain compatible.

related_files.relationship: Provides get_hermes_home and display_hermes_home used to compute plugin storage and user-facing .env path.
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing where plugins are stored or how HERMES_HOME is resolved, update this module or usage accordingly.

insights.title: Prompt for required environment variables during plugin install
insights.problem: After installing a plugin, required environment variables declared by the plugin were not surfaced to the user and had to be manually added to ~/.hermes/.env.
insights.root_cause: No interactive flow to prompt for requires_env after install; manifests support both simple and rich formats but there was no code to handle them.
insights.solution: Implemented _prompt_plugin_env_vars that normalizes simple string entries and rich dict entries, skips already-set vars, supports secret input with getpass, saves values via save_env_value, sets os.environ, and integrates prompting after install.
insights.lesson_learned: Installer UX should surface plugin-required secrets immediately; support both simple and rich manifest formats and never leak secrets (use getpass for secret inputs).

insights.title: Use display_hermes_home() when showing .env paths (profile-aware)
insights.problem: User-facing messages referenced a hardcoded ~/.hermes/.env path, which is incorrect when profiles change HERMES_HOME.
insights.root_cause: Hardcoded path strings did not respect per-profile HERMES_HOME abstraction.
insights.solution: Use display_hermes_home() to construct the path shown to the user (e.g., {display_hermes_home()}/.env).
insights.lesson_learned: User-visible path messages should be derived from configuration helpers to remain correct when profiles or HERMES_HOME vary.

tests/test_plugins_cmd.py.short_description: Unit tests for the hermes_cli.plugins_cmd plugin management CLI behaviors.
tests/test_plugins_cmd.py.description:
This test module provides comprehensive unit coverage for hermes_cli.plugins_cmd, exercising validation logic, URL resolution, repository name extraction, manifest parsing, install/update/remove/list flows, .example file copying, and interactive prompting for environment variables. Tests heavily use unittest.mock.patch to replace filesystem interactions, subprocess.run (git), and hermes_cli.config helpers, isolating CLI logic from external side effects. Certain tests assert SystemExit codes and console output behavior that represent the CLI's user-facing contract.

The tests intentionally simulate error conditions (invalid YAML, missing git, permission errors when copying example files, KeyboardInterrupt during prompting) to verify that plugin install/update/remove flows fail gracefully and that the module logs or prints appropriate messages. Tests also validate alias dispatching in plugins_command to ensure backward-compatible CLI aliases. When editing the implementation, these tests serve as a behavioural specification for the expected exit codes, message patterns and side-effects (e.g., saving env vars via save_env_value).

key_constructs.name: TestSanitizePluginName.test_valid_simple_name
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that a simple plugin name resolves to a path inside the provided plugins dir.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates expected plumbing and path resolution used by installer flows.

key_constructs.name: TestResolveGitUrl.test_owner_repo_shorthand
key_constructs.purpose: Checks owner/repo shorthand expands to GitHub https URL with .git suffix.
key_constructs.reasoning: Defines shorthand parsing contract used by cmd_install.

key_constructs.name: TestRepoNameFromUrl.test_https_with_dot_git
key_constructs.purpose: Ensures repo name extraction strips .git suffix.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used as fallback plugin naming when manifest omits name.

key_constructs.name: TestCmdInstall.test_install_rejects_manifest_name_pointing_at_plugins_root
key_constructs.purpose: Simulates a manifest declaring name '.' and asserts install exits with code 1 and does not move or remove directories.
key_constructs.reasoning: Protects against manifests that attempt to install into the plugins root; verifies defensive checks in cmd_install.

key_constructs.name: TestPromptPluginEnvVars.test_prompts_for_missing_var_simple_format
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies that when a manifest requires an env var and it's not set, input() is called and save_env_value is used to persist the value.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests the interactive save path and integration with hermes_cli.config.save_env_value.

key_behaviors: asserts path-sanitization and path-traversal protections
asserts correct URL resolution and repo name extraction
mocks git subprocesses for clone/pull and validates CLI error paths
verifies copying of .example files and graceful error reporting
verifies interactive prompting and saving of required env vars

pitfalls.mistake: Altering function names, signatures, or exit codes without updating tests
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will fail; the CLI contract (including exit codes and printed messages) will be considered broken by the test suite.
pitfalls.prevention: Run the test file after any change; maintain the exact exceptions, exit codes, and message patterns that tests assert.

pitfalls.mistake: Making tests rely on the real filesystem or calling real git
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will become flaky or slow and may perform network operations affecting CI.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep mocking approach used in the file (patch subprocess.run, patch _plugins_dir/_read_manifest when needed) or update tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the behavior of hermes_cli.config API (get_env_value/save_env_value/load_config/save_config) without updating test patches
pitfalls.consequence: Prompting tests and enable/disable tests that patch these helpers will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure any API changes in config.py are propagated to tests or adapt tests to new APIs.

reading_guide.start_here: top-level imports and helper function tests (TestSanitizePluginName, TestResolveGitUrl) to understand fundamental validation
key_sections: Tests for name sanitization and URL parsing — these define core validation contracts
Install/update/remove/list tests — mock subprocess and filesystem to validate flows and exit codes
PromptPluginEnvVars tests — cover interactive paths and error/interrupt handling
skip_unless_needed: Verbose test scaffolding for mocking console prints — focus on behavioral assertions and exit codes instead

relevant_snippets.description: Contains foundational validation and dispatch tests (sanitization, URL resolution, repo name, and plugins_command dispatch).
Contains install/update/remove/list, example file copying, and interactive env-var prompting tests that mock subprocess and filesystem operations.

related_files.relationship: Test target — these tests define the expected behaviors and error paths for the functions implemented in this file.
related_files.reason_to_check: When changing behavior in plugins_cmd, update tests in this file to match the new contract.

related_files.relationship: Mocked by tests for get_env_value/save_env_value/load_config/save_config. Tests depend on these helpers' signatures.
related_files.reason_to_check: If config.py changes, this test file's patches and expectations must be updated as well.

insights.title: Unit tests for plugin install env var prompting
insights.problem: New interactive prompting logic requires unit tests for normal, rich-format, secret, skip, and interrupt paths.
insights.root_cause: Interactive paths have multiple edge cases (already-set, secret input, KeyboardInterrupt) which need coverage.
insights.solution: Added tests verifying behavior for simple and rich manifest formats, secrets via getpass, empty input skipping, and graceful KeyboardInterrupt handling.
insights.lesson_learned: Interactive CLI features must be tested for success, skip, and abort scenarios to avoid blocking UX regressions.

hermes_cli/plugins.py.short_description: Plugin discovery, loading, and runtime management for Hermes plugins (directory + entry-point + project).
hermes_cli/plugins.py.description:
This module implements the Hermes plugin subsystem: it discovers plugins from three sources (user HERMES_HOME plugins dir, optional project ./.hermes/plugins, and pip entry points), loads them into a managed namespace, and exposes a runtime manager and a small facade (PluginContext) that plugins use to register tools, CLI commands, and lifecycle hooks. The design intentionally isolates plugin side-effects: plugins must provide a register(ctx) function and they receive a limited PluginContext that delegates tool registration into the tools.registry and records hooks and registered tool names on the manager. The manager collects plugin-provided hooks and exposes invoke_hook so the core agent can call plugin callbacks safely and receive non-None return values.

The module centralizes concerns that must be observed when editing plugin-related behavior: discovery is idempotent (guarded by self._discovered), plugin disabling can be driven by the configuration loader (load_config), and entry-point scanning supports multiple importlib.metadata shapes for Python 3.10/3.11/3.12 compatibility. Directory plugins are imported into a synthetic namespace package 'hermes_plugins' by manipulating sys.modules and creating a module spec from the plugin __init__.py, which makes plugin packages importable without installing them. Hook invocation wraps each callback in try/except and returns a list of non-None results — this return behavior is used by other subsystems (e.g., pre-LLM context injection) and is covered by tests. The module also gracefully handles optional PyYAML (yaml may be None) — if missing, directory manifest loading is skipped with a warning.

Key design decisions visible in the code: (1) plugin manifests are validated/parsed from YAML into PluginManifest dataclasses; (2) PluginContext is intentionally minimal and delegates to existing registries instead of directly mutating core state; (3) plugin errors are recorded on LoadedPlugin and do not raise to crash discovery; (4) entry-point loading is robust to importlib.metadata API changes and wraps load() calls with error handling. Because the manager mutates global module state (sys.modules) and central registries (tools.registry), edits to this file must respect global side-effects, thread/CLI state, and the semantics of the public API that tests and other modules rely on.

key_constructs.name: VALID_HOOKS
key_constructs.purpose: Set of allowed lifecycle hook names that plugins may register.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the authoritative list of hook names the core expects; other code (and tests) rely on its contents and on the fact that unknown hook names still get recorded but produce a warning.

key_constructs.name: ENTRY_POINTS_GROUP
key_constructs.purpose: Name of the importlib entry-point group to discover pip-installed plugins.
key_constructs.reasoning: Entry-point scanning uses this constant; changing it affects discovery of pip-installed plugins and tests that mock importlib.metadata.entry_points.

key_constructs.name: PluginManifest
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass representing parsed plugin manifest fields (name, version, requires_env, etc.).
key_constructs.reasoning: This struct is used to carry manifest metadata through discovery and loading; its fields (e.g., requires_env) are referenced by consumer code and tests expect these fields to exist and hold parsed values.

key_constructs.name: LoadedPlugin
key_constructs.purpose: Dataclass tracking runtime state of a plugin (manifest, module, tools/hook lists, enabled, error).
key_constructs.reasoning: Discovery and load operations create LoadedPlugin entries stored in the manager; listing, diagnostics, and tests read these fields (enabled, error) to assert plugin load outcomes.

key_constructs.name: PluginContext
key_constructs.purpose: Facade passed to plugin register(ctx) to allow registering tools, hooks, CLI commands, and injecting messages.
key_constructs.reasoning: All plugin interactions are funneled through this context — it delegates to tools.registry and mutates the manager's internal structures. Editors must preserve API surface and side-effects (e.g., register_tool delegates, register_hook appends to manager._hooks, inject_message interacts with CLI queues).

key_constructs.name: PluginManager
key_constructs.purpose: Manages discovery, loading, state, and invocation of plugins.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the core object used by the rest of the app to enumerate plugins, call their hooks, and introspect registered tools/commands. Its public methods (discover_and_load, list_plugins, invoke_hook) are exercised by tests and other modules and changing semantics will affect many components.

key_constructs.name: _env_enabled
key_constructs.purpose: Wrapper to check whether a named environment opt-in flag is truthy.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to gate project-plugin discovery (HERMES_ENABLE_PROJECT_PLUGINS) and other optional behavior. It delegates to utils.env_var_enabled so any change must keep compatibility.

key_constructs.name: _get_disabled_plugins
key_constructs.purpose: Reads config (via hermes_cli.config.load_config) to get plugins disabled by user config.
key_constructs.reasoning: Discovery respects user disabled list and records disabled plugins in manager._plugins with an error message; tests expect disabled behavior to result in stored LoadedPlugin entries with enabled=False.

key_constructs.name: _scan_directory
key_constructs.purpose: Scans a plugins directory for plugin.yaml/yml files and parses them into PluginManifest objects.
key_constructs.reasoning: Handles optional PyYAML import and reports parsing failures; it is responsible for reading on-disk manifests for user/project plugins.

key_constructs.name: _scan_entry_points
key_constructs.purpose: Discover pip-installed plugins via importlib.metadata.entry_points.
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains logic to handle different return shapes of entry_points() across Python versions (select vs dict vs list), which is important for cross-version compatibility.

key_constructs.name: _load_directory_module
key_constructs.purpose: Load a directory-based plugin by creating a module spec and executing its __init__.py into the hermes_plugins namespace.
key_constructs.reasoning: Manipulates sys.modules and creates a synthetic parent package; this global mutation is relied upon by tests that assert sys.modules contains 'hermes_plugins.<name>'.

key_constructs.name: _load_entrypoint_module
key_constructs.purpose: Locate and load a pip entry-point plugin by name using importlib.metadata entry points.
key_constructs.reasoning: Finds the matching entry point and calls ep.load(); errors are converted into ImportError and recorded on LoadedPlugin.

key_constructs.name: invoke_hook
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level convenience that proxies to PluginManager.invoke_hook and returns collected, non-None results.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests and other modules import invoke_hook from the module; its behavior (collect non-None returns, swallow exceptions) is relied on by code that expects plugin-returned context to be delivered.

key_behaviors: discovers plugins from user, project, and pip entry points
loads plugin modules into a hermes_plugins namespace
provides PluginContext for registering tools/hooks/commands
invokes registered hook callbacks and collects non-None returns

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or renaming VALID_HOOKS entries without updating tests and consumers.
pitfalls.consequence: Plugins may register hooks that the core no longer recognizes or tests asserting presence of specific hooks will fail; unknown hooks currently warn but are still stored.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect VALID_HOOKS as the authoritative set; update tests and external callers when altering it.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing how directory plugins are imported (module name or namespace) or altering sys.modules usage.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that assert module presence under hermes_plugins.<name> will fail; runtime imports of plugin modules could break in gateways or CLI.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the hermes_plugins namespace behavior or coordinate changes with tests and any code that imports plugin modules directly.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming yaml is always available; removing the conditional handling for optional PyYAML.
pitfalls.consequence: Discovery will raise at import time in environments without PyYAML, breaking startup in CI or minimal installs.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep yaml optional handling and make sure directory manifest scanning is skipped/handled gracefully when PyYAML is absent.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying invoke_hook to raise plugin exceptions or to include None returns.
pitfalls.consequence: A misbehaving plugin could crash normal flow or downstream code expecting only non-None results and exception swallowing semantics will change, breaking tests relying on current contract.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve current semantics: exceptions swallowed with a warning, collect only non-None return values.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering _scan_entry_points without preserving support for importlib.metadata differences across Python versions.
pitfalls.consequence: Entry-point discovery might break on some Python releases and tests that mock entry_points behavior will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Retain branching for .select, dict-return, and list-return shapes when interacting with entry_points().

key_sections: _scan_directory: parses on-disk plugin manifests (user/project)
_scan_entry_points: handles pip-installed plugin discovery with entry_points compatibility
_load_directory_module: imports directory plugins into the hermes_plugins namespace
PluginContext.register_tool/hook/inject_message: plugin-to-core integration points
skip_unless_needed: module-level constants and trivial helpers (_env_enabled, _get_disabled_plugins) if you only need high-level flow

related_files.relationship: PluginContext.register_tool delegates to tools.registry.register; tools registry is the authoritative place tools are stored.
related_files.reason_to_check: If registration API or registry internals change, plugin register_tool must be updated to match.

related_files.relationship: Config loader is used by _get_disabled_plugins to determine plugins disabled in config.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to config structure or key names (plugins.disabled) affect plugin disabling behavior.

related_files.relationship: invoke_hook return values (pre_llm_call) are consumed by routing/LLM invocation code to inject ephemeral context.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing invoke_hook semantics impacts how injected context is applied to user messages.

insights.title: PluginManifest.requires_env supports rich format
insights.problem: Plugins may declare required env vars in either simple string list or rich dict-with-metadata. The manifest type hinted only a simple list.
insights.root_cause: Type hint didn't reflect richer spec introduced by plugin install prompt feature.
insights.solution: Updated requires_env typing to List[Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]].
insights.lesson_learned: Manifest type hints should reflect actual accepted manifest shapes to help callers and linters; when adding richer manifest formats keep types in sync.

insights.title: Request-scoped plugin hooks renamed and payload narrowed
insights.problem: Plugin lifecycle hooks that were request-scoped previously exposed large/complex payloads and used names that conflicted with per-turn hooks.
insights.root_cause: Hook naming and payload shape conflated per-turn and per-API-request semantics (pre_llm_call vs per-request pre_llm_request), and plugins relied on full objects (messages, response) which is fragile and heavyweight.
insights.solution: Introduce new hook names pre_api_request and post_api_request (replacing pre_llm_request/post_llm_request) and restrict them to narrow summary kwargs (message_count, tool_count, usage summary, response_model, assistant_content_chars). Keep pre_llm_call/post_llm_call for per-turn hooks.
insights.lesson_learned: Plugin hook contracts must be minimal and stable; prefer summary fields over raw provider objects to avoid breaking plugins when internal response shapes change and to limit memory/exposure.

insights.title: invoke_hook returns non-None results; hook invocation activated centrally
insights.problem: Plugins couldn't return data to the caller (e.g. inject ephemeral context) because invoke_hook returned None and wasn't collecting returns.
insights.root_cause: invoke_hook was fire-and-forget, returning nothing.
insights.solution: Changed PluginManager.invoke_hook to return list of non-None return values. Updated top-level invoke_hook to return that list.

tests/test_plugins.py.short_description: Unit tests exercising plugin discovery, loading, hook semantics, and tool visibility.
tests/test_plugins.py.description:
This test module validates the behavior and contracts of the hermes_cli.plugins subsystem. It constructs temporary plugin directories (via helper _make_plugin_dir) and uses monkeypatching to drive environment variables (HERMES_HOME, HERMES_ENABLE_PROJECT_PLUGINS), as well as mocking importlib.metadata.entry_points to simulate pip-installed plugins. Tests exercise discovery idempotence, skipping directories without manifests, entry point loading, error handling when __init__.py or register() are missing, namespace import behavior, hook registration/invocation semantics (including exception swallowing and return value collection), integration with the tools registry via PluginContext.register_tool, and visibility of plugin tools through model_tools.get_tool_definitions.

The tests are organized into multiple test classes that group related behavior (discovery, loading, hooks, context, tool visibility, listing and pre-LLM hook routing). Each test uses temporary filesystem state and env var monkeypatching to avoid reliance on developer machine configuration. Many tests assert state stored on the PluginManager instance (mgr._plugins entries and their .enabled/.error fields) or that the hermes_plugins module appears in sys.modules for directory plugins. The tests also check cross-version-friendly behavior by mocking importlib.metadata.entry_points() shape using a small fake that defines .select or dict-like behavior.

Because these tests interact with global registries and sys.modules, they clean up or set expectations accordingly (example: sys.modules.pop('hermes_plugins.ns_plugin', None)). Tests are comprehensive for the visible plugin API surface and are the canonical source of expected behavior for discovery, loading, and hook invocation contracts.

key_constructs.name: _make_plugin_dir
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that creates a minimal plugin directory with plugin.yaml and __init__.py for tests.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used repeatedly to create on-disk plugin fixtures; changes to its behavior or signature will affect many tests.

key_constructs.name: TestPluginDiscovery
key_constructs.purpose: Test group asserting discovery semantics (user/project/entrypoint and idempotence).
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains tests that rely on environment variables and temporary directories to verify discovery logic.

key_constructs.name: TestPluginLoading
key_constructs.purpose: Test group asserting how plugins are loaded and error cases (missing __init__, missing register).
key_constructs.reasoning: Checks error recording behavior on LoadedPlugin and namespace import behavior.

key_constructs.name: TestPluginHooks
key_constructs.purpose: Group of tests for hook registration, exception handling, and return value collection.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates invoke_hook semantics (exception swallowing and collection of non-None returns).

key_constructs.name: TestPluginContext
key_constructs.purpose: Tests PluginContext.register_tool integration with global tools.registry.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures plugin-registered tools appear in the global tool registry and are tracked by the manager.

key_constructs.name: TestPluginToolVisibility
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies plugin tools appear in get_tool_definitions when toolsets are enabled and are excluded otherwise.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures plugin tools respect toolset filters used by the model tooling path.

key_constructs.name: TestPluginManagerList
key_constructs.purpose: Tests list_plugins() formatting and sorting behavior.
key_constructs.reasoning: Checks that the manager exposes a stable, sorted listing and includes expected keys.

key_constructs.name: TestPreLlmCallTargetRouting
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies pre_llm_call hook return formats used by routing logic (context dicts, plain strings, target keys).
key_constructs.reasoning: Although routing lives elsewhere, these tests assert invoke_hook returns the expected shapes consumed by routing code.

key_behaviors: creates temporary plugin directories for discovery tests
monkeypatches env vars to simulate different discovery modes
mocks importlib.metadata.entry_points for entry-point testing
asserts hook invocation semantics including exception swallowing and return collection

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming tests run in isolation without resetting global registries or sys.modules.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests may leak state between tests leading to false positives/negatives (e.g., a previously-registered tool remains in registry).
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure edits keep or add explicit cleanup in tests if adding new global state mutations; be mindful when changing code that affects tools.registry or sys.modules.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing plugin discovery semantics (env var gating, manifest parsing fields) without updating tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Numerous tests that assert discovery presence/absence or manifest-required fields will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing discovery contracts, run this test module and adapt fixtures accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering invoke_hook semantics (return values, exception handling) without updating tests that assert these behaviors.
pitfalls.consequence: Several tests will fail because they expect non-None results to be collected and exceptions to be swallowed.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve current invoke_hook contract or update tests to the new contract.

reading_guide.start_here: _make_plugin_dir (top) to understand how fixtures are built
key_sections: TestPluginDiscovery: how environment variables affect discovery
TestPluginLoading: behavior when plugin __init__ or register() are missing
TestPluginHooks: expected invoke_hook contract (exception handling & return collection)
TestPluginContext/TestPluginToolVisibility: interaction between PluginContext and tools.registry
skip_unless_needed: Repeated assertions and small helper usages once you understand the fixture pattern

related_files.relationship: Under test — this file contains the implementation the tests exercise.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to public plugin API or behavior requires updating these tests.

related_files.relationship: TestPluginContext interacts with the tools registry to verify plugin tool registration.
related_files.reason_to_check: If tool registry internals or API change, tests that assert registry state will need updates.

related_files.relationship: TestPluginToolVisibility calls get_tool_definitions to verify plugin tool visibility.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to toolset filtering or how tool definitions are produced will affect these tests.

insights.title: Tests added for plugin invoke_hook return collection
insights.problem: Ensure hook return values are collected and None returns excluded.
insights.root_cause: Change in invoke_hook behavior requires tests.
insights.solution: Added tests that create plugins returning dict/context or None and assert invoke_hook returned list semantics.

tools/approval.py.short_description: Central dangerous-command detection, per-session approval state, prompting and persistent allowlist.
tools/approval.py.description:
This module is the single source of truth for dangerous command handling in the agent. It detects dangerous shell/SQL/file-system commands via a curated list of regular expressions, manages per-session and permanent approvals (thread-safe), orchestrates blocking approvals for gateway flows (queue + threading.Event per pending request), implements a CLI prompt flow for manual approval, and exposes hooks for smart/auxiliary LLM-based auto-approval. The file centralizes multiple responsibilities because dangerous command detection and approval state are cross-cutting concerns referenced by CLI, gateway, and agent execution paths.

Design choices: detection is implemented with a list of regex patterns (DANGEROUS_PATTERNS) each paired with a human-readable description; detection normalizes input via strip_ansi, null-byte removal and Unicode NFKC to prevent obfuscation bypasses. Session identity is managed via a contextvars.ContextVar with a process-env fallback (HERMES_SESSION_KEY) to support concurrent gateway threads where a process-global env var is racy. Concurrency primitives (threading.Lock and threading.Event) protect global approval state: pending approvals, session approvals, permanent allowlist, gateway notify callbacks, and per-session approval queues. The module also includes helpers to load/save a persistent allowlist from hermes_cli.config and aliases old regex-derived keys to new human-readable pattern keys for backward compatibility.

key_constructs.name: DANGEROUS_PATTERNS
key_constructs.purpose: List of (regex, description) tuples defining commands that should trigger approval.
key_constructs.reasoning: Patterns are centralized to ensure all detection and approval UIs/flows use the same criteria. Regex + description allows both pattern matching and readable approval keys.

key_constructs.name: _normalize_command_for_detection
key_constructs.purpose: Preprocesses a command string (strip ANSI, remove null bytes, Unicode NFKC) before regex matching.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents evasion via ANSI escapes, nulls, or fullwidth/halfwidth Unicode variants; any change must preserve these normalizations to avoid bypasses.

key_constructs.name: detect_dangerous_command
key_constructs.purpose: Check a command string against DANGEROUS_PATTERNS and return (is_dangerous, pattern_key, description).
key_constructs.reasoning: Consumers rely on the returned pattern_key/description to decide approval flow and storage (session/permanent). The function lowercases the normalized command and uses re.search with IGNORECASE|DOTALL to match across newlines.

key_constructs.name: _legacy_pattern_key
key_constructs.purpose: Derives the historical regex-based approval key for backwards compatibility.
key_constructs.reasoning: Existing stored allowlist entries or session approvals may use this legacy key; edits affecting key derivation must preserve compatibility or update the alias mapping accordingly.

key_constructs.name: _approval_key_aliases
key_constructs.purpose: Return a set of alias keys (canonical + legacy) for a pattern key.
key_constructs.reasoning: is_approved() and related functions check all aliases; changes that change aliasing semantics affect whether previous allowlist entries remain valid.

key_constructs.name: set_current_session_key
key_constructs.purpose: Bind the active approval session key to the current context (contextvars).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by gateway runner to isolate approvals per inbound session; callers must use the returned token with reset_current_session_key to restore prior context.

key_constructs.name: reset_current_session_key
key_constructs.purpose: Restore previous contextvar session key using the token returned by set_current_session_key.
key_constructs.reasoning: Proper use prevents leaking session identity between concurrently running agent turns.

key_constructs.name: get_current_session_key
key_constructs.purpose: Return the active session key, preferring context-local value then HERMES_SESSION_KEY env var and finally default.
key_constructs.reasoning: Many callers (approval checks) use this to identify which session's approvals/state to consult; tests assert contextvar overrides env var.

key_constructs.name: _ApprovalEntry
key_constructs.purpose: Container for a pending gateway approval: threading.Event, data dict, and result string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to block agent threads until the gateway approves/denies; modifications to its fields must maintain event semantics and thread-safety expectations.

key_constructs.name: register_gateway_notify
key_constructs.purpose: Register a per-session callback to notify users of pending approvals (bridges sync→async).
key_constructs.reasoning: Gateway adapters register callbacks to send approval messages; removal/registration must be guarded by the module lock.

key_constructs.name: resolve_gateway_approval
key_constructs.purpose: Called by gateway /approve or /deny to resolve one or all pending approvals for a session, unblocking waiting threads.
key_constructs.reasoning: Resolves entries by setting entry.result and event.set(); returns number resolved. Must preserve FIFO behavior and safe clearing of _gateway_queues.

key_constructs.name: approve_session
key_constructs.purpose: Add a pattern_key to session-scoped approvals for the given session_key.
key_constructs.reasoning: Session approvals are stored in _session_approved; is_approved consults these sets (with alias expansion) to allow commands without prompting.

key_constructs.name: is_approved
key_constructs.purpose: Check whether a pattern is approved (permanent or session), considering key aliases.
key_constructs.reasoning: Consumers rely on this to skip approval prompts; edits must not break alias check or concurrent access semantics.

key_constructs.name: load_permanent_allowlist
key_constructs.purpose: Load permanent allowlist entries from hermes_cli config and sync them into module state.
key_constructs.reasoning: Called during module import (commit history) to ensure persistent allowlist is available globally; its failure is swallowed and returns an empty set, so callers must not assume config access is always successful.

key_constructs.name: save_permanent_allowlist
key_constructs.purpose: Persist permanent allowlist back to hermes_cli config.
key_constructs.reasoning: Wraps load_config/save_config and logs warnings on failure; ensure exceptions are not allowed to escape.

key_behaviors: detects dangerous shell/SQL/file commands
maintains per-session approval state
queues and blocks agent threads awaiting user gateway approval
loads/saves persistent command allowlist

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying regex patterns without accounting for normalization (ANSI strip, null-byte removal, Unicode NFKC).
pitfalls.consequence: Commands could be incorrectly allowed or incorrectly flagged because obfuscation techniques may bypass detection.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve and respect _normalize_command_for_detection behavior; update tests when adding patterns that rely on normalization.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing session identity semantics (contextvar vs env fallback) or removing set/reset contract.
pitfalls.consequence: Gateway concurrent runs could associate approvals with wrong sessions or leak approvals across sessions.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect contextvars usage: callers must call set_current_session_key and later reset_current_session_key with the returned token.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering key-aliasing logic (_legacy_pattern_key/_PATTERN_KEY_ALIASES) or canonical key strings without preserving aliases.
pitfalls.consequence: Previously saved allowlist entries (from config) may stop matching and users lose prior approvals; tests assert alias behavior and uniqueness.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing keys/aliases, update _PATTERN_KEY_ALIASES logic and all tests that assert uniqueness or alias matching.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing threading lock or event semantics around _gateway_queues/_pending/_session_approved.
pitfalls.consequence: Races leading to lost approvals, deadlocks, or unblocked threads; gateway approve handlers may not wake the correct waiting thread(s).
pitfalls.prevention: Keep use of _lock around shared state mutations and ensure Event.set() semantics remain for all queued entries during unregister/clear operations.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming hermes_cli.config exists or raising on config load/save.
pitfalls.consequence: Module import-time errors or failing test runs in CI where environment differs.
pitfalls.prevention: Note that load_permanent_allowlist wraps config access in try/except; preserve that behavior if reworking persistence.

key_sections: _normalize_command_for_detection: input normalization and why it matters
DANGEROUS_PATTERNS: canonical regexes and descriptions (primary source of detection rules)
_PATTERN_KEY_ALIASES/_legacy_pattern_key: legacy vs canonical key handling for persisted approvals
gateway queue code (register_gateway_notify, _ApprovalEntry, resolve_gateway_approval): blocking flow for gateway
skip_unless_needed: _smart_approve and auxiliary client integration (LLM auto-approval) if not working on smart approval
load/save config wrappers if not changing persistence behavior

relevant_snippets.description: Tests parsing of approval mode config values (_get_approval_mode).
Basic detection tests for rm recursive flags and other rm variants.
Session-context and gateway binding tests that assert set_current_session_key/reset_current_session_key usage.

related_files.relationship: Reads/writes command_allowlist and approvals config used by load_permanent_allowlist and save_permanent_allowlist.
related_files.reason_to_check: If config format changes (approvals block or command_allowlist key), persistence and loading logic here must be adjusted.

related_files.relationship: Gateway runner is expected to call set_current_session_key/reset_current_session_key before/after agent execution; tests assert this binding.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to session binding semantics requires coordinated updates in gateway runner to preserve per-session approval isolation.

related_files.relationship: Optional dependency for _smart_approve to get an auxiliary LLM client.
related_files.reason_to_check: If smart approval integration is modified, ensure client/task signatures are preserved.

data_constants_to_check: DANGEROUS_PATTERNS: ensure regex descriptions (canonical keys) are stable
HERMES_SESSION_KEY env behavior: contextvar fallback must remain intact

insights.title: Load permanent command allowlist on module import
insights.problem: Permanent allowlist for commands wasn't loaded early, potentially causing approval checks to be incomplete until explicit initialization.
insights.root_cause: Loading of config-derived allowlist relied on explicit call paths rather than module import time initialization.
insights.solution: Call load_permanent_allowlist() at module import to ensure allowlist is populated for approval checks.
insights.lesson: Module-level initialization for static allowlists is acceptable when the data is needed by many callers and has no heavy side effects; prefer explicit import-time init for deterministic behavior.

insights.title: Detect and gate self-termination commands (pkill/killall) via approval flow
insights.problem: Agent could inadvertently craft shell commands that terminate its own process (e.g., 'pkill -f gateway', 'killall hermes').
insights.root_cause: Dangerous command pattern list did not include pkill/killall patterns targeting hermes/gateway/cli.py.
insights.solution: Add a regex pattern to DANGEROUS_PATTERNS detecting (pkill|killall) targeting hermes, gateway, or cli.py and mark them as self-termination so approval flow can intercept.
insights.lesson_learned: Commands that could self-terminate or escalate require explicit detection; expand pattern lists and include descriptive messages to trigger approval flows.

tests/tools/test_approval.py.short_description: Unit tests that exercise dangerous command detection and the approval/session flow.
tests/tools/test_approval.py.description:
This test module provides comprehensive unit coverage for the approval subsystem. It verifies the correctness of dangerous command detection (many shell constructs, multiline cases, process substitution, tee and redirection to sensitive paths, find -exec variants, SQL statements), per-session/session-key context behavior, pending approval queue semantics, aliasing/uniqueness of pattern keys, and config-driven approval mode parsing. The tests simulate multithreaded scenarios to ensure contextvars and thread lock protections keep pending approvals attached to the originating session. They also exercise CLI prompt behavior via prompt_dangerous_approval in some cases (mocking or using provided helpers).

The test file uses unittest.mock.patch to replace hermes_cli.config.load_config in mode parsing tests and uses ast parsing of gateway/run.py to ensure the gateway runner binds session keys. Many test classes group related patterns (rm flags, process substitution, tee patterns, find -exec, sensitive redirections) so the test suite captures both positive and negative cases for each pattern. Tests also include regression checks for previously seen bugs (e.g., false positives for filenames starting with 'r' and pattern-key collisions).

key_constructs.name: TestApprovalModeParsing
key_constructs.purpose: Tests mapping of config 'approvals.mode' values to module approval modes via _get_approval_mode.
key_constructs.reasoning: Verifies YAML boolean edge-case handling where unquoted bare words may be parsed as booleans.

key_constructs.name: TestDetectDangerousRm
key_constructs.purpose: Contains tests to ensure rm -r/-rf and --recursive are detected as dangerous.
key_constructs.reasoning: Core detection behavior for destructive file operations.

key_constructs.name: TestSessionKeyContext
key_constructs.purpose: Multithreaded tests that assert contextvar session-key binding and that pending approvals remain attached to originating session.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates that the module's contextvars + threading interactions are correct; simulates worker threads.

key_constructs.name: TestPatternKeyUniqueness
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that derived pattern keys for similar regexes are distinct to avoid accidental cross-approval.
key_constructs.reasoning: Regression guard against historical bug where legacy key derivation produced collisions.

key_behaviors: verify dangerous pattern detection across many command forms
exercise per-session approval queues and contextvar session binding
regression tests for false positives and key collisions

pitfalls.mistake: Running this file in isolation while environment or imports differ (e.g., hermes_cli.config missing or gateway/run.py path not present).
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that patch or parse gateway/run.py or load config may fail unexpectedly.
pitfalls.prevention: Run tests under project root with proper package imports; use pytest invocation provided in edit_checklist.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming exact line numbers in test snippet references — tests use AST to validate gateway.run.run_sync uses session binding by name, so renaming those helper names in gateway.run will break the test.
pitfalls.consequence: False negatives in test expectations unrelated to approval module changes.
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying gateway.run you'd need to update tests or preserve function names used by AST checks.

key_sections: TestDetectDangerousRm: basic rm detection
TestSessionKeyContext: concurrency/contextvar behavior
TestPatternKeyUniqueness: key alias/uniqueness regression tests
skip_unless_needed: extensive multiline pattern tests if only changing unrelated approval persistence

relevant_snippets.description: Approval mode parsing tests that patch hermes_cli.config.load_config.
Basic rm recursive detection tests demonstrating expected outputs of detect_dangerous_command.
Session key/context tests, including the AST-based assertion that gateway.run.run_sync binds the session context.

related_files.relationship: Directly tests functions and behaviors defined in this module.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any edit to detection regexes, session handling, aliasing, or persistent allowlist behavior must be validated by updating or running these tests.

related_files.relationship: TestSessionKeyContext inspects gateway/run.py AST to assert it calls set_current_session_key/reset_current_session_key.
related_files.reason_to_check: Renaming or moving session-binding calls in gateway.run will break tests that look for those call names.

data_constants_to_check: DANGEROUS_PATTERNS: verify new/changed patterns have tests
_PATTERN_KEY_ALIASES: ensure persisted allowlist keys remain valid

insights.title: Add tests for pkill/killall self-termination detection
insights.problem: New dangerous pattern needs test coverage.
insights.root_cause: None
insights.solution: Added tests asserting dangerous detection for pkill/killall targeting hermes/gateway and not flagging unrelated pkill usage.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding safety patterns, accompany them with positive and negative unit tests to avoid false positives.

tools/credential_files.py.short_description: Registry and helpers to expose selected host files (credentials, skills, caches) into remote sandboxes safely.
tools/credential_files.py.description:
This module centralises the logic for exposing host-side files into remote execution sandboxes (Docker, Modal, SSH, Modal-like environments). It exists because remote backends create isolated sandboxes without host files; the agent needs a safe, auditable way to mount or upload credential files, skill contents, and various host-side cache directories so skills and tools can run inside those sandboxes. The module provides two complementary APIs: a session-scoped registry for credential files that skills can declare at runtime (via required_credential_files) or which users configure in config.yaml, and directory/file enumerators for skills and cache directories for backends that either bind-mount directories or upload files individually.

Key design decisions visible in the code include: (1) Using a ContextVar-backed dict (_registered_files_var) for the session-scoped credential registry to avoid cross-session leakage in long-running gateway processes (this is documented in commit metadata), (2) Strict path validation and containment checks using Path.resolve() and relative_to() to prevent absolute paths or path traversal from exposing arbitrary host files, and (3) Differentiated handling for skills directories vs. individual files: when symlinks exist in a skills directory a sanitized copy is created to avoid bind-mounting symlinks (which would follow to arbitrary host files), while the symlink-free case returns the original path to avoid unnecessary copying. The module interacts with hermes_constants.get_hermes_home/get_hermes_dir for locating the HERMES_HOME layout, and imports agent.skill_utils.get_external_skills_dirs when enumerating external skill directories (import is guarded to avoid hard dependency).

key_constructs.name: _registered_files_var
key_constructs.purpose: ContextVar that holds the session-local mapping of registered container_path -> host_path.
key_constructs.reasoning: Any edits must preserve the ContextVar-backed approach because it's the mechanism used to isolate credential registrations between sessions in a multi-session gateway process.

key_constructs.name: _get_registered
key_constructs.purpose: Return the dict stored in the ContextVar for the current context/session, creating and setting an empty dict if none exists.
key_constructs.reasoning: Callers across the module use this to access and manipulate session-scoped registrations; altering its behaviour changes registry lifetime semantics.

key_constructs.name: _resolve_hermes_home
key_constructs.purpose: Wrapper that imports and returns hermes_constants.get_hermes_home() as a Path.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralises resolution of HERMES_HOME allowing tests to patch hermes_constants. Keep this wrapper or update call sites coherently if replaced.

key_constructs.name: register_credential_file
key_constructs.purpose: Validate a relative credential path (no absolute / no traversal), ensure it exists under HERMES_HOME, and register a mapping from container path to host resolved path in the session registry.
key_constructs.reasoning: This enforces the security model (deny absolute paths & traversal) and populates the session registry used by get_credential_file_mounts; modifications should preserve these validation steps.

key_constructs.name: register_credential_files
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to register multiple credential entries (strings or dicts) and return a list of relative paths that were missing on the host.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by skill frontmatter parsing; it converts heterogeneous entries into calls to register_credential_file and aggregates missing-files results.

key_constructs.name: _load_config_files
key_constructs.purpose: Read terminal.credential_files from config.yaml under HERMES_HOME once per process (cached) and return list of host/container mappings.
key_constructs.reasoning: Caches results in _config_files. Tests may call reset_config_cache() to force reload. It uses yaml.safe_load and validates file containment similarly to runtime registration.

key_constructs.name: _config_files
key_constructs.purpose: Module-level cache of the parsed terminal.credential_files entries to avoid repeated config reads.
key_constructs.reasoning: Reset via reset_config_cache() for tests; edits must account for process-level caching behaviour and its testability.

key_constructs.name: get_credential_file_mounts
key_constructs.purpose: Combine session-registered credential files and config-based files into a list of mount dicts ({host_path, container_path}), re-checking file existence.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the canonical API remote backends call at sandbox creation time to obtain bind-mount entries; it revalidates host files to handle deletions after registration.

key_constructs.name: get_skills_directory_mount
key_constructs.purpose: Return mount entries for local and external skills directories (sanitising symlinks via _safe_skills_path when found).
key_constructs.reasoning: Backends that prefer bind mounts use this; it must correctly map local and external skills directories to the container layout and handle symlink cases carefully.

key_constructs.name: _safe_skills_path
key_constructs.purpose: If skills dir contains symlinks, create a temp directory with regular files (no symlinks), copy contents, register an atexit cleanup, and return the safe temp path; otherwise return original path string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents bind-mounting symlinks that could exfiltrate host files; it keeps a global tempdir reference to avoid accumulation and registers cleanup via atexit.

key_constructs.name: iter_skills_files
key_constructs.purpose: Enumerate individual skill files (skip symlinks) and return per-file dicts for backends that upload files individually rather than mount directories.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by upload-style backends (Modal/Daytona) to synchronise skills contents before each command; must skip symlinks and only include files.

key_constructs.name: _CACHE_DIRS
key_constructs.purpose: Mapping of new cache subpaths to old directory names used by get_hermes_dir for backwards compatibility.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by cache-mount helpers to check multiple legacy layouts; edits to directory layout must update this list.

key_constructs.name: get_cache_directory_mounts
key_constructs.purpose: Return bind-mount entries for cache subdirectories that exist on the host (documents, images, audio, screenshots).
key_constructs.reasoning: Backends that mount host directories call this; it uses hermes_constants.get_hermes_dir to resolve host directories and always maps to the new container layout.

key_constructs.name: iter_cache_files
key_constructs.purpose: Enumerate individual cache files (skip symlinks) and return per-file dicts for upload-style backends.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to resync cache files before each remote command; it must mirror the same filtering and container path layout as the mount helper.

key_constructs.name: clear_credential_files
key_constructs.purpose: Clear the session-scoped credential registry (used on session reset).
key_constructs.reasoning: Important for session lifecycle; it manipulates the ContextVar-backed registry via _get_registered().

key_constructs.name: reset_config_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Clear the parsed config cache to force _load_config_files to re-read config.yaml on next access (intended for tests).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by tests to control cached state; changes must preserve this testing hook or provide equivalent.

key_behaviors: registers credential files for mounting into remote sandboxes
validates and rejects absolute paths and path traversal
aggregates skill-registered and config-based credential mounts
creates symlink-free copy of skills directory when symlinks are present
enumerates files for upload-style backends and returns container paths
provides per-session registry that can be cleared on session reset

pitfalls.mistake: Treating the registered files dict as a simple global module-level dict instead of a ContextVar-backed value.
pitfalls.consequence: Cross-session leakage of credential file registrations in a multi-session gateway process, potentially exposing credentials between users/sessions.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve use of _registered_files_var and access it only through _get_registered(); avoid replacing it with a plain global dict.

pitfalls.mistake: Using path membership checks without resolving symlinks (e.g. not calling Path.resolve() before relative_to()).
pitfalls.consequence: Path traversal or symlink attacks could allow a declared relative path to escape HERMES_HOME and expose arbitrary host files.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the resolve() + relative_to() containment check pattern used in register_credential_file and _load_config_files.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the sanitized skills-copy logic without accounting for bind-mount symlink semantics.
pitfalls.consequence: A skills directory containing symlinks may cause a bind mount to expose host files outside the skills tree, undermining sandbox security.
pitfalls.prevention: Retain or carefully replace _safe_skills_path behaviour; if changing it, ensure new implementation still eliminates symlink-following risk and handles cleanup.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming config parsing always succeeds or that _config_files is always populated.
pitfalls.consequence: Uncaught exceptions or stale cached results could result in missing mounts or silent failures; tests relying on reset_config_cache may flake.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect reset_config_cache() test hook, and preserve try/except around config reads to avoid raising on malformed user config.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying container path layout without updating callers/backends and tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Remote backends (Docker/Modal) and code that expects certain container paths for skills or caches will break.
pitfalls.prevention: If changing container path conventions, update all backends and related code that consumes these mount/container_path values.

key_sections: register_credential_file: contains core validation/security checks and how entries are recorded
get_credential_file_mounts: canonical consumer-visible combining of session and config mounts
_load_config_files: shows config.yaml parsing and caching (reset_config_cache relates to tests)
get_skills_directory_mount and _safe_skills_path: symlink handling and temp-copy behaviour for skills
get_cache_directory_mounts and iter_cache_files: cache directory resolution via hermes_constants.get_hermes_dir
skip_unless_needed: module-level constants like _CACHE_DIRS (short and self-explanatory)
iter_skills_files if you only need mount-directory semantics (used by upload-style backends)

related_files.relationship: Provides get_hermes_home and get_hermes_dir used to resolve host paths and legacy directory layouts; authoritative source for HERMES_HOME layout.
related_files.reason_to_check: If container or host directory layout or the get_hermes_dir/get_hermes_home implementations change, update path resolution and containment checks accordingly.

related_files.relationship: Provides get_external_skills_dirs referenced when enumerating external skills directories.
related_files.reason_to_check: If external skills discovery changes (paths/indexing), the container_path mapping and enumeration logic here may need adjustment.

related_files.relationship: Consumers of the mount enumerators (get_credential_file_mounts, get_skills_directory_mount, iter_cache_files) to create bind-mounts or upload files into sandboxes.
related_files.reason_to_check: Backends assume the container_path layout returned by this module; changes here require corresponding backend updates.

insights.title: Isolate credential file registry per context using ContextVar
insights.problem: Module-level registry for credential files could leak between sessions in the gateway (cross-session credential exposure).
insights.root_cause: A global dict _registered_files was used for session-scoped files in a multi-session gateway process.
insights.solution: Back the registry with a ContextVar (_registered_files_var) and provide _get_registered() to access session-local registry; update register/get/clear callers to use _get_registered(); clear the ContextVar-backed registry on session reset.
insights.lesson_learned: In multi-session servers, per-session registries must be context-local (ContextVars or explicit session objects). Avoid global mutable state for secrets or mounts.

mini_swe_runner.py.short_description: Standalone Mini-SWE runner that uses Hermes execution environments and emits Hermes-format trajectories.
mini_swe_runner.py.description:
This script provides a compact SWE (Software Engineer) runner that drives an LLM to execute shell commands in an execution environment (local, Docker, or Modal) and emits interaction trajectories in Hermes-Agent's trajectory format. It exists to let users run single tasks or batches of prompts through a loop where an LLM suggests function/tool calls (the provided 'terminal' function) and the runner executes them in a sandboxed environment, collects results, and serializes the conversation/ tool interactions into the Hermes trajectory JSONL shape used by batch_runner.py and trajectory_compressor.py.

The script centralizes three responsibilities: (1) wiring to a provider client for chat completions (OpenAI/OpenRouter/Anthropic via auxiliary_client), (2) creating and managing a transient execution environment using tools.environments.{local,docker,modal}, and (3) converting the in-memory dialog + tool call/response sequence into the Hermes trajectory format (XML-like <tool_call>/<tool_response> wrappers). It is structured as a small CLI utility (uses python-fire) and designed for interactive command-line experimentation or batch processing of prompt files. The runner is intentionally minimal and assumes the model will return structured tool call metadata (assistant_message.tool_calls) and that tools return accessible fields like id/type/function/arguments.

key_constructs.name: TERMINAL_TOOL_DEFINITION
key_constructs.purpose: JSON schema-like definition of the 'terminal' tool exposed to the model (name, description, parameters).
key_constructs.reasoning: This constant defines the tool signature that the LLM is expected to call. Any changes to the tool contract or parameter names will need to be reflected both here and in how tool calls are parsed and executed in run_task.

key_constructs.name: create_environment
key_constructs.purpose: Factory that instantiates an execution environment object based on env_type ('local', 'docker', 'modal').
key_constructs.reasoning: This function abstracts environment creation and lazy-imports the environment implementations from tools.environments.*, which means edits here should preserve the expected constructor arguments (cwd, timeout, image) and error semantics.

key_constructs.name: MiniSWERunner
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates the runner lifecycle: LLM client initialization, environment lifecycle, iterative API loop, tool execution, and trajectory conversion.
