insights.lesson_learned: SDKs differ in how they treat missing vs empty credentials — use an explicit sentinel where code paths expect a non-empty string to indicate 'no auth required'. Keep consistent sentinel usage across similar resolvers.

insights.title: Remove MiniMax /v1→/anthropic auto-correction to respect user overrides
insights.problem: Minimax-specific auto-correction forced /v1 -> /anthropic, preventing users from overriding to OpenAI-compatible /v1 endpoints needed in some regions.
insights.root_cause: Special-case code for minimax provider modified user-specified base_url unconditionally.
insights.solution: Remove minimax-specific auto-correction. Keep generic URL-suffix detection for /anthropic to choose anthropic_messages; allow user override to /v1 to keep chat_completions mode.

insights.title: Guard .strip() against None values in model config
insights.problem: cfg.get('default').strip() crashed when YAML had key with null value.
insights.root_cause: Did not handle None returned by .get() when YAML 'key:' yields None.
insights.solution: Wrap with (cfg.get('default') or '').strip() and for base_url similarly.

tests/test_runtime_provider_resolution.p.short_description: Unit tests verifying provider resolution precedence, credential pools, explicit overrides, and env/config interactions.
tests/test_runtime_provider_resolution.p.description: This test module is the behavioral specification for the runtime provider resolver. It exercises permutations of credential pool selection, explicit override handling (explicit_api_key/explicit_base_url), model config precedence, environment variable precedence (OPENAI_API_KEY vs OPENROUTER_API_KEY and OPENROUTER_BASE_URL), and named custom provider lookups from config. Tests use monkeypatch extensively to stub resolve_provider, load_pool, _get_model_config, provider credential resolvers, and to set/del environment variables. Many historical bug fixes and edge-case semantics are asserted here (e.g., OpenRouter key priority, config api_key precedence for custom providers, skipping pools on explicit overrides, Anthropic base_url precedence when model_cfg.provider==anthropic). The tests intentionally avoid network I/O by monkeypatching functions that would call external services.

key_constructs.name: test_resolve_runtime_provider_uses_credential_pool
key_constructs.purpose: Assert that when a credential pool has credentials, resolve_runtime_provider returns the pool token and includes credential_pool metadata.
key_constructs.reasoning: Verifies pool select path and runtime dict composition from pooled credentials.

key_constructs.name: test_resolve_runtime_provider_anthropic_pool_respects_config_base_url
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure Anthropic resolution favors the model config base_url when config provider==anthropic, even if pool entry has different base_url.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encodes special-case precedence for Anthropic base_url used in production to route via proxies or custom endpoints.

key_constructs.name: test_resolve_runtime_provider_anthropic_explicit_override_skips_pool
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure explicit_api_key/explicit_base_url bypass pool selection and provider token minting (no pool.load or resolve_anthropic_token calls).
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates that explicit override semantics are enforced and side effects are avoided.

key_constructs.name: test_resolve_runtime_provider_openrouter_explicit
key_constructs.purpose: Validate openrouter explicit overrides set base_url, api_key, trims trailing slash, and label source as 'explicit'.
key_constructs.reasoning: Checks normalization and source labeling for explicit openrouter overrides.

key_constructs.name: test_openrouter_key_takes_priority_over_openai_key
key_constructs.purpose: Regression test ensuring OPENROUTER_API_KEY is preferred over OPENAI_API_KEY when targeting openrouter.ai.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents leaking an unrelated OPENAI_API_KEY to OpenRouter and encodes bugfix from issue history.

handles_entities: environment variables (OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_BASE_URL, AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY, etc.)
key_behaviors: assert credential pool selection and propagation
assert explicit overrides skip pool and token-refresh flows
assert environment/config precedence rules
assert provider-specific api_mode and base_url decisions

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying resolve_runtime_provider behavior without updating tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Many tests will fail because they capture precise precedence and error handling.
pitfalls.prevention: Run and update tests in this file when changing resolution semantics; ensure monkeypatch targets in tests still exist.

pitfalls.mistake: Allowing real network calls (e.g., _auto_detect_local_model) during tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Flaky or slow tests that depend on external services.
pitfalls.prevention: Tests rely on monkeypatching to avoid network I/O; preserve that pattern or add appropriate mocks.

pitfalls.mistake: Not isolating environment variable changes across tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Order-dependent failures or test pollution.
pitfalls.prevention: Tests use monkeypatch.setenv and monkeypatch.delenv; maintain isolation when adding tests.

OpenRouter/env precedence and custom provider tests (middle section)

insights.title: Add tests for auth fallthrough behavior
insights.problem: Auto-detection fallthrough on revoked OAuth tokens lacked tests.
insights.root_cause: Previous behavior surfaced AuthError for auto-detect cases with stale tokens; missing coverage allowed regression.
insights.solution: Add three tests validating Nous and Codex auto-fallthrough to OpenRouter, and that explicit 'nous' request still raises AuthError.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing auto-detection and fallthrough logic, add tests that assert the difference between explicit selection and auto behavior.

tools/file_tools.py.short_description: File manipulation tools used by the agent (read/write/patch with safety, dedup, and sandboxing).
tools/file_tools.py.description:
This module provides the file-related tool implementations used by the agent to read, write, patch, and track file accesses in a multi-task environment. It exists to centralise safety checks (device files, sensitive system paths, internal hermes caches), enforce read-size limits, redact sensitive content before returning it to models, and coordinate access through sandboxed terminal environments via ShellFileOperations. The module also implements per-task caching and deduplication of reads, consecutive-read loop detection, and bookkeeping used by other subsystems (e.g., context compression and the terminal tool environment lifecycle).

Design-wise the module separates pure policy/guard checks (blocked device paths, sensitive system path check) and bookkeeping (read tracker, dedup) from the I/O execution (delegated to ShellFileOperations created per task via _get_file_ops). It uses process-wide cached configuration for read-size limits (loaded once from hermes_cli.config), and leverages hermes_constants.get_hermes_home() to avoid profile escapes when blocking internal Hermes cache files. Thread-safety is a primary consideration: multiple locks exist (_file_ops_lock, _read_tracker_lock) guarding caches and task-specific state, and per-task creation locks are used to avoid duplicate environment creation. The module is integrated with other parts of the system — notably tools.terminal_tool (to obtain or create sandbox environments) and agent.redact.redact_sensitive_text (to remove secrets from returned content).

The file shown is truncated but already contains the main entrypoint for reading files (read_file_tool), the environment/file-ops lifecycle helper (_get_file_ops), and several utility guards and trackers. Edits must respect concurrency, environment lifecycle interaction, and the documented behavioural guards (device blocking, hermes internal file blocking, sensitive path write denial, read-size guard and dedup logic). A past commit fixed a security issue by switching blocked-path checks to use get_hermes_home(), so multi-profile resolution must be preserved.

key_constructs.name: _EXPECTED_WRITE_ERRNOS
key_constructs.purpose: Errno values that represent expected write-denial errors (access/permission/readonly fs).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to suppress noisy error logs for predictable write failures; keep consistent with write-path handling and error classification.

key_constructs.name: _DEFAULT_MAX_READ_CHARS
key_constructs.purpose: Fallback character limit for read responses when config is absent or invalid.
key_constructs.reasoning: Acts as a safety cap to prevent returning extremely large content that could exceed model context windows.

key_constructs.name: _get_max_read_chars
key_constructs.purpose: Load and cache the configured file_read_max_chars from config.yaml, fallback to default.
key_constructs.reasoning: Caching behavior is process-wide; callers rely on this value being stable for the process lifetime.

key_constructs.name: _BLOCKED_DEVICE_PATHS
key_constructs.purpose: Literal device paths that must never be read (infinite output or blocking).
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents deadlocks or huge unbounded reads; checks are done on literal paths (no symlink resolution).

key_constructs.name: _is_blocked_device
key_constructs.purpose: Return True if a requested path is a known-blocked device or an fd alias under /proc.
key_constructs.reasoning: Relies on expanduser-only; must not follow symlinks — the function's semantics are intentionally literal.

key_constructs.name: _SENSITIVE_PATH_PREFIXES
key_constructs.purpose: Path prefixes that should be refused for write operations without elevated approval.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to prevent accidental writes to system-critical directories; comparisons use realpath in _check_sensitive_path.

key_constructs.name: _SENSITIVE_EXACT_PATHS
key_constructs.purpose: Exact sensitive path set (e.g., docker sockets) refused for direct writes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Exact-match list for high-risk targets; keep in sync if new sensitive sockets are introduced.

key_constructs.name: _check_sensitive_path
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve a target path and return an error message if it targets a sensitive location.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses os.path.realpath and expanduser — callers should present user-specified paths; exceptions map to a safe fallback.

key_constructs.name: _is_expected_write_exception
key_constructs.purpose: Classify exceptions that are expected write-denials to avoid excessive logging.
key_constructs.reasoning: Identifies PermissionError and specific OSError.errno values; used by write/patch logic to differentiate routine denials from unexpected failures.

key_constructs.name: _file_ops_lock
key_constructs.purpose: Mutex protecting the _file_ops_cache map.
key_constructs.reasoning: Guard against concurrent creation/lookup/caching of ShellFileOperations per task.

key_constructs.name: _file_ops_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Per-task cache of ShellFileOperations instances.
key_constructs.reasoning: Reused to avoid recreating sandboxed environments unnecessarily; must be invalidated when environments are cleaned up.

key_constructs.name: _read_tracker_lock
key_constructs.purpose: Mutex protecting the _read_tracker structure.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures thread-safe updates to per-task read history, dedup maps, and consecutive-read counters.

key_constructs.name: _read_tracker
key_constructs.purpose: Per-task state tracking reads: last_key, consecutive count, dedup map, read_history, and timestamps.
key_constructs.reasoning: Central to deduplication, loop detection, and staleness checks — any change to layout must be backward-compatible.

key_constructs.name: _get_file_ops
key_constructs.purpose: Obtain (or create) a ShellFileOperations instance for a given task_id, respecting TERMINAL_ENV settings and sandbox lifecycle.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function coordinates with tools.terminal_tool internals (_active_environments, _create_environment, locks). It uses per-task creation locks and caches file_ops. Edits must maintain synchronization protocol and interaction points with terminal_tool.

key_constructs.name: clear_file_ops_cache
key_constructs.purpose: Invalidate cached ShellFileOperations for a single task or all tasks.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to force re-creation of sandbox file-ops (e.g., after environment termination); maintains thread-safety via _file_ops_lock.

key_constructs.name: read_file_tool
key_constructs.purpose: Primary exposed file-read tool: enforces guards, dedup, read-size caps, redaction, and returns JSON-serialisable results used by the agent.
key_constructs.reasoning: Entry point relied upon by the tool dispatcher; implements multiple safety layers (device checks, internal hermes file blocks using get_hermes_home, dedup checks, char-count guard, redaction), and updates per-task read trackers. Must preserve exact JSON keys expected by callers (error, content, path, dedup, _warning, _hint, etc.).

key_constructs.name: get_read_files_summary
key_constructs.purpose: Return a summary of files/regions read by a task, used by context compression to preserve read history.
key_constructs.reasoning: Reads from _read_tracker; format expected by compression routines and higher-level components; must remain stable.

key_constructs.name: clear_read_tracker
key_constructs.purpose: Clear read-tracking state for a task or all tasks (cleanup to prevent leaks).
key_constructs.reasoning: Important in long-running gateway processes to avoid memory leakage.

key_constructs.name: reset_file_dedup
key_constructs.purpose: Clear deduplication caches after context compression, so subsequent reads return full content again.
key_constructs.reasoning: Semantics are important: only the 'dedup' map is cleared, not full read history — compression relies on this behaviour.

key_constructs.name: notify_other_tool_call
key_constructs.purpose: Reset consecutive read/search counters when a tool other than read/search is called.
key_constructs.reasoning: Maintains correct 'consecutive' semantics so only truly consecutive repeated reads trigger warnings/blocks.

key_behaviors: blocks reading device files that hang or return infinite output
refuses direct reads of internal Hermes cache files to prevent prompt injection
enforces character-count read limits configurable via file_read_max_chars
redacts sensitive text from returned file content before exposing it to models
deduplicates repeated identical reads for a task and blocks read loops
provides per-task ShellFileOperations instances backed by terminal environments

pitfalls.mistake: Changing path-check semantics to resolve symlinks for device-block checks (e.g., using realpath instead of literal path)
pitfalls.consequence: Could defeat the intentional literal-path check that prevents model-specified device paths being bypassed or accidentally allowed; may incorrectly allow reads that hang or produce infinite output.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the current expanduser-only (literal) semantics when checking device paths; understand why symlink resolution was intentionally avoided.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying the structure or keys of _read_tracker/dedup maps without maintaining backwards compatibility
pitfalls.consequence: Context compression, read dedup and staleness detection may break or return incorrect stubs; other components expecting particular formats will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Retain existing shape and key names (last_key, consecutive, read_history, dedup, read_timestamps) or handle migration safely.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the use of hermes_constants.get_hermes_home() when blocking internal cache files
pitfalls.consequence: Multi-profile setups could bypass internal-file protections, enabling prompt-injection access to hub/index-cache or similar files.
pitfalls.prevention: Always use get_hermes_home() for internal path checks as committed in the security bugfix.

pitfalls.mistake: Breaking lock ordering between _file_ops_lock, _read_tracker_lock and terminal_tool locks
pitfalls.consequence: Potential deadlocks or race conditions when concurrent threads create environments or access per-task state.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect existing locking logic and the sequence used in _get_file_ops; test concurrent access paths when modifying locks.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing JSON response keys or the shape of read_file_tool outputs
pitfalls.consequence: Tool dispatcher or calling code (models/skills) expecting specific JSON fields (error, content, path, dedup, _warning, _hint) will misinterpret results.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep response schema stable or version outputs carefully and update all callers simultaneously.

key_sections: _get_file_ops: coordination with terminal_tool and sandbox lifecycle (env creation, caching, locks)
Device & internal-file guards at the top of read_file_tool: these are critical safety checks
Dedup & read-tracker logic: how dedup keys are formed, when mtime is recorded, and how consecutive reads are tracked
Character-count guard and redaction: ordering matters (check size before redaction)
Functions that manipulate _read_tracker: reset_file_dedup, clear_read_tracker, get_read_files_summary
skip_unless_needed: Constants definitions for errno or size limits (readable quickly but low cognitive load)
Lower-level or truncated write/patch sections (if not working on writes specifically)

related_files.relationship: Creates and manages sandboxed runtime environments; _get_file_ops interacts with its _active_environments, _create_environment, and locking helpers.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to environment creation, lifecycle, or lock names must be reflected here to keep _get_file_ops consistent.

related_files.relationship: Defines ShellFileOperations.read_file and other I/O primitives used by this module to perform actual file reads/writes.
related_files.reason_to_check: If read_file result shape changes (to_dict keys), read_file_tool must be updated to match formatting, total_lines, truncated, file_size fields used here.

related_files.relationship: Provides redact_sensitive_text used to remove secrets from returned content.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to redaction behaviour affect what is returned to models; redaction is intentionally performed after size checks.

related_files.relationship: get_hermes_home() is used to compute internal Hermes cache paths that must be blocked; multi-profile correctness depends on this.
related_files.reason_to_check: If hermes home resolution semantics change, the internal-file blocking logic here must be re-evaluated.

tests_to_run: Run any platform/gateway-specific tests if touching terminal environment logic

insights.title: Use get_hermes_home() for security blocked path checks
insights.problem: Security checks relied on literal ~/.hermes path to block hub index reads; profile HERMES_HOME would bypass these checks.
insights.root_cause: Hardcoded path used in blocked directories check.
insights.solution: Use hermes_constants.get_hermes_home() so blocked paths are resolved relative to the active profile.
insights.lesson_learned: Security path checks must use canonical config/home resolution to avoid escapes in multi-profile scenarios.

insights.title: Tests for per-user memory scoping
insights.problem: Validates that gateway user_id flows through MemoryManager into providers and plugins.
insights.root_cause: Emergent bug where gateway sessions shared memory across platform users required tests to lock behavior.
insights.solution: Added RecordingProvider and tests ensuring initialize_all forwards user_id, Mem0/Honcho respect kwargs, and AIAgent stores user_id.
insights.lesson: Add unit tests for runtime-scoped initialization flows for pluggable providers — they document expected kwargs and prevent regressions.

insights.title: Tests for summary cooldown and temperature omission
insights.problem: Need to ensure summary generation does not inject temperature and that failure cooldown prevents repeated LLM calls.
insights.root_cause: Behavioral changes require explicit tests.
insights.solution: Added tests that patch call_llm to assert temperature not present in kwargs and that a failing call enters cooldown so subsequent attempts are skipped.
insights.lesson_learned: Add regression tests for both correct call parameters and cooldown/backoff semantics.

insights.title: Update resume test to match corrected flush call
insights.problem: Test expected gateway session_key to be passed to _async_flush_memories; implementation no longer passes it.
insights.root_cause: Test reflected old behavior with extra argument.
insights.solution: Adjusted test to assert _async_flush_memories called with only session_id.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing helper signatures, update tests accordingly to avoid false failures and to assert corrected behavior.

insights.title: Regenerate uv.lock and bump agent-client-protocol dependency
insights.problem: Dependency versions needed updating and uv.lock needed regeneration.
insights.root_cause: Upstream bump in agent-client-protocol.
insights.solution: Updated uv.lock entries for agent-client-protocol to 0.9.0 and updated requires-dist markers.
insights.lesson_learned: Lockfiles must be regenerated and metadata updated when updating dependency versions to avoid runtime mismatch.

insights.title: Comprehensive Mem0 API v2 tests
insights.problem: Mem0 provider needed robust tests to validate filters= param, write attribution, and dict/list response shapes.
insights.root_cause: Multiple contributors changed Mem0 integration; regression risk without tests.
insights.solution: Added FakeClientV2 and tests exercising search/get_all/add flows, prefetch behavior, and default preservation.
insights.lesson_learned: When adapting to third-party API changes, add focused unit tests that capture both old and new response shapes and parameter conventions.

insights.title: kill_modal script updated to reference hermes-agent apps (not swe-rex)
insights.problem: Script referenced swe-rex app name patterns after refactor.
insights.root_cause: Modal app naming changed to hermes-agent.
insights.solution: Updated grep filters and messages to search for 'hermes-agent'.

insights.title: Comprehensive tests ensuring LLM content None is guarded across tools
insights.problem: Many tools previously used .message.content.strip() and crashed for content=None responses; tests were missing.
insights.root_cause: Diverse provider response formats not covered by tests.
insights.solution: Add test suite that constructs minimal response objects with content=None and verifies safe guards (or-guard) and extract_content_or_reasoning behavior across multiple tool call sites.
insights.lesson_learned: When integrating diverse LLM backends, add focused tests for API response variants like content=None and structured reasoning to prevent regressions.
